description:
AI Job Summary
Willing to complete a fully funded Certificate IV in Community Services or Diploma in Financial Counselling.
Understand Indigenous cultures and communicate effectively with community.
Have Microsoft Word/Excel skills (or willingness to learn).
 Sign up or log in to analyse my fit
Role Type
On-site • Permanent • Full-time • Entry Level
Pay Rate
34 AUD – 37.73 AUD (Hour)
Description
Grow Officer – Townsville

Indigenous Consumer Assistance Network (ICAN)

Empower your community. Build financial resilience. Create real change in Townsville and on Palm Island.

Kick-start a meaningful career in community services with ICAN. We’re looking for a motivated Townsville Local who wants to help strengthen financial wellbeing, support families, and contribute to long-term community empowerment.

What We Offer
Competitive salary and benefits
Full-time role with an early finish every Friday
5 weeks annual leave
Gifted time between Christmas and New Year
Ongoing paid professional development
Fully funded traineeship (Certificate IV or Diploma)
A supportive team who are committed to your success

This is an Identified Position and is open only to Aboriginal and/or Torres Strait Islander people, as permitted under section 25 of the Anti-Discrimination Act 1991 (Qld).

About Us

The Indigenous Consumer Assistance Network (ICAN) is a not-for-profit organisation supporting Indigenous peoples across Queensland through financial counselling, financial capability programs and consumer advocacy.

Our mission, “Empowering Indigenous Consumers,” guides everything we do from frontline service delivery to systemic advocacy aimed at improving laws and industry practices.

ICAN delivers services in Cairns, Townsville, Atherton, and several remote communities. We are now expanding opportunities on Palm Island, and we are committed to training, supporting, and developing a Palm Island local to grow into this important role.

Why This Opportunity Is Perfect

This is more than a job—it’s a pathway to a meaningful career. As a Grow Officer, you will:

Gain real hands-on experience working with community members
Build confidence and professional capability at your pace
Receive ongoing mentorship from experienced ICAN staff
Help strengthen financial resilience on Palm Island
Study towards a Certificate IV in Community Services or Diploma of Financial Counselling, fully funded by ICAN

Whether you’re looking for a long-term career or a stepping stone into broader community service work, this traineeship gives you the skills and support to succeed.

About the Role

As a Grow Officer, you will support financial wellbeing work in Townsville and on Palm Island through:

Training & Qualification
Complete a fully funded Cert IV or Diploma
Learn alongside ICAN’s experienced financial counsellors and capability workers
Develop practical knowledge you can use immediately in community
Financial Literacy & Client Support
Support clients with budgeting, financial education and accessing grants
Help community members understand bills, debt, consumer rights and financial options
Community Connection
Build relationships with local services, Elders, community groups and support networks
Work in a culturally safe, strengths-based way
Client Empowerment
Assist clients to access essential services, financial products and government supports
Reporting & Program Support
Collect basic data to help ICAN understand community needs and demonstrate outcomes
Who We’re Looking For
Essential Criteria
Identifies as Aboriginal and/or Torres Strait Islander, is of Indigenous descent and accepted by the community
Is excited to complete a Certificate IV in Community Services or Diploma in Financial Counselling
Has sound LLN skills (extra support available if needed)
Understands Indigenous cultures and communicates effectively with community
Has a positive attitude, willingness to learn and a strong sense of community pride
Skills & Knowledge
Eager to learn new skills and grow professionally
Passionate about helping others in a respectful and non-judgmental way
Basic computer skills (Microsoft Word/Excel or willingness to learn)
Interest in financial resilience and community support work
Why Join ICAN?
Start your career with practical skills, qualifications and ongoing mentoring
Work in a role that directly supports Townsville and Palm Island community members
Choose from two clear career pathways financial capability or financial counselling
Be part of a supportive organisation committed to Indigenous leadership and community empowerment
Selection Criteria

Please provide short responses to the following in your cover letter:

Understanding of Indigenous Cultures Describe your connection to Indigenous Culture and how this will help you engage with community members.
Interest in the Role Tell us why you are interested in a traineeship in financial capability or financial counselling.
Teamwork Skills Share an example of working with others to achieve a goal (work, school, sport, community—anything is fine).
Basic Computer Skills Explain your experience with Microsoft Word, Excel or similar programs (or your willingness to learn).

Simple, clear responses are welcome we want to get to know you.

How to Apply

Please upload:

Covering Letter (max 2 pages) addressing the selection criteria
Resume (max 4 pages) including two referees contactable by phone and email
Important Notes
Shortlisting is based on your responses to the selection criteria
Applicants who do not address the criteria will be ineligible for interview
Provide examples where possible (work, community, personal experiences)

ICAN is an Equal Employment Opportunity employer and uses merit-based selection to ensure the best person for the role.

description:
*Company Description*

Opasa is dedicated to empowering businesses by enhancing their technology capabilities and driving measurable growth through innovative solutions. With expertise in four core areas—Data/AI, Cloud, Cyber Security, and Digitization of customer journeys and business processes—Opasa offers consulting, advisory services, program management, and staff augmentation. Recognized as a trusted partner, Opasa helps solve complex IT challenges and supports the achievement of strategic business objectives. Clients rely on Opasa's technical expertise and strategic insight to deliver tangible results.

*Role Description*

Opasa is seeking an experienced IT Zabbix Consultant for a full-time, on-site role in the Greater Melbourne Area. The Consultant will be responsible for designing, implementing, and maintaining Zabbix monitoring solutions to address business and operational needs. Daily tasks include consulting with stakeholders, configuring and troubleshooting Zabbix systems, performing technical support, analyzing system performance, and ensuring efficient IT operations. The role requires close collaboration with clients and internal teams to deliver effective monitoring and IT solutions.

*Qualifications*

* 6+ years Experience in IT Consulting and Consulting with the ability to provide strategic guidance to clients
* Hands-on experience and skills with IT monitoring systems, ideally Zabbix.
* Understanding of monitoring metrics for compute and network devices.
* Strong Linux and IP network skills work experience required to work in Specialized Networks.
* Motivated and able to learn complex IT systems without significant guidance.
* Able to work constructively in a team with varying skills and capabilities
* Strong Analytical Skills and proficiency in assessing systems to deliver optimized solutions
* Practical experience and deep understanding of Information Technology and Technical Support
* Exceptional problem-solving abilities and attention to detail
* Proficient communication and collaboration skills for effective stakeholder engagement
* Experience with Zabbix is preferred.
* Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science, Information Technology, or a related field is preferred
* Relevant certifications in monitoring tools or IT consulting are a plus

Work Location: Melbourne,AUS

Employment type: Permanent FTE role

Preferred Visas: PR's , AU, NZ Citizens

Salary packages: As per market standard

Job Types: Full-time, Permanent

Pay: From $95,000.00 per year

Benefits:
* Referral program
* Work from home

Work Location: In person

positionName: Data Product Manager (12 Month Fixed Term)
description:
See yourself in our team

Retail Advanced Analytics and Data (RAAD) partners closely with Retail Banking Services to enable best practice across data, advanced analytics, and AI. The team plays a key role in shaping data‑led decision making across the Everyday Banking domain, delivering trusted, well‑governed data products that support customers, colleagues, and the broader business.

You will join a diverse community of data and analytics professionals who combine strong technical capability with business context to solve meaningful problems at scale.

Do work that matters

This opportunity is available as a 12‑month fixed‑term contract or internal secondment.

As a Data Product Manager, you will support the execution of the domain data strategy and own the delivery, governance, and lifecycle management of high‑quality, reusable data products and data assets. You will help ensure data is discoverable, trustworthy, and fit‑for‑purpose, while meeting Group standards across data management, security, privacy, and record‑keeping.

The role has a strong focus on enabling ethical and effective use of data and AI, recognising that data quality and governance directly influence outcomes.

More specifically, you will:

Support execution of the domain data strategy aligned to business priorities and outcomes

Own the delivery, governance, and lifecycle of reusable Data Products and Data Assets, including business‑critical data, in line with Group policies

Define Data Products with clear business context and metadata to ensure discoverability, reuse, trust, and interoperability

Ensure compliance with Group standards across enterprise semantics, metadata, data lineage, retention, and disposal

Champion continuous improvement of the data estate, including modernisation, rationalisation, optimisation, and transition to strategic consumption pathways

Embed strong data management practices that enable effective and ethical use of data and AI

Partner with Crew Leads, Tech Leads, Delivery Product Owners, and CIO teams to deliver data product roadmaps

Enable effective data producer–consumer collaboration across Business Units and Support Units

Ensure Data Products comply with Group Information Security and Privacy policies

Set, monitor, and maintain data quality standards, including usage and quality metrics

Drive uplift and remediation where Data Products no longer meet required standards

Define and manage appropriate access and usage of Data Products and Data Assets

Promote data literacy and adoption of Group data management best practices

We’re interested in hearing from people who have:

8+ years’ experience across data management, data governance, data engineering, or control frameworks

Proven experience delivering enterprise‑scale data initiatives in complex or regulated environments

A strong data product mindset, aligned to business outcomes

Experience engaging and influencing stakeholders across business and technology teams

Passion for building capability, fostering collaboration, and driving continuous improvement

Experience supporting data cloud migration and exposure to the AWS ecosystem (desirable)

Tech skills

Strong background in data management, data engineering, stewardship, and large‑scale data platforms

Experience designing, deploying, and operating solutions in AWS or Snowflake environments

Hands‑on experience with language such as Python, SQL and tools like Alation, AWS Data Exchange, Glue and AWS integration services

Exposure to version control and CI/CD pipelines (for example, GitHub) is desirable

Qualifications

Bachelor’s degree in Engineering, or

Master’s degree in Computer Science or a related discipline

If you're already part of the Commonwealth Bank Group (including Bankwest, x15ventures), you'll need to apply through Sidekick to submit a valid application. We’re keen to support you with the next step in your career.

We're aware of some accessibility issues on this site, particularly for screen reader users. We want to make finding your dream job as easy as possible, so if you require additional support please contact HR Direct on 1800 989 696.

Advertising End Date: 17/05/2026

description:
Are you passionate about education and eager to be part of Australia’s most innovative hybrid teaching company?

At *Realus Education*, we combine *Human Expertise × AI Intelligence* to deliver a next-generation learning experience for students from *Year 1 to Year 12*.

Realus is part of *First Education & Technology Group Pty Ltd*, founded in 2009. With campuses in *Box Hill (Melbourne), Glen Waverley (Melbourne)* and *Chatswood (Sydney)*, and our award-winning AI platform *MarsLadder*, we are redefining how children learn – blending face-to-face teaching with intelligent, personalised AI support.

*Our Achievements*

Since *2009*, First Education & Technology Group has helped *over 10,000 K–12 students* achieve their academic goals.

From *2016 to 2024*, more than *20% of our senior students* achieved an *ATAR of 99.00+*, and across *nine consecutive years*, we proudly nurtured *25 ATAR 99.95 top scorers*.

Many of our students have gone on to win *scholarships*, gain entry to *selective schools*, and secure places in *Australia’s top universities* — making Realus Education a trusted choice for ambitious learners and families.

*Why Join Us*

✅ *Be part of an EdTech pioneer* – MarsLadder is recognised as a finalist for the *Bett Awards, The EdTech Awards, and GESS Education Awards*.

✅ *Clear career pathway* – Tutor → Senior Tutor → *Mentor* → *Campus Director*.

✅ *Equity opportunities* – Mentors and Campus Directors are eligible to join our *company equity option pool* and share in the company’s growth.

✅ *Supportive culture* – Warm, inclusive team with strong training and development support.

✅ *Cutting-edge teaching tools* – Teach alongside an advanced AI platform that automates feedback, tracks student progress, and enhances every lesson.

*Your Role*

As a Y1–Y6 English Tutor/Lecturer at Realus, you will:

· Deliver engaging lessons that spark curiosity and build strong foundations in English.

· Teach not only enrichment classes, but also exam-focused programs (e.g. Scholarship, Selective School and OC) where required.

· Use MarsLadder AI to create a seamless after-class learning loop – guiding students from explanation to practice, feedback, and mastery.

· Provide constructive feedback to parents and collaborate with the teaching team to improve lesson materials.

· Actively contribute to our mentor-led training system, sharing insights and improving our hybrid teaching model.

· Inspire students with positivity, patience, and energy.

*What We’re Looking For*

· *Strong communication skills in English*, with the ability to teach in full English.

· *Passion for education* and the ability to teach in a lively, inspiring, and student-friendly way.

· Willingness to learn and integrate AI tools into teaching.

· Attention to detail, professionalism, and a high standard of work.

· Experience in teaching exam-focused programs (e.g. Scholarship, Selective, OC, AMC) is highly regarded.

· Previous K–6 enrichment or tutoring experience preferred.

· Must hold (or be able to obtain) a *Working With Children Check* (Employee type).

· Availability to teach weekend classes.

*Benefits & Perks*

· ✅ Warm and inclusive work environment.

· ✅ Competitive salary and bonus structure.

· ✅ Clear promotion pathway leading to Mentor and Campus Director positions.

· ✅ Eligibility to join the *company equity option pool* at senior levels.

· ✅ Comprehensive training program (“Firestarter Plan”) – learn our *AI-powered teaching workflows, class engagement techniques, and company culture*.

*How to Apply*

We value *teaching quality, professionalism, and cultural fit*. Our recruitment process is designed to ensure every tutor we hire meets the highest standards.

*1 Initial Phone Call* – We will contact shortlisted candidates for a brief phone conversation to understand your background, subject expertise, and availability.

*2 Demo Assignment *– You will receive an email with *Demo instructions* (prepare a 10–15 minute teaching video)

*3 Demo Video Submission* – Record and submit your *10–15 minute demo video* for evaluation.

*4 Panel Review* – All of our full-time lecturers will review your demo to assess your teaching style, clarity, and engagement. *Only candidates who pass this stage will move forward.*

*5 On-Site Interview & Trial Lesson* – Selected candidates will be invited for a *30-minute on-site trial lesson*, where we evaluate your live teaching ability and classroom presence.

*6 Final Evaluation & Offer* – Successful candidates will receive an official offer to join *Realus Education* as part of our tutor team.

*Apply now* by sending your CV to *Chatswood@realus.com.au* with the subject line *“Y1–Y12 Tutor Application”*.

Job Types: Full-time, Part-time, Casual

Pay: $40.00 – $90.00 per hour

Work Location: In person

positionName: Student Work Experience – Digital Marketing and Community
description:
*Job Title: Student Work Experience – Digital Marketing and Community*

*Location:* Sydney NSW (Hybrid) *Job Type:* Vocational / Work Experience

*Role Overview:* We are offering a structured work experience placement for a student passionate about digital storytelling and community building. This placement provides a unique opportunity to learn how a startup builds a national brand identity across multiple social platforms.

*Educational & Learning Outcomes:*

* *Multi-Channel Strategy:* Learn how to establish and synchronize a brand voice across Instagram, Facebook, LinkedIn, and RedNote.
* *Growth Marketing:* Study the mechanics of membership acquisition and community engagement within the student demographic.
* *Digital Content Creation:* Gain experience using Canva and AI-driven creative tools to develop professional marketing assets.
* *Forum Moderation:* Learn community management best practices using the Discourse platform.

*Requirements:*

* Current student in Marketing, Communications, or Digital Media.
* Active interest in social media trends and community-led growth.
* Enthusiastic about using AI to enhance creative workflows.

*Placement Structure:* Highly flexible remote-first structure (up to 8 hours per week). This is a non-salaried role for students looking to build a professional portfolio or meet course requirements. Travel and meal expenses for Sydney-based collaboration sessions are reimbursed.

Benefits:
* Employee mentoring program
* Work from home

Work Location: Hybrid remote in Surry Hills NSW 2010
descriptionHTML:
Job Title: Student Work Experience – Digital Marketing and Community

Location: Sydney NSW (Hybrid) Job Type: Vocational / Work Experience

Role Overview: We are offering a structured work experience placement for a student passionate about digital storytelling and community building. This placement provides a unique opportunity to learn how a startup builds a national brand identity across multiple social platforms.

Educational & Learning Outcomes:

  • Multi-Channel Strategy: Learn how to establish and synchronize a brand voice across Instagram, Facebook, LinkedIn, and RedNote.
  • Growth Marketing: Study the mechanics of membership acquisition and community engagement within the student demographic.
  • Digital Content Creation: Gain experience using Canva and AI-driven creative tools to develop professional marketing assets.
  • Forum Moderation: Learn community management best practices using the Discourse platform.

Requirements:

  • Current student in Marketing, Communications, or Digital Media.
  • Active interest in social media trends and community-led growth.
  • Enthusiastic about using AI to enhance creative workflows.

Placement Structure: Highly flexible remote-first structure (up to 8 hours per week). This is a non-salaried role for students looking to build a professional portfolio or meet course requirements. Travel and meal expenses for Sydney-based collaboration sessions are reimbursed.

Benefits:

  • Employee mentoring program
  • Work from home

Work Location: Hybrid remote in Surry Hills NSW 2010

description:
We help the world run better
At SAP, we keep it simple: you bring your best to us, and we'll bring out the best in you. We're builders touching over 20 industries and 80% of global commerce, and we need your unique talents to help shape what's next. The work is challenging – but it matters. You'll find a place where you can be yourself, prioritize your wellbeing, and truly belong. What's in it for you? Constant learning, skill growth, great benefits, and a team that wants you to grow and succeed.

Lead the Future of ERP Migrations – Join Our Centre of Excellence

Are you passionate about the power of the cloud? Are you ready to help Australia's public sector unlock greater efficiency and productivity through smarter ERP migrations?

Join SAP and lead a bold new initiative that’s redefining how key government agencies move to the cloud.

With the creation of a Centre of Excellence (CoE) for Federal Australian Agencies we’re building a high-performance team to guide major agencies through the how of ERP migration — with proven strategy, expert delivery, and scalable, lasting impact.

Initially, an 18-month transformation program, this high-profile, strategic initiative will be the successful candidate’s initial assignment, before transitioning into the broader Customer Success and Delivery area to extend the best practices and knowledge gained there.

This is your chance to set new industry benchmarks and drive real transformation where it matters most.

Please note - This role is base in Canberra and given the role works with Australian Public Sector - Australian Citizens with the capability to secure and maintain government security clearance are preferred.

About the Role:
This is a role for a highly respected and senior resource with strong solution knowledge and deep implementation experience, capable of designing, driving and rationalising solutions to support customer outcomes. The Services Solutioning Architect is essential to translate customer expectations into a sellable and deliverable compelling proposal based on CS&D Portfolio and in close coordination with Subject Matter Experts and all Sales teams (Services Sales, Industry Account Executives and Solution Sales Specialists).

Key Responsibilities:

Orchestrate the overall solution to meet the customer requirements, addressing solution and implementation approach, completing effort and cost estimates for the proposed solution, leveraging and coordinating input from subject matter experts as required. Formulate the solution details including timelines, approach, scope, risks and assumptions. Take into account various delivery options considering, scope, cost, quality and risk.

Contribute actively towards the development of Business Cases for clients

Work collaboratively with clients to solution and generate Rough Order of Magnitude estimates

Consolidation of all requirements and inputs from multiple SME’s into a single solution response, including completion of solutioning details in a Deal Sheet and Scope Document.

Align and engage all stakeholders required for Deal Execution – including Delivery, Risk, Bid Management, License and Commercial Finance.

Act as a Single Point of Contact (SPOC) for the collation and overall design of the solution bid response or pro-active proposal.

Provide input to the services sales strategy for each opportunity, acting as part of the overall bid team.

Liaise and coordinate with other knowledgeable resources including Delivery, Practice Leads, other Solutioning Team members, Lines of Service and Industry experts as necessary to ensure delivery feasibility.

Engage with Adoption Solution Centre (ASC) as necessary where they are engaged to support the bid response.

Willingness to extend knowledge to other solution areas, especially the SAP cloud offerings, to a level satisfactory to support initial service solutioning for opportunities.

Ensure the effective positioning and usage of relevant Service Portfolio items in the solution.

Understand the RISE with SAP methodology for Cloud deployments.

Support and reflect relevant regional and global services strategies into the services deals.

Responsible for contribution of services solution content to the proposal response, including reviewing, writing, or editing, aligned with the proposal win themes.

Proactively identify opportunities for the cross selling of services.

Communication and handover of the solution design to the project delivery team, supporting the initial stage of project execution.

Participate in and support regional Solutioning community (knowledge share and contributions).

Experience:

The applicant should be highly experienced with at least 5 years implementation and have fully participated in multiple full cycle implementations. A thorough understanding of SAP project methodologies for Cloud is necessary.

The applicant should have experience in building Value Based business cases.

Applicant should have significant experience in project estimation methodologies.

Applicant should have significant exposure to the services sales process, and have been involved in successful services sales campaigns.

Ideally have deep experience in more than one functional solution area, with some broader knowledge of some other solution suite areas.

Ideally have knowledge of SAP’s Premium Engagement Services and Cloudify Services portfolio.

Bring out your best
SAP innovations help more than four hundred thousand customers worldwide work together more efficiently and use business insight more effectively. Originally known for leadership in enterprise resource planning (ERP) software, SAP has evolved to become a market leader in end-to-end business application software and related services for database, analytics, intelligent technologies, and experience management. As a cloud company with two hundred million users and more than one hundred thousand employees worldwide, we are purpose-driven and future-focused, with a highly collaborative team ethic and commitment to personal development. Whether connecting global industries, people, or platforms, we help ensure every challenge gets the solution it deserves. At SAP, you can bring out your best.

We win with inclusion
SAP’s culture of inclusion, focus on health and well-being, and flexible working models help ensure that everyone – regardless of background – feels included and can run at their best. At SAP, we believe we are made stronger by the unique capabilities and qualities that each person brings to our company, and we invest in our employees to inspire confidence and help everyone realize their full potential. We ultimately believe in unleashing all talent and creating a better world.

SAP is committed to the values of Equal Employment Opportunity and provides accessibility accommodations to applicants with physical and/or mental disabilities. If you are interested in applying for employment with SAP and are in need of accommodation or special assistance to navigate our website or to complete your application, please send an e-mail with your request to Recruiting Operations Team: Careers@sap.com.

For SAP employees: Only permanent roles are eligible for the SAP Employee Referral Program, according to the eligibility rules set in the SAP Referral Policy. Specific conditions may apply for roles in Vocational Training.

Qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to their age, race, religion, national origin, ethnicity, gender (including pregnancy, childbirth, et al), sexual orientation, gender identity or expression, protected veteran status, or disability, in compliance with applicable federal, state, and local legal requirements.

Successful candidates might be required to undergo a background verification with an external vendor.

AI Usage in the Recruitment Process

For information on the responsible use of AI in our recruitment process, please refer to our Guidelines for Ethical Usage of AI in the Recruiting Process.

Please note that any violation of these guidelines may result in disqualification from the hiring process.

description:
See yourself in our team

Retail Advanced Analytics and Data (RAAD) partners closely with Retail Banking Services to enable best practice across data, advanced analytics, and AI. The team plays a key role in shaping data‑led decision making across the Everyday Banking domain, delivering trusted, well‑governed data products that support customers, colleagues, and the broader business.

You will join a diverse community of data and analytics professionals who combine strong technical capability with business context to solve meaningful problems at scale.

Do work that matters

As a Data Product Manager, you will support the execution of the domain data strategy and own the delivery, governance, and lifecycle management of high‑quality, reusable data products and data assets. You will help ensure data is discoverable, trustworthy, and fit‑for‑purpose, while meeting Group standards across data management, security, privacy, and record‑keeping.

The role has a strong focus on enabling ethical and effective use of data and AI, recognising that data quality and governance directly influence outcomes.

More specifically, you will:

Support execution of the domain data strategy aligned to business priorities and outcomes
Own the delivery, governance, and lifecycle of reusable Data Products and Data Assets, including business‑critical data, in line with Group policies
Define Data Products with clear business context and metadata to ensure discoverability, reuse, trust, and interoperability
Ensure compliance with Group standards across enterprise semantics, metadata, data lineage, retention, and disposal
Champion continuous improvement of the data estate, including modernisation, rationalisation, optimisation, and transition to strategic consumption pathways
Embed strong data management practices that enable effective and ethical use of data and AI
Partner with Crew Leads, Tech Leads, Delivery Product Owners, and CIO teams to deliver data product roadmaps
Enable effective data producer–consumer collaboration across Business Units and Support Units
Ensure Data Products comply with Group Information Security and Privacy policies
Set, monitor, and maintain data quality standards, including usage and quality metrics
Drive uplift and remediation where Data Products no longer meet required standards
Define and manage appropriate access and usage of Data Products and Data Assets
Promote data literacy and adoption of Group data management best practices

We’re interested in hearing from people who have:

8+ years’ experience across data management, data governance, data engineering, or control frameworks
Proven experience delivering enterprise‑scale data initiatives in complex or regulated environments
A strong data product mindset, aligned to business outcomes
Experience engaging and influencing stakeholders across business and technology teams
Passion for building capability, fostering collaboration, and driving continuous improvement
Experience supporting data cloud migration and exposure to the AWS ecosystem (desirable)

Tech skills

Strong background in data management, data engineering, stewardship, and large‑scale data platforms
Experience designing, deploying, and operating solutions in AWS or Snowflake environments
Hands‑on experience with language such as Python, SQL and tools like Alation, AWS Data Exchange, Glue and AWS integration services
Exposure to version control and CI/CD pipelines (for example, GitHub) is desirable

Qualifications

Bachelor’s degree in Engineering, or
Master’s degree in Computer Science or a related discipline

If you're already part of the Commonwealth Bank Group (including Bankwest, x15ventures), you'll need to apply through Sidekick to submit a valid application. We’re keen to support you with the next step in your career.

We're aware of some accessibility issues on this site, particularly for screen reader users. We want to make finding your dream job as easy as possible, so if you require additional support please contact HR Direct on 1800 989 696.

Advertising End Date: 17/05/2026

description:
Starting your marketing career and want real responsibility from day one? This is not a “sit and observe” role. It is hands on, fast moving, and gives you exposure across the full marketing mix.

We are looking for a Marketing Assistant to join our commercial team in Melbourne, Australia.

You will play a key role in supporting the delivery and maintenance of sales and marketing materials, and bring both internal and customer-facing initiatives to life - all while building practical experience across digital content, events and marketing operations.

This role is ideal for someone early in their career who wants to learn quickly, contribute meaningfully, and be part of a collaborative and growing business.

What you will be doing

You will be working closely with both marketing and sales, supporting a wide range of activities:

Create and update marketing and sales materials such as presentations, brochures, banners and digital content

Write and edit internal and customer-facing communications, product and website content.

Keep our marketing assets, templates, and image libraries organised and up to date

Support the sales team with proposals, presentations and client communications

Maintain CRM data and enquiry tracking to ensure accuracy and usability

Update website content using our CMS

Help plan and execute events and internal initiatives

Coordinate with external partners and suppliers to deliver marketing materials

Ensure brand consistency across everything we produce

Support general coordination and admin tasks where needed

What you bring

You do not need years of experience, but you do need curiosity, structure, and drive:

Comfortable using Microsoft Word, Excel and PowerPoint

Familiar with tools like Adobe Creative Suite (Illustrator, InDesign, Photoshop), Canva or Adobe Express

Basic video editing skills are a plus

Strong attention to detail and ability to stay organised across multiple tasks

A proactive mindset and willingness to take initiative

Clear communication skills and a professional approach

Ability to work in a fast paced environment and manage deadlines

A positive attitude and openness to learning

Why this role

This is a great opportunity to build real marketing experience across multiple disciplines, not just one niche. You will work closely with experienced colleagues, get exposure to commercial decision making, and see how marketing directly supports business growth.

There are no management responsibilities in this role, but there is strong potential to grow your skills and develop your career as the business evolves.

About the role and structure

This position is part of Safer Storage Systems (SSS) and currently follows established ways of working within that business. As integration with Element Logic continues, the role may evolve over time to align with the wider organisation.

If you are looking for a role where you can learn fast, contribute from day one, and build a strong foundation in marketing, we would like to hear from you.

For further questions about the position or process, contact by email to Vice President Talent Acquisition & Employer Branding: beate.hall-hofso@elementlogic.net.

Agency notice: We’re proud to handle all our recruitment in-house at Element Logic. That means we don’t accept candidates or approaches from external recruiters or headhunters. All hiring processes are managed exclusively by our own specialized Talent Acquisition team.

Be yourself, in your element.

At Element Logic, our people are our greatest resource. We believe individuals should be in their element and we recognize, celebrate, and nurture the strength of diversity. We offer equal opportunities and welcome applicants regardless of age, race, ethnicity, gender identity and expression, sexual orientation, language, national origin, mental and physical abilities, religious and political affiliations, stage of life, or family circumstances. We desire a work environment where people flourish with their unique abilities, perspectives, and life experiences.

About Element Logic
Element Logic is a global technology company specializing in intelligent warehouse automation. Since its founding in 1985, the company has grown to employ over 950 people across 30 countries and has established itself as the first - and leading - AutoStore partner worldwide.

Renowned for its commitment to innovation and people-centric approach, Element Logic enhances warehouse performance through a unique combination of cutting-edge technology, AI-powered software, and tailor-made solutions. This enables businesses across industries to improve efficiency, accuracy, and scalability in their logistics operations.

With a proven track record and deep expertise in intralogistics, Element Logic has become the go-to partner for organizations seeking to gain a competitive edge through automation. The company’s culture is rooted in bravery, passion, and reliability - qualities that continue to drive its mission of optimizing warehouse performance. In 2024, Element Logic reported revenue of EUR 549 million.

positionName: Vocational Placement – Events and Business Development
description:
*Job Title: Vocational Placement – Events and Business Development*

*Location:* Sydney NSW (Hybrid) *Job Type:* Vocational / Work Experience

*Role Overview:* This is a structured vocational placement designed for a current tertiary student seeking to complete industry-relevant experience as part of their studies. You will be mentored by the founders of ISM (www.ism.org.au), an organization dedicated to improving the international student experience in Australia. This placement focuses on the strategic planning and logistics of a major brand launch in Sydney.

*Educational & Learning Outcomes:*

* *Event Management:* Observe and participate in the end-to-end planning of launch events.
* *Stakeholder Engagement:* Gain exposure to identifying and communicating with potential corporate sponsors and venue partners.
* *AI-First Operations:* Learn how to utilize emerging AI technologies (Gemini, ChatGPT, and automation tools) to optimize business research and event logistics.
* *Networking:* Develop professional connections within the Sydney business and student services sector.

*Requirements:*

* Currently enrolled in a relevant course (Marketing, Business, or Event Management) at a Sydney-based institution.
* A proactive interest in supporting the international student community.
* Familiarity with modern AI productivity tools.

*Placement Structure:* Flexible hours (up to 8 hours per week) designed to integrate with your academic calendar. This placement is a non-salaried vocational position; however, all work-related travel and meal expenses for in-person workshops are fully reimbursed.

Benefits:
* Employee mentoring program
* Work from home

Work Location: Hybrid remote in Surry Hills NSW 2010

  • Employee mentoring program
  • Work from home

Work Location: Hybrid remote in Surry Hills NSW 2010

description:
AI Job Summary
4+ years on a mine site in an environmental role with responsibilities including monitoring, reporting, and managing env
Coordinate and prepare internal and external environmental reports.
Basic knowledge of Environmental Management Systems, including interpreting monitoring results against standards.
 Sign up or log in to analyse my fit
Role Type
On-site • Contract • Full-time • Mid-level Senior
Description

Senior Environmental Advisor – Senior Environmental Advisor – 1-year Tenure with possibility of extension

This exciting role is reporting to the Environment Superintendent, and you will be part of an outstanding team of dynamic environment, approvals, and community professionals, committed to high performance.

Key Responsibilities Include:

An absolute commitment to working safely, complying with all site safety standards and ensuring a safe and environmentally healthy workplace is essential.
Coordinate and track monitoring and sampling for Air, Surface water, Groundwater Soils, Sediments, potable water and wastewater.
Assessment of results against standards
Participation and input into the ongoing development and implementation of the site’s Environmental Management System, including the development of Work Instructions and Procedures and assisting with Plans
Coordinate reporting programs involving NGERS, NPI, weekly/monthly reporting, DES waste reporting and Permit Returns
Managing, investigating, and reporting on environmental incidents
Providing general environmental advice and assistance to the site operations
Coordinating and development of environmental projects
Assist EAC department and others as required.
Learning and growing with our team, sharing your ideas and being part of creating innovative solutions to complex issues

About you:

Essential

A passion to excel as an environmental professional.
4+ years’ experience working on a mine (preferably metalliferous) site in an environmental role with similar responsibilities
Strong commitment and focus on achieving positive environmental and safety outcomes.
Tertiary qualification(s) in environmental science or closely related disciplines.
High level of attention to detail, an ability to interpret data and problem solve.
Basic knowledge of Environmental Management Systems
Experienced at coordinating and preparing internal and external reports
A current manual drivers’ licence approved in Queensland.
Experience in managing environmental consultants and contractors
Well-developed written and verbal communication skills
Ability to deliver quality work within tight deadlines
Ability to work both independently and as part of a team
Experience with Microsoft suite, GIS software and environmental databases
Must live within the Townsville, Burdekin, Charters Towers region, or surrounding areas (within – 200km of Ravenswood) or be willing to relocate to the area.

Desirable

Experience in the Queensland legislative environment
AUSRIVAS accreditation.
4WD driving experience
Strong QGIS skills and experience.

Nestled 120kms south of Townsville, in the vibrant landscapes of north-east Queensland, Ravenswood Gold Mine stands as a testament to both a rich legacy and the forefront of innovation. Ravenswood Gold is a private Australian company jointly owned by EMR Capital (EMR) and Golden Energy and Resources Limited (GEAR).

What We Offer:

An opportunity to work within a reputable mining organisation, with a long term future, and to be part of its growth and development.
Contract position with a reputable mining company with a mine life in excess of 10 years with potential for extension
A competitive remuneration package based on experience and skills
Support from a team with extensive industry, site knowledge and experience
DIDO on an 8/6 roster
On-site accommodation and Meals provided

Applications should include the following documentation in PDF format:

Current Resume
Cover letter addressing suitability as outlined above.

All preferred candidates will undergo pre-employment checks, including a Nationally Coordinated Police Check, medical assessments, reference checks, and verification of qualifications.

Ravenswood Gold acknowledges the Traditional Owners of the Country on which we walk, live and work. We recognise their long history on the lands upon which our operations exist today and the care they gave to these lands for thousands of years. We pay our respect to their cultures and Elders past, present and future, and recognise their continuing connection to land, air and sea. Ravenswood Gold specifically acknowledges the Birriah People who are the Traditional Owners of the Country where the Ravenswood Gold Mine operates, the Bindal People who are the Traditional Owners of the Country where the Ravenswood Gold Townsville office and warehouse are located and the Yuggera and Turrbal People who are the Traditional Owners of the Country where the Ravenswood Gold head office is located in Brisbane.

positionName: Team Lead – Aged Care Customer Service (Call Centre)
description:
AI Job Summary
Experience managing a team of customer service officers in a call centre environment.
Experience coaching/supporting/motivating for high-quality, person-centred service and service-level/quality targets.
High proficiency in ICT—databases, data analysis, and Microsoft Office Suite.
 Sign up or log in to analyse my fit
Role Type
On-site • Permanent • Full-time • Associate
Pay Rate
80000 AUD – 90000 AUD (Annum)
Description

Indigo (formerly Independent Living Centre WA) is a leading provider of Assistive technology (devices and equipment), home modifications, therapy, and professional education. We provide tailored allied health and AT solutions to people with disability, seniors, carers, health professionals and educators. Through our customer-centric services we aim to achieve greater independence and inclusion for all.

The Role

With the continued success of Indigo and the GEAT2Go (Goods, Equipment and Assistive Technology) program, we are seeking a passionate and professional Team Lead to join our Aged Care Customer Service call centre.

The Team Lead is responsible for the day-to-day leadership and performance management of a team of Customer Service Officers, operating within call centre environment. The role focuses on coaching, supporting and motivating the team to deliver high-quality, person-centred service outcomes while consistently meeting service levels, quality standards and operational targets.

The duties will include but not limited to:

Lead a team of customer service officers to provide assessment and recommendation of assistive technology that support individuals to meet their goals, retain their independence and remain living at home.
Ensure customers of the goods and equipment and home modification programs are reviewed to ensure goals are met prior to discharge from service.
Develop strategies to ensure customers receive timely and appropriate services to meet their needs.
Contribute to the development of the service, raise opportunities for innovation and ensure clinical best practice in service delivery.
Coach, educate, inspire and provide clinical supervision and performance reviews
Identify any training gaps for individual staff, and through leadership support team members to achieve within their role
Identify staffing requirements and assisting with recruitment and retention
Contribute to, and demonstrate by example, the vision, mission and values

The Candidate

Experience managing a team
High standard of communication, problem solving, and initiative
Demonstrated high proficiency in ICT – databases, data analysis, and Office Suite
Experience in aged care and/or disability services and an understanding of the standards
Ability to liaise with leaders and key stakeholders both internally and externally
Call centre environment experience Highly desirable

We Offer

Rewarding work and being part of a “Values based” organisation – making a difference in people’s lives
Ongoing support, mentorship, and professional development
Generous salary packaging, up to an additional $18,549 tax free FBT (including the ability to package relocation expenses for those eligible)
True work life balance with additional 3.5 days leave (pro-rata) / ability to purchase additional leave.
Employee Assistance Program for you and your immediate family to access in time of need
Health & Well-being Program including flu vaccination, EAP support and fun active social committee

Apply

If you are ready to join a supportive team and are passionate about the work you do – Register your interest today by submitting your curriculum vitae and a cover letter, addressing how you meet the work-related requirements (in no more than 2 pages).

Short listing will commence immediately, along with interviewing. We thank you for your interest in Indigo and reserve the right to fill the position before the close date.

A NDIS Worker Screener Check is a pre-requisite for all positions at Indigo.

Only those with the right to live & work in Australia need apply. No agency applications at this time.

Mission: We strive towards our mission of a community in which all abilities are valued, where people have the knowledge, tools, and confidence to participate.

Values: Passion, Integrity, Creativity, Together

We value our people at Indigo and strive to have a positive and adaptive culture, one that aligns with our values; so cultural fit is just as important to us as qualifications, technical skill and experience.

We are committed to equal employment opportunities and embracing diversity and inclusion within our workforce. People from diverse backgrounds including people with disabilities, Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander people and people from culturally diverse backgrounds are encouraged to apply. If you require any additional support during the recruitment/interview phase, please contact hr@indigo.org.au

positionName: Senior Analyst (Public Sector, Defense and Security) - BCG Vantage
description:
Who We Are

Boston Consulting Group partners with leaders in business and society to tackle their most important challenges and capture their greatest opportunities. BCG was the pioneer in business strategy when it was founded in 1963. Today, we help clients with total transformation-inspiring complex change, enabling organizations to grow, building competitive advantage, and driving bottom-line impact.

To succeed, organizations must blend digital and human capabilities. Our diverse, global teams bring deep industry and functional expertise and a range of perspectives to spark change. BCG delivers solutions through leading-edge management consulting along with technology and design, corporate and digital ventures-and business purpose. We work in a uniquely collaborative model across the firm and throughout all levels of the client organization, generating results that allow our clients to thrive.

What You'll Do

As a Senior Analyst – BCG Vantage on our Client Focus path within BCG's Public Sector Practice Area for Defence & Security, you will work in a growing global team, delivering value to clients via individual expertise and/or institutionalized BCG Vantage assets (products, tools, data, workshops, frameworks, surveys, domain-specific data and related expertise, etc.). You will contribute on cases and proposals by contributing relevant analysis and insights and help case teams create strong impact for the client. While contributing to client cases, workshops and proposals, occasional travel to serve clients onsite during staffing assignments is expected to understand client requirements, and design / develop solutions.
You will support commercialisation efforts for the topic/sector working, in conjunction with business leaders, through contributing analysis and insights to proposals, client workshops and marketing materials. Additionally, as a Senior Analyst – BCG Vantage, you will contribute to developing intellectual property & assets for the business and assist in onboarding and training junior colleagues based on your topic/sector expertise.
Your work within our Defence & Security topic will primarily focus on supporting our engagement with Australian defence ministry and industry clients, providing expertise and insights to advance client objectives. You will work on topics that include acquisition, sustainment, shipbuilding, organisational design, and IT and technology transformations. Your focus will be on helping defence clients to adapt to a fast-changing security environment that increasingly depends upon spurring innovation and leveraging technology for national defence.

YOU'RE GOOD AT

Solving client problems through formulating relevant research and/or analytical approaches in defence and security topics
Communicating with case teams and clients team members, in a credible and confident way
Working collaboratively and effectively in a group dynamic often virtual, proficient in agile ways of working
Being flexible and bringing a curious and creative mindset, open to new things and able to propose innovative ideas
Navigating complexity and ambiguity

What You'll Bring

2+ years of consulting experience in defence and/or technology required; candidates with consulting experience strongly preferred
In lieu of consulting experience, 3+ years minimum industry experience required; 4-6+ years of industry experience strongly preferred
Strong knowledge and experience in the Australian defence enterprise, or prior military or civilian service in the Australian Ministry of Defence or the Australian Defence Force, is highly desirable
Experience with technology or digital strategy to include developing technology strategies, forming business cases, supporting large-scale technology transformations, or assisting AI transformations
Bachelor's Degree required (advanced degree preferred)
Fluency in English
Successful candidate is subject to obtain and maintain an Australian government security clearance
Strong business acumen and problem-solving capabilities
Strong written and verbal communication skills
Outstanding interpersonal and communication skills to interact with internal and external stakeholders while working in a global collaborative team environment
Self-starting and entrepreneurial spirit

Who You'll Work With

As a Senior Analyst – BCG Vantage on our Client Focus path, you will work face to face with clients and with BCG Project Leaders, Associates and Consultants. In the period between cases, you will collaborate with global topic leads and colleagues across BCG to bring expertise and capabilities to the service of our internal and external clients. Our team is made up a diverse pool of BCG Vantage positions that allow us to pursue exciting, innovative BCG Vantage careers.

Additional info

BCG Vantage is a powerhouse of expertise, seamlessly embedded within Practice Areas and markets to drive extraordinary impact. It equips BCG case teams and clients with cutting-edge expertise, proprietary assets, actionable data, and transformative insights that empower our case teams and enable our clients to achieve their aspirations.
With global experts across 20 Practices, covering more than 100 topics, sectors and offers, plus four focused geographic markets, BCG Vantage delivers unparalleled depth and breadth of insight & expertise. BCG Vantage consists of three career paths, each playing a crucial role in delivering value to clients, case teams and practice teams.

Client Focus (Embedded Expertise)

Integrates into client-facing teams to deliver direct client impact through expertise, know-how, and proprietary tools and assets

Topic Activation (Sector Expertise with Proprietary Tool & Data Solutions)

Combines deep domain insights with our know-how and proprietary assets to address targeted challenges directly with clients and project teams; co-creates insights on industry trends and functional topics, proprietary data and tools, and teams to codify IP that all enables BCG to deliver excellent business value with its clients

Research (Practice Topic and Sector-Aligned Research and Insights)

Delivers actionable research and insights tailored to the client project’s needs; works with qualitative and quantitative data, conducts research and provides synthesized results from sources including commercial data, public and open source, company and industry research, macro-economic, commodities and trade data.

Boston Consulting Group is an Equal Opportunity Employer. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, age, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity / expression, national origin, disability, protected veteran status, or any other characteristic protected under national, provincial, or local law, where applicable, and those with criminal histories will be considered in a manner consistent with applicable state and local laws.
BCG is an E - Verify Employer. Click here for more information on E-Verify.

description:
Build a Safer World.

TRM Labs provides AI-powered intelligence solutions that help public and private sector agencies investigate and disrupt crime. TRM's platforms enable investigators to trace illicit activity, build cases, and construct operating pictures of threat networks. Leading agencies and businesses worldwide rely on TRM to make the world safer and more secure.

As a Blockchain Intelligence Analyst, you will drive TRM’s collection, analysis, and reporting on key areas of the crypto economy (e.g., crypto exchanges, fraud). You will ensure that TRM has best-in-class intelligence through your subject matter expertise, first-principles problem solving, and ability to turn data into insights. You will collaborate with an experienced team of engineers, data scientists, and product managers to build scalable systems to detect, prevent, and mitigate cryptocurrency fraud and financial crime.

The impact you’ll have here:

You’ll track, analyze, and report on key areas of the crypto economy to detect suspicious activities.

Your OSINT skills will help uncover hidden threats and improve our security posture.

You’ll provide key insights that contribute to real-world action against threat actors.

You’ll collaborate with data and engineering teams to enhance the TRM product.

Your intelligence reports will help law enforcement, internal teams, and clients stay ahead of cyber risks.

What we’re looking for:

Proven experience working in intelligence-related positions previously, ideally in Blockchain Intelligence (This is not an entry-level position).

OSINT Expertise - Proficiency in using open-source tools to gather and analyze intelligence.

Analytical Skills - Strong ability to synthesize and report complex information clearly and accurately.

Experience in Blockchain Analysis - Knowledge of blockchain technologies, with a focus on tracing transactions and identifying malicious activity.

Communication & Collaboration - Excellent communication skills to collaborate with cross-functional teams and produce actionable intelligence reports.

Ownership - You demonstrate full ownership of your work, taking an 80/20 approach to accomplish objectives.

Certifications & Background - Relevant certifications (e.g., GIAC, CEH) and experience in cybersecurity or intelligence gathering.

Fluency in Russian, Chinese (Mandarin or Cantonese), or Arabic, with the ability to conduct research and analysis in that language, is strongly preferred and may be required in some cases. Please specify your language proficiency and any relevant experience in your application.

About the team:

Our team values open communication and a collaborative work environment, where feedback and ideas are encouraged from all members.

We prioritize flexibility and adaptability, ensuring that everyone stays aligned through regular check-ins and progress updates. Operating with a strong focus on shared goals, we work together to meet deadlines while supporting individual growth and innovation.

We are a globally distributed team that leans heavily on asynchronous communication. While we aim for ~3–4 hours of overlap with the Pacific Time Zone, schedules are flexible and may occasionally adjust to support team collaboration.

Learn about TRM Speed in this position:

Utilizes sound judgment and the 80/20 principle to drive rapid, high-impact outcomes.

Demonstrates the ability to iterate quickly in response to evolving threat landscapes.

Leverages creative problem-solving to ensure prompt and effective threat actor engagement to acquire attribution within 24 hours.

Employs a diverse set of methodologies to scale and optimize threat intelligence production for customer needs within 72 hours.

Life at TRM

We are building a safer world. That promise shows up in how we work every day.

TRM moves quickly. We are a high velocity, high ownership team that expects clarity, follow-through, and impact. People who thrive here are energized by hard problems, experimentation, and continuous feedback. If something takes months elsewhere, it will ship here in days.

Our work sits at the intersection of AI, national security, and fighting crime. The problems are complex, the stakes are real, and the environment evolves quickly. The pace and intensity of the work reflect the importance of the mission. As a result, the way we operate requires a high level of ownership, adaptability, collaboration, and creative problem-solving.

At TRM, you should expect:

Priorities and targets to change quickly as we experiment and iterate

Work that often requires operating with a high degree of ambiguity

A high level of personal ownership and accountability

Close collaboration across teams and functions

Frequent, high-touch communication

Creative problem solving and out-of-the-box thinking

A pace that rewards urgency, adaptability, and outcomes

This environment is energizing for people who enjoy building, solving hard problems, and making progress in situations that are not always fully defined. It also requires comfort navigating ambiguity, adjusting course as new information emerges, and maintaining focus and positivity in a fast-moving and intense environment.

We also recognize that this style of operating is not for everyone. If you are primarily optimizing for predictability or a consistently balanced workload, we encourage you to use the interview process to pressure test whether this environment is truly the right fit. We want teammates who thrive here, not just survive here.

At the same time, many people find this work deeply rewarding. If you are excited by meaningful problems, motivated by ambitious goals, and energized by working alongside mission-driven colleagues, there is a good chance you will find TRM to be an exceptional place to grow and contribute. Learn more: Interviewing at TRM: How We Hire and What Success Looks Like

AI Fluency at TRM

AI fluency is a baseline expectation at TRM.

We believe AI meaningfully changes how top performers operate. We expect every team member to use AI to accelerate and reimagine their craft, not just automate surface tasks.

At TRM, AI fluency means you are among the top 10 percent of operators in your function in how you apply AI to:

Accelerate repeatable workflows

Structure and solve problems

Improve output quality

Increase speed and leverage

You will be evaluated on applied AI fluency during the interview process.

Leadership Principles

We hire and grow against three leadership principles. They’re the standards for how we operate, treat each other, and make decisions.

Impact-Oriented Trailblazer: We put customers first and move with speed, focus, and adaptability. We treat every plan like an experiment – test, ship, measure, and iterate quickly.

Master Craftsperson: We care deeply about our craft. We balance speed with high standards, own outcomes end‑to‑end, and invest in getting better everyday.

Inspiring Colleague: We add clarity and energy, not noise. We bring humility, candor, and a one‑team mindset — giving and receiving feedback to make the team stronger.

Join our Mission

At TRM we care deeply about our craft. We are looking for individuals who want their work to matter, who experiment with speed and rigor, and who take pride in building a safer world for billions of people. If you’re excited by TRM’s mission but don’t check every box, we encourage you to apply — we hire for slope, judgment, and the will to learn fast.

TRM is a Series C company with $220M in total funding, backed by Blockchain Capital, Goldman Sachs, Bessemer, Y Combinator, Thoma Bravo, and others. Headquartered in San Francisco, TRM operates as a distributed-first company with hubs in Los Angeles, San Francisco, New York, Washington D.C., London, and Singapore.

Privacy Policy and Additional Information

By submitting your application, you are agreeing to allow TRM to process your personal information in accordance with the TRM Privacy Policy.

Our typical hiring cycles for specialized roles span 24 to 36 months. Accordingly, we retain your personal information for up to 36 months to evaluate your application and to consider you for current and future employment opportunities, unless you request earlier deletion or a different retention period is required or permitted by law.

To notify TRM Labs that you believe this job posting is non-compliant, please submit a report through this form. No response will be provided to inquiries unrelated to job posting compliance.

The use of AI tools of any kind (including but not limited to notetakers, interview assistants, and real-time coaching tools such as Otter.ai, Fireflies, Fathom, Cluey, or similar) during TRM interviews is not permitted without prior approval from TRM. TRM uses its own internal tools for note-taking to ensure a consistent and confidential experience for all candidates.

We are committed to providing reasonable accommodations to applicants with disabilities, and requests can be made via this form.

Recruitment agencies

TRM Labs does not accept unsolicited agency resumes. Please do not forward resumes to TRM employees. TRM Labs is not responsible for any fees related to unsolicited resumes and will not pay fees to any third-party agency or company without a signed agreement.

Learn More: Company Values | Interviewing | FAQs
descriptionHTML:
Build a Safer World.

TRM Labs provides AI-powered intelligence solutions that help public and private sector agencies investigate and disrupt crime. TRM's platforms enable investigators to trace illicit activity, build cases, and construct operating pictures of threat networks. Leading agencies and businesses worldwide rely on TRM to make the world safer and more secure.

As a Blockchain Intelligence Analyst, you will drive TRM’s collection, analysis, and reporting on key areas of the crypto economy (e.g., crypto exchanges, fraud). You will ensure that TRM has best-in-class intelligence through your subject matter expertise, first-principles problem solving, and ability to turn data into insights. You will collaborate with an experienced team of engineers, data scientists, and product managers to build scalable systems to detect, prevent, and mitigate cryptocurrency fraud and financial crime.

The impact you’ll have here:

  • You’ll track, analyze, and report on key areas of the crypto economy to detect suspicious activities.

  • Your OSINT skills will help uncover hidden threats and improve our security posture.

  • You’ll provide key insights that contribute to real-world action against threat actors.

  • You’ll collaborate with data and engineering teams to enhance the TRM product.

  • Your intelligence reports will help law enforcement, internal teams, and clients stay ahead of cyber risks.

What we’re looking for:

  • Proven experience working in intelligence-related positions previously, ideally in Blockchain Intelligence (This is not an entry-level position).

  • OSINT Expertise - Proficiency in using open-source tools to gather and analyze intelligence.

  • Analytical Skills - Strong ability to synthesize and report complex information clearly and accurately.

  • Experience in Blockchain Analysis - Knowledge of blockchain technologies, with a focus on tracing transactions and identifying malicious activity.

  • Communication & Collaboration - Excellent communication skills to collaborate with cross-functional teams and produce actionable intelligence reports.

  • Ownership - You demonstrate full ownership of your work, taking an 80/20 approach to accomplish objectives.

  • Certifications & Background - Relevant certifications (e.g., GIAC, CEH) and experience in cybersecurity or intelligence gathering.

  • Fluency in Russian, Chinese (Mandarin or Cantonese), or Arabic, with the ability to conduct research and analysis in that language, is strongly preferred and may be required in some cases. Please specify your language proficiency and any relevant experience in your application.

About the team:

  • Our team values open communication and a collaborative work environment, where feedback and ideas are encouraged from all members.

  • We prioritize flexibility and adaptability, ensuring that everyone stays aligned through regular check-ins and progress updates. Operating with a strong focus on shared goals, we work together to meet deadlines while supporting individual growth and innovation.

  • We are a globally distributed team that leans heavily on asynchronous communication. While we aim for ~3–4 hours of overlap with the Pacific Time Zone, schedules are flexible and may occasionally adjust to support team collaboration.

Learn about TRM Speed in this position:

  • Utilizes sound judgment and the 80/20 principle to drive rapid, high-impact outcomes.

  • Demonstrates the ability to iterate quickly in response to evolving threat landscapes.

  • Leverages creative problem-solving to ensure prompt and effective threat actor engagement to acquire attribution within 24 hours.

  • Employs a diverse set of methodologies to scale and optimize threat intelligence production for customer needs within 72 hours.


Life at TRM

We are building a safer world. That promise shows up in how we work every day.

TRM moves quickly. We are a high velocity, high ownership team that expects clarity, follow-through, and impact. People who thrive here are energized by hard problems, experimentation, and continuous feedback. If something takes months elsewhere, it will ship here in days.

Our work sits at the intersection of AI, national security, and fighting crime. The problems are complex, the stakes are real, and the environment evolves quickly. The pace and intensity of the work reflect the importance of the mission. As a result, the way we operate requires a high level of ownership, adaptability, collaboration, and creative problem-solving.

At TRM, you should expect:

  • Priorities and targets to change quickly as we experiment and iterate

  • Work that often requires operating with a high degree of ambiguity

  • A high level of personal ownership and accountability

  • Close collaboration across teams and functions

  • Frequent, high-touch communication

  • Creative problem solving and out-of-the-box thinking

  • A pace that rewards urgency, adaptability, and outcomes

This environment is energizing for people who enjoy building, solving hard problems, and making progress in situations that are not always fully defined. It also requires comfort navigating ambiguity, adjusting course as new information emerges, and maintaining focus and positivity in a fast-moving and intense environment.

We also recognize that this style of operating is not for everyone. If you are primarily optimizing for predictability or a consistently balanced workload, we encourage you to use the interview process to pressure test whether this environment is truly the right fit. We want teammates who thrive here, not just survive here.

At the same time, many people find this work deeply rewarding. If you are excited by meaningful problems, motivated by ambitious goals, and energized by working alongside mission-driven colleagues, there is a good chance you will find TRM to be an exceptional place to grow and contribute. AI Job Summary
Registration with AHPRA or membership with ASORC.
Minimum 1 year occupational rehabilitation and/or clinical experience.
Exceptional verbal and written communication skills.
 Sign up or log in to analyse my fit
Role Type
Permanent • Full-time • Experienced
Pay Rate
85000 AUD – 110000 AUD (Annum)
Description

Rehabilitation Consultant (NES) – Rehabilitation Counsellor or Psychologist

Work type: Full time (minimum 4 days part time also available)

Location: Melbourne VIC

About PHSA

Prestige Health Services Australia (PHSA) is a national rehabilitation provider delivering best in industry injury management; disability management and occupational rehabilitation services.

Your Role

PHSA are seeking Rehabilitation Consultants to deliver New Employer Services (NES) including assessment, intervention and case management services to clientele across varying schemes including TAC (Transport Accident Commission), WorkSafe VIC, Comcare, Life Insurance and Self Insurance with the primary function of supporting recovery and return to work.

What You’ll Need

• Registration with AHPRA (Australian Health Practitioner Regulation Agency) or membership with ASORC (Australian Society of Rehabilitation Counsellors)

• Exceptional verbal and written communication skills

• Minimum 1 year experience in occupational rehabilitation and/or clinical experience highly regarded

• Direct experience working with TAC referred cases desirable

• Have a current driver’s licence, motor vehicle and willingness to travel to deliver face-to-face servicing as required

Why Choose PHSA?

• Flexibility & Autonomy: Shape your own work schedule and enjoy the freedom to work independently, with support always within reach. Flexible work arrangements also available.

• Career Growth: Unlock pathways to advancement with our comprehensive induction, ongoing training, and mentoring programs.

• Tools for Success: We equip you with a laptop, smartphone, and access to the latest technology.

• Rewarding Salary & Bonuses: Enjoy a highly competitive salary, tailored to your experience, plus a lucrative bonus system for employees.

• Supportive Team Culture: Be part of a passionate, purpose-driven team that values your contributions and celebrates your achievements.

Ready to take the next step in your career? Apply today!

Company Overview
Prestige Health Services Australia (PHSA) is a national rehabilitation provider delivering best in industry injury management; disability management and workplace rehabilitation services. Our mission is to achieve sustainable health, social and return to work outcomes for our clientele through the delivery of innovative workplace; injury management; disability management and rehabilitation services undertaken by experienced consultants with a focus on quality, integrity and belief that we can and will achieve outcomes.
