description:
Join a fast growing & market leading health brand!
Work remote or in our Melbourne, AU office.
Excellent career progression and bonus opportunities!
About Us

We are a fast-growing D2C ecommerce group selling eco-friendly and sustainable products designed to make everyday life better for people and the planet.

We are building a lean, high-output business and want AI, automation and smart systems to become a core part of how we operate.

About the Role

We are looking for an exceptional AI Automation & Ecommerce Systems Lead to help us scale our ecommerce business without scaling headcount at the same rate.

This is a hands-on builder role for someone who understands ecommerce, digital marketing, automation, AI tools, workflows and commercial outcomes.

Your role will be to identify high-leverage manual work across the business, then build AI-powered workflows, automations and internal systems that reduce labour, increase output, improve marketing performance and help a small team operate like a much larger one.

This is not a role for someone who only uses ChatGPT casually or simply recommends tools. We need someone who can design, build, implement and improve practical systems that the team actually uses.

Key Responsibilities AI Automation & Workflow Building
Identify repetitive, manual or inefficient workflows across marketing, ecommerce, customer service, operations and reporting
Build AI-powered automations, agents, workflows and internal tools to reduce manual workload
Connect tools and systems using APIs, webhooks, Make, Zapier, n8n, Airtable, Google Apps Script, Python, JavaScript or similar
Create practical workflows that improve speed, output quality, accuracy and consistency
Document systems clearly so the team can use and maintain them
Ecommerce Growth & Marketing Systems
Build AI-assisted workflows across SEO, PPC, CRO, lifecycle marketing, content, creative production and analytics
Help scale content, landing page briefs, product copy, ad creative ideas, research and reporting
Improve marketing efficiency across channels such as Google Ads, Meta, TikTok, email/SMS and organic search
Support smarter creative testing, campaign analysis, customer research and competitor monitoring
Use AI and automation to improve website performance, conversion rate, AOV and customer journeys
Customer Service & Operations Automation
Identify opportunities to improve customer service efficiency using AI, automation and better knowledge systems
Build or improve AI-assisted response templates, macros, helpdesk workflows, FAQs and self-service flows
Use customer enquiry data to identify recurring issues, product questions, website friction and improvement opportunities
Help automate operational workflows across tasks, SOPs, planning, product research, supplier research and internal communication
Data, Reporting & Insights
Build dashboards and reporting workflows that give clear visibility across sales, marketing, customer service and operations
Use AI and data to identify growth opportunities, efficiency gains and business risks
Track the impact of automation projects on time saved, cost reduced, output increased and revenue improved
Present recommendations clearly and commercially to leadership
What We’re Looking For

We are looking for someone who is highly practical, commercially minded and technically capable.

You should be able to look at a manual business process and ask:

Can this be automated?
Can AI do 70–90% of this?
Can we make this faster, cheaper or more accurate?
Can we turn this into a repeatable system?
Can we increase output without adding more people?
Essential Skills & Experience
Strong hands-on experience building AI automations, workflows, agents or internal tools
Practical understanding of ecommerce, digital marketing and customer journeys
Experience using AI to improve marketing, content, analysis, customer service, reporting or workflow efficiency
Experience with automation platforms such as Make, Zapier, n8n, Airtable, Relevance AI, Retool, Pipedream or similar
Ability to work with APIs, webhooks, data flows and system integrations
Strong understanding of prompt engineering, AI workflow design and reusable prompt systems
Experience with tools such as Shopify, Klaviyo, Google Analytics, Google Search Console, Meta Ads, Google Ads, helpdesks or similar ecommerce platforms
Ability to turn messy processes into clean, usable workflows
Strong analytical skills and commercial judgement
Ability to move quickly, test ideas, implement solutions and improve over time
Highly Regarded
Python, JavaScript, Google Apps Script or SQL experience
Experience with RAG systems, embeddings, vector databases or AI knowledge bases
Experience building dashboards, reporting systems or internal tools
Experience with AI creative, image, video or content production workflows
Experience in D2C ecommerce, retail, consumer brands or digital agencies
Experience with Shopify-based businesses
Understanding of SEO, PPC, CRO, email marketing and lifecycle marketing
What Success Looks Like

In this role, success means:

Manual work is reduced across key areas of the business
Marketing, creative, research and reporting output increases
Team members spend less time on repetitive admin and more time on high-value work
AI workflows are reliable, documented and actually used by the team
Customer service and operational processes become faster and more consistent
Growth initiatives are prioritised based on commercial impact
The business becomes more efficient, scalable and data-driven
The Type of Person We Want

You are likely a strong fit if you are:

A builder, not just a strategist
Commercially focused, not just technically curious
Fast, practical and resourceful
Obsessed with AI, automation and better systems
Comfortable working in a fast-moving ecommerce environment
Able to work with ambiguity and turn ideas into working solutions
Interested in creating real business leverage, not just experimenting with tools
Why Join Us

This is a high-impact role in a growing ecommerce business where your work will directly shape how the company operates.

You will work closely with leadership to build AI systems that improve growth, efficiency, customer experience and profitability across multiple brands.

If you are already experimenting with AI every day and want to apply it to real commercial ecommerce problems, this is an opportunity to build meaningful systems with real business impact.

How to Apply

Please apply with:

A short summary of your AI automation and ecommerce experience
Examples of AI workflows, automations, agents, tools or internal systems you have built
The platforms and tools you are strongest with
A brief explanation of how you would use AI to help an ecommerce business operate like a much larger team

positionName: Senior Manager, Business Controls & Monitoring (Mob Way Applicants must identify as Aboriginal and / or Torres Strait Islander)
description:
Senior Manager, Business Controls & Monitoring – Mob Way Applicants must identify as Aboriginal and / or Torres Strait Islander

What’s the role?

This role sits within the Product Governance & Business Controls & Monitoring (BCM) Centre of Excellence and is part of Westpac’s Mob Way program a culturally grounded pathway into senior leadership for Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander peoples. As a Senior Manager, you’ll design, build and operate AI-enabled and automated solutions that uplift group wide Risk Practices, improve insight quality, and reduce reliance on manual processes.

Furthermore, you will ensure solutions are explainable and auditable to meet AI risk standards, acts as an escalation point for complex matters, evolving risk standards/tooling, and mentoring team members.

Through Mob Way, you’ll be supported with strong executive connection, opportunities to build your networks, and dedicated time for community connection and elder-led cultural immersion.

This is a full-time, permanent role based in Sydney , with working options available.

What do I need?

Experience applying analytics, automation or AI-enabled approaches in complex environments
Ability to design or contribute to data, analytics or technology solutions
Demonstrated experience developing Power Platform solutions that embed analytics into business-as-usual (BAU) workflows
Experience building dashboards, tools, workflows or analytical pipelines
Ability to work with senior stakeholders and explain complex topics clearly

Why join us?

As part of Mob Way, you will receive tailored career support through executive coaching, culturally safe leadership development, and opportunities to strengthen your connection to community and culture.

At Westpac, we’re passionate about becoming our customers’ #1 banking partner for life and we know that starts with creating the best workplace in the country. For Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander peoples, that means more than just a job.

Special offers on banking products and discounts from top brands, including generous employee-only mortgage rates!
We offer flexible work arrangements to support your work/life balance, along with a variety of leave options including Culture (such as dedicated Sorry Business Leave), Lifestyle, and Wellbeing Leave.
Tailored career support through our Indigenous Careers Team, and growth opportunities like the Echo mentoring program and Jawun secondments.
Lots of opportunities to ‘give back’ to the Community by getting involved in our many volunteering initiatives including our Brothers & Sisters Employee Advocacy Group.

Create your future today

To get started, simply click on the APPLY or APPLY NOW button

#mobway

Create your future today through a role supported by our Mob Way program and a workplace committed to supporting your career.

We’re all about creating a supportive and inclusive community. We welcome everyone – no matter your age, gender, background, or abilities. We also provide additional support to welcome our veterans, Indigenous Australians and neurodiverse community.

If you need any adjustments during the recruitment process, you can find out more information and additional contact details by visiting the "People with Disability and/or needing Accessibility Requirements" page on our website.

At Westpac we aim to help Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander Australians succeed by supporting Indigenous employees to build meaningful careers. Westpac has been granted an S126 Exemption to sections eight (8) and fifty-one (51) of the Anti-Discrimination Act 1977 (NSW); this allows us to advertise and recruit this role exclusively to Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander applicants. This forms part of Westpac’s strategy to foster a multicultural, vibrant and diverse workforce
descriptionHTML:
Senior Manager, Business Controls & Monitoring – Mob Way Applicants must identify as Aboriginal and / or Torres Strait Islander

What’s the role?

This role sits within the Product Governance & Business Controls & Monitoring (BCM) Centre of Excellence and is part of Westpac’s Mob Way program a culturally grounded pathway into senior leadership for Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander peoples. As a Senior Manager, you’ll design, build and operate AI-enabled and automated solutions that uplift group wide Risk Practices, improve insight quality, and reduce reliance on manual processes.

Furthermore, you will ensure solutions are explainable and auditable to meet AI risk standards, acts as an escalation point for complex matters, evolving risk standards/tooling, and mentoring team members.

Through Mob Way, you’ll be supported with strong executive connection, opportunities to build your networks, and dedicated time for community connection and elder-led cultural immersion.

This is a full-time, permanent role based in Sydney , with working options available.

What do I need?

  • Experience applying analytics, automation or AI-enabled approaches in complex environments
  • Ability to design or contribute to data, analytics or technology solutions
  • Demonstrated experience developing Power Platform solutions that embed analytics into business-as-usual (BAU) workflows
  • Experience building dashboards, tools, workflows or analytical pipelines
    • Ability to work with senior stakeholders and explain complex topics clearly

Why join us?

As part of Mob Way, you will receive tailored career support through executive coaching, culturally safe leadership development, and opportunities to strengthen your connection to community and culture.

At Westpac, we’re passionate about becoming our customers’ #1 banking partner for life and we know that starts with creating the best workplace in the country. For Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander peoples, that means more than just a job.

  • Special offers on banking products and discounts from top brands, including generous employee-only mortgage rates!
  • We offer flexible work arrangements to support your work/life balance, along with a variety of leave options including Culture (such as dedicated Sorry Business Leave), Lifestyle, and Wellbeing Leave.
  • Tailored career support through our Indigenous Careers Team, and growth opportunities like the Echo mentoring program and Jawun secondments.
    • Lots of opportunities to ‘give back’ to the Community by getting involved in our many volunteering initiatives including our Brothers & Sisters Employee Advocacy Group.

Create your future today

To get started, simply click on the APPLY or APPLY NOW button

#mobway

Create your future today through a role supported by our Mob Way program and a workplace committed to supporting your career.

We’re all about creating a supportive and inclusive community. We welcome everyone – no matter your age, gender, background, or abilities. We also provide additional support to welcome our veterans, Indigenous Australians and neurodiverse community.

If you need any adjustments during the recruitment process, you can find out more information and additional contact details by visiting the "People with Disability and/or needing Accessibility Requirements" page on our website.

At Westpac we aim to help Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander Australians succeed by supporting Indigenous employees to build meaningful careers. Westpac has been granted an S126 Exemption to sections eight (8) and fifty-one (51) of the Anti-Discrimination Act 1977 (NSW); this allows us to advertise and recruit this role exclusively to Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander applicants. This forms part of Westpac’s strategy to foster a multicultural, vibrant and diverse workforce

description:
Build a Safer World.

TRM Labs provides AI-powered intelligence solutions that help public and private sector agencies investigate and disrupt crime. TRM's platforms enable investigators to trace illicit activity, build cases, and construct operating pictures of threat networks. Leading agencies and businesses worldwide rely on TRM to make the world safer and more secure.

Role Overview:

The threats TRM's analysts work to disrupt aren't abstract — they fund violence, enable exploitation, and undermine global security. As a Threat Intelligence Analyst, Financial Networks, you will drive TRM's collection, analysis, and reporting on the actors and networks behind these harms, applying threat intelligence tradecraft to identify, track, and expose adversaries who use traditional financial systems alongside crypto to move value, evade sanctions, and obscure their activity.

The impact you’ll have here:

Develop novel collection strategies and analytic methodologies to surface threat actor activity that isn't visible through conventional tooling or open databases

Conduct proactive threat hunting across on-chain and off-chain data sources to identify emerging illicit networks before they're widely known

Map financial networks used for sanctions evasion — including commodity flows, corporate ownership structures, and offshore financial centers — and connect them to on-chain activity

Track, analyze, and report on threat actors, campaigns, and illicit networks that operate across both traditional financial infrastructure and the crypto ecosystem

Identify and assess adversary TTPs, infrastructure, and behavior to support detection and attribution efforts

Leverage OSINT and other intelligence methods to uncover hidden threats and generate actionable insights

Produce high-quality intelligence that reaches law enforcement and government partners worldwide

Collaborate with data, engineering, and product teams to enhance TRM's intelligence capabilities

Be a leading voice on how adversaries exploit the intersection of traditional finance and crypto to move illicit value

What we’re looking for:

Proven experience in threat intelligence, cyber intelligence, or national security intelligence roles (this is not an entry-level position)

Fluency in Russian, Chinese (Mandarin or Cantonese), or Farsi, with the ability to conduct research and analysis in that language, is strongly preferred and may be required in some cases. Please specify your language proficiency and any relevant experience in your application.

A track record of generating net-new intelligence — developing original hypotheses, pursuing non-obvious investigative threads, and surfacing findings that others miss

Background in sanctions enforcement, financial intelligence, or corporate investigations is strongly preferred

Working knowledge of blockchain and cryptocurrency — including how transactions work, on-chain tracing concepts, and the role of crypto in financial crime (e.g., ransomware, sanctions evasion, darknet markets)

Demonstrated ability to analyze corporate ownership structures, beneficial ownership, and cross-border fund flows — including how adversaries use trade finance, commodity flows, and offshore financial centers to move value

Experience tracking threat actors, nation-state activity, or sanctioned entities — including analysis of TTPs, infrastructure, and financial behavior to support attribution

Strong analytical and communication skills with the ability to produce clear, actionable intelligence reports with high intelligence tradecraft standards.

Ability to collaborate cross-functionally with technical and non-technical stakeholders

Comfort operating in ambiguous, low-signal environments where the analytic path forward has to be constructed, not followed — and the ownership mindset to drive it independently

Hands-on experience with blockchain analysis tools (e.g., Chainalysis Reactor, TRM, Elliptic) or formal cryptocurrency investigation experience

Relevant certifications (e.g., GIAC, CEH, Chainalysis Reactor Certification) or background in cybersecurity, intelligence, or investigations

About the team:

Our team values open communication and a collaborative work environment, where feedback and ideas are encouraged from all members.

We prioritize flexibility and adaptability, ensuring that everyone stays aligned through regular check-ins and progress updates. Operating with a strong focus on shared goals, we work together to meet deadlines while supporting individual growth and innovation.

We are a globally distributed team that leans heavily on asynchronous communication. While we aim for ~3–4 hours of overlap with the Pacific Time Zone, schedules are flexible and may occasionally adjust to support team collaboration.

Learn about TRM Speed in this position:

Utilizes sound judgment and the 80/20 principle to drive rapid, high-impact outcomes.

Demonstrates the ability to iterate quickly in response to evolving threat landscapes.

Leverages creative problem-solving to ensure prompt and effective threat actor engagement to acquire attribution within 24 hours.

Employs a diverse set of methodologies to scale and optimize threat intelligence production for customer needs within 72 hours.

Life at TRM

We are building a safer world. That promise shows up in how we work every day.

TRM moves quickly. We are a high velocity, high ownership team that expects clarity, follow-through, and impact. People who thrive here are energized by hard problems, experimentation, and continuous feedback. If something takes months elsewhere, it will ship here in days.

Our work sits at the intersection of AI, national security, and fighting crime. The problems are complex, the stakes are real, and the environment evolves quickly. The pace and intensity of the work reflect the importance of the mission. As a result, the way we operate requires a high level of ownership, adaptability, collaboration, and creative problem-solving.

At TRM, you should expect:

Priorities and targets to change quickly as we experiment and iterate

Work that often requires operating with a high degree of ambiguity

A high level of personal ownership and accountability

Close collaboration across teams and functions

Frequent, high-touch communication

Creative problem solving and out-of-the-box thinking

A pace that rewards urgency, adaptability, and outcomes

This environment is energizing for people who enjoy building, solving hard problems, and making progress in situations that are not always fully defined. It also requires comfort navigating ambiguity, adjusting course as new information emerges, and maintaining focus and positivity in a fast-moving and intense environment.

We also recognize that this style of operating is not for everyone. If you are primarily optimizing for predictability or a consistently balanced workload, we encourage you to use the interview process to pressure test whether this environment is truly the right fit. We want teammates who thrive here, not just survive here.

At the same time, many people find this work deeply rewarding. If you are excited by meaningful problems, motivated by ambitious goals, and energized by working alongside mission-driven colleagues, there is a good chance you will find TRM to be an exceptional place to grow and contribute. Learn more: Interviewing at TRM: How We Hire and What Success Looks Like

AI Fluency at TRM

AI fluency is a baseline expectation at TRM.

We believe AI meaningfully changes how top performers operate. We expect every team member to use AI to accelerate and reimagine their craft, not just automate surface tasks.

At TRM, AI fluency means you are among the top 10 percent of operators in your function in how you apply AI to:

Accelerate repeatable workflows

Structure and solve problems

Improve output quality

Increase speed and leverage

You will be evaluated on applied AI fluency during the interview process.

Leadership Principles

We hire and grow against three leadership principles. They’re the standards for how we operate, treat each other, and make decisions.

Impact-Oriented Trailblazer: We put customers first and move with speed, focus, and adaptability. We treat every plan like an experiment – test, ship, measure, and iterate quickly.

Master Craftsperson: We care deeply about our craft. We balance speed with high standards, own outcomes end‑to‑end, and invest in getting better everyday.

Inspiring Colleague: We add clarity and energy, not noise. We bring humility, candor, and a one‑team mindset — giving and receiving feedback to make the team stronger.

Join our Mission

At TRM we care deeply about our craft. We are looking for individuals who want their work to matter, who experiment with speed and rigor, and who take pride in building a safer world for billions of people. If you’re excited by TRM’s mission but don’t check every box, we encourage you to apply — we hire for slope, judgment, and the will to learn fast.

TRM is a Series C company with $220M in total funding, backed by Blockchain Capital, Goldman Sachs, Bessemer, Y Combinator, Thoma Bravo, and others. Headquartered in San Francisco, TRM operates as a distributed-first company with hubs in Los Angeles, San Francisco, New York, Washington D.C., London, and Singapore.

Privacy Policy and Additional Information

By submitting your application, you are agreeing to allow TRM to process your personal information in accordance with the TRM Privacy Policy.

Our typical hiring cycles for specialized roles span 24 to 36 months. Accordingly, we retain your personal information for up to 36 months to evaluate your application and to consider you for current and future employment opportunities, unless you request earlier deletion or a different retention period is required or permitted by law.

To notify TRM Labs that you believe this job posting is non-compliant, please submit a report through this form. No response will be provided to inquiries unrelated to job posting compliance.

The use of AI tools of any kind (including but not limited to notetakers, interview assistants, and real-time coaching tools such as Otter.ai, Fireflies, Fathom, Cluey, or similar) during TRM interviews is not permitted without prior approval from TRM. TRM uses its own internal tools for note-taking to ensure a consistent and confidential experience for all candidates.

We are committed to providing reasonable accommodations to applicants with disabilities, and requests can be made via this form.

Recruitment agencies

TRM Labs does not accept unsolicited agency resumes. Please do not forward resumes to TRM employees. TRM Labs is not responsible for any fees related to unsolicited resumes and will not pay fees to any third-party agency or company without a signed agreement.

Learn More: Company Values | Interviewing | FAQs

description:
NeoXam is a growing international financial software company headquartered in Paris, with a strong and expanding presence across Australia. Through our combined expertise and transparent approach, we help Buy-Side and Sell-Side organizations - across banking, asset management, and insurance - navigate the continuous changes shaping the financial industry. Our solutions span the complete financial value chain, from centralized data management to front- and back-office software.

With 750+ dedicated staff across 21 offices worldwide - including Sydney, Melbourne, New York, Boston, Paris, Frankfurt, Luxembourg, Zurich, Geneva, Milan, London, Hong Kong, Shanghai, Singapore, Noida, Chennai, and more - NeoXam combines global scale with an entrepreneurial, collaborative culture. More than 150 clients across the globe trust NeoXam to run their most critical workflows.

Join us and be part of a thriving, internationally recognized financial software company making waves in the industry.
As part of our expanding NeoXam as a Service (NXaaS) operating model, we are looking for a motivated and technically strong Technical Support & Cloud Consultant to join our Australian team. This is a hands-on role that blends client-facing support with cloud infrastructure ownership, making it ideal for someone who thrives at the intersection of financial software and modern cloud operations. As we continue to grow our global SaaS footprint, this role will evolve alongside increasing monitoring, observability, and operational responsibilities. NXaaS operates a follow-the-sun support model across APAC, EMEA, and the Americas - this role covers Australian business hours.

You will support NeoXam's core product suite, including:

NeoXam DataHub – a centralized data management platform purpose-built for financial institutions, serving as a hub for all data consumed or produced across the enterprise.

NeoXam IBOR – a uniquely independent and flexible Investment Book of Record, providing asset managers with a single, continuously updated source for positions, valuations, and forecasts across multiple views.

NeoXam Aro – an intelligent, low-code reconciliation and data lifecycle platform that transforms fragmented, manual processes into a unified, AI-powered solution.

NeoXam Impress – an end-to-end solution for client, digital, and regulatory reporting, covering data integration, performance calculations, production workflows, and data visualization.

Your Missions
· Serve as the first technical point of contact for NeoXam's global clients, covering Australian business hours within NeoXam's follow-the-sun support model and ensuring seamless handoff with APAC and EMEA counterparts.

· Triage, analyze, and own support tickets end-to-end - from anomaly identification and root cause investigation through to resolution, including software-level fixes in Java and escalation to R&D when required.

· Review and respond to monitoring alerts and operational dashboards, identifying and acting on anomalies before they impact clients; monitor and manage cloud resources (primarily AWS) to ensure performance and availability against production SLAs.

· Troubleshoot and enhance cloud-based deployments; implement patches and configuration fixes; support data migration and cloud onboarding initiatives in collaboration with clients and internal teams.

· Manage technical installations, upgrades, and environment refreshes for client-facing deployments; maintain internal environment replicas for issue reproduction and testing.

· Collaborate with the Paris R&D team and global support counterparts to resolve complex issues and contribute to the NXaaS knowledge base.

· Support pre-sales and implementation teams on technical matters; participate in client calls, project updates, and cross-team coordination.

Your Profile

· Bachelor's or Master's degree in Computer Science, Engineering, or a related field.

· 3-5 years of experience in technical support, implementation, or infrastructure roles within financial software or the banking/financial services industry.

· Solid working knowledge of AWS (EC2, RDS, ALB, S3, CloudWatch, VPC, IAM); AWS certification is a plus.

· Proficient in Java application troubleshooting - log analysis, memory management, thread dumps, and configuration.

· Ability to diagnose and interpret common database performance issues - including slow queries, missing indexes, lock contention, and execution plan analysis. Experience with Oracle and/or PostgreSQL query tuning is strongly preferred.

· Experience with relational databases - Oracle, PostgreSQL, or SQL Server.

· Scripting ability (Shell, Python, or equivalent) for automation and diagnostics.

· Familiarity with web application servers (Tomcat, JBoss, or WebSphere) and messaging systems (IBM MQ, Kafka, or equivalent).

· Working knowledge of XML, REST/SOAP web services, and API integrations.

· EKS (Elastic Kubernetes Service): Strong understanding of Kubernetes fundamentals and practical experience with Amazon EKS for container orchestration from support perspective.

· Monitoring & Observability Tools: Hands-on experience with solutions such as Datadog, Grafana, CloudWatch or similar platforms for system monitoring, logging, and performance visualization.

· Familiarity with Git and standard version control workflows.

· Hands-on experience with Linux/Unix environments and command-line operations.

· Understanding of Agile/Scrum methodology and ITIL support practices.

· Strong written and verbal communication skills; ability to manage client relationships professionally and independently.

· Self-motivated, proactive, and collaborative — comfortable working across time zones with global teams.

Why NeoXam?

At NeoXam, we love people who are curious, involved, and self-driven. We encourage creativity and strive to promote an environment and culture based on personal growth and development. We want you to express your full potential and become a major contributor to our continued growth across Australia and beyond.

description:
AI Job Summary
Manage contracts, meet deadlines, and complete reports.
Strong knowledge of best-practice dementia care and quality assurance principles.
Build and nurture stakeholder relationships with professionalism and integrity.
 Sign up or log in to analyse my fit
Role Type
On-site • Permanent • Full-time • Mid-level Senior
Pay Rate
46.15 AUD – 51.28 AUD (Hour)
Description

About Us

Since 1982, Alzheimer’s WA has been a leading voice and support for people living with dementia, their families, and carers. As we continue to grow and expand our services, we are moving in a positive and promising direction—championing person-centred care, advocacy, education, and holistic support through our CHSP, SaH, and Grant funding, as well as day and overnight respite across Perth, Mandurah, and Albany. Our mission is to walk alongside those living with dementia, making a meaningful difference every day.

Why Join Us?

Salary packaging up to $18,550 per annum.
5 weeks of annual leave and 17.5% leave loading.
On-site free parking.
Market leading Employee Assistance Program.
On-going professional development.

Position Summary

Are you passionate about making a difference in the lives of people living with dementia? Reporting to the Education/Consulting Lead, Alzheimer’s WA is seeking an experienced Dementia Consultant to lead strategic initiatives, drive innovation, and champion best-practice dementia care across Western Australia.

Key Responsibilities

Develop and implement strategies that position Alzheimer’s WA as a leader in dementia care.
Meet service agreements and grant funding budgets, reporting requirements and drive outcomes
Lead research and data-driven projects to inform service delivery and enhance outcomes.
Liaise with and build industry Dementia Change Champions through a scholarship program
Build and nurture stakeholder relationships, representing Alzheimer’s WA with professionalism and integrity.
Guide campaigns and key activities, increasing awareness and driving growth in line with our strategic plan.

About You

Tertiary qualifications in social sciences, human services, aged care, or equivalent experience (desirable).
Strong knowledge of best-practice dementia care and quality assurance principles.
Proven ability to manage contracts, meet deadlines and complete reports.
Excellent communication and stakeholder engagement skills.
Advanced proficiency in Microsoft 365 and related tools.
Experience in health or aged care, regional service delivery, and understanding of Support at Home frameworks is preferred.

Why Join Us?

Be part of a growing organisation with a bright future
Make a real difference in the lives of people living with dementia and their carers
Shape the future of dementia care in WA
Mentor and support junior staff as you grow your own career
Blend creativity with passion in a values-driven environment
Enjoy a supportive, collaborative team that values your contribution

Ready to Grow Your Career with Purpose? Apply now and help us build a safer, more supportive environment for our staff and the people we care for.

If you have questions about the position, you can contact us at

hr@alzheimerswa.org.au

Please note: We reserve the right to close the position without notice.

At Alzheimer’s WA, we believe that we have an important role to play in promoting diversity, equality, respect and inclusion in our society. People from culturally diverse backgrounds are encouraged to apply.

description:
AI Job Summary
Foundational knowledge of networking, server environments, and end-user computing.
Ability to troubleshoot and resolve hardware, software, and network issues methodically.
Full Australian work rights to be eligible for the role.
 Sign up or log in to analyse my fit
Role Type
On-site • Permanent • Full-time • Entry Level
Description

About the role

NetStrategy works exclusively in K–12 education. We have supported over 400 schools across Australia for more than 40 years. ICT is not a sideline for us — it is the business. The people we hire are responsible for the technology that keeps schools running.

This ICT Support role sits at the centre of that work. You will be based on-site at a leading independent school, embedded in the day-to-day technology operations of the school. You will work alongside experienced engineers and service delivery professionals, contributing to real infrastructure across servers, networks, cloud platforms, and end-user environments.

This is an entry-to-junior role. We hire on aptitude and attitude as much as experience. If you are early in your career and serious about growing into a systems and infrastructure specialist, this is a structured path to do that.

What you’ll be doing

Providing day-to-day ICT support across end-user computing, connectivity, and core infrastructure
Troubleshooting hardware, software, and network issues — working through problems methodically and escalating when needed
Contributing to infrastructure projects alongside senior engineers
Work within a team to support broad and varied systems from leading vendors.
Maintaining clear, timely communication with school staff and stakeholders when issues arise
Keeping technical documentation accurate and up to date

Who we’re looking for

You may be finishing a degree or certification, or you may have one to three years of hands-on experience. What matters more than credentials is how you approach the work.
You work through problems logically and know when to ask for help
You communicate clearly with people who aren’t technical — and you don’t make them feel like they should be
You follow through. If you say you’ll do something, you do it
You’re curious about technology and take it on yourself to stay current
You take the work seriously but don’t need to be chased to do it well

Skills and Experience

Essential:

Foundational knowledge of networking, server environments, and end-user computing
Familiarity with Microsoft 365 and cloud-based infrastructure
Ability to troubleshoot and resolve technical issues methodically
Clear written and verbal communication
Full Australian work rights

Advantageous:

Experience with VMware, Hyper-V, or Azure environments
Familiarity with ITSM tools or ticketing systems (ConnectWise is a plus)
Exposure to Extreme Networks, HPE Aruba, Juniper MIST, or similar switching/Wi-Fi platforms
Experience with backup technologies (Veeam, Barracuda)
MacOS and JAMF experience
CCNA, JNCP, ACMP or equivalent certification
Any prior experience in or with a school environment

Why NetStrategy?

We work in one sector. That means the context you build here is deep rather than scattered. You will understand schools — their operations, their rhythms, their risk profile — in a way that generalist IT providers never do.

NetStrategy invests in the development of its technical staff. This role has a clear path forward, and we support people who want to move along it.

Our Values

Own It
Go Further
Lift Others
Stay Grounded

Note: NetStrategy is not in a position to offer visa sponsorship. Applications open to those with full Australian Work Rights. Only shortlisted candidates will be contacted. No recruiters please.

Company Overview
For over 35 years, NetStrategy has partnered with Australian K–12 schools to deliver technology solutions that enhance learning without adding complexity. As the only wholly education-focused provider in the Australian market, we combine specialised IT support, consulting, and cybersecurity services with a collaborative approach that strengthens and uplifts in-house IT teams. Guided by our values—Own It, Stay Grounded, Lift Others, Go Further—we continue to deliver trusted, education-centric technology outcomes.

description:
The mission of Speechify is to make sure that reading is never a barrier to learning.

Over 50 million people use Speechify's text-to-speech products to turn whatever they're reading – PDFs, books, Google Docs, news articles, websites – into audio, so they can read faster, read more, and remember more. Speechify's text-to-speech reading products include its iOS app, Android App, Mac App, Chrome Extension, and Web App. Google recently named Speechify the Chrome Extension of the Year and Apple named Speechify its 2025 Design Award winner for Inclusivity.

Today, nearly 200 people around the globe work on Speechify in a 100% distributed setting – Speechify has no office. These include frontend and backend engineers, AI research scientists, and others from Amazon, Microsoft, and Google, leading PhD programs like Stanford, high growth startups like Stripe, Vercel, Bolt, and many founders of their own companies.

Overview

The responsibilities of our Platform team include building and maintaining all backend services, including, but not limited to, payments, analytics, subscriptions, new products, text to speech, and external APIs.

This is a key role and ideal for someone who thinks strategically, enjoys fast-paced environments, is passionate about making product decisions, and has experience building great user experiences that delight users.

We are a flat organization that allows anyone to become a leader by showing excellent technical skills and delivering results consistently and fast. Work ethic, solid communication skills, and obsession with winning are paramount.

Our interview process involves several technical interviews and we aim to complete them within 1 week.

What You'll Do

Design, develop, and maintain robust APIs including public TTS API, internal APIs like Payment, Subscription, Auth and Consumption Tracking, ensuring they meet business and scalability requirements
Oversee the full backend API landscape, enhancing and optimizing for performance and maintainability
Collaborate on B2B solutions, focusing on customization and integration needs for enterprise clients
Work closely with cross-functional teams to align backend architecture with overall product strategy and user experience

An Ideal Candidate Should Have

Proven experience in backend development: TS/Node (required)
Direct experience with GCP and knowledge of AWS, Azure, or other cloud providers
Efficiency in ideation and implementation, prioritizing tasks based on urgency and impact
Preferred: Experience with Docker and containerized deployments
Preferred: Proficiency in deploying high availability applications on Kubernetes

What We Offer

A dynamic environment where your contributions shape the company and its products
A team that values innovation, intuition, and drive
Autonomy, fostering focus and creativity
The opportunity to have a significant impact in a revolutionary industry
Competitive compensation, a welcoming atmosphere, and a commitment to an exceptional asynchronous work culture
The privilege of working on a product that changes lives, particularly for those with learning differences like dyslexia, ADD, and more
An active role at the intersection of artificial intelligence and audio – a rapidly evolving tech domain

Think you're a good fit for this job?

Tell us more about yourself and why you're interested in the role when you apply.
And don't forget to include links to your portfolio and LinkedIn.

Not looking but know someone who would make a great fit?

Refer them!

Speechify is committed to a diverse and inclusive workplace.

Speechify does not discriminate on the basis of race, national origin, gender, gender identity, sexual orientation, protected veteran status, disability, age, or other legally protected status.

description:
AI Job Summary
Registration with AHPRA or membership with ESSA.
Minimum 1 year experience in occupational rehabilitation and/or clinical experience.
Experience delivering Original Employer Services: assessment, intervention and case management.
 Sign up or log in to analyse my fit
Role Type
Permanent • Full-time • Experienced
Pay Rate
85000 AUD – 111000 AUD (Annum)
Description

Rehabilitation Consultant – OT, Physio, EP, Osteo

Work type: Full time (minimum 4 days part time)

Location: Melbourne VIC

About PHSA

Prestige Health Services Australia (PHSA) is a national rehabilitation provider delivering best in industry injury management; disability management and occupational rehabilitation services.

Your Role

PHSA are seeking Rehabilitation Consultants to deliver Original Employer Services (OES) including assessment, intervention and case management services to clientele across varying schemes including TAC (Transport Accident Commission), WorkSafe VIC, Comcare, Life Insurance and Self Insurance with the primary function of supporting recovery and return to work.

What You’ll Need

• Registration with AHPRA (Australian Health Practitioner Regulation Agency) or membership with ESSA (Exercise and Sports Science Australia)

• Exceptional verbal and written communication skills

• Minimum 1 year experience in occupational rehabilitation and/or clinical experience highly regarded

• Direct experience working with TAC referred cases desirable

• Have a current driver’s licence, motor vehicle and willingness to travel to deliver face-to-face servicing as required

Why Choose PHSA?

• Flexibility & Autonomy: Shape your own work schedule and enjoy the freedom to work independently, with support always within reach. Flexible work arrangements also available.

• Career Growth: Unlock pathways to advancement with our comprehensive induction, ongoing training, and mentoring programs.

• Tools for Success: We equip you with a laptop, smartphone, and access to the latest technology.

• Rewarding Salary & Bonuses: Enjoy a highly competitive salary, tailored to your experience, plus a lucrative bonus system for employees.

• Supportive Team Culture: Be part of a passionate, purpose-driven team that values your contributions and celebrates your achievements.

Ready to take the next step in your career? Apply today!

Company Overview
Prestige Health Services Australia (PHSA) is a national rehabilitation provider delivering best in industry injury management; disability management and workplace rehabilitation services. Our mission is to achieve sustainable health, social and return to work outcomes for our clientele through the delivery of innovative workplace; injury management; disability management and rehabilitation services undertaken by experienced consultants with a focus on quality, integrity and belief that we can and will achieve outcomes.

Administer and maintain Microsoft SharePoint environments for optimal performance and user experience.
Design, implement, and manage infrastructure solutions supporting business operations.
Troubleshoot and resolve technical issues related to SharePoint and infrastructure components.
 Sign up or log in to analyse my fit
Role Type
On-site • Contract • Full-time • Mid-level Senior
Description

Summary:

We are seeking a skilled SharePoint and Infrastructure Engineer to join our IT team in Canberra on a contract basis. This role is crucial for enhancing our digital collaboration tools and ensuring robust infrastructure support. The ideal candidate will have a strong background in SharePoint administration and infrastructure management, contributing to the optimisation of our IT services and systems.

Responsibilities:

Administer and maintain SharePoint environments, ensuring optimal performance and user experience.
Design, implement, and manage infrastructure solutions that support business operations.
Collaborate with cross-functional teams to gather requirements and deliver effective IT solutions.
Troubleshoot and resolve technical issues related to SharePoint and infrastructure components.
Monitor system performance and implement improvements to enhance efficiency and reliability.
Provide technical support and training to end-users on SharePoint functionalities.
Document processes, configurations, and changes to maintain accurate records.

Qualifications:

Bachelor’s degree in Information Technology, Computer Science, or a related field.
Minimum of 5 years of experience in SharePoint administration and infrastructure management.
Strong knowledge of Microsoft SharePoint, Windows Server, and networking concepts.
Experience with cloud services and virtualisation technologies.
Proficient in scripting and automation tools to streamline processes.
Excellent problem-solving skills and attention to detail.
Strong communication and interpersonal skills to work effectively with team members and stakeholders.

description:
AI Job Summary
Proven experience working with property developers and major growth projects.
Strong background in greenfield and brownfield utilities infrastructure, ideally in water and/or wastewater networks.
Demonstrated capability in capacity planning, development approvals and growth-driven infrastructure delivery.
 Sign up or log in to analyse my fit
Role Type
Permanent • Full-time • Mid-level Senior
Description

Planning Manager – Development & Growth Enablement

Water, Wastewater & Recycled Water Networks

We are seeking an experienced Planning Manager with a strong development-sector background to lead the planning and delivery of developer-driven water, wastewater and recycled water infrastructure. This is a leadership role at the interface between the utility and the development industry, enabling sustainable growth across residential, commercial and industrial developments.

This role is ideal for a planning or infrastructure leader who thrives in fast-paced growth environments, understands development pressures, and can balance commercial outcomes with technical integrity and long‑term network performance.

The Opportunity

As Planning Manager, you will be the primary point of contact for the development sector, coordinating servicing strategies, growth infrastructure, and capacity planning to ensure developments are delivered on time, on budget and compliant. You will influence outcomes across councils, regulators, developers, consultants and internal stakeholders, ensuring network capacity stays ahead of growth.

Key Responsibilities

Development & Growth Leadership

Act as the key interface between the utility and the development industry, providing clear, consistent and timely servicing outcomes
Lead the planning, coordination and delivery of developer-funded infrastructure, including reticulation, augmentations and growth-driven upgrades
Review developer reticulation and connection applications, designs and staging plans, issuing permits and approvals that protect asset performance and service quality

Planning, Capacity & Program Delivery

Manage long-range capacity forecasting for treatment, storage and network assets across multiple schemes
Plan and schedule greenfield and brownfield upgrades, aligning growth triggers, delivery timing and funding
Administer Project Delivery Agreements (PDAs), including forecasting, budgeting and capacity tracking (estimate vs design vs actuals)

Commercial, Regulatory & Stakeholder Management

Maintain strong controls over financial performance, forecasting and funding requirements
Manage regulatory approvals and compliance (EPA, IPART, Councils), ensuring schemes remain fully licensed and audit-ready
Represent the organisation professionally with developers, councils, regulators and industry bodies

Leadership & Governance

Lead with a strong safety focus, embedding WHS compliance across all activities
Establish and maintain fit-for-purpose policies, plans and procedures
Develop and mentor a high-performing team, building capability in development servicing and infrastructure planning

What You’ll Bring

Essential Experience

Proven experience working with property developers and major growth projects
Strong background in greenfield and brownfield utilities infrastructure, ideally within water and/or wastewater networks
Demonstrated capability in capacity planning, development approvals and growth-driven infrastructure delivery

Qualifications & Background

Engineering degree or 10+ years relevant industry experience (wastewater and/or water networks)
Utilities construction experience
Utilities project management experience
Strong stakeholder and commercial management skills
Current driver’s licence

If you are a strategic planning leader with deep development experience and a passion for enabling growth through smart infrastructure, we’d love to hear from you.

Why Join Us?

We are an innovator and look to solve problems no one else can
Supportive leadership and clear development pathways
Modern office in North Sydney with great transport access and end of trip facilities
Leadership that invests in coaching, not just metrics
A team environment that backs each other during peak periods
We value performance, ownership and collaboration and we reward people who step up and contribute.

Apply now. Let’s have a conversation. We review applications as they come in so don’t delay.

Company Overview
Are you a forward-thinker? Are you passionate about creating a brighter future for all? Are you full of ideas on how to better utilise power, water and data? If so, Altogether could just be the place for you. Working alongside developers and body corporates you have the opportunity to play an influential role in delivering ethical, sustainable solutions for communities and their residents – making positive change with real impact. So what’s it like working at Altogether? Here, we take a refreshing and diverse approach to culture. We understand the power of brilliant ideas. It’s what drives us. By prioritising collaboration and championing forethought, together we can solve some of the planet’s most pressing issues. We’re looking for pioneers and creators, people who are eager to make a difference. It’s not about former glories or fancy CV’s. If you’re a good human being and brimming with potential we will provide you with an opportunity. One where you’re able to craft a unique career path, supported by a range of initiatives including transformational eLearning, mentoring and professional development. Over the last two years, the idea of the office has shifted dramatically and we’ve shifted with it with technology and systems adapting to individual needs. There is life outside of work. We understand that. Your wellbeing is of the utmost importance and is key to producing consistently great work. From the introduction of Wellbeing Day to health discounts and transformational parental leave, we place the emotional and physical wellness of its staff above all. At Altogether, we’re not just going through the motions. What we do here matters. Working here, you’ll make a direct and lasting impact on the quality of life of people all across Australia. It’s about creating a better future for communities – an ambition you’ll feel driven to achieve each and every day. Altogether Group Pty Ltd is an equal opportunities employer and prohibits discrimination and harassment of any kind. All employment decisions at Altogether are based on business needs, job requirements and individual qualifications, without regard to race, colour, religion or belief, family or parental status, or any other status protected by the laws or regulations in the locations where we operate. Altogether will not tolerate discrimination or harassment based on any of these characteristics. We encourage applicants of all ages.
descriptionHTML:
AI Job Summary

  • Proven experience working with property developers and major growth projects.
  • Strong background in greenfield and brownfield utilities infrastructure, ideally in water and/or wastewater networks.
  • Demonstrated capability in capacity planning, development approvals and growth-driven infrastructure delivery.

Sign up or log in to analyse my fit

Role Type

Permanent • Full-time • Mid-level Senior

Description

Planning Manager – Development & Growth Enablement

Water, Wastewater & Recycled Water Networks

We are seeking an experienced Planning Manager with a strong development-sector background to lead the planning and delivery of developer-driven water, wastewater and recycled water infrastructure. This is a leadership role at the interface between the utility and the development industry, enabling sustainable growth across residential, commercial and industrial developments.

This role is ideal for a planning or infrastructure leader who thrives in fast-paced growth environments, understands development pressures, and can balance commercial outcomes with technical integrity and long‑term network performance.

The Opportunity

As Planning Manager, you will be the primary point of contact for the development sector, coordinating servicing strategies, growth infrastructure, and capacity planning to ensure developments are delivered on time, on budget and compliant. You will influence outcomes across councils, regulators, developers, consultants and internal stakeholders, ensuring network capacity stays ahead of growth.

Key Responsibilities

Development & Growth Leadership

  • Act as the key interface between the utility and the development industry, providing clear, consistent and timely servicing outcomes
  • Lead the planning, coordination and delivery of developer-funded infrastructure, including reticulation, augmentations and growth-driven upgrades
  • Review developer reticulation and connection applications, designs and staging plans, issuing permits and approvals that protect asset performance and service quality

Planning, Capacity & Program Delivery

  • Manage long-range capacity forecasting for treatment, storage and network assets across multiple schemes
  • Plan and schedule greenfield and brownfield upgrades, aligning growth triggers, delivery timing and funding
  • Administer Project Delivery Agreements (PDAs), including forecasting, budgeting and capacity tracking (estimate vs design vs actuals)

Commercial, Regulatory & Stakeholder Management

  • Maintain strong controls over financial performance, forecasting and funding requirements
  • Manage regulatory approvals and compliance (EPA, IPART, Councils), ensuring schemes remain fully licensed and audit-ready
  • Represent the organisation professionally with developers, councils, regulators and industry bodies

Leadership & Governance

  • Lead with a strong safety focus, embedding WHS compliance across all activities
  • Establish and maintain fit-for-purpose policies, plans and procedures
  • Develop and mentor a high-performing team, building capability in development servicing and infrastructure planning

What You’ll Bring

Essential Experience

  • Proven experience working with property developers and major growth projects
  • Strong background in greenfield and brownfield utilities infrastructure, ideally within water and/or wastewater networks
  • Demonstrated capability in capacity planning, development approvals and growth-driven infrastructure delivery

Qualifications & Background

  • Engineering degree or 10+ years relevant industry experience (wastewater and/or water networks)
  • Utilities construction experience
  • Utilities project management experience
  • Strong stakeholder and commercial management skills
  • Current driver’s licence

If you are a strategic planning leader with deep development experience and a passion for enabling growth through smart infrastructure, we’d love to hear from you.

Why Join Us?

  • We are an innovator and look to solve problems no one else can
  • Supportive leadership and clear development pathways
  • Modern office in North Sydney with great transport access and end of trip facilities
  • Leadership that invests in coaching, not just metrics
  • A team environment that backs each other during peak periods
  • We value performance, ownership and collaboration and we reward people who step up and contribute.

Apply now. Let’s have a conversation. We review applications as they come in so don’t delay.


Company Overview

Are you a forward-thinker? Are you passionate about creating a brighter future for all? Are you full of ideas on how to better utilise power, water and data? If so, Altogether could just be the place for you. Working alongside developers and body corporates you have the opportunity to play an influential role in delivering ethical, sustainable solutions for communities and their residents – making positive change with real impact. So what’s it like working at Altogether? Here, we take a refreshing and diverse approach to culture. We understand the power of brilliant ideas. It’s what drives us. By prioritising collaboration and championing forethought, together we can solve some of the planet’s most pressing issues. We’re looking for pioneers and creators, people who are eager to make a difference. It’s not about former glories or fancy CV’s. If you’re a good human being and brimming with potential we will provide you with an opportunity. One where you’re able to craft a unique career path, supported by a range of initiatives including transformational eLearning, mentoring and professional development. Over the last two years, the idea of the office has shifted dramatically and we’ve shifted with it with technology and systems adapting to individual needs. There is life outside of work. We understand that. Your wellbeing is of the utmost importance and is key to producing consistently great work. From the introduction of Wellbeing Day to health discounts and transformational parental leave, we place the emotional and physical wellness of its staff above all. At Altogether, we’re not just going through the motions. What we do here matters. Working here, you’ll make a direct and lasting impact on the quality of life of people all across Australia. It’s about creating a better future for communities – an ambition you’ll feel driven to achieve each and every day. All employment decisions at Altogether are based on business needs, job requirements and individual qualifications, without regard to race, colour, religion or belief, family or parental status, or any other status protected by the laws or regulations in the locations where we operate. description:
We’re a leading business management solution with a core purpose: helping more businesses in Australia and New Zealand start, survive and succeed.

At MYOB, we believe what’s good for one business is good for all business—and for all of us. Whether you support them, work for them, or dream of building your own, when businesses run smoothly, everybody feels it. Owners, employees, customers, suppliers—even families. That’s why we’re here: to give every person in business the tools they need to focus on what really matters and do Big Things—whatever big looks like for them.

And for you? Joining MYOB means being part of that impact. It means using your skills to help businesses thrive, shaping the future of work, and growing alongside the people and communities we support. Because while we’re the business of software, we’re really in the business of people. And that makes MYOB Everyone’s Business.

Help Shape the Future of AI-Powered Innovation at MYOB

This is a high-impact role at the heart of our AI transformation journey. As an AI Platform Engineer, you’ll design and build the cloud foundations that empower teams across the organization to innovate with AI—safely, at scale, and with speed.

Your work will support the development of innovative products, insights, and efficiencies that will impact the future of our business. From embedding FinOps and observability to staying ahead of the latest in AI infrastructure, you’ll play a critical role in making advanced AI capabilities accessible, sustainable, and enterprise-ready.

What You’ll Do
Build secure, reusable AWS infrastructure components to support internal AI and data solution teams.
Implement platform observability with proactive monitoring and alerting for all AI and ML workloads.
Embed FinOps practices into platform design to drive cloud cost visibility and optimisation.
Stay across emerging AI tech and AWS updates to continually enhance platform capabilities.
Influence internal standards and tooling to reflect high-quality AI infrastructure.
Provide clear guidance and enablement materials to support team adoption of platform services.
What You’ll Bring
Solid experience in cloud platform engineering, DevOps, data infrastructure, or MLOps roles.
Strong hands-on skills with AWS services like SageMaker, Bedrock, Lambda, S3, IAM, CloudWatch, Step Functions, and API Gateway.
Consistent record applying FinOps practices for cost tracking, tagging, reporting, and optimisation.
Experience deploying and running data and ML workloads in AWS environments.
Skilled in infrastructure-as-code using Terraform, AWS CDK, or CloudFormation.
Experience integrating security, compliance, and governance into cloud-native platforms.
Familiarity with emerging AI technologies such as LLMs, vector databases, and orchestration frameworks.
Bachelor’s or Master’s degree or equivalent experience in Computer Science, Data Science, Machine Learning, or a related field and relevant certifications (e.g., AWS Certified Machine Learning – Specialty) are highly desirable.
Our Culture & Benefits
At MYOB our values are at the heart of everything we do. If you want to work and collaborate where opinions are valued, and your ideas can make a difference, come to a place where Your Work Matters.

Enjoy a flexible, hybrid workplace that brings balance to how you work, all of our centrally located offices come together for a number of in-person celebrations and social events
Set up your home office with a little financial help from us, and take advantage of great corporate discounts and vouchers from top retailers
Benefit from our partnership with Sonder, which supports the wellbeing of our team members
Unlock your potential via in-house training and programs, LinkedIn Learning, conferences and study assistance
Access to a variety of leave options available to support your wellbeing, including up to an additional four weeks of purchased leave, generous parental leave, domestic violence leave, transgender leave, community leave, study leave, and more
Join our communities, built around ‘Wellness’, ‘Belonging’ and the ‘Planet’ where you can make a meaningful contribution

At MYOB, we’re all about embracing difference and backing people to be their true selves. We’re proud to be an equal opportunity employer and big believers that the best hires don’t just fit our culture — they add to it. That means bringing your unique contributions, lived experiences, and fresh perspective to the table.

Love the sound of MYOB and this role but don’t tick every single box in this ad? Apply anyway! Studies show that women and underrepresented groups often hold back if they don’t meet every requirement — but we want to hear from you.

We’re proud to be a Circle Back Initiative Employer and we commit to responding to every applicant.
We may use artificial intelligence (AI) tools to support parts of the hiring process, such as reviewing applications, analyzing resumes, or assessing responses. These tools assist our recruitment team but do not replace human judgment. Final hiring decisions are ultimately made by humans. If you would like more information about how your data is processed, please contact us.

SalaryNegotiable
LocationSydney CBD
Job type Contract
Discipline Digital & Product Engineering
Reference90M0707195_1777609373
Job description

FinXL by Randstad Digital focuses on accelerating client's infrastructure, digital and AI transformation journeys by providing specialised skills, managed programs and strategic consulting across various industries.

We solve complex technological and business challenges with highly talented people.

We are currently seeking a Full Stack Developer to join FinXL and drive the architectural uplift, build complex wealth calculation engines and modernise legacy platforms into a scalable, cloud-native ecosystem.

Skills and Experience

Full Stack Engineer experience
Experience in Java, React, MongoDB and JBoss
Experience in Financial Advice, Wealth Management - Superannuation-Insurance or BFSI
Experience in XPLAN, Endeavour or Midwinter
Experience utilising AI productivity tools like GitHub Copilot or Claude
Experience designing & developing robust back-end services and responsive front-end interfaces
Experience writing clean, secure, and scalable code and leveraging AI productivity tools
Experience integrating APIs and working within microservices architectures
Experience mentoring mid-level engineers and providing technical leadership

Next Steps

If you would like to apply to this fantastic opportunity please apply online or call Jackie Gibbons on 0432 268 298

Code: S12

description:
Who we are:

Burson, part of WPP, is the global communications leader built to create value for clients through reputation. With highly specialized teams, industry-leading technologies and breakthrough creative, we help brands and businesses redefine reputation as a competitive advantage so they can lead today and into the future. When you work at Burson, you are part of a global community of lifelong learners who thrive at the edge of innovation.

WPP (LSE/NYSE: WPP) is the trusted growth partner for the world’s leading brands. We unite cutting-edge media intelligence and data solutions, world-class creativity, next-generation production, transformative enterprise solutions and expert strategic counsel in a single company – powered by exceptional talent and our agentic marketing platform, WPP Open, to help our clients navigate change, capture opportunity and deliver transformational growth. For more information, visit WPP.com

For more information visit bursonglobal.com and follow us on LinkedIn and Instagram.

More About The Role

We’re on the lookout for our next Account Coordinator to join our Corpsumer team at Burson

If you’re early in your PR & communications career and keen to work across exciting consumer brands, campaigns, and culture led moments, this could be the role for you.

You’ll join our corpsumer team, supporting integrated campaigns that span media, social, and influencer. This is a great opportunity to build your foundations in PR while gaining hands on experience across the full communications mix, from day to day media relations through to larger campaign moments.

You’ll be part of a collaborative team, learning directly from experienced consultants and getting exposure to how we bring ideas to life for some of the world’s most recognised and emerging brands.

What You’ll Do

Support day-to-day client servicing across consumer accounts
Monitor media, trends, and cultural moments relevant to your clients
Assist with drafting materials including media releases, reports, and campaign summaries
Help coordinate press materials, events, influencer activity, and media outreach
Support social and digital campaign execution
Contribute to research, ideas, and new business opportunities
Learn from senior team members to build your storytelling and client management skills

Experience That Contributes To Success

6-12 months experience or internship in PR, communications, or a related field
Passion for consumer brands, media, and pop culture and corporate
Strong written and verbal communication skills
Curious, proactive, and eager to learn
Interest in social, influencer, and digital trends
Strong attention to detail and ability to multitasks
A team player who thrives in a fast-paced environment

Advantageous: exposure or interest in one or more of our key capability areas:

Earned creative
Social & influence
Data & intelligence (including AI/digital)
Strategy

At Burson, we’re driven by Craft, Curiosity, Counsel, and Camaraderie and we’re always looking for people who bring energy, ideas, and a willingness to grow.

If this sounds like you (or someone in your network), we’d love to hear from you.

#PRJobs #ConsumerPR #Communications #Burson #AgencyLife #Hiring

You belong at Burson:

Our vision is for Burson to be the leading ‘academy company’ for creative communications professionals and emerging leaders. We provide a dynamic environment where ambitious colleagues come to elevate their skills, expand their networks, and ultimately, produce the best solutions for today’s leading brands. Our commitment to your growth is reflected in our robust benefits and people programs, which include professional development opportunities, mentorship programs with industry leaders, a vibrant and inclusive company culture fostering collaboration and innovation and packages designed to reward and retain top talent. We invest in our people because we believe their success is our success.

At Burson, we’re an agency of learn-it-alls. We recognize that the things we do best, creativity and communications, require a level of nuanced understanding. That’s why it’s imperative for us to hire extraordinarily talented people of all backgrounds and identities. Without that we can’t do the essential work of inspiring bold ideas, fostering constructive dialogue, cultivating meaningful experiences, and driving innovation and creativity for our partners. Your unique point of view—your talent—is what makes you a #BursonPerson, and it’s how we deliver exceptional results for our clients, together.

We believe the best work happens when we're together, fostering creativity, collaboration, and connection. That's why we’ve adopted a hybrid approach, with teams in the office around four days a week. If you require accommodations or flexibility, please discuss this with the hiring team during the interview process.

 this will send any information you add below to Burson. Before you do this, we think it's a good idea to read through our Recruitment Privacy Policy. California residents should read our California Recruitment Privacy Notice. This explains what we do with your personal data when you apply for a role with us, and, how you can update the information you have provided us with or how to remove it.

\uD83D\uDFE3Join our communities, built around ‘Wellness’, ‘Belonging’ and the ‘Planet’ where you can make a meaningful contribution

At MYOB, we’re all about embracing difference and backing people to be their true selves. We’re proud to be an equal opportunity employer and big believers that the best hires don’t just fit our culture — they add to it. That means bringing your unique contributions, lived experiences, and fresh perspective to the table.

Love the sound of MYOB and this role but don’t tick every single box in this ad? Apply anyway! Studies show that women and underrepresented groups often hold back if they don’t meet every requirement — but we want to hear from you.

We’re proud to be a Circle Back Initiative Employer and we commit to responding to every applicant.

We may use artificial intelligence (AI) tools to support parts of the hiring process, such as reviewing applications, analyzing resumes, or assessing responses. These tools assist our recruitment team but do not replace human judgment. Final hiring decisions are ultimately made by humans. If you would like more information about how your data is processed, please contact us.
descriptionHTML:
We’re a leading business management solution with a core purpose: helping more businesses in Australia and New Zealand start, survive and succeed.

At MYOB, we believe what’s good for one business is good for all business—and for all of us. Whether you support them, work for them, or dream of building your own, when businesses run smoothly, everybody feels it. Owners, employees, customers, suppliers—even families. That’s why we’re here: to give every person in business the tools they need to focus on what really matters and do Big Things—whatever big looks like for them.

And for you? Joining MYOB means being part of that impact. It means using your skills to help businesses thrive, shaping the future of work, and growing alongside the people and communities we support. Because while we’re the business of software, we’re really in the business of people. And that makes MYOB Everyone’s Business.

Help Shape the Future of AI-Powered Innovation at MYOB

This is a high-impact role at the heart of our AI transformation journey. As an AI Platform Engineer, you’ll design and build the cloud foundations that empower teams across the organization to innovate with AI—safely, at scale, and with speed.

Your work will support the development of innovative products, insights, and efficiencies that will impact the future of our business. From embedding FinOps and observability to staying ahead of the latest in AI infrastructure, you’ll play a critical role in making advanced AI capabilities accessible, sustainable, and enterprise-ready.

What You’ll Do
  • Build secure, reusable AWS infrastructure components to support internal AI and data solution teams.
  • Implement platform observability with proactive monitoring and alerting for all AI and ML workloads.
  • Embed FinOps practices into platform design to drive cloud cost visibility and optimisation.
  • Stay across emerging AI tech and AWS updates to continually enhance platform capabilities.
  • Influence internal standards and tooling to reflect high-quality AI infrastructure.
  • Provide clear guidance and enablement materials to support team adoption of platform services.
What You’ll Bring
  • Solid experience in cloud platform engineering, DevOps, data infrastructure, or MLOps roles.
  • Strong hands-on skills with AWS services like SageMaker, Bedrock, Lambda, S3, IAM, CloudWatch, Step Functions, and API Gateway.
  • Consistent record applying FinOps practices for cost tracking, tagging, reporting, and optimisation.
  • Experience deploying and running data and ML workloads in AWS environments.
  • Skilled in infrastructure-as-code using Terraform, AWS CDK, or CloudFormation.
  • Experience integrating security, compliance, and governance into cloud-native platforms.
  • Familiarity with emerging AI technologies such as LLMs, vector databases, and orchestration frameworks.
  • Bachelor’s or Master’s degree or equivalent experience in Computer Science, Data Science, Machine Learning, or a related field and relevant certifications (e.g., AWS Certified Machine Learning – Specialty) are highly desirable.
Our Culture & Benefits
At MYOB our values are at the heart of everything we do. If you want to work and collaborate where opinions are valued, and your ideas can make a difference, come to a place where Your Work Matters.
\uD83C\uDFA5 See what it's like to work at MYOB and what we're all about.
\uD83D\uDFE3Enjoy a flexible, hybrid workplace that brings balance to how you work, all of our centrally located offices come together for a number of in-person celebrations and social events
\uD83D\uDFE3Set up your home office with a little financial help from us, and take advantage of great corporate discounts and vouchers from top retailers
\uD83D\uDFE3Benefit from our partnership with Sonder, which supports the wellbeing of our team members
\uD83D\uDFE3Unlock your potential via in-house training and programs, LinkedIn Learning, conferences and study assistance
\uD83D\uDFE3Access to a variety of leave options available to support your wellbeing, including up to an additional four weeks of purchased leave, generous parental leave, domestic violence leave, transgender leave, community leave, study leave, and more
\uD83D\uDFE3Join our communities, built around ‘Wellness’, ‘Belonging’ and the ‘Planet’ where you can make a meaningful contribution
At MYOB, we’re all about embracing difference and backing people to be their true selves. We’re proud to be an equal opportunity employer and big believers that the best hires don’t just fit our culture — they add to it. That means bringing your unique contributions, lived experiences, and fresh perspective to the table.

Love the sound of MYOB and this role but don’t tick every single box in this ad? Apply anyway! Studies show that women and underrepresented groups often hold back if they don’t meet every requirement — but we want to hear from you.
