descriptionHTML:
Job details

  • Posted01 May 2026
  • SalaryNegotiable
  • LocationSydney
  • Job type Contract
  • Discipline Customer Experience
  • Reference90M0707174_1777604364

Job description

About FinXL IT
FinXL IT Professional Services is a leading IT consultancy that partners with clients to bridge the gap between business needs and technology capabilities. Since 2003, we've been delivering high-value ICT services across government, finance, telecommunications, and enterprise sectors.

The Opportunity
FinXL IT is seeking a Senior Project Manager/ Business Readiness Implementation Manager to join our consulting team for a strategic engagement with a leading Insurance client. You will lead the business readiness and implementation of SAP Signavio, uplifting the division's Business Process Management (BPM) and Customer Journey capabilities.

Responsibilities

  • Signavio Implementation: Drive the rollout of SAP Signavio as the tool of choice for BPM and Customer Journeys.

  • Business Readiness: Link operational processes to customer experience (CX), ensuring a smooth transition from iServer and mitigating process decay.

  • Delivery Management: Execute the delivery plan, managing scope, sequencing, and trade-offs across complex business and tech workstreams.

  • Governance & Standards: Establish clear ownership and governance frameworks enabled by modern AI-supported tooling (mining and analytics).

  • Process Redesign: Enable the redesign of workflows from the ground up to effectively shape and capture AI opportunities.

  • Stakeholder Integration: Build deep relationships with business and technology leads to drive decisions, manage dependencies, and ensure high user adoption.

  • Financial Oversight: Manage project budgeting and forecasting to ensure delivery stays within financial parameters.

Key Skills & Experience

  • Proven background as a Senior Project Manager or Implementation Manager within the General Insurance industry.

  • Proven hands-on experience as part of a team responsible for the implementation and delivery of SAP Signavio in a similar sized environment and ecosystem is non-negotiable.

  • Demonstrated experience delivering diverse portfolios including business transformation and cultural change.

  • Exceptional senior executive engagement skills with the ability to align diverse parties under tight timeframes.

  • Ability to drive business-led solutions while navigating complex technology impacts and legacy system transitions.

  • Strong judgement with the ability to provide practical support and apply design-thinking principles to process uplift.

What's On Offer?

  • Initial 12 months' contract, with a view to extend
  • Located in Sydney CBD
  • Hybrid working arrangement to get your best work done!
  • Lead a mission-critical project moving an industry leader toward AI-supported process management.
  • Join a consultancy that values professional development and high-level stakeholder partnership.

Next Steps: If you would like to apply for this opportunity, please submit your CV via SEEK. Please note that due to the volume of responses, only shortlisted consultants will be contacted.

Job number (S4)

description:
Are you a seasoned Principal Engineer who thrives on driving technical excellence and delivering seamless digital experiences?

As we expand our presence in the Sydney area, we are seeking an experienced Principal Engineer to join our Digital Experience practice. This role involves leading our team in developing innovative go-to-market solutions for our customers and supporting their digital transformations to achieve outstanding outcomes.

As a Principal Engineer, you will be crucial in setting the technical direction for our customers. You will act as the liaison between the engineering team and stakeholders, lead a small team of engineers, and provide guidance and support in implementing cutting-edge technologies. Your expertise will be key to ensuring seamless delivery and surpassing client expectations.

On a day to day, you'll be doing;

Lead an engagement technically providing thought leadership and technical direction focusing on customer needs and achieving project goals.
Leading and building engineering and design best practice, standards and deliverables in production ready systems, to meet customer agreed outcomes to time, budget and quality.
Producing and gaining customer approval on high level designs, detailed designs, solution architectures, operational and handover documentation; sequencing of projects and backlog that provide clarity and direction for the engineering team with team endorsement and support as the key metric.
Lead a team of engineers, providing leadership, direction and mentorship
Drive innovation and excellence in digital and cloud native solutions
Collaborate with cross-functional teams to ensure alignment with business goals
Utilise your Digital AppDev and Cloud expertise to deliver high-quality technical solutions
Champion craftsmanship and quality outcomes in all aspects of technology delivery
Extensive experience in software engineering, across AWS and Azure platforms
Proven track record of technical leadership and delivering successful projects
Strong communication and collaboration skills, with the ability to work effectively in a team environment
Passion for technology craftsmanship and a commitment to delivering quality outcomes
People lead a small team of seniors and engineers
Required Skills & Experience
Proven experience as a Principal or Lead Engineer delivering complex, cloud-native solutions in enterprise environments
Strong full stack engineering capability, with deep expertise in backend services and distributed systems
Proficiency in core languages including Java (Spring Boot), TypeScript/JavaScript, Python or similar
Frontend development experience using React and/or Next.js or similar
Deep hands-on experience with AWS, including designing and building cloud-native architectures
Strong experience with microservices and event-driven architectures
Hands-on expertise with Docker and Kubernetes in production environments
Solid understanding of DevOps practices, including CI/CD pipelines, Infrastructure as Code, and observability
Experience using AI-assisted coding tools (e.g., GitHub Copilot, Claude Code or similar) ideally in real-world development
Proven ability to lead technical delivery, including architecture, design, and implementation oversight
Highly Desirable
Experience in financial services (banking, payments, or superannuation)
Experience building go-to-market products or reusable engineering platforms
Track record of mentoring and developing engineers in high-performing teams

Life at Versent

We're a diverse team of motivated humans who think outside the box and strive to deliver what others might consider impossible. At Versent we truly care about our people and put real focus on cultivating a culture of learning, collaboration and growth. We provide our teams with the space, trust, and support, to deliver the best work of their careers. To enable this, we have a culture that places a high value on continual feedback, excellence, and invention anchored in customer success.

We also offer our team:

Flexibility: We give you the freedom to excel, providing as much autonomy and flexibility around when you work so you can manage your time in a way that best suits you to get the job done.

Learning: We are passionate about continuous improvement and care about you investing in and developing your craft. Our Craft 180 Program is a holistic strategy for ongoing, meaningful skill and capability development with an ultimate goal to create an environment where people can learn and contribute in different ways to develop, practice and become masters of their craft.

Access to Benefits That Fit Your Life: From salary packaging and purchased leave to novated leasing options, we offer a range of benefits designed to suit different lifestyles and priorities. There’s something for everyone.

Health & Wellbeing Programmes: We focus on you as a whole person, with wellbeing initiatives that are to support you mentally, physically, emotionally and financially.

Connection: Regular social activities across your team and the organisation.

Recognition: We offer several reward & recognition programmes to acknowledge the amazing work that you do!

Career opportunity: We provide the opportunity to work on diverse projects and initiatives that enable you to create a brilliant career.

At Versent, we don't adhere to a one-size-fits-all hiring strategy. We understand that you might not fulfill all the criteria for this position. Our celebration of diverse perspectives isn't just a value, it's a way to gain an edge. If you believe this opportunity is where you can thrive, don't be shy and apply!

If you require any accommodations throughout the interview process, please get in touch with us by emailing careers@versent.com.au.

description:
The APAC Global Web Production Manager (GWPM) leads the coordination, planning, and execution of adobe.com projects across the Asia Pacific region, ensuring alignment with Adobe's strategy and business objectives. Leading the APAC production team, the GWPM ensures projects are delivered on time and to the highest standard, through effective management of schedules, resources and risks.

The role involves daily communication with partners from both Sales and Marketing to ensure the wider team is well-informed of project status. The GWPM champions AI adoption across the web production function by embedding AI-first thinking into daily workflows and upskilling the team on emerging tools. Operating within an agile delivery model, they are accountable for the smooth running of sprints and ongoing optimization of team processes.

Responsibilities
Partner with the Director of Global Web Production to support revenue growth across international sites
Lead, mentor, and grow a distributed team of web producers and specialists,
Develop and embed an AI-first approach to web production, driving team proficiency in AI tools, agentic workflows, and emerging tech
Run agile delivery, including sprints, ceremonies, and continuous improvement of ways of working across cross-functional business and implementation partners
Own process and quality improvements, gathering input from the wider Marketing team to remove blockers and streamline workflows
Manage team budget, expenses, and operational administration

Requirements
5+ years managing web production or web project delivery in a complex, global environment, including multiple successful global site launches
Hands-on experience with AI tools and agentic workflows, with a track record of embedding them into production
Strong command of agile and sprint-based delivery (Scrum certification a plus)
Experience with web content management systems; working knowledge of Jira and AEM desirable
Proven leader who builds credibility with teams and senior collaborators, and influences outcomes across functions
Sharp prioritization, problem-solving, and decision-making under pressure
Bachelor's degree or equivalent experience
APAC market experience with a strong grasp of regional nuances a plus
About Adobe
Adobe empowers everyone to create through innovative platforms and tools that unleash creativity, productivity and personalized customer experiences. Adobe’s industry-leading offerings including Adobe Acrobat Studio, Adobe Express, Adobe Firefly, Creative Cloud, Adobe Experience Platform, Adobe Experience Manager, and GenStudio enable people and businesses to turn ideas into impact, powered by AI and driven by human ingenuity.

Our 30,000+ employees worldwide are creating the future and raising the bar as we drive the next decade of growth. We’re on a mission to hire the very best and believe in creating a company culture where all employees are empowered to make an impact. At Adobe, we believe that great ideas can come from anywhere in the organization. The next big idea could be yours.

Let’s Adobe together
At Adobe, we believe in creating a company culture where all employees are empowered to make an impact. Learn more about Adobe life, including our values and culture , focus on people, purpose and community , Adobe for All , comprehensive benefits programs , the stories we tell , the customers we serve, and how you can help us advance our mission of empowering everyone to create.

Adobe is proud to be an Equal Employment Opportunity employer. We do not discriminate based on gender, race or color, ethnicity or national origin, age, disability, religion, sexual orientation, gender identity or expression, veteran status, or any other protected characteristic. Learn more.

Adobe aims to make our Careers website and recruiting process accessible to any and all users. If you have a disability or special need that requires accommodation to navigate our website or complete the application process, email accommodations@adobe.com or call +1 408-536-3015.

AI Use Guidelines for Interviews:
Our interviews are designed to reflect your own skills and thinking. The use of AI or recording tools during live interviews is not permitted unless explicitly invited by the interviewer or approved in advance as part of a reasonable accommodation. If these tools are used inappropriately or in a way that misrepresents your work, your application may not move forward in the process.

At Adobe, we empower employees to innovate with AI — and we look for candidates eager to do the same. As part of the hiring experience, we provide clear guidance on where AI is encouraged during the process and where it’s restricted during live interviews. See how we think about AI in the hiring experience .

description:
About Us:
Infosys is a global leader in next-generation digital services and consulting. We enable clients in more than 56 countries to navigate their digital transformation. With over four decades of experience in managing the systems and workings of global enterprises, we expertly steer our clients through their digital journey. We do it by enabling the enterprise with an AI-powered core that helps prioritize the execution of change. We also empower the businesses with agile digital at scale to deliver unprecedented levels of performance and customer satisfaction. Our always-on learning agenda drives their continuous improvement through building and transferring digital skills, expertise, and ideas from our innovation ecosystem.
Visit www.infosys.com to see how Infosys (NYSE: INFY) can help your enterprise navigate your next.

Role Description: Are you passionate about leading transformative technology strategies? We are looking for a seasoned SAP SuccessFactors Payroll Consultant to act as a subject matter expert for SAP Payroll solutions, specifically tailored to Australian payroll processes. The consultant will lead Employee Central Payroll implementations, collaborate with global delivery teams, and work closely with clients to design, build, and support scalable payroll solutions.

Location: Melbourne (VIC), Australia
Salary: AUD 150742 - AUD 171215 (Annual Gross)
Please Note:- The above salary range is only indicative and maybe subject to suitable enhancements, based on internal company processes.

Flexible working arrangements (hybrid, reviewed case-by-case basis). Feel free to connect with your recruiter to learn more about how this works in practice.

Roles and Responsibilities:
Serve as the SME for SAP Payroll, guiding solution design and implementation.
Lead hands-on configuration and deployment of Employee Central Payroll.
Liaise with clients for workshops, solution presentations, POCs, and approvals.
Estimate efforts for proposed solutions and own delivery outcomes.
Recommend innovative, best-practice-based solutions aligned with SAP standards.
Develop business process documentation and functional specifications.
Conduct implementation and training workshops for SAP power users.
Lead testing cycles (unit, SIT, regression) and support UAT.
Coordinate integration requirements with other SAP modules (e.g., Finance, HCM).
Mentor junior consultants and contribute to payroll competency development.
Participate in strategic initiatives, proposals, and thought leadership activities.
Skill / Competencies / Experience:

ESSENTIAL
Good knowledge of technology landscape to be able to guide team.
Tertiary Qualification in IT / Computer Science / related discipline.
PREFERRED
Expertise in SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central Payroll.
8–10 years of experience in Australian payroll implementations and support.
Strong understanding of HCM processes and statutory compliance in Australia.
Experience in integration with S/4HANA HCM and Finance modules.
Excellent communication, stakeholder engagement, and problem-solving skills.
Ability to work effectively in an onshore-offshore delivery model.

ADDITIONAL
Excellent customer-facing skills
Excellent written and verbal communication skills
Strong attention to detail and outstanding analytical and problem-solving skills.
In addition to compensation aligned with your role, you’ll have access to a wide range of benefits that support your health, career growth, and overall well-being (AU)
Income Protection Insurance
Paid Parental and Volunteer leaves
Employee Assistance Program (EAP)
Flexible working arrangements (hybrid, reviewed case-by-case basis)
Health Insurance Discount and Well-being Program
Access to Fitness and Gym Memberships
Salary packaging and novated leasing
Our recruitment and selection process is designed to create the best match for both you and Infosys. Depending on the role, it may include an initial screening, technical or functional interviews, and conversations focused on cultural alignment and shared values. We are committed to making the experience transparent, respectful, and engaging at every step.

Hear from our recruiter on their experience working with Infosys as an Employer
At Infosys, we recognize that everyone has individual requirements. If you are a person with disability, illness, or injury and require adjustments to the recruitment and selection process, please contact our Recruitment team for adjustment either via the following email Infy_REC_Helpdesk@infosys.com or call 1-866-472-0935. Alternatively, you can include your preferred method of communication in email, and someone will be in touch.

Please note in order to protect the interest of all parties involved in the recruitment process, Infosys does not accept any unsolicited resumes from third-party vendors. In the absence of a signed agreement, any submission will be deemed as non-binding and Infosys explicitly reserves the right to pursue and hire the submitted profile. All recruitment activity must be coordinated through the Talent Acquisition department.

“All aspects of employment at Infosys are based on merit, competence and performance. We are committed to embracing diversity and creating an inclusive environment for all employees. Infosys is proud to be an equal opportunity employer.”

description:
1 year fixed term part time contract available

Contribute to the SLEEP – MSK study

Base Salary starting from $89,755 + 17% superannuation ( Pro rata)

About the opportunity

The Sydney School of Health Sciences is in a period of exciting and significant investment by the Faculty of Medicine and Health. In order to deliver on the University’s ambitious strategic plan, the University is seeking academics who are passionate about improving health outcomes and/or health equity in the local, national and global communities, who are committed to teaching and research, and area team player ready to contribute across the broad field of academic activities.

The University is seeking a highly motivated Clinical Trial Coordinator ( HEO 5) to contribute to the SLEEP-MSK adaptive basket randomised controlled trial, which evaluates whether adding cognitive behavioural therapy for insomnia (CBT-I) to usual physiotherapy care improves pain, sleep, and function in people with chronic low back pain, hip osteoarthritis, or knee osteoarthritis.

SLEEP-MSK is a two-arm, superiority, adaptive basket randomised controlled trial including participants with one of three chronic MSK conditions—chronic low back pain, hip osteoarthritis, or knee osteoarthritis—and co-existing insomnia

Your key responsibilities will be to:

Support the end-to-end clinical trial process, assisting with the planning, initiation, and execution of trials, and ensuring smooth operations from start-up to close-out

Support data capture and verification for clinical trials, maintaining accurate records and ensuring compliance with regulations

Follow ethics guidelines and workplace health and safety regulations, preparing materials for committee submissions

Build productive relationships with research staff, trial sponsors, and external vendors, facilitating effective communication and collaboration

Uphold the principles of Good Clinical Practice (GCP) in all clinical trials, ensuring that ethical and scientific quality standards are met in design, conduct, recording, and reporting

Take the lead in handling stakeholder communications, ensuring that concerns are addressed effectively and timely while fostering a culture of trust and collaboration within the work area and beyond

Facilitate timely report submission, uphold data integrity according to university systems, and partner with managers to advance compliance initiatives

Supported by the FMH media team, create patient avatars & eLearning for training physiotherapists to deliver Cognitive Behavioural Therapy for Insomnia in the trial.

About you

Bachelors degree in relevant field or equivalent combination of relevant training and experience

Strong knowledge of complex musculoskeletal conditions & their management

Experience in delivering clinical skills training activities

Desirable to have experience as a practising health professional

Experience in manuscript writing

Willingness to learn new skills in AI related fields

Willingness to be trained in Phlebotomy for the trial

Experience in clinical trial coordination or a similar role

Solid understanding of clinical research and Good Clinical Practice

Experience with ethics and governance processes and preparing and submitting trial documentation

Effective communication, planning and organisation skills and strong attention to detail

Proven experience in a research-intensive environment.

Sponsorship / work rights for Australia

You must have unrestricted work rights in Australia for the duration of this employment to be eligible to apply. Visa sponsorship is not available for this appointment.

Pre-employment checks and declarations

 Your employment is conditional upon the successful completion of all pre-employment or background checks required for the role in terms satisfactory to the University. Also, to meet the University’s obligations under the National Higher Education Code to Prevent and Eliminate Gender-Based Violence you will be asked to declare if you have been investigated for, or found to engaged in, sexual harm or gender-based violence in the course of previous employment or in a legal process. Similarly, your ongoing employment is conditional upon the satisfactory maintenance of all relevant clearances and background check requirements. If you do not meet these conditions, the University may take any necessary step, including the termination of your employment.

As this position is located on an NSW Health site, it is a regulatory requirement to complete compulsory employment checks prior to starting in this position. For more information and instructions, please visit: NSW Health employment requirements

descriptionHTML:
• 1 year fixed term part time contract available

  • Contribute to the SLEEP – MSK study

  • Base Salary starting from $89,755 + 17% superannuation ( Pro rata)

About the opportunity

The Sydney School of Health Sciences is in a period of exciting and significant investment by the Faculty of Medicine and Health. In order to deliver on the University’s ambitious strategic plan, the University is seeking academics who are passionate about improving health outcomes and/or health equity in the local, national and global communities, who are committed to teaching and research, and area team player ready to contribute across the broad field of academic activities.

The University is seeking a highly motivated Clinical Trial Coordinator ( HEO 5) to contribute to the SLEEP-MSK adaptive basket randomised controlled trial, which evaluates whether adding cognitive behavioural therapy for insomnia (CBT-I) to usual physiotherapy care improves pain, sleep, and function in people with chronic low back pain, hip osteoarthritis, or knee osteoarthritis.

SLEEP-MSK is a two-arm, superiority, adaptive basket randomised controlled trial including participants with one of three chronic MSK conditions—chronic low back pain, hip osteoarthritis, or knee osteoarthritis—and co-existing insomnia

Your key responsibilities will be to:

  • Support the end-to-end clinical trial process, assisting with the planning, initiation, and execution of trials, and ensuring smooth operations from start-up to close-out

  • Support data capture and verification for clinical trials, maintaining accurate records and ensuring compliance with regulations

  • Follow ethics guidelines and workplace health and safety regulations, preparing materials for committee submissions

  • Build productive relationships with research staff, trial sponsors, and external vendors, facilitating effective communication and collaboration

  • Uphold the principles of Good Clinical Practice (GCP) in all clinical trials, ensuring that ethical and scientific quality standards are met in design, conduct, recording, and reporting

  • Take the lead in handling stakeholder communications, ensuring that concerns are addressed effectively and timely while fostering a culture of trust and collaboration within the work area and beyond

  • Facilitate timely report submission, uphold data integrity according to university systems, and partner with managers to advance compliance initiatives

  • Supported by the FMH media team, create patient avatars & eLearning for training physiotherapists to deliver Cognitive Behavioural Therapy for Insomnia in the trial.

About you

  • Bachelors degree in relevant field or equivalent combination of relevant training and experience

  • Strong knowledge of complex musculoskeletal conditions & their management

  • Experience in delivering clinical skills training activities

  • Desirable to have experience as a practising health professional

  • Experience in manuscript writing

  • Willingness to learn new skills in AI related fields

  • Willingness to be trained in Phlebotomy for the trial

  • Experience in clinical trial coordination or a similar role

  • Solid understanding of clinical research and Good Clinical Practice

  • Experience with ethics and governance processes and preparing and submitting trial documentation

  • Effective communication, planning and organisation skills and strong attention to detail

  • Proven experience in a research-intensive environment.

Sponsorship / work rights for Australia

You must have unrestricted work rights in Australia for the duration of this employment to be eligible to apply. Visa sponsorship is not available for this appointment.

Pre-employment checks and declarations

Your employment is conditional upon the successful completion of all pre-employment or background checks required for the role in terms satisfactory to the University. Also, to meet the University’s obligations under the National Higher Education Code to Prevent and Eliminate Gender-Based Violence you will be asked to declare if you have been investigated for, or found to engaged in, sexual harm or gender-based violence in the course of previous employment or in a legal process. Similarly, your ongoing employment is conditional upon the satisfactory maintenance of all relevant clearances and background check requirements. If you do not meet these conditions, the University may take any necessary step, including the termination of your employment.

description:
The Opportunity
We are hiring an exceptionally talented Sales Retention Operations & Analytics Manager to drive flawless retention programs, analytics, and operational consistency across the JAPAC region.

As part of our world-class Retention Strategy & Operations team, you will collaborate with Renewals Sales, Solution Account Managers, Customer Success Operations, Finance, and Sales Operations teams.

You will successfully implement retention programs, study data to identify trends and risks, and build decisions that improve renewal performance and increase customer retention throughout different JAPAC markets.

This position provides wide-ranging experience across various locations, business units, and priorities, with opportunities to expand your influence over time!

What You’ll Do
Retention Programs & Execution

Coordinate with regional leadership to establish and provide retention programs across JAPAC.

Plan and roll out initiatives passionate about renewal performance, customer health, and risk reduction.

Monitor program progress and share insights to inform next steps and improvements.

Retention Data, Reporting & Analytics

Manage retention and attrition data across JAPAC, ensuring accuracy and consistency.

Analyse data sets to identify trends, risks, and opportunities that inform action.

Build regular reports and deliver analysis for regional and leadership reviews.

Respond to data requests with clear analysis that shapes renewal planning and decision making.

QBR & Leadership Readiness

Prepare retention inputs for QBRs spanning regional and geographic areas within JAPAC.

Build data packs, insights, and materials for leadership and performance discussions.

Contribute to consistency in important measurements and definitions used in reviews.

Planning, Coverage & Operations

Gather and examine data to support planning activities throughout the region.

Coordinate coverage, quota, and goal validation with Sales Operations and Finance.

Work with teams across the business to address data or process questions.

Collaboration & Governance

Collaborate with regional and global teams to carry out retention initiatives and improvements.

Contribute to training efforts, pilots, and operational improvements related to renewals.

Participate in testing and validation activities as tools or processes evolve.

What You'll Need to Succeed

Experience in program management, sales operations, retention operations, or analytics.

Experience handling data to guide business decisions and convey a clear narrative.

Ability to translate data into clear insights and recommendations.

Comfort working across regions, teams, and time zones.

Strong communication skills and a collaborative working style.

Adobe for All
Adobe strives to create an environment where our employees can do their best work and drive their career growth and development based on their personal goals. From fair-pay practices to Employee Networks and programs designed to make everyone feel included, we're committed to fostering a diverse and inclusive workplace for all.

Take the plunge and jump in
Liking what you have read and keen to jump in? Thinking you might not have all the skills we are looking for? Just take the plunge and apply – we know that by bringing together a diverse group of people, we become so much better together. We'd love to see where we can help drive Creativity for All together!

Adobe Australia acknowledges Traditional Custodians of Country throughout Australia and recognises the continuing connection to lands, waters and communities. We pay our respect to Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander cultures; and to Elders past, present and emerging.

We will ensure that individuals with disabilities are provided reasonable accommodation to participate in the job application or interview process, to perform essential job functions, and to receive other benefits and privileges of employment. Please contact us to request accommodation.
About Adobe
Adobe empowers everyone to create through innovative platforms and tools that unleash creativity, productivity and personalized customer experiences. Adobe’s industry-leading offerings including Adobe Acrobat Studio, Adobe Express, Adobe Firefly, Creative Cloud, Adobe Experience Platform, Adobe Experience Manager, and GenStudio enable people and businesses to turn ideas into impact, powered by AI and driven by human ingenuity.

Our 30,000+ employees worldwide are creating the future and raising the bar as we drive the next decade of growth. We’re on a mission to hire the very best and believe in creating a company culture where all employees are empowered to make an impact. At Adobe, we believe that great ideas can come from anywhere in the organization. The next big idea could be yours.

Let’s Adobe together
At Adobe, we believe in creating a company culture where all employees are empowered to make an impact. Learn more about Adobe life, including our values and culture , focus on people, purpose and community , Adobe for All , comprehensive benefits programs , the stories we tell , the customers we serve, and how you can help us advance our mission of empowering everyone to create.

Adobe is proud to be an Equal Employment Opportunity employer. We do not discriminate based on gender, race or color, ethnicity or national origin, age, disability, religion, sexual orientation, gender identity or expression, veteran status, or any other protected characteristic. Learn more.

Adobe aims to make our Careers website and recruiting process accessible to any and all users. If you have a disability or special need that requires accommodation to navigate our website or complete the application process, email accommodations@adobe.com or call +1 408-536-3015.

AI Use Guidelines for Interviews:
Our interviews are designed to reflect your own skills and thinking. The use of AI or recording tools during live interviews is not permitted unless explicitly invited by the interviewer or approved in advance as part of a reasonable accommodation. If these tools are used inappropriately or in a way that misrepresents your work, your application may not move forward in the process.

At Adobe, we empower employees to innovate with AI — and we look for candidates eager to do the same. As part of the hiring experience, we provide clear guidance on where AI is encouraged during the process and where it’s restricted during live interviews. See how we think about AI in the hiring experience .

description:
Job details
Posted01 May 2026
SalaryAU$800 - AU$900 per day
LocationSydney CBD
Job type Contract
Discipline Digital & Product Engineering
Reference90M0707180_1777606934
Job description
QA Automation Engineer with a leading finance client (consulting through FinXL)

FinXL Professional Services is a leading IT consultancy that partners with clients to bridge the gap between business needs and technology capabilities. Since 2003, we've been delivering high-value ICT services across government, finance, telecommunications, and enterprise sectors.

FinXL is seeking a QA Automation Engineer to join our consulting team and be deployed with a leading finance client on a 12 months contract in Melbourne. FinXL is also able to take over existing visas and provide sponsorship for consultants.

Responsibilities
Design, build, and maintain automated testing frameworks from scratch using Playwright.
Implement non-functional testing including performance and security for calculation engines.
Design control frameworks to validate the accuracy of AI-generated advice logic.
Integrate automated test suites directly into established CI/CD pipelines.
Ensure the platform supports frequent, low-risk releases through robust automation.
Define the overarching testing strategy for a newly formed onshore agile squad.
Mentor associate testers to maintain high quality standards across the team.
Key Skills & Experience
Extensive commercial experience in QA automation specifically using Playwright.
Strong technical background working with JBoss environments.
Proven track record of integrating automated tests into CI/CD pipelines.
Experience testing within highly regulated environments, preferably Banking & Finance.
Demonstrated ability to develop testing strategies and frameworks from the ground up.
Excellent communication and interpersonal skills.
Engagement Details
12 months contract with a leading finance client.
Hybrid working model with 2-3 days/ week in the office in Sydney CBD.
Next Steps
Apply now highlighting relevant skills and experience and address your application to Julia Leung. Due to the high volume of applications only shortlisted candidates can be contacted.

positionName: Senior GenAI/Agentic AI Sales Specialist, AWS ANZ Specialist Team
company: Amazon Web Services Australia Pty Ltd
description:
DESCRIPTION

AWS Global Sales drives adoption of the AWS cloud worldwide, enabling customers of all sizes to innovate and expand in the cloud. Our team empowers every customer to grow by providing tailored service, unmatched technology, and consistent support. We dive deep to understand each customer's unique challenges, then craft innovative solutions that accelerate their success. This customer-first approach is how we built the world's most adopted cloud. Join us and help us grow.

Would you like to be part of a team shaping the future of the IT industry through responsible and innovative use of Artificial Intelligence? Amazon Web Services (AWS) is advancing the next evolution of cloud computing with Generative AI and Agentic AI technologies. We are seeking a Senior GenAI / Agentic AI Sales Specialist who brings strong technical AI knowledge together with a customer-centric, outcome-focused approach to revenue growth.

This role partners closely with customers to understand their goals, translate complex AI capabilities into clear business value, and support successful AI adoption at scale. If you enjoy collaborating with diverse stakeholders, communicating technical concepts with clarity, and contributing to long-term customer success, we encourage you to apply.

AWS Global Sales supports customers of all sizes across the region to innovate and grow in the cloud. As organizations increasingly adopt foundation models, large language models (LLMs), and agentic AI, we are expanding our team of technical sales professionals. We are seeking to hire people who can help customers identify and prioritise AI use cases to create AWS opportunities, build sustainable pipelines, and guide customers through complex AI adoption journeys across Australia and New Zealand.

Key job responsibilities
Sales & Revenue Contribution
• Support the achievement of sales targets by developing and maintaining a healthy pipeline and guiding customers through end-to-end sales cycles, from discovery through to agreement;
• Create thoughtful account plans and territory strategies in partnership with internal teams, while maintaining accurate forecasting using CRM tools;
• Collaborate with customers to identify and qualify high-impact AI use cases aligned to their business priorities and strategic objectives.
Technical Sales & Solution Advisory
• Act as a trusted advisor on AWS GenAI and Agentic AI services, helping customers understand potential outcomes, value, and return on investment;
• Lead technical discovery sessions and contribute to demonstrations, proofs of concept, and solution design, including model selection, fine-tuning, and deployment approaches;
• Provide guidance on model evaluation, responsible AI practices, governance considerations, and relevant compliance requirements.
Customer Engagement & Market Development
• Build and maintain strong relationships with decision-makers and executive stakeholders through a consultative, collaborative approach;
• Partner with internal teams to deliver workshops, enablement sessions, and solution reviews that support customer learning and adoption;
• Share customer insights and feedback with AWS AI product teams to help inform product direction and improvements.

About the team
About the team
Diverse Experiences
AWS values diverse experiences. Even if you do not meet all of the qualifications and skills listed in the job description, we encourage candidates to apply. If your career is just starting, hasn’t followed a traditional path, or includes alternative experiences, don’t let it stop you from applying.

Why AWS?
Amazon Web Services (AWS) is the world’s most comprehensive and broadly adopted cloud platform. We pioneered cloud computing and never stopped innovating — that’s why customers from the most successful startups to Global 500 companies trust our robust suite of products and services to power their businesses.

Inclusive Team Culture
Here at AWS, it’s in our nature to learn and be curious. Our employee-led affinity groups foster a culture of inclusion that empower us to be proud of our differences. Ongoing events and learning experiences, including our Conversations on Race and Ethnicity (CORE) and AmazeCon (gender diversity) conferences, inspire us to never stop embracing our uniqueness.

Mentorship & Career Growth
We’re continuously raising our performance bar as we strive to become Earth’s Best Employer. That’s why you’ll find endless knowledge-sharing, mentorship and other career-advancing resources here to help you develop into a better-rounded professional.

Work/Life Balance
We value work-life harmony. Achieving success at work should never come at the expense of sacrifices at home, which is why we strive for flexibility as part of our working culture. When we feel supported in the workplace and at home, there’s nothing we can’t achieve in the cloud.

What if I don’t meet all the requirements?

That’s okay! We hire people who have a passion for learning and are curious. You will be supported in your career development here at AWS. You will have plenty of opportunities to build your technical, leadership, business and consulting skills. Your onboarding will set you up for success, including a combination of formal and informal training. You’ll also have a chance to gain AWS certifications and access mentorship programs. You will learn from and collaborate with some of the brightest technical minds in the industry today.

BASIC QUALIFICATIONS

- 7+ years of direct sales or business development in software, cloud or SaaS markets selling to C-level executives experience
- 10+ years of business development, partner development, sales or alliances management experience
- Experience positioning and selling technology to new customers and new market segments
- Experience proactively growing customer relationships within an account while expanding their understanding of the customer's business
- Ability to operate with a high degree of ambiguity and in a matrix organization
- Proven track record in engaging with C-Suite contacts in customers communicating and present with impact towards agreed project outcomes.
- Proven track record of successful engagements with customers in Australia & New Zealand.

PREFERRED QUALIFICATIONS

- Track record of success driving adoption of new and disruptive technologies within Enterprise accounts.
- Track record of consistent territory growth and sales quota attainment

Acknowledgement of country:
In the spirit of reconciliation Amazon acknowledges the Traditional Custodians of country throughout Australia and their connections to land, sea and community. We pay our respect to their elders past and present and extend that respect to all Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander peoples today.

IDE statement:
Amazon is an equal opportunity employer and does not discriminate on the basis of protected veteran status, disability, or other legally protected status.

Our inclusive culture empowers Amazonians to deliver the best results for our customers. If you have a disability and need a workplace accommodation or adjustment during the application and hiring process, including support for the interview or onboarding process, please visit https://amazon.jobs/content/en/how-we-hire/accommodations for more information. If the country/region you’re applying in isn’t listed, please contact your Recruiting Partner.

  • Experience positioning and selling technology to new customers and new market segments
  • Experience proactively growing customer relationships within an account while expanding their understanding of the customer's business
  • Ability to operate with a high degree of ambiguity and in a matrix organization
  • Proven track record in engaging with C-Suite contacts in customers communicating and present with impact towards agreed project outcomes.
  • Proven track record of successful engagements with customers in Australia & New Zealand.

PREFERRED QUALIFICATIONS

  • Track record of success driving adoption of new and disruptive technologies within Enterprise accounts.
  • Track record of consistent territory growth and sales quota attainment

positionName: Principal Business Development Specialist APJC, Betting and Gaming Technology
company: Amazon Web Services Australia Pty Ltd - D81
description:
DESCRIPTION

The Principal Business Development Specialist for Betting & Gaming serves as a dedicated industry expert embedded within Asia Pacific Japan China (APJC) account teams, driving AWS adoption through line-of-business opportunities across the betting, gaming, and lottery sectors. This role combines deep industry expertise with strategic account planning to identify, develop, and launch opportunities that address specific Betting and Gaming customer workflows and business challenges across the APJC region.

Key job responsibilities
Account Strategy & Planning
- Contribute Betting and Gaming industry perspective to Account Plans for assigned proactive coverage accounts in APJC
- Identify and map Line of Business (LOB) contacts and stakeholders within betting and gaming operators
- Utilize Industry Score Card to identify whitespace opportunities across five Betting and Gaming solution areas: Player Engagement, Compliance & Player Protection, Payment & Risk Management, Operations, and Casino Game Development
- Collaborate with account teams to develop jurisdiction-specific strategies aligned with APJC regulatory environments
Opportunity Development & Execution
- Drive Betting and Gaming opportunities from Qualified stage through Launch, focusing on measurable business outcomes
- Evangelize AWS services, partner solutions, and Professional Services tailored to betting and gaming use cases
- Achieve 70% partner attachment rate on launched opportunities, leveraging Betting and Gaming ecosystem partners
- Participate in Executive Briefing Centers (EBCs) and executive engagements with C-suite stakeholders
- Act as facilitator to bring in specialized AWS resources, world-wide specialist teams, ProServe, and partner capabilities
Thought Leadership & Enablement
- Conduct internal enablement sessions for APJC account teams on Betting and Gaming industry trends, use cases, and AWS solutions
- Lead external thought leadership sessions with customers and at industry events
- Contribute to Go-to-Market packages for scale Betting and Gaming customers
- Build and maintain relationships with key industry associations and regulatory bodies in APJC jurisdictions
Regional Market Development

- Develop deep understanding of APJC betting and gaming regulatory landscape across key markets (Australia, NZ, Philippines, Singapore, Vietnam, Hong Kong, etc.)
- Identify regional trends, competitive dynamics, and market opportunities specific to APJC
- Build partner ecosystem relationships with regional Betting and Gaming technology providers and system integrators

A day in the life
Your primary focus is being the go-to expert on betting and gaming for our teams working with customers across Asia-Pacific. You're the person who understands how operators actually run their businesses and can connect those challenges to solutions.

Most days, you're embedded with account teams having conversations with betting and gaming companies. You might start your morning on a call with a sports betting operator in Australia, helping the team understand what their Chief Marketing Officer really cares about when it comes to player engagement. You're translating between the business side and the technical side, making sure everyone's speaking the same language.

You'll spend a good chunk of time in customer meetings - sometimes it's a formal executive briefing where you're presenting to C-level folks, other times it's a working session with their operations team diving into the specifics of how they could modernize their platform. You're not just talking about technology; you're talking about their business outcomes, their regulatory challenges, and how they can actually move faster in their market.

Between customer engagements, you're also teaching. You'll run sessions for our account teams to help them understand what's happening in the industry - new trends in responsible gaming, how payment fraud is evolving, what's changing in different regulatory environments across the region. You become their trusted advisor on all things betting and gaming.

What makes this role interesting is the variety. One day you might be helping a casino game studio figure out how to deploy across multiple countries, the next you're at an industry conference sharing insights on player protection technology, and the day after that you're working with partners to put together a comprehensive solution for a major operator.

You'll also get pulled into strategic planning - helping teams think through which opportunities to pursue, where the white space is in an account, and how to approach complex deals that might take months to close. It's part detective work, part strategy, part relationship building.

Travel is definitely part of the mix - you'll be visiting customers, attending industry events, and collaborating with teams across the region. But it's purposeful travel that lets you build real relationships and stay connected to what's actually happening in the market.

About the team
The AWS Industries team is committed to helping industry customers enable their digital transformation journeys. Our primary goal is to meet customers in their cloud journeys and work backwards from their industry-specific needs to transform their businesses and industries. Our team focuses on business outcomes and industry use cases on behalf of our customers, whether that is how we build products and solutions, how we sell, how we deliver, or how we partner.

You'll be part of the Geo Industry Specialist Team (GIST) within the broader Media, Entertainment & Games and Sports Industry Business Unit. The team operates with a strong focus on customer obsession and delivering results, guided by organizational tenets that emphasize measuring what matters, building diverse perspectives, and scaling bright spots.

The team works collaboratively across multiple segments including studios, platforms, publishers, game services, and the full spectrum of betting and gaming operators and suppliers as well as regulators. The team culture emphasizes going forward with the customer, fostering innovation through curiosity, and empowering high-velocity decisions.

You'll work closely with account teams, Solutions Architects, AWS Professional Services, Industry Marketing, and an extensive partner ecosystem. The team values collaboration and operates with a builder mentality, focusing on strategic initiatives like accelerating time to market, market expansion, systems modernization, optimizing player acquisition and engagement, and helping customers achieve sustainable profitability through data-driven personalization and AI.

Based in the APJC region (Hong Kong, or Australia preferred), you'll be part of a geographically distributed team that comes together regularly for strategy sessions, planning meetings, and collaborative workshops focused on driving customer success and business growth.

BASIC QUALIFICATIONS

- 7+ years of developing, negotiating and executing business agreements experience
- 10+ years of Go-To-Market, Business Development, Sales, or Consulting experience
- 5+ years of working with Core Cloud Technology Services, including, but not limited to Compute, Edge, Hybrid, Security, and/or Networking experience
- 7+ years of solving problems with technology in the Media & Entertainment Industry experience
- 7+ years of solving problems with technology in the Global Financial Services Industry experience
- 5+ years of working with Enterprise Application Modernization and Migration technologies, including, but not limited to, Mainframe, Serverless, Containers, or Cloud Operations experience
- Bachelor's degree
- Experience developing strategies that influence leadership decisions at the organizational level

PREFERRED QUALIFICATIONS

- Experience as a founder or executive focused on related segments, or as a practice leader or business unit owner
- Experience selling to Fortune 1000 or Global 2000 organizations

Acknowledgement of country:
In the spirit of reconciliation Amazon acknowledges the Traditional Custodians of country throughout Australia and their connections to land, sea and community. We pay our respect to their elders past and present and extend that respect to all Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander peoples today.

IDE statement:
Amazon is an equal opportunity employer and does not discriminate on the basis of protected veteran status, disability, or other legally protected status.

Our inclusive culture empowers Amazonians to deliver the best results for our customers. If you have a disability and need a workplace accommodation or adjustment during the application and hiring process, including support for the interview or onboarding process, please visit https://amazon.jobs/content/en/how-we-hire/accommodations for more information. If the country/region you’re applying in isn’t listed, please contact your Recruiting Partner.

positionName: Sr. Analytics Sales Specialist, AWS ANZ Specialist Team
company: Amazon Web Services Australia Pty Ltd
description:
DESCRIPTION

AWS Global Sales drives adoption of the AWS cloud worldwide, enabling customers of all sizes to innovate and expand in the cloud. Our team empowers every customer to grow by providing tailored service, unmatched technology, and consistent support. We dive deep to understand each customer's unique challenges, then craft innovative solutions that accelerate their success. This customer-first approach is how we built the world's most adopted cloud. Join us and help us grow.

Would you like to be part of a team shaping the future of business, helping customers put their data to work by building modern data foundations with cloud services? Amazon Web Services (AWS) is advancing the next evolution of cloud computing with comprehensive analytics, business intelligence and AIML technologies. We are seeking a Senior Analytics Sales Specialist who brings a combination of customer transformation experience and strong technical analytics knowledge together with a customer-centric, outcome-focused approach to revenue growth for our Analytics business in ANZ.

This role partners closely with customers to understand their goals, translate complex analytics capabilities into clear business value, and support successful analytics adoption at scale. If you enjoy collaborating with diverse stakeholders, communicating technical concepts with clarity, and contributing to long-term customer success, we encourage you to apply.

AWS Global Sales supports customers of all sizes across the region to innovate and grow in the cloud. As organizations seek to use AIML more effectively, we need to help them transform their data lakes, real-time analytics, and business intelligence solutions into a modern data architecture. We are expanding our team of technical sales professionals to scale our impact, hiring people who can help customers identify and prioritise analytics use cases to create AWS opportunities, build sustainable pipelines, and guide customers through complex analytics adoption journeys across Australia and New Zealand.

Key job responsibilities
Sales & Revenue Contribution

• Support the achievement of sales targets by developing and maintaining a healthy pipeline and guiding customers through end-to-end sales cycles, from discovery through to agreement;
• Create thoughtful account plans and territory strategies in partnership with internal teams, while maintaining accurate forecasting using CRM tools;
• Collaborate with customers to identify and qualify high-impact AI use cases aligned to their business priorities and strategic objectives.
Technical Sales & Solution Advisory
• Act as a trusted advisor on AWS GenAI and Agentic AI services, helping customers understand potential outcomes, value, and return on investment;
• Lead technical discovery sessions and contribute to demonstrations, proofs of concept, and solution design, including model selection, fine-tuning, and deployment approaches;
• Provide guidance on model evaluation, responsible AI practices, governance considerations, and relevant compliance requirements.

Customer Engagement & Market Development

• Be the leading evangelist for our prescriptive recommendations to customer business and technology leaders on transforming to a modern data architecture. Visibly lead this externally and internally on stage, on social media and in-person;
• Build and maintain strong relationships with decision-makers and executive stakeholders through a consultative, collaborative approach;
• Partner with internal teams to deliver workshops, enablement sessions, and solution reviews that support customer learning and adoption;
• Share customer insights and feedback with AWS Analytics and AIML product teams to help inform product direction and improvements.

About the team
Diverse Experiences
AWS values diverse experiences. Even if you do not meet all of the qualifications and skills listed in the job description, we encourage candidates to apply. If your career is just starting, hasn’t followed a traditional path, or includes alternative experiences, don’t let it stop you from applying.

Why AWS?
Amazon Web Services (AWS) is the world’s most comprehensive and broadly adopted cloud platform. We pioneered cloud computing and never stopped innovating — that’s why customers from the most successful startups to Global 500 companies trust our robust suite of products and services to power their businesses.

Inclusive Team Culture
Here at AWS, it’s in our nature to learn and be curious. Our employee-led affinity groups foster a culture of inclusion that empower us to be proud of our differences. Ongoing events and learning experiences, including our Conversations on Race and Ethnicity (CORE) and AmazeCon conferences, inspire us to never stop embracing our uniqueness.

Mentorship & Career Growth
We’re continuously raising our performance bar as we strive to become Earth’s Best Employer. That’s why you’ll find endless knowledge-sharing, mentorship and other career-advancing resources here to help you develop into a better-rounded professional.

Work/Life Balance
We value work-life harmony. Achieving success at work should never come at the expense of sacrifices at home, which is why we strive for flexibility as part of our working culture. When we feel supported in the workplace and at home, there’s nothing we can’t achieve in the cloud.

What if I don’t meet all the requirements?

That’s okay! We hire people who have a passion for learning and are curious. You will be supported in your career development here at AWS. You will have plenty of opportunities to build your technical, leadership, business and consulting skills. Your onboarding will set you up for success, including a combination of formal and informal training. You’ll also have a chance to gain AWS certifications and access mentorship programs. You will learn from and collaborate with some of the brightest technical minds in the industry today.

BASIC QUALIFICATIONS

- 10+ years of business development, partner development, sales or alliances management experience
- Experience positioning and selling technology to new customers and new market segments
- Experience proactively growing customer relationships within an account while expanding their understanding of the customer's business
- Experience with customer relationship management, especially C-suite level customer engagement
- 7+ years of direct sales or business development in software, cloud or SaaS markets selling to C-level executives experience
- Experience building and leading large teams and have worked in matrixed operating structures
- Experience architecting, migrating, transforming or modernizing customer requirements to the cloud

PREFERRED QUALIFICATIONS

- Experience influencing innovation through a partner ecosystem with either solutions or programs that have demonstrated success at enterprise customers
- Experience meeting revenue targets and quotas

Acknowledgement of country:
In the spirit of reconciliation Amazon acknowledges the Traditional Custodians of country throughout Australia and their connections to land, sea and community. We pay our respect to their elders past and present and extend that respect to all Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander peoples today.

IDE statement:
Amazon is an equal opportunity employer and does not discriminate on the basis of protected veteran status, disability, or other legally protected status.

Our inclusive culture empowers Amazonians to deliver the best results for our customers. If you have a disability and need a workplace accommodation or adjustment during the application and hiring process, including support for the interview or onboarding process, please visit https://amazon.jobs/content/en/how-we-hire/accommodations for more information. If the country/region you’re applying in isn’t listed, please contact your Recruiting Partner.

positionName: Partner Success Specialist, AWS Specialist and Partner Organization
company: Amazon Web Services Australia Pty Ltd - D81
description:
DESCRIPTION

As a Partner Success Specialist (PSS) within the AWS Specialists & Partners (ASP) org, you will be responsible for scaling AWS's customer success methodologies across our partner ecosystem to drive measurable business outcomes and accelerate customer value realization. You will architect and implement frameworks that enable partners to drive and measure customer value post-implementation. This role combines strategic enablement with hands-on execution to establish partners as extensions of AWS's customer success organization.

Key job responsibilities
Strategy and approach:
- Develop strategies to align partner success initiatives with AWS’ long-term growth objectives
- Design and adapt CS CoE's 'Activate to Advocate' framework for partner implementation
- Create partner-specific success metrics, health scoring models, and value realization playbooks
- Develop governance models and operational excellence guidelines for partners
- Architect scalable approaches to measure and improve partner-led customer outcomes
- Contribute to the AWS Value Realization Toolkit for Partners
Partner enablement and program execution:
- Enable partners to build and scale their customer success practices by helping demonstrate how creating a customer success COE/customer success capabilities positively impacts their business model
- Guide partners through post-sale stages
- Deploy customer health frameworks and intervention strategies through partner channels
- Build adoption, change management and stakeholder engagement toolkits
- Coach partners in establishing systematic approaches to value realization
- Build feedback mechanisms to continuously evolve success frameworks based on partner insights
- Establish mechanisms to measure and track partner-led customer value realization
- Drive partner-led adoption through technical milestones and usage metrics
- Implement value realization frameworks to meet business KPIs
Organizational Leadership & Influence:
- Partner with PDMs to drive adoption of success frameworks across partner organizations
- Collaborate with Partner Sales to accelerate consumption through systematic approaches
- Work with Partner Development Specialists to create industry-specific success playbooks
- Build relationships across AWS organizations to drive program adoption and refinement
- Influence senior stakeholders to drive organizational transformation
- Develop business cases demonstrating quantifiable business impact and ROI of systematic partner enablement
We're looking for individuals with:
- Deep expertise in partner enablement and post-sale value delivery
- Strong program management and operational excellence capabilities
- Proven ability to influence and drive change across complex organizations
- Experience working with enterprise partners and cloud services
- Experience using AI to automate and scale work more effectively

About the team
About AWS

Diverse Experiences
AWS values diverse experiences. Even if you do not meet all of the preferred qualifications and skills listed in the job description, we encourage candidates to apply. If your career is just starting, hasn’t followed a traditional path, or includes alternative experiences, don’t let it stop you from applying.

Why AWS?
Amazon Web Services (AWS) is the world’s most comprehensive and broadly adopted cloud platform. We pioneered cloud computing and never stopped innovating — that’s why customers from the most successful startups to Global 500 companies trust our robust suite of products and services to power their businesses.

Inclusive Team Culture
AWS values curiosity and connection. Our employee-led and company-sponsored affinity groups promote inclusion and empower our people to take pride in what makes us unique. Our inclusion events foster stronger, more collaborative teams. Our continual innovation is fueled by the bold ideas, fresh perspectives, and passionate voices our teams bring to everything we do.

Mentorship & Career Growth
We’re continuously raising our performance bar as we strive to become Earth’s Best Employer. That’s why you’ll find endless knowledge-sharing, mentorship and other career-advancing resources here to help you develop into a better-rounded professional.

Work/Life Balance
We value work-life harmony. Achieving success at work should never come at the expense of sacrifices at home, which is why we strive for flexibility as part of our working culture. When we feel supported in the workplace and at home, there’s nothing we can’t achieve.

BASIC QUALIFICATIONS

- 10+ years of progressive technology implementation experience

PREFERRED QUALIFICATIONS

- Experience working with Global System Integrators or strategic technology partners
- Deep understanding of cloud services and enterprise digital transformation
- Track record of building partner capabilities and driving partner-led growth

Acknowledgement of country:

descriptionHTML:
DESCRIPTION

As a Partner Success Specialist (PSS) within the AWS Specialists & Partners (ASP) org, you will be responsible for scaling AWS's customer success methodologies across our partner ecosystem to drive measurable business outcomes and accelerate customer value realization. You will architect and implement frameworks that enable partners to drive and measure customer value post-implementation. This role combines strategic enablement with hands-on execution to establish partners as extensions of AWS's customer success organization.

Key job responsibilities
Strategy and approach:
  • Develop strategies to align partner success initiatives with AWS’ long-term growth objectives
  • Design and adapt CS CoE's 'Activate to Advocate' framework for partner implementation
  • Create partner-specific success metrics, health scoring models, and value realization playbooks
  • Develop governance models and operational excellence guidelines for partners
  • Architect scalable approaches to measure and improve partner-led customer outcomes
  • Contribute to the AWS Value Realization Toolkit for Partners
Partner enablement and program execution:
  • Enable partners to build and scale their customer success practices by helping demonstrate how creating a customer success COE/customer success capabilities positively impacts their business model
  • Guide partners through post-sale stages
  • Deploy customer health frameworks and intervention strategies through partner channels
  • Build adoption, change management and stakeholder engagement toolkits
  • Coach partners in establishing systematic approaches to value realization
  • Build feedback mechanisms to continuously evolve success frameworks based on partner insights
  • Establish mechanisms to measure and track partner-led customer value realization
  • Drive partner-led adoption through technical milestones and usage metrics
  • Implement value realization frameworks to meet business KPIs
Organizational Leadership & Influence:
  • Partner with PDMs to drive adoption of success frameworks across partner organizations
  • Collaborate with Partner Sales to accelerate consumption through systematic approaches
  • Work with Partner Development Specialists to create industry-specific success playbooks
  • Build relationships across AWS organizations to drive program adoption and refinement
  • Influence senior stakeholders to drive organizational transformation
  • Develop business cases demonstrating quantifiable business impact and ROI of systematic partner enablement
We're looking for individuals with:
  • Deep expertise in partner enablement and post-sale value delivery
  • Strong program management and operational excellence capabilities
  • Proven ability to influence and drive change across complex organizations
  • Experience working with enterprise partners and cloud services
  • Experience using AI to automate and scale work more effectively

About the team
About AWS

Diverse Experiences
AWS values diverse experiences. Even if you do not meet all of the preferred qualifications and skills listed in the job description, we encourage candidates to apply. If your career is just starting, hasn’t followed a traditional path, or includes alternative experiences, don’t let it stop you from applying.

Why AWS?
Amazon Web Services (AWS) is the world’s most comprehensive and broadly adopted cloud platform. We pioneered cloud computing and never stopped innovating — that’s why customers from the most successful startups to Global 500 companies trust our robust suite of products and services to power their businesses.

Inclusive Team Culture
AWS values curiosity and connection. Our employee-led and company-sponsored affinity groups promote inclusion and empower our people to take pride in what makes us unique. Our inclusion events foster stronger, more collaborative teams. Our continual innovation is fueled by the bold ideas, fresh perspectives, and passionate voices our teams bring to everything we do.

Mentorship & Career Growth
We’re continuously raising our performance bar as we strive to become Earth’s Best Employer. That’s why you’ll find endless knowledge-sharing, mentorship and other career-advancing resources here to help you develop into a better-rounded professional.

Work/Life Balance
We value work-life harmony. Achieving success at work should never come at the expense of sacrifices at home, which is why we strive for flexibility as part of our working culture. When we feel supported in the workplace and at home, there’s nothing we can’t achieve.

BASIC QUALIFICATIONS

  • 10+ years of progressive technology implementation experience

PREFERRED QUALIFICATIONS

  • Experience working with Global System Integrators or strategic technology partners
  • Deep understanding of cloud services and enterprise digital transformation
  • Track record of building partner capabilities and driving partner-led growth

positionName: Head of Security, Public Sector ANZ, Worldwide Public Sector
company: Amazon Web Services Australia Pty Ltd
description:
DESCRIPTION

Overview: The Principal Security Leader role drives strategic security adoption across the entire Australia and New Zealand Public Sector portfolio, encompassing Federal Government, National Security, Defence, Healthcare and Life Sciences, Education and Research, and State/Local Government segments. This role combines deep cross-sector security expertise with business development acumen to create repeatable patterns for security modernization and adoption at scale within diverse government contexts.
Strategic Impact: Operating across multiple public sector domains with distinct security requirements, regulatory and compliance frameworks, this role architects and executes security-focused GTM strategies that enable both "market creation" and "market scaling" across varied maturity levels. The position requires sophisticated engagement across technical and business stakeholders to drive cloud adoption through both one-to-one and one-to-many motions, while understanding the unique security and regulatory requirements of each sector. The position works in collaboration with partner teams including Compliance & Security Assurance, Public Policy and OCISO capabilities ensuring proactive positioning of cloud centric security models that enable broad based adoption whilst lifting the watermark of government and customer cyber capability.

Key job responsibilities
Cross-Sector Security Leadership
• Drive security adoption strategies across all public sector segments
• Develop sector-specific security frameworks and adoption patterns
• Navigate diverse regulatory requirements including:
• ISM ‘Protected’/NZISM ‘Restricted/Confidential’ data classifications for Federal Government
• IRAP and Essential Eight for National Security and Regulated Industries
• NEHTA and Privacy Act for Healthcare
• CAUDIT and AARNet requirements for Education
• State-specific security frameworks for State Government
• Enable security modernization across all government tiers
Technical Security Excellence
• Design multi-sector security architectures and patterns
• Guide security implementations across varied compliance regimes
• Develop sector-specific security reference architectures
• Enable cross-sector security integration patterns
Market Development
• Create sector-specific security adoption programs
• Design vertical-aligned security GTM strategies
• Build security patterns that scale across sectors
• Enable cross-sector security knowledge sharing
Stakeholder Collaboration
• Engage with sector-specific regulatory bodies
• Partner with vertical-focused AWS teams
• Coordinate with state and federal security agencies
• Build relationships with industry peak bodies including:
• Digital Health Agency
• Education Standards bodies
• State Government CIO forums
• Federal security agencies
• Local Government Associations
Security Policy Influence
• Shape multi-jurisdiction security frameworks
• Drive alignment across federal and state requirements
• Influence sector-specific security standards
• Enable cross-sector security initiatives
Program Development:
• Create vertical-specific security adoption frameworks
• Develop cross-sector security implementation patterns
• Design security programs that scale across jurisdictions
• Build sector-specific security modernization roadmaps

BASIC QUALIFICATIONS

- 7+ years of developing, negotiating and executing business agreements experience
- 7+ years of business development, partnership management, or sourcing new business experience
- 7+ years of professional or military experience
- Experience writing technical articles, speaking at technology conferences, and contributing to open source
- Experience developing and executing on GTM strategies that are large in scope

PREFERRED QUALIFICATIONS

- Experience developing, deploying and managing AI products at scale
- Experience interfacing with customers (Enterprise, SMB, and Startups) and ability to convert customer requirements to high level architecture solutions to build on

Acknowledgement of country:
In the spirit of reconciliation Amazon acknowledges the Traditional Custodians of country throughout Australia and their connections to land, sea and community. We pay our respect to their elders past and present and extend that respect to all Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander peoples today.

IDE statement:
Amazon is an equal opportunity employer and does not discriminate on the basis of protected veteran status, disability, or other legally protected status.

Our inclusive culture empowers Amazonians to deliver the best results for our customers. If you have a disability and need a workplace accommodation or adjustment during the application and hiring process, including support for the interview or onboarding process, please visit https://amazon.jobs/content/en/how-we-hire/accommodations for more information. If the country/region you’re applying in isn’t listed, please contact your Recruiting Partner.
