positionName: Senior AI Specialist Sales, Data and AI, Specialist Team
company: Amazon Web Services Australia Pty Ltd
description:
DESCRIPTION

Would you like to be part of a team shaping the future of the IT industry through responsible and innovative use of Artificial Intelligence? AWS seeks a Senior AI Specialist Sales professional to drive adoption of AWS's full stack AI services across Australia.
Amazon Web Services (AWS) is advancing the next evolution of AI adoption. We are seeking a Senior AI Sales Specialist who brings strong technical AI knowledge together with a customer-centric, outcomes-focused approach to revenue growth.

This role partners closely with customers to understand their goals, translate complex AI capabilities into clear business value, and support successful adoption at scale. If you enjoy collaborating with diverse stakeholders, communicating technical concepts with clarity, and contributing to long-term customer success, we encourage you to apply.

Ability to engage with CEO/LOB owners for strategic conversations to leverage AI for business transformation, as well as with CTO/CAIO for AI-implementation linked technical architecture conversations. AI specialist solution architects will support technical deep dives.

AWS Global Sales supports customers of all sizes across the region to innovate and grow in the cloud. As organizations increasingly adopt foundation models, large language models (LLMs), and autonomous AI agents, we are expanding our team of technical sales professionals who can identify opportunities, build sustainable pipelines, and guide customers through complex AI adoption journeys across Australia.

Key job responsibilities
Sales & Revenue Contribution
• Support the achievement of sales targets by developing and maintaining a healthy pipeline and guiding customers through end-to-end sales cycles, from discovery through agreement
• Create thoughtful account plans and territory strategies in partnership with internal teams, while maintaining accurate forecasting using CRM tools
• Collaborate with customers to identify and qualify high-impact AI use cases aligned to their business priorities and strategic objectives
Technical Sales & Solution Advisory
• Act as a trusted advisor on AWS GenAI and Agentic AI services, helping customers understand potential outcomes, value, and return on investment
• Lead technical discovery sessions and contribute to demonstrations, proofs of concept, and solution design, including model selection, fine-tuning, and deployment approaches
• Provide guidance on model evaluation, responsible AI practices, governance considerations, and relevant compliance requirements
Customer Engagement & Market Development
• Build and maintain strong relationships with decision-makers and executive stakeholders through a consultative, collaborative approach
• Partner with internal teams to deliver workshops, enablement sessions, and solution reviews that support customer learning and adoption
• Share customer insights and feedback with AWS AI product teams to help inform product direction and improvements
• Engage C-suite executives to drive AWS AI services adoption: CEOs (business transformation), CTOs/CAIOs (technical architecture), CFOs (ROI models), line of business owners (use cases). Lead AI-focussed Executive Briefing Centers, board presentations, and strategic planning workshops.”

BASIC QUALIFICATIONS

- 7+ years of experience in technology roles, including technical or solution-oriented sales, with a demonstrated history of contributing to revenue goals
- Experience supporting complex, multi-stakeholder sales engagements and managing pipelines with a focus on accuracy and transparency
- Strong communication skills, with the ability to explain complex technical topics in a clear, engaging way for both technical and executive audiences

PREFERRED QUALIFICATIONS

- Experience working with Generative AI technologies, including foundation models, LLMs, or autonomous agent frameworks
- Exposure to AI model development, fine-tuning, or production deployment
- Familiarity with prompt engineering, retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) architectures, fine-tuning techniques, and AI deployment patterns
- Bachelor's degree in computer science, machine learning, engineering, or related fields, or Master's degree

Acknowledgement of country:
In the spirit of reconciliation Amazon acknowledges the Traditional Custodians of country throughout Australia and their connections to land, sea and community. We pay our respect to their elders past and present and extend that respect to all Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander peoples today.

IDE statement:
Amazon is an equal opportunity employer and does not discriminate on the basis of protected veteran status, disability, or other legally protected status.

Our inclusive culture empowers Amazonians to deliver the best results for our customers. If you have a disability and need a workplace accommodation or adjustment during the application and hiring process, including support for the interview or onboarding process, please visit https://amazon.jobs/content/en/how-we-hire/accommodations for more information. If the country/region you’re applying in isn’t listed, please contact your Recruiting Partner.

positionName: Sr AI Solution Architect, Data & AI Specialist Solutions Architect team
company: Amazon Web Services Australia Pty Ltd
description:
DESCRIPTION

Amazon Web Services (AWS) is leading the next phase of AI adoption and is seeking a hands-on AI Specialist Solution Architect (SSA). AWS Specialist Solutions Architects (SSAs) are technologists with deep domain-specific expertise, able to address advanced concepts and feature designs.
As part of the AWS sales organization, SSAs work with customers who have complex challenges that require expert-level knowledge to solve. SSAs craft scalable, flexible, and resilient technical architectures that address those challenges. This might involve guiding customers as they refactor an application or design an entirely new cloud-based system.
Specialist SAs play a critical role in capturing customer feedback, advocating for roadmap enhancements and anticipating customer requirements as they work backwards from their needs. As domain experts, SSAs also participate in field engagement and enablement, producing content such as whitepapers, blogs, and workshops for customers, partners, and the AWS Technical Field.
This role focuses on converting AI ambition into programs that can be delivered, operated, and scaled in production environments.

Key job responsibilities
• The AI Specialist SA team builds technical relationships with customers of all sizes and operate as their trusted advisor, ensuring they get the most out of the cloud at every stage of their journey while adopting GenAI/ML and Agentic technologies across their organisation.
• You’ll manage the overall technical relationship between AWS and our customers, making recommendations on security, cost, performance, reliability and operational efficiency to accelerate their challenging GenAI/ML and Agentic projects.
• Internally, you will be the voice of the customer, sharing their needs with regard to their usage of our services impacting the roadmap of AWS GenAI/ML and Agentic features.
• In this role, your creativity will link technology to tangible solutions, with the opportunity to define cloud-native GenAI/ML and Agentic architectural patterns for a variety of use cases.
• You will participate in the creation and sharing of best practices, technical content and new reference architectures (e.g. white papers, code samples, blog posts) and evangelize and educate about running GenAI/ML and Agentic workloads on AWS technology (e.g. through workshops, user groups, meetups, public speaking, online videos or conferences).
• Technical Leadership & Mentorship: Lead hands-on deep dives and technical workshops, contributing reusable code, reference architectures, and internal technical assets for the broader engineering organization.

About the team
#genai

BASIC QUALIFICATIONS

- 7+ years of design, implementation, or consulting in applications and infrastructures experience
- 5+ years of specific technology domain areas (e.g. software development, cloud computing, systems engineering, infrastructure, security, networking, data & analytics) experience
- Ability to influence customer and internal business decision makers as a technical thought leader and ability to effectively communicate across an increasing diversity of audiences internally and externally
- Hands-on experience with AWS ecosystems (including Bedrock, AgentCore, and SageMaker) to set up secure, private-network AI environments, and practical experience implementing Retrieval-Augmented Generation using embeddings, vector stores, and semantic search optimization.
- Experience implementing AI solutions that can include integration of LLMs/multi-modal FMs in large scale systems, fine-tuning LLMs, deployment and distributed inference of LLMs, RAG, FM evaluation, Vector DBs, Agentic workflows, prompt/context engineering, and MLOps.
- 6+ years of design/implementation of production AI systems
- 5+ years management of technical, customer facing resources

PREFERRED QUALIFICATIONS

- Cloud Technology Certification (such as Solutions Architecture, Cloud Security Professional or Cloud DevOps Engineering)
- Ability to lead a team or small organization-wide initiative with business objectives that are partially defined. Strong ability to determine solution strategy and where to simplify or extend solutions for the best outcome
- Proven ability to lead projects with complex challenges with extensible, operationally excellent, cost optimized, and aligned solutions outcomes
- Technical Cloud Certification & Master's degree in computer science, mathematics, statistics, machine learning or equivalent quantitative field, or PhD
- Experience in running & fine-tuning Large and Small Language Models using advanced techniques like LoRA/QLoRA, Instruction Tuning, and RLHF to optimize for specific domain tasks.
- Expertise in architecting AI systems within highly regulated or security-sensitive environments (e.g., Financial Services, Healthcare, Public Sector).

Acknowledgement of country:
In the spirit of reconciliation Amazon acknowledges the Traditional Custodians of country throughout Australia and their connections to land, sea and community. We pay our respect to their elders past and present and extend that respect to all Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander peoples today.

IDE statement:
Amazon is an equal opportunity employer and does not discriminate on the basis of protected veteran status, disability, or other legally protected status.

Our inclusive culture empowers Amazonians to deliver the best results for our customers. If you have a disability and need a workplace accommodation or adjustment during the application and hiring process, including support for the interview or onboarding process, please visit https://amazon.jobs/content/en/how-we-hire/accommodations for more information. If the country/region you’re applying in isn’t listed, please contact your Recruiting Partner.

positionName: Senior Applied AI Solutions Architect, Amazon Connect, AWS Applied AI Solutions
company: Amazon Web Services Australia Pty Ltd
description:
DESCRIPTION

The Applied AI Solutions Architecture team within AWS is seeking a hands-on, customer-obsessed Solutions Architect to accelerate customer adoption of Amazon Connect's AI capabilities. This role is part of the AI Velocity Team, a service-specific approach that assigns dedicated advisory and hands-on development resources directly to customers to achieve production-ready outcomes in weeks instead of months.

As an Applied AI Solutions Architect, you will be embedded with customers to help them prepare their Amazon Connect implementations for production by focusing on three pillars of agentic AI:
Model Selection: Guiding customers through evaluating and selecting the right foundation models (via Amazon Bedrock) for their contact center use cases, balancing latency, accuracy, cost, and compliance requirements.

Prompt Engineering: Designing, testing, and optimizing AI prompts and system instructions for Amazon Connect AI agents, including self-service agents, answer recommendation agents, and custom orchestrator agents.

Tooling: Architecting and building the tool integrations (APIs, Lambda functions, data connectors, knowledge bases) that agentic AI systems use to take actions on behalf of customers and agents. This included configuring MCP (Model Context Protocol) servers for standardized tool discovery and invocation, and enabling A2A (Agent-to-Agent) communication patterns for multi-agent orchestration across enterprise systems.

A key dimension of this role is Customer Data Readiness. Assessing, preparing, and structuring customer data assets so that AI agents can reliably access, retrieve, and act on the right information. You will help customers evaluate their data landscape, identify gaps, establish data pipelines, and ensure their knowledge bases, CRMs, and backend systems are AI-ready before agents go live.

You will work at the intersection of contact center operations and applied AI, helping customers move from proof-of-concept to pre-production for their Amazon Connect Unlimited AI deployments. This is a deeply technical, hands-on role, you will write code, build integrations, configure agents, and pair-program with customer engineering teams.

Key job responsibilities
Lead technical discovery sessions to understand customer business requirements, contact center architecture, and AI readiness, translating these into solution designs and implementation plans.

Conduct data readiness assessments across customer systems (CRMs, knowledge bases, ticketing, order management) to identify gaps, recommend remediation, and establish the data foundation for AI agent tool use and RAG-powered responses.

Design and configure agentic AI solutions within Amazon Connect, including multi-agent orchestration, prompt engineering, model selection, guardrails, and tool integration.

Deploy Model Context Protocol (MCP) servers to expose customer tools, data sources, and APIs in a standardized format for dynamic AI agent discovery and invocation.

Architect agent-to-agent communication patterns enabling multi-agent workflows across enterprise boundaries (e.g., billing, order management, IT support).

Build serverless integrations (Lambda, API Gateway, Step Functions, Python/Node.js) connecting AI agents with customer data systems and cloud-based data stores (DynamoDB, RDS, S3, OpenSearch, Kendra/Knowledge Bases for Bedrock).

Leverage agentic development environments (e.g., Kiro) to accelerate spec-driven development, MCP configuration, and AI-assisted code generation.

Guide customers through AI agent testing, evaluation, and validation against success criteria prior to production deployment.

Create reusable artifacts (reference architectures, implementation guides, prompt libraries, data readiness checklists) to scale best practices.

A day in the life
Diverse Experiences
AWS values diverse experiences. Even if you do not meet all of the preferred qualifications and skills listed in the job description, we encourage candidates to apply. If your career is just starting, hasn’t followed a traditional path, or includes alternative experiences, don’t let it stop you from applying.

Why AWS?
Amazon Web Services (AWS) is the world’s most comprehensive and broadly adopted cloud platform. We pioneered cloud computing and never stopped innovating — that’s why customers from the most successful startups to Global 500 companies trust our robust suite of products and services to power their businesses.

Inclusive Team Culture
AWS values curiosity and connection. Our employee-led and company-sponsored affinity groups promote inclusion and empower our people to take pride in what makes us unique. Our inclusion events foster stronger, more collaborative teams. Our continual innovation is fueled by the bold ideas, fresh perspectives, and passionate voices our teams bring to everything we do.

Mentorship & Career Growth
We’re continuously raising our performance bar as we strive to become Earth’s Best Employer. That’s why you’ll find endless knowledge-sharing, mentorship and other career-advancing resources here to help you develop into a better-rounded professional.

Work/Life Balance
We value work-life harmony. Achieving success at work should never come at the expense of sacrifices at home, which is why we strive for flexibility as part of our working culture. When we feel supported in the workplace and at home, there’s nothing we can’t achieve.

About the team
Amazon Web Services (AWS) is the world’s most comprehensive and broadly adopted cloud platform. We pioneered cloud computing and never stopped innovating, that’s why customers from the most successful startups to Global 500 companies trust our robust suite of products and services to power their businesses.

This position is part of the Amazon Specialist and Partner Organization (ASP). Specialists own the end-to-end go-to-market strategy for their respective technology domains, providing the business and technical expertise to help our customers succeed. Partner teams own the strategy, recruiting, development, and growth of our key technology and consulting partners. Together they provide our customers with the expertise and scale needed to build innovative solutions for their most complex challenges

BASIC QUALIFICATIONS

- Experience in design, implementation, or consulting in applications and infrastructures
- Experience communicating across technical and non-technical audiences, including executive level stakeholders or clients
- 5+ years of experience building integrations across AI, Agentic AI, CRM, and contact center solutions or experience with communications protocols and APIs such as WebRTC and SIP or similar CCaaS technology.
- 5+ years of experience building integrations across WFM, CRM, and contact center solutions or experience with communications protocols and APIs such as WebRTC and SIP or similar CCaaS technology
- Experience with complex Contact Center as a Service (CCaaS) implementations

PREFERRED QUALIFICATIONS

- Experience in infrastructure architecture, database architecture and networking
- AWS certification, such as, AWS Solutions Architect, or a similar cloud certification, or Associate's degree or above
- Knowledge of presentations and whiteboarding skills with a high degree of comfort speaking with internal and external executives, IT management, and developers
- History of successful technical consulting and/or architecture engagements with large-scale customers or enterprises.

In the spirit of reconciliation Amazon acknowledges the Traditional Custodians of country throughout Australia and their connections to land, sea and community. We pay our respect to their elders past and present and extend that respect to all Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander peoples today.

DESCRIPTION

The Applied AI Solutions Architecture team within AWS is seeking a hands-on, customer-obsessed Solutions Architect to accelerate customer adoption of Amazon Connect's AI capabilities. This role is part of the AI Velocity Team, a service-specific approach that assigns dedicated advisory and hands-on development resources directly to customers to achieve production-ready outcomes in weeks instead of months.

As an Applied AI Solutions Architect, you will be embedded with customers to help them prepare their Amazon Connect implementations for production by focusing on three pillars of agentic AI:
Model Selection: Guiding customers through evaluating and selecting the right foundation models (via Amazon Bedrock) for their contact center use cases, balancing latency, accuracy, cost, and compliance requirements.

Prompt Engineering: Designing, testing, and optimizing AI prompts and system instructions for Amazon Connect AI agents, including self-service agents, answer recommendation agents, and custom orchestrator agents.

company: Amazon Web Services Australia Pty Ltd
description:
DESCRIPTION

Are you passionate about helping Government, Healthcare, and Not-for-Profit organizations transform how they deliver services to citizens and communities through cloud technology?

As a Greenfield Account Executive within Amazon Web Services (AWS) ANZ Public Sector, you will join a newly formed team focused on expanding AWS adoption in emerging public sector accounts. You will own the full sales cycle—from prospecting through close—helping public sector organizations reinvent their IT strategy through cloud computing.

This role demands creative problem-solving, tenacity, and the ability to develop novel approaches for reaching and engaging prospective customers. You will engage customers across all organizational levels, building relationships with both technical teams and business leaders to drive cloud adoption.

This position requires working from the local office when not at customer sites.

Key job responsibilities
Sales Execution

- Prospect and qualify emerging public sector accounts across Government, Healthcare, Education, and Not-for-Profit sectors
- Manage the full sales cycle from prospecting through close
- Meet or exceed quarterly revenue targets while maintaining robust pipeline coverage
- Maintain accurate sales forecasts and pipeline reporting in Salesforce

Customer Engagement

- Develop and execute territory plans to maximize market penetration
- Translate cloud and AI capabilities into mission-relevant use cases for public sector organizations
- Build relationships across customer organizations, including executive leadership, technical teams, IT/operations, and procurement
- Conduct discovery calls to understand organizational missions and technical challenges

Public Sector Expertise

- Navigate public sector procurement processes including RFPs, cooperative contracts, and federal/state/local contract vehicles
- Apply knowledge of public sector compliance requirements, certifications, and fiscal year buying cycles
- Structure deals that meet public sector regulatory, compliance, and grant-funded operating requirements

Collaboration

- Partner with Solutions Architects, AWS Professional Services, AWS partners, and internal stakeholders to accelerate deal velocity
- Collaborate with the partner ecosystem (systems integrators, resellers, distributors) to drive customer success and extend market reach
- Use data, AI, and insights to prioritize outreach and identify high-potential prospects

A day in the life
Your day starts reviewing your pipeline and identifying which emerging accounts need attention. You'll research target organizations across Government, Healthcare, Education, and Not-for-Profit sectors—understanding their missions and key stakeholders. You'll execute outreach campaigns, make prospecting calls, and secure meetings with decision-makers exploring cloud transformation.

You'll conduct discovery conversations, collaborate with Solutions Architects and AWS Professional Services to shape compelling propositions, and navigate procurement processes. You'll update your CRM, refine your territory strategy, and forecast accurately. This role offers the autonomy to innovate, think differently, and make an impact across a new team whilst helping public sector organisations transform through cloud technology.

About the team
Diverse Experiences
AWS values diverse experiences. Even if you do not meet all of the preferred qualifications and skills listed in the job description, we encourage candidates to apply. If your career is just starting, hasn’t followed a traditional path, or includes alternative experiences, don’t let it stop you from applying.

Why AWS?
Amazon Web Services (AWS) is the world’s most comprehensive and broadly adopted cloud platform. We pioneered cloud computing and never stopped innovating — that’s why customers from the most successful startups to Global 500 companies trust our robust suite of products and services to power their businesses.

Inclusive Team Culture
AWS values curiosity and connection. Our employee-led and company-sponsored affinity groups promote inclusion and empower our people to take pride in what makes us unique. Our inclusion events foster stronger, more collaborative teams. Our continual innovation is fueled by the bold ideas, fresh perspectives, and passionate voices our teams bring to everything we do.

Mentorship & Career Growth
We’re continuously raising our performance bar as we strive to become Earth’s Best Employer. That’s why you’ll find endless knowledge-sharing, mentorship and other career-advancing resources here to help you develop into a better-rounded professional.

Work/Life Balance
We value work-life harmony. Achieving success at work should never come at the expense of sacrifices at home, which is why we strive for flexibility as part of our working culture. When we feel supported in the workplace and at home, there’s nothing we can’t achieve.

BASIC QUALIFICATIONS

- Experience in consulting, sales strategy, sales operations, business development or program management at a mid-to-large scale company with increasing influence and responsibility
- Experience with sales CRM tools such as Salesforce or similar software
- 5+ years technology sales, business development or sales program management experience with a track record of exceeding sales goals

PREFERRED QUALIFICATIONS

- Experience managing and growing complex business relationship at scale
- Experience in strategic thinking about business, enterprise software products, and new technology platforms and architectures or equivalent
- Experience driving new business in greenfield accounts, working with partners to drive customer engagements and revenue growth
- Experience navigating public sector procurement processes and contract vehicles with demonstrated ability to build and execute territory plans
- Experience building and managing sales pipeline (directly or indirectly) focused on driving revenue, adoption, and market penetration

Acknowledgement of country:
In the spirit of reconciliation Amazon acknowledges the Traditional Custodians of country throughout Australia and their connections to land, sea and community. We pay our respect to their elders past and present and extend that respect to all Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander peoples today.

IDE statement:
Amazon is an equal opportunity employer and does not discriminate on the basis of protected veteran status, disability, or other legally protected status.

Our inclusive culture empowers Amazonians to deliver the best results for our customers. If you have a disability and need a workplace accommodation or adjustment during the application and hiring process, including support for the interview or onboarding process, please visit https://amazon.jobs/content/en/how-we-hire/accommodations for more information. If the country/region you’re applying in isn’t listed, please contact your Recruiting Partner.

positionName: Amazon Quick Strategist, Amazon Quick Customer Success Team, AWS
company: Amazon Web Services Australia Pty Ltd
description:
DESCRIPTION

AWS is known for its relentless customer obsession. Our Professional Services teams are on the front line of helping customers succeed using AWS technologies that align with the new ways of managing people and rearchitecting critical processes to leverage the capabilities of Gen AI. AWS Amazon Q Customer Success Team (Q-CST) members leverage their deep knowledge of AWS services and market trends in collaboration with our sales and partner teams to propose, architect, and implement transformational solutions for customers. We make customer success a reality using AWS Gen AI services as an enabler.

Amazon Quick Strategists are dynamic professionals who architect and propel customer success, expertly guiding organizations from initial proof-of-concept to full-scale production implementation of Amazon Quick. These visionaries excel at crafting strategic roadmaps that expand Amazon Quick footprint across the enterprise, driving innovation and efficiency. With a strong executive presence, they skillfully navigate interactions with internal leadership (VPs, Directors) and diverse stakeholders, while rapidly building trust with Account Teams and customers at all levels, from Enterprise Architects to C-Suite executives. As technology specialists with keen business acumen, they possess the unique ability to identify high-impact use cases, quantify value, and drive measurable ROI, positioning them as indispensable partners in their customer' digital transformation journeys.

Strategists orchestrate contractual agreements, align customer expectations, and ensure flawless delivery of solutions. This hybrid role combines AWS expertise, hands-on building skills, and cross-industry business acumen. Ideal candidates are customer-obsessed professionals who seamlessly blend technical expertise with strategic insight to drive outcomes, while maintaining a strong commitment to continuous learning and growth.

Key job responsibilities
- Lead comprehensive generative AI transformation programs from sales to delivery, partnering closely with customers to drive successful adoption, implementation, and measurable business value realization
- Orchestrate high-level discussions on complex, industry-specific business and technical concepts, fostering alignment among C-suite executives, line managers, and technologists.
- Design and facilitate engaging workshops that bridge customer technical and business teams, showcasing the transformative potential of Amazon Q and our Gen AI offerings to uncover high-impact business value opportunities
- Support delivery teams with hands-on programming, blocker remediation, escalations, and ideating and delivering custom solutions when needed
- Cultivate and maintain strong, trusting relationships with both customers and internal stakeholders including the Generative AI Innovation Center, solutions architects, service teams, business development, marketing, industry specialists, partners and regional organizations
- Stay informed on Amazon Q service updates and advancements in generative AI technology through continuous learning and industry engagement
- Proactively identify areas for improvement across processes and initiatives, taking ownership to develop and implement effective solutions

A day in the life
Diverse Experiences
AWS values diverse experiences. Even if you do not meet all of the preferred qualifications and skills listed in the job description, we encourage candidates to apply. If your career is just starting, hasn’t followed a traditional path, or includes alternative experiences, don’t let it stop you from applying.

Why AWS?
Amazon Web Services (AWS) is the world’s most comprehensive and broadly adopted cloud platform. We pioneered cloud computing and never stopped innovating — that’s why customers from the most successful startups to Global 500 companies trust our robust suite of products and services to power their businesses.

Inclusive Team Culture
AWS values curiosity and connection. Our employee-led and company-sponsored affinity groups promote inclusion and empower our people to take pride in what makes us unique. Our inclusion events foster stronger, more collaborative teams. Our continual innovation is fueled by the bold ideas, fresh perspectives, and passionate voices our teams bring to everything we do.

Mentorship & Career Growth
We’re continuously raising our performance bar as we strive to become Earth’s Best Employer. That’s why you’ll find endless knowledge-sharing, mentorship and other career-advancing resources here to help you develop into a better-rounded professional.

Work/Life Balance
We value work-life harmony. Achieving success at work should never come at the expense of sacrifices at home, which is why we strive for flexibility as part of our working culture. When we feel supported in the workplace and at home, there’s nothing we can’t achieve.

BASIC QUALIFICATIONS

- 5+ years of cloud architecture and solution implementation experience
- 7+ years of cloud architecture and solution implementation experience
- 5+ years of external or internal customer facing, complex and large scale project management experience
- 3+ years of working with Data & AI related technologies, including, but not limited to, AI/ML, GenAI, Analytics, Database, and/or Storage experience

PREFERRED QUALIFICATIONS

- Experience developing, deploying and managing AI products at scale
- 5+ years of highly scalable systems experience
- Experience in representing and advocating for a variety of critical customers and stakeholders during executive-level prioritization and planning
- Experience with vertical industry sales and delivery work of contemporary services and solutions
- Experience with business development work including complex agreements w/ integrators and ISVs
- Experience with international sales and delivery work with global F500 enterprise customers and partners

Acknowledgement of country:
In the spirit of reconciliation Amazon acknowledges the Traditional Custodians of country throughout Australia and their connections to land, sea and community. We pay our respect to their elders past and present and extend that respect to all Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander peoples today.

AWS values curiosity and connection. Our employee-led and company-sponsored affinity groups promote inclusion and empower our people to take pride in what makes us unique. Our inclusion events foster stronger, more collaborative teams. Our continual innovation is fueled by the bold ideas, fresh perspectives, and passionate voices our teams bring to everything we do.

Mentorship & Career Growth
We’re continuously raising our performance bar as we strive to become Earth’s Best Employer. That’s why you’ll find endless knowledge-sharing, mentorship and other career-advancing resources here to help you develop into a better-rounded professional.

Work/Life Balance
We value work-life harmony. Achieving success at work should never come at the expense of sacrifices at home, which is why we strive for flexibility as part of our working culture. When we feel supported in the workplace and at home, there’s nothing we can’t achieve.

BASIC QUALIFICATIONS

  • 5+ years of cloud architecture and solution implementation experience
  • 7+ years of cloud architecture and solution implementation experience
  • 5+ years of external or internal customer facing, complex and large scale project management experience
  • 3+ years of working with Data & AI related technologies, including, but not limited to, AI/ML, GenAI, Analytics, Database, and/or Storage experience

PREFERRED QUALIFICATIONS

  • Experience developing, deploying and managing AI products at scale
  • 5+ years of highly scalable systems experience
  • Experience in representing and advocating for a variety of critical customers and stakeholders during executive-level prioritization and planning
  • Experience with vertical industry sales and delivery work of contemporary services and solutions
  • Experience with business development work including complex agreements w/ integrators and ISVs
  • Experience with international sales and delivery work with global F500 enterprise customers and partners

Acknowledgement of country:
In the spirit of reconciliation Amazon acknowledges the Traditional Custodians of country throughout Australia and their connections to land, sea and community. We pay our respect to their elders past and present and extend that respect to all Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander peoples today.

IDE statement:
Amazon is an equal opportunity employer and does not discriminate on the basis of protected veteran status, disability, or other legally protected status.

Our inclusive culture empowers Amazonians to deliver the best results for our customers. If you have a disability and need a workplace accommodation or adjustment during the application and hiring process, including support for the interview or onboarding process, please visit https://amazon.jobs/content/en/how-we-hire/accommodations for more information. If the country/region you’re applying in isn’t listed, please contact your Recruiting Partner.

description:
AI Job Summary
Proven experience as a Print Technician or similar technical support role.
Strong ability to diagnose and repair multi-function printers and print devices.
Current and valid driver’s licence required for the role.
 Sign up or log in to analyse my fit
Role Type
On-site • Permanent • Full-time • Mid-level Senior
Description

Deliver reliable print performance through expert support, maintenance, and smart solutions.

At efex, no two days are the same. You’ll be hands-on across diverse client sites, diagnosing print issues, maintaining devices, and ensuring minimal downtime, backed by a supportive team that values practical problem solving and great service.

Who we are:

With over 20 local branches supported by a strong national presence, efex is one of Australia’s leading IT and digital services providers. We deliver right-sized solutions in IT, print, and communications that help businesses run smarter, simpler, and more efficiently.

Our culture is approachable and business-focused: we speak in plain language, build real relationships, and always put people first, our clients and our team.

The Role:

We’re looking for a Print Technician to join our Bendigo team full-time.

In this role, you’ll play a key part in delivering high-quality print support across our Managed IT and Print customer base. You’ll be responsible for installations, maintenance, and troubleshooting, ensuring our clients experience minimal downtime and maximum efficiency.

Key Responsibilities

Provide onsite and remote support for print devices and related software
Deliver repairs, installations, and preventative maintenance across a range of devices
Build strong relationships with customers through clear communication and reliable service
Ensure all work is completed in line with SLA requirements and service delivery processes
Maintain accurate job documentation, system updates, and stock management
Proactively identify opportunities to improve service delivery and customer outcomes

About You

Proven experience as a Print Technician or in a similar technical support role
Strong experience diagnosing and repairing multi-function printers and print devices
Solid understanding of print software and troubleshooting
Exposure to IT environments will be highly regarded
Experience in production print environments will be highly regarded
Experience working with Canon devices/solutions will be highly regarded
Excellent customer service and communication skills, with the ability to explain technical issues clearly
Strong attention to detail and commitment to quality outcomes
Ability to work independently and as part of a team
Current and valid driver’s licence
Blue Card (or ability to obtain one) for working with children

What we offer:

Competitive Salary: We recognize and value your expertise, offering top-tier compensation commensurate with your skills and experience.
Birthday Leave: Take a well-deserved day off to celebrate your special day, because we believe in recognizing and celebrating our team members’ milestones.
Wellbeing leave: take up to two days from your personal leave each year to focus on your wellbeing
Varied, hands-on role across multiple clients and technologies
Opportunities to grow your technical skills in both print and IT environments

If you’re passionate about delivering exceptional service and enjoy working in a hands-on technical role, we’d love to hear from you.

Please note: all shortlisted candidates will be required to undergo a National Police Check as part of the recruitment process.

positionName: Staff or Senior Technical Application Engineer – Windows 11 PC Ecosystem (Australia)
description:
Company:
Qualcomm International, Inc. Australia Branch Office

Job Area:
Engineering Group, Engineering Group > Software Applications Engineering

General Summary:

General Summary:

Qualcomm is enabling a world where everyone and everything can be intelligently connected. You interact with products and technologies made possible by Qualcomm every day, including 5G-enabled smartphones that double as pro-level cameras and gaming devices, smarter vehicles and cities, and the technology behind the smart, connected factories that manufactured your latest purchase. Qualcomm 5G and AI innovations are the power behind the connected intelligent edge. You will find our technologies behind and inside the innovations that deliver significant value across multiple industries and to billions of people every day.

Windows Copilot+ PCs powered by Snapdragon are revolutionizing the PC industry by leading the technology transformation for AI, graphics, multimedia, connectivity, power, and performance. The Field Application Engineer – Windows Copilot+ PC Ecosystem, will have a pre-sales focus and will drive technical adoption of the Windows on Snapdragon Compute platform across the Commercial space.

In this position you will be responsible for helping enterprise customers with Windows on Snapdragon (WoS) compute product commercialization and support enterprise needs for a timely, faster, and smooth product evaluation. You will have the opportunity to show your strong customer support skills by resolving enterprise issues on the WoS platform and the Windows application ecosystem.

Job Responsibilities:

Assisting with the Execution of Qualcomm’s Worldwide Compute Go-To-Market strategy
Support enterprise customers trouble shooting and helping with detailed issue triage/analysis/resolution on BIOS/Driver.
Support debugging enterprise application issues and collaborate with platform and technology teams. This includes reproducing the issues and helping with detailed issue triage/analysis/resolution.
Analyze and root cause the application issue on customer software/hardware systems.
Support Snapdragon eco system issue analysis and engagement .

Skills, Knowledge, & Expertise:

Application development, debugging, testing with C/C++ & familiarity with embedded system software.
Experience with IT working and debugging issues in Window 11 environment and tools preferred.
Driver debugging techniques, problem solving, and documentation skills.
Debugging with tools such as WinDbg, MSFT Trace tool.
Familiarity with Windows OS such as Windows drivers development or Windows debugging, or familiarity with Windows command lines tools such as PowerShell.
Strong knowledge of C/C++ programming. Experience on window application developer a plus.

Minimum Qualifications:

• Bachelor's degree in Engineering, Information Systems, Computer Science, or related field and 4+ years of Software Applications Engineering, Software Development experience, or related work experience.

OR

Master's degree in Engineering, Information Systems, Computer Science, or related field and 3+ years of Software Applications Engineering, Software Development experience, or related work experience.

OR

PhD in Engineering, Information Systems, Computer Science, or related field and 2+ years of Software Applications Engineering, Software Development experience, or related work experience.

• 2+ years of experience with window 11/10 troubleshooting with i ndependent problem solving , etc .

Minimum Qualifications:

Bachelor’s or Master’s degree in engineering, Information Systems, Computer Science, or related field
5 to 8 years of working experience including application engineering, Field application engineering on window 10/11 OS .
Able to duplicate issue and analysis on customer side to identify solution by remotely support from internal team.

Preferred Qualifications:

Technical sales management expertise, the ability to manage change and the desire to operate effectively across organizations and customers
Experience building complex solutions and driving new device adoption in Commercial accounts
Experience in support of launching new technologies/products
Ability to communicate, educate and evangelize both internally & externally
Excellent communication and people skills
Creative problem solving with strong analytical skills

Minimum Qualifications:

• Bachelor's degree in Engineering, Information Systems, Computer Science, or related field and 4+ years of Software Applications Engineering, Software Development experience, or related work experience.
OR
Master's degree in Engineering, Information Systems, Computer Science, or related field and 3+ years of Software Applications Engineering, Software Development experience, or related work experience.
OR
PhD in Engineering, Information Systems, Computer Science, or related field and 2+ years of Software Applications Engineering, Software Development experience, or related work experience.

• 2+ years of experience with Programming Language such as C, C++, Java, Python, etc.
• 1+ year of experience with debugging techniques.

Applicants : Qualcomm is an equal opportunity employer. If you are an individual with a disability and need an accommodation during the application/hiring process, rest assured that Qualcomm is committed to providing an accessible process. You may e-mail disability-accomodations@qualcomm.com or call Qualcomm's toll-free number found here . Upon request, Qualcomm will provide reasonable accommodations to support individuals with disabilities to be able participate in the hiring process. Qualcomm is also committed to making our workplace accessible for individuals with disabilities. (Keep in mind that this email address is used to provide reasonable accommodations for individuals with disabilities. We will not respond here to requests for updates on applications or resume inquiries).

Qualcomm expects its employees to abide by all applicable policies and procedures, including but not limited to security and other requirements regarding protection of Company confidential information and other confidential and/or proprietary information, to the extent those requirements are permissible under applicable law.

To all Staffing and Recruiting Agencies : Our Careers Site is only for individuals seeking a job at Qualcomm. Staffing and recruiting agencies and individuals being represented by an agency are not authorized to use this site or to submit profiles, applications or resumes, and any such submissions will be considered unsolicited. Qualcomm does not accept unsolicited resumes or applications from agencies. Please do not forward resumes to our jobs alias, Qualcomm employees or any other company location. Qualcomm is not responsible for any fees related to unsolicited resumes/applications.

If you would like more information about this role, please contact Qualcomm Careers .

description:
AI Job Summary
At least five years’ post-admission experience.
Experience advising on energy transition matters incl. decarbonisation, renewables (solar/BESS), and emerging tech (hyd/
Well-developed drafting and negotiation skills; strong client outcomes on project/commercial documents.
 Sign up or log in to analyse my fit
Role Type
Permanent • Full-time • LP – Senior Associate
Description

HopgoodGanim is a leading independent Australian law firm, known for delivering exceptional outcomes for our clients while staying deeply connected to the communities in which we work. We are proud to be a firm where people genuinely enjoy coming to work. Our lawyers are trusted with challenging, meaningful matters and supported to grow through a culture that is inclusive, collaborative and uncompromising on our values.

About The Role

Join our Perth-based Resources & Energy team, advising on complex energy transition matters including decarbonisation initiatives, renewable energy developments and emerging technologies. You will work closely with a highly regarded Partner and a supportive, collaborative team delivering practical, commercial advice to leading Australian and international clients.

In this role, you will draft and negotiate key project and commercial documents, manage matters day-to-day with appropriate supervision, and support the team across a diverse pipeline of work including:

Decarbonisation and emissions reduction projects for major Australian companies
Renewable energy projects including grid-connected solar and battery energy storage systems (BESS), as well as remote hybrid systems for mining operations
New technology trials and demonstration projects, including international collaborations across battery minerals and hydrogen
Development projects involving Traditional Owner groups, including renewable energy for data centres and export-focused green hydrogen and ammonia initiatives
Regulatory advice and assessment of evolving industry frameworks as the energy transition progresses
Support on broader team matters across mining and oil and gas, including due diligence, contracting and project support

About You

You are an experienced and motivated Senior Associate who enjoys working on complex matters, building strong client relationships and contributing to a supportive, collaborative team environment. You will also bring:

At least five years’ post-admission experience
Well-developed drafting and negotiation skills, with a demonstrated ability to deliver strong client outcomes
Experience in a projects or commercial team at an Australian or international law firm (energy transition or broader energy exposure is advantageous)
Some experience providing regulatory advice and/or working within regulated markets
A collaborative, team-oriented mindset, with a commitment to mentoring and supporting junior lawyers
Motivation to expand your knowledge and expertise across new industries and technologies
Ability to build trusted client relationships through clear communication, responsiveness and professional insight

At HopgoodGanim, you’re not just a small piece of a big machine; you’re a valued, integral member of the team. We are committed to delivering exceptional service and while being recognised for our deeply connected and collaborative culture, shared by a team of people who have a genuine love for the work we do. As a law firm of just over 300 people nationally, HopgoodGanim offers flexible and hybrid working arrangements and a number of benefits including:

Dedicated learning & development programs for all employees
Realistic billable targets for legal practitioners
Practitioner performance and new client incentives
A dedicated in-house business development team to build your external profile
Pathways to Partnership program for legal practitioners
Clear annual review and promotion cycle
Onsite gym and end-of-trip facilities
Access to paid parental leave (up to 20 weeks for eligible employees)
Paid fertility leave
Paid birthday leave
Fun mid-year and end-of-year team celebrations including our HG Way Awards night
Employee assistance program and wellbeing initiatives

positionName: Referrer Engagement Manager | Business Development | Healthcare
description:
AI Job Summary
3 to 5 years in referrer engagement, business development, account management, or similar field-based role.
Build/manage relationships across GP practices, urgent care centres, ambulance services, employers, Defence and DVA.
Valid driver’s licence and flexibility to travel nationally more than 50% of the time.
 Sign up or log in to analyse my fit
Role Type
Permanent • Full-time • Mid-level Senior
Pay Rate
120000 AUD – 140000 AUD (Annum)
Description

Private Emergency Health Australia (PEHA), Brisbane, Queensland (with national travel >50%) Full-time, Permanent

About PEHA

Private Emergency Health Australia (PEHA) is Australia’s leading provider of emergency medicine services across the private hospital sector. We partner with private hospitals to deliver high-quality, patient-centred emergency care across a growing national network of emergency departments and emergency care centres.

We are a values-driven organisation built on care, confidence, partnership, and opportunity. As we continue to grow, we are investing in the people and relationships that will drive sustainable long-term performance across our existing sites.

The Opportunity

This is a rare opportunity to join PEHA’s leadership team in a newly created, highly autonomous role focused on growing patient referrals across our existing national ED network.

As Referrer Engagement Manager, you will be the face of PEHA in the field. This is an execution-focused business development role where your success is measured by real, tangible growth with more patients being seen, more referral pathways activated, and stronger relationships built with the right people in the right communities.

You will report directly to the General Manager and work closely with our site-based Practice Managers and Site Directors across the country.

What You Will Be Doing

Your focus will be on growing referrals at existing PEHA sites through hands-on, relationship-based business development. Specifically, you will:

Spend a minimum of 50% of your time in the field, building and managing relationships across GP practices, urgent care centres, ambulance services, employers, Defence and DVA networks, and large community and tourism organisations
Develop and formalise Workplace Injury Management SLAs with employers, maintain quarterly contact cadence, and track injury management presentation growth
Establish and manage GP and specialist referral relationships across all sites, with a focus on measurable referral volume growth
Map and profile local urgent care centres by site to identify diversion and referral opportunities, and develop strategies to reduce avoidable presentation loss
Activate and track Defence and DVA presentation pathways
Plan and execute GP education dinners, employer events, and injury management forums
Coach and develop Practice Managers to independently own stakeholder relationships at the site level
Maintain a structured stakeholder database, engagement logs, and monthly KPI reporting
Develop and maintain relationships with hospital stakeholders
Monitor local competitive dynamics and incorporate market intelligence into site-specific action plans

What We Are Looking For

You are a natural relationship builder who is equally comfortable pitching to a GP principal partner, negotiating a WorkCover SLA with an employer, and coaching a Practice Manager through their first stakeholder conversation. You are organised, accountable, and comfortable working autonomously across a national portfolio.

You will bring:

3 to 5 years of experience in referrer engagement, business development, account management, or a similar field-based role
Experience in healthcare is preferred; pharmaceutical or injury management sales experience would be well-regarded
A genuine understanding of healthcare financing streams and the ability to navigate clinical environments with credibility
Demonstrated ability to manage and report against KPIs and performance metrics
Experience coaching, mentoring, or developing others
A bachelor’s degree in business, management, marketing, health services, or a related field
A valid driver’s licence and the flexibility to travel nationally for more than 50% of your time

Why Join PEHA?

Report to the General Manager and work with PEHA’s operations team in a role that genuinely shapes the business
Join a high-growth,purpose-driven private healthcare organisation with a national footprint
Be given real autonomy and accountability to drive measurable outcomes
Play a central role in one of private emergency medicine’s most exciting growth phases

How to Apply

If you have a track record in healthcare business development or referrer engagement and you are ready to take on a nationally significant role with real impact, we would love to hear from you.

Apply now with your resume and a brief cover letter outlining your relevant experience.

What You Will Be Doing

Your focus will be on growing referrals at existing PEHA sites through hands-on, relationship-based business development. Specifically, you will:

  • Spend a minimum of 50% of your time in the field, building and managing relationships across GP practices, urgent care centres, ambulance services, employers, Defence and DVA networks, and large community and tourism organisations
  • Develop and formalise Workplace Injury Management SLAs with employers, maintain quarterly contact cadence, and track injury management presentation growth
  • Establish and manage GP and specialist referral relationships across all sites, with a focus on measurable referral volume growth
  • Map and profile local urgent care centres by site to identify diversion and referral opportunities, and develop strategies to reduce avoidable presentation loss
  • Activate and track Defence and DVA presentation pathways
  • Plan and execute GP education dinners, employer events, and injury management forums
  • Coach and develop Practice Managers to independently own stakeholder relationships at the site level
  • Maintain a structured stakeholder database, engagement logs, and monthly KPI reporting
  • Develop and maintain relationships with hospital stakeholders
  • Monitor local competitive dynamics and incorporate market intelligence into site-specific action plans

What We Are Looking For

You are a natural relationship builder who is equally comfortable pitching to a GP principal partner, negotiating a WorkCover SLA with an employer, and coaching a Practice Manager through their first stakeholder conversation. You are organised, accountable, and comfortable working autonomously across a national portfolio.

You will bring:

  • 3 to 5 years of experience in referrer engagement, business development, account management, or a similar field-based role
  • Experience in healthcare is preferred; pharmaceutical or injury management sales experience would be well-regarded
  • A genuine understanding of healthcare financing streams and the ability to navigate clinical environments with credibility
  • Demonstrated ability to manage and report against KPIs and performance metrics
  • Experience coaching, mentoring, or developing others
  • A bachelor’s degree in business, management, marketing, health services, or a related field
  • A valid driver’s licence and the flexibility to travel nationally for more than 50% of your time

Why Join PEHA?

  • Report to the General Manager and work with PEHA’s operations team in a role that genuinely shapes the business
  • Join a high-growth,purpose-driven private healthcare organisation with a national footprint
  • Be given real autonomy and accountability to drive measurable outcomes
  • Play a central role in one of private emergency medicine’s most exciting growth phases

How to Apply

If you have a track record in healthcare business development or referrer engagement and you are ready to take on a nationally significant role with real impact, we would love to hear from you.

Apply now with your resume and a brief cover letter outlining your relevant experience.

positionName: ANZ AI Leader – AWS Global Sales (AGS)
company: Amazon Web Services Australia Pty Ltd
descriptionHTML:
DESCRIPTION

The ANZ AI Leader creates and sets the vision to drive AI/ML and Agentic AI adoption across the ANZ region. Leading a team of Specialists, you will partner with and influence local sales teams to accelerate customer adoption of AWS AI/ML services, Agentic AI solutions, and Accelerated Compute capabilities. This is a critical role reporting directly to the Area VP with ownership of developing and executing the strategic plan for ANZ-wide AI/ML specialist resource investments and go-to-market execution, accelerating customer outcomes from current and future AI/ML services.

This team encompasses two core areas: 1) AI/ML Sales Team providing Level 200-300 support for complex, high-value solution selling to qualified AI/ML use cases, and 2) AI/ML Specialist SA’s providing Level 300-400 support for complex AI/ML architectures, integrations and agentic workflows in Tech Validation, Committed and Launched stages of the sales cycle. The team drives both customer organizations and AWS Sales teams to adopt Agentic AI to transform operations, addressing technical and non-technical topics with C-suite and boards including AI ethics, governance, security, risk, change management, and Agentic Ops.

AWS is looking for an accomplished leader with experience across product, business, and technology who has worked with large Enterprises and high-growth organizations to accelerate their AI/ML and cloud-native journey. In this capacity, the leader will have revenue responsibility while leading a cross-functional team of Sales, and Solution Architects,. The Specialist team acts as an important feedback ingestion point from customers that directly feeds into the roadmap for AWS AI/ML product and services teams.

You will lead AI/ML and Agentic AI discussions with C-suite executives and boards of directors and play a critical role in taking the voice of the customer and sharing it back to the Worldwide Specialist Organization (WWSO) team to ensure customer feedback effectively influences our technology roadmaps and Go-To-Market strategy. This role will contribute to the overall performance of the business globally measured by revenue growth, net new AI/ML customers, and product and service influence.

You will have experience as a leader of leaders, with significant multi-vertical ANZ exposure. You will have overachieved throughout your career and built strong, deep relationships with F1000 and/or G2000 organizations.

Key job responsibilities
  • Influence at the CxO/VP and Board level, as well as developers and technical architects, to develop AI/ML pipeline, secure lighthouse customers, drive top-line revenue, and explore strategic partnerships in AI/ML and Agentic AI;

  • Build, manage, and close a pipeline which exceeds the Area AI/ML revenue goal while driving Agentic AI transformation across customer organizations;

  • Lead and develop the AI/ML Sales and SA Team, s to deliver exceptional customer outcomes;

  • Develop and deploy AI/ML sales motions and programs that leverage AWS Field Sales teams, as well as technology and consulting partner channels. Go-to-market elements may include consultative workshops on Agentic AI, sales/partner/customer enablement, solution blueprints, customer proposal and offer development, internal and external collateral, Proofs of Concept (POC), incentives, target customers, and success measures;

  • Define, develop, and execute against comprehensive account/territory plans supporting multiple account teams to drive achievement of AI/ML revenue and win goals;

  • Create & articulate compelling value propositions for AI/ML services (AgentCore, Kiro, Quick Suite, Nova models) and address competitive positioning a;

  • Drive customer adoption of Agentic AI through well-developed sales engagements and successful GTM Strategy, addressing AI ethics, governance, security, risk, and transformational change management;

  • Interface with WWSO GTM and global AI/ML leadership, manage technical escalations for GPU capacity and AIML service/feature availability;

  • Partner with cross-functional teams across Solution Architecture, Business Development, Marketing, Partners, and Training to execute customer acquisition programs and strategies focused on AI/ML adoption;

  • Define and drive operational processes and excellence with sales teams, ensuring AI/ML thought leadership and specialist request resolution;

  • Achieve personal AI/ML sales quota while leading the team to exceed Area AI/ML goals.

About the team
Diverse Experiences
AWS values diverse experiences. Even if you do not meet all of the qualifications and skills listed in the job description, we encourage candidates to apply. If your career is just starting, hasn’t followed a traditional path, or includes alternative experiences, don’t let it stop you from applying.

Why AWS?
Amazon Web Services (AWS) is the world’s most comprehensive and broadly adopted cloud platform. We pioneered cloud computing and never stopped innovating — that’s why customers from the most successful startups to Global 500 companies trust our robust suite of products and services to power their businesses.

Inclusive Team Culture
Here at AWS, it’s in our nature to learn and be curious. Our employee-led affinity groups foster a culture of inclusion that empower us to be proud of our differences. Ongoing events and learning experiences, including our Conversations on Race and Ethnicity (CORE) and AmazeCon (gender diversity) conferences, inspire us to never stop embracing our uniqueness.

Mentorship & Career Growth
We’re continuously raising our performance bar as we strive to become Earth’s Best Employer. That’s why you’ll find endless knowledge-sharing, mentorship and other career-advancing resources here to help you develop into a better-rounded professional.

Work/Life Balance
We value work-life harmony. Achieving success at work should never come at the expense of sacrifices at home, which is why we strive for flexibility as part of our working culture. When we feel supported in the workplace and at home, there’s nothing we can’t achieve in the cloud.

BASIC QUALIFICATIONS

  • Demonstrated expertise in technical sales of AI/ML, Agentic AI, and cloud computing products and services. 15+ years of enterprise technology sales with 10+ years leading sales teams;
  • Proven track record of taking ownership and driving results. Ability to effectively lead a scaled sales organization focused on emerging technologies, and work with a variety of organizations, management levels, cultures, and personalities;
  • Exceptional interpersonal and communication (both written and verbal) skills, particularly in explaining complex AI/ML concepts to non-technical audiences;
  • Experience communicating with both technical and non-technical stakeholders across multiple teams, as well as internal and external executive stakeholders including boards of directors;
  • Established track record of building rapport with customer executives (e.g. CEO, COO, CIO, CTO, CMO, Chief AI Officer), building close relationships, and closing large revenue accounts in AI/ML or emerging technology domains;
  • Experience and success in negotiating complex deals with customers and partners. You've not only carried quotas, you've consistently exceeded them by doing the right thing for your customers;
  • Hiring, developing and motivating a sales organization to consistently exceed expectations against defined goals and objectives, particularly in fast-moving technology domains;
  • BA/BS degree required, MBA or advanced technical degree preferred.

PREFERRED QUALIFICATIONS

  • Relevant AI/ML sales experience. Breadth and depth in multiple enterprise functional areas in multiple industries a plus;
  • Experience in a heavily matrixed sales environment, including developing, implementing, managing, and executing cross-functional solutions-led go-to-market growth initiatives and sales motion for AI/ML services;
  • Deep understanding of AI/ML technologies, Agentic AI, Large Language Models, and Accelerated Compute (GPU) infrastructure; technical background in engineering, computer science, data science, or MIS a plus;
  • Understanding of AI ethics, governance, security frameworks, and risk management in enterprise AI deployments;
  • Recognized industry expertise in AI/ML with proven public speaking and media prowess;
  • Professional traits that are not unique to this position, but necessary for Amazon leaders:
  • Exhibits excellent judgment;
  • Inclusive leader who inspires others to share unique points of view to better inform decisions;
  • Hires and develops great people;
  • Has relentlessly high standards;
  • Thinks strategically, but stays on top of tactical execution;
  • Expects and requires innovation of her/his team;
  • Thinks big and has convictions;
  • Results-oriented;
  • Has the innate ability to inspire passion in others.

Acknowledgement of country:
In the spirit of reconciliation Amazon acknowledges the Traditional Custodians of country throughout Australia and their connections to land, sea and community. We pay our respect to their elders past and present and extend that respect to all Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander peoples today.

IDE statement:
Amazon is an equal opportunity employer and does not discriminate on the basis of protected veteran status, disability, or other legally protected status.

Our inclusive culture empowers Amazonians to deliver the best results for our customers. If you have a disability and need a workplace accommodation or adjustment during the application and hiring process, including support for the interview or onboarding process, please visit https://amazon.jobs/content/en/how-we-hire/accommodations for more information. If the country/region you’re applying in isn’t listed, please contact your Recruiting Partner.

positionName: Partner Account Manager, Worldwide Public Sector - ANZ
company: Amazon Web Services Australia Pty Ltd
description:
DESCRIPTION

AWS Partners are an integral part of the success that drives adoption of AWS, enabling customers of all sizes to innovate. Partners empower our customers by providing tailored service and unmatched technology guidance.

Partners specialising in Public Sector have also chosen to invest in delivering services for customers that ensure the ongoing delivery of core citizen facing services in a secure, reliable and scalable way.

We work together to dive deep to understand our customer's unique challenges, then craft innovative solutions that accelerate their success. Join us and help us support the SI partners that share our customer obsessed mindset.

Do you want to own the AWS relationship with some of the world's and our region's largest and most trusted Solutions Integration Partners? Want to be part of a cross-functional team focused on increasing technology adoption of cloud computing in public sector by engaging directly with C-level executives and influencers at all levels?

As part of a single-threaded team focusing on partner management, you will be working with enterprise Public Sector SI partners. You will engage with our Solution Architects, Sales, and Marketing organizations to drive opportunities to closure at a large scale. Your commitments will include driving platform adoption, identifying use cases with our partners to transform their business outcomes, and ensuring success at scale.

Your responsibilities will include working within a Public Sector partner team, partnering to co-sell partner solutions with account and channel teams, as well as working with partners to define key market segments to target, identifying ways to deliver increased value via use of cloud. You will drive the necessary business and technical relationships with partners to establish new business in those markets. You’ll also work closely with other specialist teams to help partners evolve their products/services and address concerns.

The ideal candidate will possess both a business background that enables them to drive an engagement and interact at the CxO/VP level, as well as a technical background that enables them to easily interact with services partners. Thinking strategically and analytically about business, product, and technical challenges, you’ll have the ability to build and convey compelling value propositions, and work organisationally to build consensus. The ideal candidate will also have experience working in a partner channel, be it in a partner / alliance management role or for a vendor as a partner manager and ideally with public sector partners or customers.

Key job responsibilities
• Support partners to leverage AWS to successfully deliver and grow their solutions across their market segments using all available program and technical assistance ay your disposal.
• Contribute to the strategic partner plan to execute for target markets in line with both AWS and partner strategic direction
• Execute the strategic business development plan while working with key internal stakeholders (e.g. account teams, partner teams, specialist teams, Solution Architects and support).
• Relevant technical experience/knowledge is helpful, such as: AI (Generative & Agentic), data and analytics, core distributed computing concepts, fundamentals of cloud computing and migration, big data concepts, storage systems.

BASIC QUALIFICATIONS

- 7+ years of direct sales or business development in software, cloud or SaaS markets selling to C-level executives experience
- 8+ years of business development, partner development, sales or alliances management experience
- Experience proactively growing customer relationships within an account while expanding their understanding of the customer's business
- Experience working with global teams and diverse partners
- Experience engaging with customer relationships at the CXO level
- Ability to operate with a high degree of ambiguity and in a matrix organization
- Proven track record of successful engagements with Public Sector customers in Australia & New Zealand

PREFERRED QUALIFICATIONS

- Experience influencing innovation through a partner ecosystem with either solutions or programs that have demonstrated success at enterprise customers

Acknowledgement of country:
In the spirit of reconciliation Amazon acknowledges the Traditional Custodians of country throughout Australia and their connections to land, sea and community. We pay our respect to their elders past and present and extend that respect to all Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander peoples today.

IDE statement:
Amazon is an equal opportunity employer and does not discriminate on the basis of protected veteran status, disability, or other legally protected status.

Our inclusive culture empowers Amazonians to deliver the best results for our customers. If you have a disability and need a workplace accommodation or adjustment during the application and hiring process, including support for the interview or onboarding process, please visit https://amazon.jobs/content/en/how-we-hire/accommodations for more information. If the country/region you’re applying in isn’t listed, please contact your Recruiting Partner.

description:
AI Job Summary
Bachelor of Education (Primary), Certificate IV in Education Support, or related qualification.
Experience working with primary school aged children (Years 2–6) and supporting diverse backgrounds.
Understanding of phonics-based literacy instruction and child-centred learning principles.
 Sign up or log in to analyse my fit
Role Type
On-site • Contract • Part-time • Mid-Level
Pay Rate
42 AUD – 43.49 AUD (Hour)
Description

WHO ARE WE?

The Rev. Bill Crews Foundation (‘BCF’) is one of Sydney’s most trusted frontline charities and has been supporting the community for over 40 years. Our mission is to address homelessness and hardship by providing essential services including food, social welfare, healthcare, education and community programs.

For more than 20 years BCF has also delivered literacy programs in primary schools across NSW and the Northern Territory. Our program supports children who have fallen behind in reading to rebuild confidence and strengthen their literacy skills.

Using the ‘All About Reading’program and other structured resources, our Literacy Instructors create engaging, supportive learning environments that help students improve their reading, spelling and phonics skills and return to grade level.

THE ROLE

We are currently seeking a Literacy Instructor to deliver BCF’s literacy program at a participating primary school in Willmot.

This role works with students in Years 2–6 in small groups of 3–6 students, allowing you to tailor teaching approaches to individual learning needs. Each instructor supports approximately 20–30 students per school.

This is a part time role working Monday to Thursday from 9.00am to 2.00pm within the school environment.

WHAT YOU’LL DO

Deliver structured and engaging literacy sessions using evidence-based teaching resources
Plan lessons and set weekly learning objectives
Support students to strengthen reading, spelling and phonics skills
Conduct regular student assessments and track progress
Maintain accurate records of attendance and student performance
Liaise with school staff and the NSW Literacy Program Coordinator
Prepare learning materials and teaching resources
Write student progress reports and assist with end of term graduations
Participate in training to support high quality program delivery

WHAT YOU’LL BRING

You are a patient and engaging educator who enjoys working with children and supporting their learning development.

You will also bring:

Bachelor of Education (Primary), Certificate IV in Education Support or related qualification
Experience working with primary school aged children
Experience supporting children from diverse backgrounds
Understanding of phonics based literacy instruction and child centred learning
Strong organisational and communication skills
Confidence building positive relationships with teachers, students and school staff
Proficiency using Microsoft Office programs such as Word and Excel
Willingness to travel to school locations if required
Alignment with the mission and values of BCF

WHY JOIN US?

Professional Development Opportunities
EAP (Employee Assistance Program)
NFP Salary Packaging Benefit ($15,900 Tax Free Benefit)
Health and Wellbeing Program (WHEREFIT)

OUR RECRUITMENT PROCESS

If your resume is shortlisted the next steps may include:

Phone screen
Interview
A short trial which allows you to meet the team observe the program and experience the environment before making a decision

This process helps ensure you feel informed supported and confident about whether BCF is the right fit for you.

READY TO APPLY?

If you are passionate about helping children build confidence in their literacy skills we would love to hear from you.

The Bill Crews Charitable Trust and the Exodus Foundation are separate but closely aligned entities. Together, they deliver services and programs under the Rev. Bill Crews Foundation brand, working in partnership to support those most in need.

All Bill Crews Foundation employees are required to have completed at least three (3) COVID-19 vaccinations, including booster doses approved by the Therapeutic Goods Administration (TGA). Proof of vaccination will be required prior to commencing employment.

Applicants must have the right to work in Australia. The successful candidate will also be required to complete a Working With Children Check and a National Police Check as part of the employment process.

Please Note: This position is managed directly by the BCF People and Culture team. While we appreciate the interest of recruitment agencies and external providers, we are not seeking third-party assistance for this role at this time.

Company Overview
The Rev. Bill Crews Foundation provides direct assistance to address the cause and effect of homelessness, poverty and disadvantage. We succeed in achieving this through caring services including food provision, social welfare and education support.
