description:
Join the National Leader in Cloud-Based Property Management

 Our Story

PropertyMe is Australia’s #1 real estate agency platform, with 60%+ market share. More than 35,000 real estate professionals use PropertyMe to manage over 1.9 million properties.

Your work will shape the day-to-day experience of real estate professionals, tenants, and owners across Australia and New Zealand.

 What you bring

You bring 3+ years of commercial software engineering experience, with practical experience in:

Modern .NET / C# and building maintainable backend services
Designing and building Web APIs
JavaScript or TypeScript in modern web applications

How you work
You’re comfortable owning well-defined problems and collaborating with Product, Design, and other engineers to deliver high-quality solutions. You write clean, reliable code and value testing and maintainable software practices.

You’re growing in confidence when making technical decisions and weighing trade-offs across speed, quality, and maintainability. You use modern development workflows, including AI-assisted engineering tools, to improve productivity and quality

Nice to have

Bonus points if you have experience with:

Vue in production
AWS or other cloud platforms
CI/CD pipelines and automated testing practices
Working in Agile teams and contributing to technical discussions

 Responsibilities

Design and implement high-quality features and improvements
Work closely with Product and Design to solve meaningful customer problems
Write maintainable code and automated tests
Participate in technical design discussions and contribute ideas
Provide constructive feedback through pull requests and collaboration
Continuously improve your technical skills and engineering practices
Support the practical use of Lean and Agile principles in software delivery

 The benefits and perks

Job security working for one of Australia's leading PropTech companies
Small Scrum teams with a lot of autonomy
No on-call duty - you focus on delivering solutions
Fully remote
Regular milestone parties and events

It’s uncertain times at the moment, but there’s nothing uncertain about the opportunity here at PropertyMe. We’re building something special, and we want to chat with you about joining us, no matter your background. Apply now or check out our Talent Acquisition team on LinkedIn; we’re always open for a chat!

description:
Are you passionate about coding and inspiring young minds?
*ENJOY AI Australia* is seeking enthusiastic *Python Tutors* to join our growing education team, with opportunities available at our *Mount Waverley* campus in Melbourne.

*What You’ll Do*

* Teach *Python programming* to students aged *8–18* in small, engaging classes.
* *Guide students from zero-based Python learning — starting with fundamental programming concepts, advancing to the Pygame Sprite Module, and eventually exploring basic game development.*
* Foster creativity, problem-solving, and confidence through interactive learning.
* Provide feedback and track student progress.

*What We’re Looking For*

* *Bachelor’s degree or higher.*
* *Proficiency in Python*; experience with *Pygame* or *problem-solving algorithms* is preferred.
* *Teaching or tutoring experience* with children or teenagers preferred.
* Strong *communication* and *interpersonal skills*.
* *English fluency* required, *Mandarin or Cantonese* a plus.

*Hours & Pay*

* *Flexible schedule* – mainly *after school hours and weekends*.
* *$30 per hour*, depending on experience.
* *Full-attendance bonus available for tutors with no absences or cancellations during the term.*

*About ENJOY AI*

*ENJOY AI* is a premier educational robotics and AI program designed specifically for young technology enthusiasts worldwide and a *global leader in AI robotics education and training*.
With a presence in *56 countries* and over *600,000 students*, we are committed to spreading knowledge of AI and robotics to youth across the globe.

Our flagship program, the *ENJOY AI Global Youth AI Campaign*, includes:

* *ENJOY AI Global Final* – Annual global championship.
* *ENJOY AI Open* – Country-level competitions held annually.
* *ENJOY AI Trial* – City/State-level competitions held annually.

ENJOY AI also partners with *U.S. News Global Education* and *IELTS*, providing outstanding participants with *pre-admission opportunities to prestigious universities worldwide*.

Job Types: Part-time, Graduate, Casual

Pay: From $30.00 per hour

Benefits:
* Salary packaging



Education:
* Bachelor Degree (Preferred)

Language:
* Mandarin or Cantonese (Required)

Licence/Certification:
* Working with Children Check (Preferred)

Work Authorisation:
* Australia (Preferred)

Work Location: In person

description:
As one of the world’s leading IT distributors, we’re constantly seeking exceptional talent to help create and drive impact across the IT ecosystem. By joining our dynamic team, you’ll become part of a vibrant Australian workplace culture that celebrates success and is focused on what truly matters to our partners and vendors - being a valued added distributor! You’ll feel welcomed into an organisation that values diversity, fosters inclusion, and operates with a clear sense of purpose.

If you’re a seasoned professional, here is a great opportunity to develop your career in the technology industry alongside our network of best-in-class vendors and caring and collaborative co-workers.

We now have a great opportunity for a Business Development Manager within our Cloud business.

As the Microsoft CSP – Azure Business Development Manager, you will support the growth of Azure and Microsoft CSP outcomes across a defined set of channel partners. You will work with partners and internal teams to identify opportunities, assist with enablement activities, progress deals through the sales cycle, and maintain strong sales cadence and CRM discipline to meet agreed targets.

Key responsibilities

• Partner engagement: Build relationships with assigned partners and internal stakeholders; maintain regular contact to progress opportunities and action plans.

• Opportunity management: Prospect within the partner base, qualify opportunities, and support partners to progress deals through to close.

• Enablement and onboarding: Assist partners to understand Microsoft CSP offers, processes and tools; coordinate training and workshops with Microsoft and internal teams.

• Solution knowledge: Maintain working knowledge of Azure, AI and Security offers and common partner use-cases (migration, modernisation, backup, security baseline, data/AI starters).

• Sales cadence and reporting: Maintain pipeline hygiene, forecasting and activity tracking in CRM; provide regular updates to stakeholders.

• Programs and incentives: Help partners access applicable Microsoft programs, incentives and promotions and ensure follow-through on required actions.

• Collaboration: Work with customer success, operations, finance and marketing to deliver a smooth partner experience and resolve day-to-day issues.

• Continuous improvement: Capture partner feedback, identify gaps and contribute ideas to improve partner tools, processes and campaigns.

Knowledge, skills and experience

• Experience: 1–3 years in business development, channel sales, inside sales or partner-facing roles within IT, software or cloud.

• Learning mindset: Interest in building knowledge across Microsoft Azure and the partner ecosystem; ability to learn programs, tools and processes quickly.

• Sales fundamentals: Comfortable prospecting, qualifying opportunities, following sales process and meeting activity targets.

• Communication: Clear written and verbal communication; confident on calls and able to support basic presentations and enablement sessions.

• Organisation: Strong time management and attention to detail; able to manage multiple partner requests and priorities.

• Tools: Proficiency in Microsoft 365; experience using a CRM (HubSpot preferred) or willingness to learn.

• Qualifications: Tertiary qualification in business, IT or related discipline (or equivalent experience) desirable.

• Certifications (desirable): Azure Fundamentals (AZ-900) and/or AI-900 (or willingness to obtain within an agreed timeframe).

Whats on offer

Besides offering a very competitive remuneration package, we also offer:

• Great team collaborative culture.

• Great learning opportunities to grow professionally and personally through dedicated learning platforms, our education assistance program, focused individual development plans and from your co-workers.

• Volunteering opportunities to give back to our community.

• Flexible work arrangements.

• Paid parental leave scheme including options to ramp up your hours back to your role, a great program that we are especially proud of;

• Strong reward and recognition programs.

If you are seeking a new role or opportunity to further grow your sales and/ or business development career, we look forward to hearing from you soon!

Please note: candidates from Brisbane are also welcome to apply for this role.

If this role is not for you at this time or you are looking for other opportunities within Tech Data, you can join our Talent Communityhere

Key Skills
AtTech Data, a TD SYNNEX Company,our values guide everything we do: Together, We Own It, We Dare to Go, We Grow and Win, and above all, We Do the Right Thing. These principles shape how we work with each other, our partners, and our communities as we drive innovation and create lasting impact.

What’s In It For You?

• Elective Benefits: Our programs are tailored to your country to best accommodate your lifestyle.
• Grow Your Career: Accelerate your path to success (and keep up with the future) with formal programs on leadership and professional development, and many more on-demand courses.
• Elevate Your Personal Well-Being: Boost your financial, physical, and mental well-being through seminars, events, and our global Life Empowerment Assistance Program.
• Diversity, Equity Inclusion: It’s not just a phrase to us; valuing every voice is how we succeed. Join us in celebrating our global diversity through inclusive education, meaningful peer-to-peer conversations, and equitable growth and development opportunities.
• Make the Most of our Global Organization: Network with other new co-workers within your first 30 days through our onboarding program.
• Connect with Your Community: Participate in internal, peer-led inclusive communities and activities, including business resource groups, local volunteering events, and more environmental and social initiatives.

Don’t meet every single requirement? Apply anyway.
At Tech Data, a TD SYNNEX Company, we’re proud to be recognized as a great place to work and a leader in the promotion and practice of diversity, equity and inclusion. If you’re excited about working for our company and believe you’re a good fit for this role, we encourage you to apply. You may be exactly the person we’re looking for!

We are an equal opportunity employer and committed to building a diverse team that represents and empowers a variety of backgrounds, perspectives, and skills. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment based on merit, without regard to race, colour, religion, national origin, gender, gender identity or expression, sexual orientation, protected veteran status, disability, genetics, age, or any other characteristic protected by law. To support our diversity and inclusion efforts, we may ask for voluntary gender disclosure information. This data will be used solely to improve our hiring practices and ensure fair treatment for all candidates.

description:
AI Job Summary
At least five years’ post-admission experience.
Senior Associate experience in mining, oil & gas, renewable energy or native title.
Experience advising complex matters for mid to large Australian clients across WA and QLD.
 Sign up or log in to analyse my fit
Role Type
Permanent • Full-time • LP – Senior Associate
Description

HopgoodGanim is a leading independent Australian law firm, known for delivering exceptional outcomes for our clients while staying deeply connected to the communities in which we work. We are proud to be a firm where people genuinely enjoy coming to work. Our lawyers are trusted with challenging, meaningful matters and supported to grow through a culture that is inclusive, collaborative and uncompromising on our values.

About the role

Join our Perth-based Resources & Energy team as a Senior Associate. You will be part of a collaborative, high‑performing projects and commercial work group with exposure to some of the most dynamic work in the energy and resources sector. This role offers both breadth of experience and the opportunity to build a meaningful specialisation aligned to your interests and strengths.

Work as part of a multi‑disciplinary team advising across mining, oil and gas, renewable energy and native title
Be mentored by three recognised partners, each providing strong technical guidance and support
Join a team known for its supportive, collaborative culture
Gain broad exposure across practice areas, with the opportunity to develop a specialisation aligned to your interests and capabilities
Advise on interesting and complex matters for mid to large Australian clients across WA and QLD, including:
Mining company acquisitions and divestments, due diligence, farm-in, joint ventures, split commodity and royalty agreements, land access, native title and heritage negotiations.
Upstream and midstream oil & gas projects, including project development, joint ventures and long-term commercial arrangements.
Decarbonisation projects, supporting some of Australia’s largest companies as they reduce emissions
Renewable energy matters, including utility‑scale developments, BESS, and green development projects for traditional owner groups

About You

You are an experienced and motivated Senior Associate who enjoys working on complex matters, building strong client relationships and contributing to a supportive team environment. You will also bring:

At least five years’ post‑admission experience
Demonstrated experience at Senior Associate level in one or more of mining, oil and gas, renewable energy or native title, within an Australian or overseas law firm
A genuine motivation to expand your knowledge and expertise, with the flexibility to engage across new work areas
Strong drafting and negotiation skills, with the ability to lead discrete project components and deliver high‑quality client outcomes
Proven ability to build trusted client relationships through clear communication, responsiveness and professional judgement
A collaborative, team‑oriented mindset, with a commitment to mentoring and supporting junior lawyers

At HopgoodGanim, you’re not just a small piece of a big machine; you’re a valued, integral member of the team. We are committed to delivering exceptional service and while being recognised for our deeply connected and collaborative culture, shared by a team of people who have a genuine love for the work we do. As a law firm of just over 300 people nationally, HopgoodGanim offers flexible and hybrid working arrangements and a number of benefits including:

Dedicated learning & development programs for all employees
Realistic billable targets for legal practitioners
Practitioner performance and new client incentives
A dedicated in-house business development team to build your external profile
Pathways to Partnership program for legal practitioners
Clear annual review and promotion cycle
Onsite gym and end-of-trip facilities
Access to paid parental leave (up to 20 weeks for eligible employees)
Paid fertility leave
Paid birthday leave – because your day should be celebrated!
Fun mid-year and end-of-year team celebrations including our HG Way Awards night
Employee assistance program and wellbeing initiatives

positionName: Remote Founding Team Opportunities - AutoCyber AI
description:
*Remote Founding Team Opportunities - AutoCyber AI*

*Type:* Internship (Unpaid) / Expression of Interest for Future Paid Roles
*Location:* Remote

*About Us*

AutoCyber AI is an Australian, remote-first startup building *AI-driven cybersecurity and compliance products*.

We focus on:

* Secure, privacy-first systems
* Agentic AI for real-world use
* Practical tools for businesses, not theory

We’re early-stage, execution-focused, and building fast.

*The Opportunity*

We’re not hiring traditionally.

We’re building a *Founding Talent Network* — a group of high-potential individuals who want to contribute early and grow with the company.

*Open Tracks*

* Customer Success & Product Enablement
* Full Stack Development
* Frontend / UI/UX
* Sales & Outreach
* Programs & Partnerships

You may be matched to one or more tracks.

*What You’ll Actually Do*

Depending on your track:

* Work on real product features
* Support customers and onboarding
* Contribute to growth and outreach
* Improve UX and usability
* Help shape early-stage systems

This is *hands-on execution*, not observation.

*Who This Is For*

You’re:

* A strong communicator
* Quick to learn
* Comfortable with ambiguity
* Self-driven and reliable
* Interested in AI, cybersecurity, or SaaS

No coding required for non-technical tracks.

*Who This Is NOT For*

* Passive applicants
* People looking for structured corporate roles
* Those expecting immediate paid positions

*What You’ll Gain*

* Real startup experience
* Exposure to AI cybersecurity products
* Priority for future paid roles
* Direct involvement in building something early

*How We Work*

* Remote-first, structured but flexible
* Calm internally, professional externally
* High ownership, low micromanagement
* Execution over theory

*Why This Matters*

Early-stage startups are built by people who show up early.

This is your chance to:

* Get inside
* Contribute
* Grow with the company

*How to Apply*

Apply here:
https://forms.gle/qdo2iy3fj9A2toZz9

*Important*

Before applying:
Follow our page for updates, selection rounds, and future opportunities.

We prioritise candidates who stay engaged.

*Want to stand out?*

Comment your preferred track (1–5) on the linkedin post or reach out directly.



Application Question(s):
* Are you comfortable taking ownership of customer-facing challenges, learning complex products quickly, and proactively solving problems in a remote startup environment? 
* Are you confident guiding customers through new technology, answering questions clearly, and helping build trust from first interaction?
* Can you quickly learn technical products independently and confidently explain them to customers in a clear, professional way?

Work Location: Remote

description:
Position Purpose:

Serve as a Senior Full Stack Developer responsible for designing and delivering high-quality features
Hands-on development role while contributing to and influencing the overall technical direction of the product.
Take ownership of feature technical design across the full stack, including cloud infrastructure.
Mentor and support junior engineers, fostering best practices and continuous improvement within the team.

Key Responsibilities:

Design and develop product software using appropriate modelling techniques, adhering to agreed design standards, patterns, and methodologies. Balance complexity, business requirements, cost, and timelines to achieve optimal outcomes for customers and the business.
Create and communicate multiple architectural and design views to address stakeholder concerns and ensure alignment with both functional and non-functional requirements.
Develop models and prototypes to validate proposed designs, secure stakeholder approval, and enable effective implementation.
Recommend and refine designs that consider current and target environments, performance, security, and integration with existing systems. Conduct design reviews, providing constructive feedback to improve quality across the team.
Analyse and document the technical impact of new product features and architectural enhancements, including estimating effort and identifying dependencies.
Contribute to the selection of appropriate software development approaches to meet quality standards, delivery timelines, and risk management objectives.
Apply agreed development standards, tools, and best practices to deliver robust, scalable, and maintainable software solutions.

Essential Skills and Experience:

Demonstrated expertise in software design patterns and coding best practices
AI Assisted Development – tools like GitHub Copilot to write, review, debug code
Prompt Engineering
Experience in SaaS-based full stack development, with proficiency in Java and modern JavaScript frameworks.
Hands-on experience with Spring (or similar backend frameworks).
Experience working with frontend frameworks such as Angular or React.
Proven experience designing and implementing infrastructure-as-code on AWS, including services such as CloudFormation, CDK, load balancers, EC2, S3, Lambda, and container-based runtime environments.
Solid understanding of CI/CD principles and experience with tools such as GitHub, CircleCI, CodePipeline, or Bamboo.
Strong problem-solving and system design capabilities.
Ability to mentor, coach, and effectively share knowledge within the team.
Proven track record of proactive contribution across the Software Development Lifecycle (SDLC).

Desirable Skills and Experience:

Previous experience with monolithic to microservices App migration
Experience with NoSQL/Document/Vector databases
Performance optimizations with data caching and related designs
Have worked in Data pipelines/ETL
Application monitoring with CloudWatch, New Relic
RAG Pipeline Design

Qualifications:

Bachelor of Computer Science degree or equivalent academic qualification

description:
At Metcash, you’re part of something bigger. We’ve got the heart of a small business with the strength and support of a big business. When you join Metcash, your work makes a real difference to independent businesses and local communities, empowering you to:

Grow your career with tailored development programs
Make your mark in a high-performing, values-led team
Thrive your way with flexible work options

About the Opportunity

At Metcash, we’re transforming how IT operates—making it simpler, smarter, and more customer-focused. We’re looking for an IT Optimisation Manager who can improve processes, drive change, and partner with stakeholders to deliver real impact.

In this role, you’ll work closely with IT leaders to identify opportunities to standardise and simplify processes, embed change, and uplift capability. You’ll also act as a trusted advisor, coaching leaders through change initiatives while leading key optimisation projects end-to-end.

In this Role, You Will:

Partner with IT stakeholders and offshore teams to identify and implement process improvements across IT demand, contracts, time sheeting, and controls, including driving AI-led productivity initiatives
Lead IT tools rationalisation and optimisation projects end-to-end, acting as Project Manager to deliver standardisation and efficiency outcomes
Coach leaders and project teams on change management practices, ensuring successful adoption and maintaining the IT Change Heat Map for visibility and alignment
Collaborate with Change & Communications teams and support PI Planning to deliver a consistent, customer-centric IT experience

You’ll bring:

Strong experience in IT environments, with a focus on projects, process improvement, and transformation
Proven ability to lead change initiatives in complex environments
Solid understanding of change management frameworks (Prosci, AMPG or similar preferred)
Confidence to coach, influence, and engage stakeholders at all levels
Exceptional communication and storytelling skills, with the ability to simplify complex ideas
A proactive mindset with strong planning, organisation, and problem-solving skills
Experience in learning, capability uplift, and stakeholder engagement

What’s on Offer

️ Extra Leave: 5th week of annual leave, 2 well-being days, 1 volunteer day
Career Growth: Endless learning and development opportunities
Parental Leave: 12 weeks of gender-neutral paid leave for primary carers
Inclusive Culture: Bronze Employer for LGBTQ Inclusion
 ️ Neutral Pay Gap: Gender pay gap under 5% (WGEA 2024–2025)
Mental Health: Gold accreditation by Mental Health Australia
Flexibility: Flex Ready and Family Friendly Workplace accredited

About Us

Metcash is Australia’s leading wholesale distribution company, with $19.5 billion in sales (FY25) and a proud spot in the ASX Top 125. We believe in the power of independent, family-owned businesses to shape vibrant communities. Through our food, liquor, and hardware pillars, we help retailers become the ‘Best Store in Their Town’ by providing merchandising, operational and marketing support.
We’re committed to diversity, inclusion, and sustainability — and we welcome applicants from all backgrounds, including First Nations. If you need support during the application process, just let us know. Your privacy and dignity are always respected.

Join us and be part of something bigger!

#INFLA

#LI-SS1
descriptionHTML:
At Metcash, you’re part of something bigger. We’ve got the heart of a small business with the strength and support of a big business. When you join Metcash, your work makes a real difference to independent businesses and local communities, empowering you to:

  • Grow your career with tailored development programs
  • Make your mark in a high-performing, values-led team
  • Thrive your way with flexible work options

About the Opportunity

At Metcash, we’re transforming how IT operates—making it simpler, smarter, and more customer-focused. We’re looking for an IT Optimisation Manager who can improve processes, drive change, and partner with stakeholders to deliver real impact.

In this role, you’ll work closely with IT leaders to identify opportunities to standardise and simplify processes, embed change, and uplift capability. You’ll also act as a trusted advisor, coaching leaders through change initiatives while leading key optimisation projects end-to-end.


In this Role, You Will:

  • Partner with IT stakeholders and offshore teams to identify and implement process improvements across IT demand, contracts, time sheeting, and controls, including driving AI-led productivity initiatives
  • Lead IT tools rationalisation and optimisation projects end-to-end, acting as Project Manager to deliver standardisation and efficiency outcomes
  • Coach leaders and project teams on change management practices, ensuring successful adoption and maintaining the IT Change Heat Map for visibility and alignment
  • Collaborate with Change & Communications teams and support PI Planning to deliver a consistent, customer-centric IT experience

You’ll bring:

  • Strong experience in IT environments, with a focus on projects, process improvement, and transformation
  • Proven ability to lead change initiatives in complex environments
  • Solid understanding of change management frameworks (Prosci, AMPG or similar preferred)
  • Confidence to coach, influence, and engage stakeholders at all levels
  • Exceptional communication and storytelling skills, with the ability to simplify complex ideas
  • A proactive mindset with strong planning, organisation, and problem-solving skills
  • Experience in learning, capability uplift, and stakeholder engagement

What’s on Offer

️ Extra Leave: 5th week of annual leave, 2 well-being days, 1 volunteer day
Career Growth: Endless learning and development opportunities
Parental Leave: 12 weeks of gender-neutral paid leave for primary carers
Inclusive Culture: Bronze Employer for LGBTQ Inclusion
  • ️ Neutral Pay Gap: Gender pay gap under 5% (WGEA 2024–2025)
Mental Health: Gold accreditation by Mental Health Australia
Flexibility: Flex Ready and Family Friendly Workplace accredited

About Us

Metcash is Australia’s leading wholesale distribution company, with $19.5 billion in sales (FY25) and a proud spot in the ASX Top 125. We believe in the power of independent, family-owned businesses to shape vibrant communities. Through our food, liquor, and hardware pillars, we help retailers become the ‘Best Store in Their Town’ by providing merchandising, operational and marketing support.
We’re committed to diversity, inclusion, and sustainability — and we welcome applicants from all backgrounds, including First Nations. If you need support during the application process, just let us know. Your privacy and dignity are always respected.


Join us and be part of something bigger!


#INFLA

#LI-SS1

description:
About us:

Spectrum.Life is a whole-of-health digital partner delivering clinically backed digital health, mental health and wellbeing solutions internationally to over 7.2 million insurance members, 3,000 corporate clients, and 650,000 university students. Established in 2018, we employ over 450 people and our vision is simple: to change and save as many lives as possible.

We are not a traditional EAP. We are a clinically led digital health partner building something genuinely different for the Australian market — integrating AI-assisted triage, innovative technology, and a multidisciplinary clinical team united by one mission.

Role Brief:

Why Spectrum.Life

- Clinical depth at scale.
You will work within a stepped care model designed to PHN standards, backed by a clinical governance framework that takes quality seriously. Your clinical decisions matter and are supported.

- Industry-leading tools.
Spectrum.Life integrates AI-assisted triage, digital outcome measurement, and a best-in-class telehealth platform. You will be working at the frontier of what clinically governed digital mental health looks like in practice.

- Real flexibility.
Remote, part-time through to full-time, with a schedule that can flex to your life. We trust our clinicians to work independently and we build our model around that trust.

- Supervision and CPD included.
Individual clinical supervision is provided in-house, alongside access to professional development pathways that support your growth — clinically and professionally.

- Meaningful work.
Every contact matters. Our outcomes data shows it. When you work at Spectrum.Life, you are part of a service that measurably changes lives.

The Role
This is a front-line clinical role within our Band 2 stepped care pathway. As a Client Care Counsellor, you will carry two core responsibilities from the moment a client makes contact:

Triage — rapid, structured clinical assessment to determine the right care pathway at first contact.
Single Session Therapy (SST) — purposeful, evidence-based brief intervention where one well-structured session is complete and sufficient.

You will take calls on demand across a 24/7 service, delivering support to clients when they need it — including evenings, weekends, and public holidays on a rostered basis. A typical shift involves up to 8 consultations, combining on-demand triage contacts and pre-scheduled SST sessions.

You will work fully remotely from anywhere in Australia, supporting individuals experiencing mild to moderate mental health difficulties including anxiety, depression, work-related stress, and adjustment challenges. You will work within a NICE-aligned, clinically governed model with real infrastructure, real supervision, and technology that works with you.

This is not a role for practitioners who need multiple sessions to orient before acting clinically. Every contact requires immediate clinical judgement.

24/7
On-demand service
Calls come in when clients need them, across all days and hours Up to 8
Consultations per day
Scheduled and on-demand contacts across your shift 1–6
Sessions per client
Brief, purposeful intervention. One session can be enough.

Responsibilities:

What You Will Do

Take calls on demand as part of a 24/7 rostered service — responding to clients when they make contact, not when it is convenient. Your shift may include evenings, weekends, or public holiday cover on a rotating roster.
Triage incoming contacts through structured clinical assessment — determining risk level, presenting need, and the right care pathway at first contact. Triage is a clinical act, not an administrative one.
Deliver up to 8 consultations per day across your shift, combining on-demand triage contacts and pre-scheduled Single Session Therapy sessions.
Deliver Single Session Therapy and brief structured counselling (1–6 sessions) via phone, video, and live chat using evidence-based approaches including CBT, Behavioural Activation, Problem-Solving Therapy, and Person-Centred brief models.
Conduct structured mental health assessments, clinical formulations (5-P model), and risk assessments at every contact — including suicidality, self-harm, domestic violence, and safeguarding.
Use validated outcome measures (CORE-10, PHQ-9, GAD-7, K10, ORS, SRS, WSAS) as active clinical tools to guide treatment planning, stepped care routing, and progress review.
Make evidence-based stepped care decisions — stepping clients up, across, or down as clinically indicated, with documented rationale.
Maintain accurate, timely clinical case notes that document reasoning, risk, and clinical decisions promptly after each contact.
Work confidently within AI-integrated digital platforms, maintaining full clinical accountability for all decisions — including those that follow AI-assisted triage.
Connect clients with relevant community supports and referral pathways as a clinical decision, not an afterthought.
Engage actively with clinical supervision, CPD, and team governance processes.

Requirements:

AQF Level 7 minimum — Bachelor of Counselling, Graduate Diploma, accredited Bachelor or qualifying Master of Social Work, or equivalent.
Current full membership with ACA (Level 2+), PACFA (Registered Counsellor or Clinical Member), or AASW (full member) — active, not provisional or lapsed.
Minimum 2 years post-qualification clinical mental health experience (direct client-facing; pre-qualification placements excluded).
Demonstrated competence with brief structured therapeutic models and documented, regular structured risk assessment practice.
Comfortable working across a 24/7 rostered service — including availability for evening, weekend, and public holiday shifts.
Confident delivering via telehealth modalities — phone, video, and live chat.
Digitally confident — comfortable with digital case management, PROMs tools, and AI-assisted platforms.
Accurate, timely clinical documentation completed promptly after each contact.
Active CPD plan and regular clinical supervision engagement.
Working knowledge of mandatory reporting obligations, Privacy Act 1988, and Australian Privacy Principles in a digital health context.
Cultural safety awareness — knowledge of the SEWB framework and culturally safe practice for First Nations peoples and CALD communities.

Desirable:

EAP, PHN-funded, or primary care mental health experience.
Formal SST training (Talmon / Hoyt / Young model) or supervised SST practice.
Solution-Focused Brief Therapy (SFBT) competency.
iCBT or guided self-help delivery experience.
Prior experience with AI-integrated clinical platforms.
Dual PACFA / AASW registration.
Second language relevant to Australian CALD communities.
Experience with children, young people, or workplace mental health.

description:
SailPoint is the leading, AI-driven security platform providing the autonomous governance that best solves the needs for the modern enterprise. SailPoint makes it possible to automate and streamline the identity journey. We marry technical expertise with business acumen to deliver identity security that will drive business forward.

We are proud of our team and the culture we have built which has led to our employees voting us “best places to work” – 15 years in a row.

The role:

SailPoint is seeking a Solution Engineering Manager to support our A/NZ solution engineering team. You will play a critical role in growing SailPoint’s business by building and leading a high performing team of Solution Engineers. Our Solution Engineers play a vital role in demonstrating and communicating the value of SailPoint’s products and thought leadership to our prospective customers in the Identity Governance market.

What you will do:

Hire, coach, and drive an outstanding Solution Engineering team to deliver against assigned goals

Manage individual and team performance ensuring that we are taking the necessary actions to maintain high levels of performance

Demonstrate analytical and problem-solving skills, and strong management consulting skills to influence thinking or gain acceptance of multiple customer constituencies

Work to improve the SailPoint sales methodology and processes effectively, including development of product presentations and demonstrations tailored to our partners requirements and tuned to suit the level and nature of the audience

Assess gaps in evolving training needs and then create and implement programs to improve sales, product and technology skills of the Sales Engineering team

Work with Regional Sales directors on account planning, strategy, and sales call activity

Provide timely and insightful input back to other corporate functions, particularly field sales, product management and marketing

Manage relationships with product teams & decision makers both internally and externally

What we are looking for:

Previous experience, 5+ years as a sales engineering manager and 5+ years as a sales engineer

Excellent communication skills including experience speaking to a technical and business audiences

The ideal candidate will already have a good understanding of the solutions, market dynamics and best practices in the Identity Security market

Path to Success:

Within 30 Days:

Onboarded and met all team members and sales leaders the territory supports.

Within 60 Days:

Familiar with business and top opportunities and established cadences with peers, sales, and team members.

Within 90 Days:

Complete product training and into rhythm with team members, peers, and leadership.

Within 1-Year:

Continue in full partnership with sales leaders and trust of your team while achieving budget for the sales territories supported.

Education:

Preferred, not required: Bachelor’s in Computer Science or global equivalent experience

SailPoint is an equal opportunity employer and we welcome all qualified candidates to apply to join our team. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, national origin, disability, protected veteran status, or any other category protected by applicable law.

Alternative methods of applying for employment are available to individuals unable to submit an application through this site because of a disability. Contact applicationassistance@sailpoint.com or mail to 11120 Four Points Dr, Suite 100, Austin, TX 78726, to discuss reasonable accommodations. NOTE: Any unsolicited resumes sent by candidates or agencies to this email will not be considered for current openings at SailPoint.

description:
Kick‑start your cloud career and help shape the future of Tech!
As a Graduate Cloud Associate, you’ll work alongside experienced Cloud and DevOps professionals to design, build and optimise cloud environments for real customers. You’ll gain hands‑on experience across AWS, Azure, automation tools and modern platforms — all while learning best practices that set you up for a long-term career in cloud engineering.

This role is perfect for curious problem solvers who love technology, enjoy learning new tools, and want to make a real impact from day one.

Our Why

Datacom works with organisations and communities across Australia and New Zealand to make a difference in people’s lives and help organisations use the power of tech to innovate and grow.

What you'll be doing

As part of our cloud team, you’ll:

Support customers to build, migrate and optimise cloud environments using AWS and/or Azure.
Help resolve technical incidents and service requests, investigating issues and learning how to troubleshoot cloud-based systems.
Learn and apply Infrastructure as Code (IaC) practices to automate and standardise deployments.
Assist with cloud migrations, automation and DevOps workflows, including CI/CD pipelines.
Contribute to the creation and maintenance of coding, implementation and design standards.
Participate in planning and delivery of work packages, tracking progress and celebrating wins.
Create and maintain documentation such as solution designs, test plans and support guides.
Get hands-on with automation, orchestration and modern toolchains that power scalable systems.
Collaborate with teammates, mentors and customers to continuously learn and improve.
What we’re looking for

We’re not expecting you to know everything — we’re looking for potential, curiosity and a willingness to learn.

You’ll thrive in this role if you have:

A degree or relevent qualification in IT, Computer Science, Engineering or a related field.
Exposure to cloud platforms such as AWS or Microsoft Azure through study, labs, projects or internships.
A basic understanding of scripting or coding (e.g. PowerShell, Python, or similar).
Familiarity with concepts like DevOps, CI/CD, containers or automation — even at a foundational level.
Interest in Infrastructure as Code tools such as Terraform, Ansible or GitHub.
Curiosity about AI tools and prompt engineering, and how they can enhance productivity.
Strong problem‑solving skills and a proactive mindset.
Good communication skills and the ability to learn collaboratively.

About our Early Careers Programme

As a graduate, you’ll be supported by our dedicated Early Careers Team throughout your first 12 months. We’ll guide you through onboarding, mentoring, events, workshops, and ongoing development opportunities—ensuring you’re set up for success while you settle into your new permanent role.

Why join us here at Datacom?

Datacom is one of Australia and New Zealand’s largest suppliers of Information Technology professional services. We have managed to maintain a dynamic, agile, small business feel that is often diluted in larger organisations of our size. It's our people that give Datacom its unique culture and energy that you can feel from the moment you meet with us.

We care about our people and provide a range of perks such as social events, chill-out spaces, remote working, flexi-hours and professional development courses to name a few. You’ll have the opportunity to learn, develop your career, connect and bring your true self to work. You will be recognised and valued for your contributions and be able to do your work in a collegial, flat-structured environment.

#LI-DNI

Requirements

Australian Citizenship
Currently living in Sydney, Australia
Available full time from May 2026

description:
We power insights for better, safer workplaces…together.

Are you ready to be Kin-at-i-can? As our Client Executive Lead, you’ll own a portfolio of Enterprise accounts, lead a high-performing team, and sit at the intersection of client strategy, product collaboration, and commercial growth. You’ll do it at a company that’s genuinely changing the way organisations manage workforce compliance, and you’ll have the tools, the trust, and the technology to do it well.

If you’re a senior client leader who loves building relationships, driving outcomes, and working in an environment where innovation is part of the everyday. Read on.

About Kinatico

Kinatico is an ASX-listed Reg-Tech company with a product suite built on nearly two decades of trust. Our background screening platform, Kinatico CVCheck, has been in market since 2005 and today is serving over 10,000 organisations, has helped more than 1.6 million individuals meet regulatory requirements, and processes over 900,000 secure verifications every year. That kind of scale and longevity is rare in this space, and it gives our sales team something genuinely compelling to take to market. Alongside Kinatico CVCheck, our newest product Kinatico Compliance is a simple, secure and synchronised platform that gives organisations everything they need to manage their people’s compliance in one centralised place. From automated workflows and real-time credential tracking to proactive alerts and seamless HRIS integration, it’s designed to reduce admin burden and keep organisations audit-ready at every stage of the employee lifecycle. For a sales professional, this means you’re walking into conversations backed by a product with genuine market credibility, a strong reference base, and a clear growth story still being written.

We’re a team of curious, committed people who care about doing good work and making a real difference. We move quickly, think commercially, and back each other.

OUR TEAM’S STRENGTH COMES FROM EVERYONE’S INDIVIDUALITY

What you’ll bring:

You’re a commercially sharp, relationship-led senior professional who knows how to grow accounts, lead teams, and get things done. You’re just as comfortable in a strategic account planning session as you are on a call with a client who needs a trusted voice. You bring:

Significant experience in senior client management, account direction, or client partner roles, ideally within B2B software, SaaS, or technology-enabled services
A proven track record of retaining and growing Enterprise accounts against revenue targets
Demonstrated experience leading, coaching, and developing a high-performing team
Deep knowledge of workforce compliance, HCM software, or adjacent HR technology solutions
Familiarity with structured sales methodologies (Miller Heiman or equivalent) and the discipline to apply them consistently
A data-driven mindset, comfortable using Salesforce, reporting dashboards, and AI-assisted tools to make better decisions and manage your team more effectively
Genuine curiosity about how technology (including AI) can improve client outcomes and team ways of working, without any expectation of being a technical expert
Demonstrated experience working within or alongside compliance, workforce screening, background verification, or regulatory technology environments. You understand the stakes involved when organisations are making trust-critical decisions about their people, and you know how to speak the language of risk, obligation, and assurance with clients
Human resource, recruitment, or workplace safety experience is a bonus

DELIVERING THE GOOD, STRIVING FOR THE GREAT

About the role:

This is a senior commercial leadership role with real scope and real impact. You’ll manage a portfolio of Kinatico’s Enterprise clients, driving retention, growth, and satisfaction, while leading a team of Client Executives and owning the processes and rhythm that keep everything running smoothly.

You’ll also play a meaningful role in how Kinatico evolves. You’ll be the voice of the customer in product conversations, the bridge between clients and internal teams, and a champion for modern, technology-enabled ways of working. AI tools and intelligent insights will be part of how you work, not a stretch goal, just how things get done.

What you’ll be doing:

Own and grow a portfolio of Enterprise accounts, driving retention, expansion, and outstanding client satisfaction
Build trusted, senior-level relationships across your clients’ organisations and act as a genuine strategic advisor
Lead, coach, and develop your team of Client Executives to achieve individual and collective performance goals
Establish and maintain team operating cadences (pipeline reviews, account planning, performance check-ins) so everyone knows where they stand and what’s next
Define and continuously improve the team’s account management processes, building playbooks and frameworks that scale best practice
Use AI-assisted tools and CRM data to strengthen account strategies, improve forecasting, and move from instinct-led to insight-led decisions
Collaborate with Product, Engineering, and Marketing teams to shape solutions that genuinely meet customer needs
Present and demonstrate Kinatico’s solutions, translating complex compliance capabilities into clear, compelling business value
Contribute to roadmap discussions and ensure the voice of your clients is heard in product prioritisation
Represent Kinatico at industry events and contribute to the organisation’s growth story

ARE YOU READY TO BE A KINATICAN?

Where our passion, talent, delivery and impact collide our purpose lies. Powering insights for better, safer workplaces, helping organisations to know their people, we are a global leader in Reg-Tech. Our technology helps organisations to know their people.

Flexible and supportive work environment
A culture of development and recognition, we want our people to thrive!
Attend our Friday Funaticans in person and virtual events
Bright, spacious office in the Perth CBD
Work for an ASX listed company and a leader in Reg-Tech
Join the company at an incredibly exciting time and enter a world of energy, enthusiasm and innovation

Research shows that while men apply to jobs when they meet an average of 60% of the criteria, women and other marginalised individuals tend to only apply when they check every box. If you think you have what it takes (a growth mindset) but don’t necessarily meet every single point on the job description, please still get in touch. We’d love to have a chat and see if you could be a great fit.

Apply Now!
descriptionHTML:
We power insights for better, safer workplaces…together.

Are you ready to be Kin-at-i-can? As our Client Executive Lead, you’ll own a portfolio of Enterprise accounts, lead a high-performing team, and sit at the intersection of client strategy, product collaboration, and commercial growth. You’ll do it at a company that’s genuinely changing the way organisations manage workforce compliance, and you’ll have the tools, the trust, and the technology to do it well.


If you’re a senior client leader who loves building relationships, driving outcomes, and working in an environment where innovation is part of the everyday. Read on.


About Kinatico

Kinatico is an ASX-listed Reg-Tech company with a product suite built on nearly two decades of trust. Our background screening platform, Kinatico CVCheck, has been in market since 2005 and today is serving over 10,000 organisations, has helped more than 1.6 million individuals meet regulatory requirements, and processes over 900,000 secure verifications every year. That kind of scale and longevity is rare in this space, and it gives our sales team something genuinely compelling to take to market. Alongside Kinatico CVCheck, our newest product Kinatico Compliance is a simple, secure and synchronised platform that gives organisations everything they need to manage their people’s compliance in one centralised place. From automated workflows and real-time credential tracking to proactive alerts and seamless HRIS integration, it’s designed to reduce admin burden and keep organisations audit-ready at every stage of the employee lifecycle. For a sales professional, this means you’re walking into conversations backed by a product with genuine market credibility, a strong reference base, and a clear growth story still being written.

We’re a team of curious, committed people who care about doing good work and making a real difference. We move quickly, think commercially, and back each other.


OUR TEAM’S STRENGTH COMES FROM EVERYONE’S INDIVIDUALITY


What you’ll bring:


You’re a commercially sharp, relationship-led senior professional who knows how to grow accounts, lead teams, and get things done. You’re just as comfortable in a strategic account planning session as you are on a call with a client who needs a trusted voice. You bring:


  • Significant experience in senior client management, account direction, or client partner roles, ideally within B2B software, SaaS, or technology-enabled services
  • A proven track record of retaining and growing Enterprise accounts against revenue targets
  • Demonstrated experience leading, coaching, and developing a high-performing team
  • Deep knowledge of workforce compliance, HCM software, or adjacent HR technology solutions
  • Familiarity with structured sales methodologies (Miller Heiman or equivalent) and the discipline to apply them consistently
  • A data-driven mindset, comfortable using Salesforce, reporting dashboards, and AI-assisted tools to make better decisions and manage your team more effectively
  • Genuine curiosity about how technology (including AI) can improve client outcomes and team ways of working, without any expectation of being a technical expert
  • Demonstrated experience working within or alongside compliance, workforce screening, background verification, or regulatory technology environments. You understand the stakes involved when organisations are making trust-critical decisions about their people, and you know how to speak the language of risk, obligation, and assurance with clients
  • Human resource, recruitment, or workplace safety experience is a bonus

DELIVERING THE GOOD, STRIVING FOR THE GREAT

About the role:

This is a senior commercial leadership role with real scope and real impact. You’ll manage a portfolio of Kinatico’s Enterprise clients, driving retention, growth, and satisfaction, while leading a team of Client Executives and owning the processes and rhythm that keep everything running smoothly.


You’ll also play a meaningful role in how Kinatico evolves. You’ll be the voice of the customer in product conversations, the bridge between clients and internal teams, and a champion for modern, technology-enabled ways of working. AI tools and intelligent insights will be part of how you work, not a stretch goal, just how things get done.


What you’ll be doing:

  • Own and grow a portfolio of Enterprise accounts, driving retention, expansion, and outstanding client satisfaction
  • Build trusted, senior-level relationships across your clients’ organisations and act as a genuine strategic advisor
  • Lead, coach, and develop your team of Client Executives to achieve individual and collective performance goals
  • Establish and maintain team operating cadences (pipeline reviews, account planning, performance check-ins) so everyone knows where they stand and what’s next
  • Define and continuously improve the team’s account management processes, building playbooks and frameworks that scale best practice
  • Use AI-assisted tools and CRM data to strengthen account strategies, improve forecasting, and move from instinct-led to insight-led decisions
  • Collaborate with Product, Engineering, and Marketing teams to shape solutions that genuinely meet customer needs
  • Present and demonstrate Kinatico’s solutions, translating complex compliance capabilities into clear, compelling business value
  • Contribute to roadmap discussions and ensure the voice of your clients is heard in product prioritisation
  • Represent Kinatico at industry events and contribute to the organisation’s growth story

ARE YOU READY TO BE A KINATICAN?

Where our passion, talent, delivery and impact collide our purpose lies. Powering insights for better, safer workplaces, helping organisations to know their people, we are a global leader in Reg-Tech. Our technology helps organisations to know their people.

  • Flexible and supportive work environment
  • A culture of development and recognition, we want our people to thrive!
  • Attend our Friday Funaticans in person and virtual events
  • Bright, spacious office in the Perth CBD
  • Work for an ASX listed company and a leader in Reg-Tech
  • Join the company at an incredibly exciting time and enter a world of energy, enthusiasm and innovation

Research shows that while men apply to jobs when they meet an average of 60% of the criteria, women and other marginalised individuals tend to only apply when they check every box. If you think you have what it takes (a growth mindset) but don’t necessarily meet every single point on the job description, please still get in touch. We’d love to have a chat and see if you could be a great fit.


Apply Now!

description:
*eCommerce Marketing & Content Specialist *

*Part-Time 20 - 25 hrs p/week | 2 positions to fill*

Unboxed Talent is proud to be partnering with an amazing Australian entrepreneur. An owner operator of 2 fast-growing eCommerce brands. The success of both brands has been built, not just by running ads but by building brands that grow through the right content. Each brand has a clear identity, strong community, and serious growth potential.

The 2 brands are truly amazing: Beautiful personalised handmade bags and accessories and one of Australia's leading footcare brands.

Fast growth means it's now time to invest in 2 x eCommerce and Content Specialists to be a key factor in taking the business to its next level. The success of this business is a result of posting not just good, but great content. Not stuff where it's a hope for the best approach. They test, learn and scale what works. And it is working. If you are someone who loves turning ideas into content and content into revenue, keep reading.

*What you'll be doing!*

* Plan, launch, and optimise Meta ad campaigns (this is a core focus)
* Create high-performing short-form content (Reels, ads, UGC-style)
* Manage and grow social media / community across both brands
* Write and execute EDM campaigns via Klaviyo
* Continuously test hooks, creatives, and angles - this business moves fast!
* Use AI tools to generate ideas and improve performance
* Schedule and manage content through Planoly
* Support Shopify operations and product launches
* Work closely with the founder to execute and scale winning ideas

*What You'll Bring*

* Experience with Meta Ads - you understand testing, structure, and scaling
* Strong content instincts - you know what makes people stop scrolling
* Comfortable creating and editing video (CapCut, Canva, etc.)
* Experience with EDMs (Klaviyo is a bonus)
* A self-starter - confident taking ownership of projects and figuring things out!
* Data-driven - you optimise based on results, not guesswork
* Comfortable using AI tools (ChatGPT, Claude, etc.)

*It's a bonus if you:*

* Already use AI daily in your workflow
* Have previously worked with eCommerce or product-based brands
* Have created ads or content that has actually performed

*Why This Role Is Different*

* You’ll work directly with the founder, no layers, no slow approvals
* Your ideas actually get tested and executed
* You’ll work across two brands, not just one!
* Ongoing support and training in Meta Ads
* Fast-moving environment where you’ll grow quickly
* Permanent part-time role (20-25 hrs/week)

*IMPORTANT*

This is *not* a social media manager role.

We’re looking for someone who wants to test, learn, and improve constantly and cares about performance, not just posting.

*Apply ... *Send your resume, including any links to content, ads or work you've created to *helen@unboxedtalent.com.au*

Pay: $70,000.00 – $75,000.00 per year

Benefits:
* Employee discount
* Employee mentoring program
* Professional development assistance
* Work from home

Work Location: Hybrid remote in Inner West NSW

description:
CHEP helps move more goods to more people, in more places than any other organization on earth via our 347 million pallets, crates and containers. We employ approximately 13,000 people and operate in 60 countries. Through our pioneering and sustainable share-and-reuse business model, the world’s biggest brands trust us to help them transport their goods more efficiently, safely and with less environmental impact.

What does that mean for you? You’ll join an international organization big enough to take you anywhere, and small enough to get you there sooner. You’ll help change how goods get to market and contribute to global sustainability. You’ll be empowered to bring your authentic self to work and be surrounded by diverse and driven professionals. And you can maximize your work-life balance and flexibility through our Hybrid Work Model .

Job Description

At CHEP , we help move essential goods, reduce waste and make supply chains more resilient and sustainable. As part of Brambles , we operate at global scale — but it’s the decisions our people make every day that create real-world impact.

We’re now looking for a Manager, Talent Acquisition – Supply Chain to lead the end-to-end hiring strategy for our Supply Chain workforce across Australia.

This is a hands-on leadership role where you’ll shape how we attract, assess and hire talent across executive, professional and frontline roles — while building a future-ready, inclusive workforce that underpins business performance. As part of a global HR function, you will also have the opportunity to contribute to global best practice through HR programs of work

Your impact in this role

You’ll be a trusted partner to Supply Chain and HR leaders, owning the Talent Acquisition strategy and translating workforce plans into scalable, high-quality hiring outcomes. This is an opportunity to build the function embedding automation and building best practice maturity for hiring across our network.

What you'll expect:

Lead end-to-end Talent Acquisition, guiding a small team of talent professionals across the Supply Chain function, balancing strategic leadership with hands-on delivery.

Act as a strategic advisor to senior leaders on talent risk, succession, internal mobility and workforce sustainability.

Embed DEI, EVP activation and workforce planning into hiring strategies to create sustainable pipelines.

Drive operational excellence, governance and compliance across high-volume, professional and executive hiring.

Leverage Workday ATS, AI-enabled tools and talent analytics to improve speed, quality and candidate experience, moving from manual to automated discipline across our sites.

Contributing to the AMETA TA Leadership Team, sharing insights and shaping regional and global TA capability.

What you’ll bring

Proven experience leading Talent Acquisition in an operations frontline environment, ideally supply chain, logistics, manufacturing, industrial or similar.

Strong capability translating business strategy into scalable, agile hiring models and influencing leader mindset within an established business to embed change.

Experience leading recruitment transformation, including ATS optimisation, AI tools and automation, through demonstrated curiosity and root cause problem solving.

A track record of EVP activation, campaign hiring and DEI-led approaches in competitive labour markets.

Confidence operating as a trusted advisor to senior leaders in complex, fast-paced environments.

Demonstrated ability to build engaged, resilient TA teams.

Why join CHEP / Brambles

Be part of a global organisation with real-world purpose.

Work in a culture grounded in trust, accountability and inclusion.

Access strong career development and internal mobility opportunities.

Shape strategic outcomes while staying close to delivery and impact.

Remote Type

Hybrid Remote

Skills to succeed in the role

Coaching, Collaboration, Compliance and Adaptability, Data-Driven Insights, Disruptive Thinking, Diversity and Inclusion in Hiring, Employer Branding, Feedback, Inclusive Leadership, Leading Change, Leading Customer Centric Teams, Mentorship, Motivating Teams, Prioritization, Project Delivery, Recruitment Technology, Relationship Management, Self-Awareness, Talent Sourcing and Acquisition, Vendor Management

We are an Equal Opportunity Employer, and we are committed to developing a diverse workforce in which everyone is treated fairly, with respect, and has the opportunity to contribute to business success while realizing his or her potential. This means harnessing the unique skills and experience that each individual brings and we do not discriminate against any employee or applicant for employment because of race, color, sex, age, national origin, religion, sexual orientation, gender identity, status as a veteran, and basis of disability or any other federal, state, or local protected class.

Individuals fraudulently misrepresenting themselves as Brambles or CHEP representatives have scheduled interviews and offered fraudulent employment opportunities with the intent to commit identity theft or solicit money. Brambles and CHEP never conduct interviews via online chat or request money as a term of employment. If you have a question as to the legitimacy of an interview or job offer, please contact us at recruitment@brambles.com.

description:
About Zscaler

Zscaler accelerates digital transformation to ensure our customers can be more agile, efficient, resilient, and secure. As an AI-forward enterprise, we are constantly pushing the envelope, leveraging the world's largest security data lake to power our cloud-native Zero Trust Exchange platform. This innovation protects our customers from cyberattacks and data loss by securely connecting users, devices, and applications in any location.

Here, impact in your role matters more than title and trust is built on results. We say, impact over activity. We seek innovators who actively use AI to amplify their impact and who thrive in an environment where we leverage intelligent systems to stay ahead of evolving threats. We believe in transparency and value constructive, honest debate—we're focused on getting to the best ideas, faster. We build high-performing teams that can make an impact quickly and with high quality. To do this, we are building a culture of execution centered on customer obsession, collaboration, ownership, and accountability.

We value high-impact, high-accountability with a sense of urgency where you're enabled to do your best work and embrace your potential. If you're driven by purpose, thrive on solving complex challenges, and want to be part of the team that's helping to secure the AI age, we invite you to bring your talents to Zscaler and help shape the future of cybersecurity.

Role

We are looking for a Senior Partner Business Manager to join our Global Partner Ecosystem department. This is a hybrid role based in Melbourne or Canberra (requiring 3 days a week in the office), reporting to the Senior Director, Partner Sales. Your core purpose is to build and scale high-performing partner relationships by aligning joint strategy, enablement, and go-to-market execution to drive mutual revenue growth. You will drive healthy partner engagement measured through pipeline, faster deal velocity, service creation, expanded market coverage, and improved customer outcomes.

What you'll do (Role Expectations)

Drive joint regional GTM sales execution in lockstep with Sales Directors
Develop and lead the regional partner strategy and long-term growth plan
Spearhead partner activation through enablement, sales engagement, and pipeline development
Own partner performance management and maintain rigorous forecast governance

Who You Are (Success Profile)

You act like an owner. Your passion for the mission fuels your bias for action. You operate with integrity because you genuinely care about the outcome. You adapt to what's needed, navigating seamlessly between high-level strategy and hands-on execution.
You operate with urgency. You understand that in a high-growth environment, speed and quality are not mutually exclusive. You have a relentless focus on execution and a bias for action, delivering high-impact results quickly to win for the customer and the team.
You think at scale. You connect your day-to-day work to the larger company mission and think globally. You build solutions, processes, and teams that are not just effective today but are built to last and support a high-growth, global organization.
You are resilient and adaptable. You view change as an opportunity and setbacks as temporary. You maintain composure and focus in high-pressure situations, guiding yourself and your team through complexity with a steady, positive hand.
You lead with integrity. You do the right thing, even when it's hard. You hold yourself and others to a high standard of accountability and build trust by matching your words with consistent, transparent action.

What We're Looking for (Minimum Qualifications)

10+ years in partner/channel sales, alliances, or regional partner management
Proven track record building joint GTM plans with regional sales leadership and executing co-sell motions such as account mapping and deal reviews
Experience managing strategic partners—including GSIs, RSIs, VARs, distributors, MSSPs, and ISVs—across recruitment, activation, and performance management
Deep expertise in network, cyber, and cloud security solutions
Willingness to travel within the region as required to support partners and the sales team

What Will Make You Stand Out (Preferred Qualifications)

Direct experience within Public Sector and Education industry verticals
Professional background in partner services capability development
Strong executive communication skills with a proven ability to engage and influence senior partner leadership

#LI-Hybrid

#LI-NL1

At Zscaler, we are committed to building a team that reflects the communities we serve and the customers we work with. We foster an inclusive environment that values all backgrounds and perspectives, emphasizing collaboration and belonging. Join us in our mission to make doing business seamless and secure.

Our Benefits program is one of the most important ways we support our employees. Zscaler proudly offers comprehensive and inclusive benefits to meet the diverse needs of our employees and their families throughout their life stages, including:

Various health plans
Time off plans for vacation and sick time
Parental leave options
Retirement options
Education reimbursement
In-office perks, and more!

Learn more about Zscaler's Future of Work strategy, hybrid working model, and benefits here.

By applying for this role, you adhere to applicable laws, regulations, and Zscaler policies, including those related to security and privacy standards and guidelines.

Zscaler is committed to providing equal employment opportunities to all individuals. We strive to create a workplace where employees are treated with respect and have the chance to succeed. All qualified applicants will be considered for employment without regard to race, color, religion, sex (including pregnancy or related medical conditions), age, national origin, sexual orientation, gender identity or expression, genetic information, disability status, protected veteran status, or any other characteristic protected by federal, state, or local laws. See more information by clicking on the Know Your Rights: Workplace Discrimination is Illegal link.

Pay Transparency

Zscaler complies with all applicable federal, state, and local pay transparency rules.

Zscaler is committed to providing reasonable support (called accommodations or adjustments) in our recruiting processes for candidates who are differently abled, have long term conditions, mental health conditions or sincerely held religious beliefs, or who are neurodivergent or require pregnancy-related support.

description:
Neuroscience Specialist - AI Training

About Prolific

Prolific is not just another player in the AI space – we are building the biggest pool of quality human data in the world.

Over 35,000 AI developers, researchers, and organizations use Prolific to gather data from paid study participants with a wide variety of experiences, knowledge, and skills.

The role

We are seeking Neuroscience Experts to act as evaluators for a high-level AI verification project. AI models are now generating complex technical summaries, and we need specialists to ensure these outputs maintain scientific integrity and factual accuracy.

We need experts who can navigate dense scientific literature to judge the accuracy and structural integrity of AI-generated graphical abstracts. This is a flexible, ad hoc project paying $40+/h where your technical expertise directly influences how AI understands and summarizes complex research.

What you'll bring

Specialized knowledge: Deep expertise in Neuroscience

Advanced Academic Credentials: you must hold a Master's or PhD in Neuroscience or a related field.

Analytical Skills: history with fact-checking tasks and strong attention to detail

Nice to have: Previous experience with AI-related training or evaluation tasks and a background in scientific publishing or peer review.

What you'll be doing in the role
Scientific Review: review scientific papers alongside LLM-generated graphical abstracts.
Fact-Checking: identify issues and inaccuracies where the graphical abstract does not faithfully represent the source scientific paper.
Expert Verification: use your domain knowledge to ensure that technical concepts are visually and textually summarized correctly.
Please note that to qualify for this study you will be required to pass a paid skills assessment with a sample paper and graphical abstract
Why Prolific is a great platform to join as a Participant

Joining our platform as a Prolific participant will give you the chance to influence the AI models of the future using professional legal expertise. Once you pass our assessment, you can join Prolific in just 15 minutes, and start enjoying competitive pay rates, flexible hours, and the ability to work from home.

We've built a unique platform that connects researchers and companies with a global pool of participants, enabling the collection of high-quality, ethically sourced human behavioural data and feedback. This data is the cornerstone of developing more accurate, nuanced, and aligned AI systems.

We believe that the next leap in AI capabilities won't come solely from scaling existing models, but from integrating diverse human perspectives and behaviours into AI development. By providing this crucial human data infrastructure, Prolific is positioning itself at the forefront of the next wave of AI innovation – one that reflects the breadth and the best of humanity.

Links to more information on Prolific

Website

Youtube

Privacy Statement

By submitting your application, you agree that Prolific may collect your personal data for recruiting and global organisation planning. Prolific's Candidate Privacy Notice explains what personal information Prolific may process, where Prolific may process your personal information, its purposes for processing your personal information, and the rights you can exercise over Prolific use of your personal personal information.

description:
Data#3 Limited is an Australian IT services and solutions company and headquartered in Brisbane, Queensland. The company provides a wide range of technology services including cloud solutions, cybersecurity, software licensing, hardware procurement, managed IT services, and consulting for government, education, and enterprise clients.

*Senior Data Scientist -Job Description (Brisbane)*

Across companies like Suncorp Group, Queensland Rail, and government roles, the job is very similar.

*Core responsibilities*

Turn business problems into data solutions

Work with stakeholders to define problems and translate them into data questions

Build models & advanced analytics

*Machine learning, AI, predictive modelling*

Forecasting (e.g. customer behaviour, asset performance)

*Data preparation*

Clean, merge, and transform large datasets from multiple sources

*Communicate insights*

Dashboards, reports, presentations for non-technical teams

*Lead & mentor*

Guide junior data scientists and influence best practices

*Deploy solutions*

Build and operationalise ML pipelines in real systems.

Pay: $10,000.00 – $13,000.00 per month

Benefits:
* Childcare assistance
* Health insurance
* Life insurance
* Parental leave
* Vision insurance

Work Location: In person
