  • You’ll create and lead a dynamic, child-centered program, filled with exciting activities like STEM, sports, cooking, arts and crafts—all designed to inspire and engage.

  • You’ll take full ownership of your team’s roster, manage day-to-day operations, ensure compliance, and handle admin tasks—all while fostering an environment of teamwork and growth.

With mentorship from regional managers and support from a dedicated office team, you’ll have everything you need to develop your leadership skills and make a real impact.


Qualifications


Who we are looking for?

If you have a passion for supporting children, we’d love to hear from you! Here’s what we’re looking for:

✔️ A natural leader who enjoys guiding a team and helping others grow.
✔️ Strong communication skills—you’ll be leading by example!
✔️ A team player who inspires others and encourages growth.
✔️ You have (or are willing to obtain) a National Police Check and Working with Children Check.
✔️ Australian work rights.
✔️ A relevant qualification (or working towards it) as per ACECQA.

✔ National Child Safety Training completion prior to onboarding


Additional Information


Next Steps?

We do things a bit differently here at JAG. After submitting your application, you’ll be invited to complete a quick 10-15 min chat-based interview. It’s part of our innovative and cutting-edge process to find the best people to work with us at JAG. It also gives you a chance to showcase your superior talents that aren’t easily captured on paper. Once you complete, we can move you through to the next stage. Since it’s such a valuable tool for career development, we will send you an overview of the results for your own benefit.

Click ‘Apply’ and start your AI-powered chat today!

#junioradventuresgroup

At Junior Adventures Group, child safety and child safeguarding principles are our top priorities. Our recruitment process includes thorough background checks and screenings to ensure the wellbeing of all children in our care.

We welcome individuals from all backgrounds, abilities, and identities, including Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander people.

description:
Company Description

For a winning team that is evolving. Forward with Cuscal.

At Cuscal, you’ll find a strong, successful company that’s reimagining the future. And our team is right there at the heart of it all. Here, you’ll deliver or support interesting, ground-breaking projects that have real impact - on Australia’s financial services sector and the millions of customers it serves. You’ll innovate alongside skilled, smart, connected teams. And you’ll build an impressive, fulfilling career that continues to grow. As the largest independent payment solution providers, we’ve set the standard for over 50 years. Now, we’re preparing to pioneer the next 50.

Job Description

What is this role about?

The Quality Assurance Automation Engineer is responsible for ensuring the reliability, security, and efficiency of our systems and applications by:

• Overseeing the entire quality assurance process, from planning and strategizing to executing and maintaining test protocols.

• Identifying and mitigating potential risks but also ensuring that all software products comply with regulatory standards and industry best practices.

• By implementing testing methodologies and automated solutions.

• Fostering a culture of quality and continuous improvement within the development team. This involves mentoring, collaborating with cross-functional teams, and driving the adoption of advanced testing techniques and tools.

• Agile methodologies and test automation.

• Understanding of financial processes allows them to design and execute tests.

• Delivering reliable, high-quality financial services and products to its customers.

Qualifications

What can you bring?

5+ years of experience in quality assurance and automation testing, with a strong emphasis on software development lifecycle (SDLC) processes and methodologies.
Experience with Python, SQL and Pyspark.
Experience with Git (Bitbucket, Github etc.) and CI/CD tools and pipelines (e.g. Bamboo, Github Actions etc.).
Experience using AI tools

Although not required, any experience in the following would be considered highly desirable:

Knowledge of PCI/DSS compliance standards
Knowledge of AS2805 / ISO8353 standards
Payment industry knowledge
Databricks
SAP BODs

Additional Information

Why Cuscal?
We are in the rapidly evolving world of payments, and we are committed to providing a diverse and inclusive workplace where the very best talent in Australia chooses to work. We support our colleagues with flexible work arrangements through our hybrid model whilst also offering a wide range of educational, financial, lifestyle, health & wellbeing benefits.

Next Step
If you think this role is the right fit for you, we invite you to apply. Let’s explore who you are and what drives you. We’d love to share our vision for the future of payments sector. Please note candidate screening and interviews may be conducted prior to the closing date of the job advert.

Cuscal does not accept unsolicited resumes from recruitment agencies and search firms. Please do not email or send unsolicited resumes to any Cuscal employee, location or address.

positionName: Senior Software Engineer, AI-Assisted Full Stack Development
description:
Forwood Safety is on a mission to make every shift safer and every workplace fatality-free. We build enterprise SaaS solutions that help organisations identify, manage and eliminate critical risks before serious incidents occur. Our engineering team embraces modern, AI-assisted development workflows to deliver high-quality software faster without compromising safety, security, or reliability.

As a Senior Software Engineer, you will help design, build, and maintain high-impact front-end and back-end systems while using AI co-pilot tooling as a core part of your daily workflow. You will own end-to-end feature delivery across our React/Remix + TypeScript front end and NestJS/AWS microservices back end, while helping shape how AI is used across the engineering team to improve velocity, quality, and consistency.

This is a 100% remote role based in Australia, working closely with teams in AU time zones.

Main Responsibilities:

Design, build, and ship high-impact features across React/Remix front-end applications and NestJS/Node.js back-end services.
Develop and maintain secure, scalable REST and GraphQL APIs and microservices deployed on AWS.
Own features end-to-end, from design and implementation through to production release and ongoing optimisation.
Use AI co-pilot tools (e.g. Cursor, GitHub Copilot, Windsurf) for code generation, refactoring, test creation, and documentation.
Develop and share effective prompt patterns and AI best practices to improve team productivity and code quality.
Validate and secure AI-assisted code through testing, reviews, and static analysis.
Write and maintain unit, integration, and end-to-end tests (Jest, React Testing Library, Playwright/Cypress).
Contribute to CI/CD pipelines with automated quality gates, security scanning, and rollback strategies.
Mentor engineers on strong engineering fundamentals and effective AI-driven development workflows.
Stay current with emerging AI tools and identify opportunities to improve delivery through AI-enabled solutions.

Requirements:

5–7+ years of professional software engineering experience, with strong full-stack capability.
Advanced experience with React, TypeScript, and modern frameworks such as Remix or Vite.
Strong back-end experience with Node.js/NestJS and microservice architectures.
Hands-on, daily use of AI co-pilot tools (Cursor, GitHub Copilot, Windsurf or similar). Proven ability to validate, test, and secure AI-assisted code.
Solid experience with AWS (ECS, Lambda, RDS, S3, IAM, CloudWatch).
Experience building and maintaining CI/CD pipelines (GitHub Actions, Bitbucket Pipelines).
A production mindset with a strong focus on maintainability, security, and scalability.
Excellent communication skills and a genuine interest in mentoring others.
Based in Australia and able to collaborate effectively across AU time zones.

If you’re excited to combine solid engineering practices with AI-assisted workflows, and to use that capability to help prevent workplace fatalities, we’d love to hear from you.

This role is 100% remote but must be based in Australia.

description:
Systems‑savvy. Solutions‑focused. Obsessed with using technology to support teams to work better.

If that sounds like you, this could be your next move.

Due to continued growth across our Recruitment, HR Consulting and Learning and Development teams, Allan Hall HR is looking for a proactive and highly organised Operations Coordinator to join our Operations Team. This is a hands‑on role where you’ll help improve efficiency, support our teams, and enhance both employee and client experience across the business.

This role is open to early‑career candidates, as well as those with more experience who enjoy being close to the detail and making things run smoothly. In this role, you will be trusted to take ownership, spot opportunities for improvement, and help shape how we work.

What You’ll Be Doing

Working closely with senior team members, you will support the smooth running of the business as well as future business success and innovation. Your role will include:

Providing day‑to‑day support across business systems including JobAdder, HubSpot, WorkflowMax, Employment Hero, Aircall and Xero.
Troubleshooting system and process issues and working with our external IT provider to resolve technical matters.
Supporting office operations, including meeting rooms, technology and facilities.
Recording, documenting and maintaining business systems, processes and workflows.
Managing data cleansing and coordinating reporting and insights across out teams.
Identifying inefficiencies and recommending automations and improvements to systems, processes and tools.
Researching new technologies and best‑practice approaches to support business performance and improvement.
Coordinating onboarding and offboarding across tools and systems.

You will be supported and trained - curiosity, willingness to learn and attention to detail matter more than knowing everything on day one.

What You’ll Bring

You’ll thrive in this role if you’re organised, curious and enjoy working behind the scenes to make teams more effective. You’ll bring:

Previous experience in an office, admin, operations or professional services environment with a strong interest in systems and business support.
Confidence across systems and applications, in particular all Microsoft 365 tools.
Experience or aptitude with AI enabled tools (eg, HubSpot, Copilot / ChatGPT, JobAdder, Power BI).
A high level of attention to detail and a natural ability to spot issues and improvement opportunities.
A solutions‑focused, proactive and positive approach.
The ability to juggle multiple tasks and priorities.
A collaborative, team-first approach and clear communication skills.
Full Australian working rights (sponsorship is not available for this role).

Why Join Us?

Allan Hall HR is a people‑first, established HR consulting firm based on Sydney’s Northern Beaches. We partner with a broad and growing client base to deliver practical, commercially focused and tailored people solutions, underpinned by a strong reputation and a highly collaborative team culture.

What you can expect:

A newly created role with genuine scope to add value, create efficiencies and automations and shape to how the business operates.
Training, support and mentoring from experienced leaders.
Opportunities to develop your skills in operations, systems and continuous improvement.
A collaborative, professional environment where initiative and ideas are encouraged.

Let’s Talk

If you’re looking for a role where you can make a meaningful operational impact and grow your career in a supportive, high‑performing team, we’d love to hear from you.

Apply now with your resume and a brief cover note outlining your interest in the role

Company Overview

Please visit our company website for more information

description:
Amaya AI is a fast-growing SaaS Product that helps businesses better understand and optimise their sales and customer conversations using AI-driven insights.

We are currently expanding our remote sales team and are hiring *Sales Closers* to handle qualified meetings and convert them into paying customers.

This is a *fully remote, commission-based role* suited to individuals who are confident communicating with others, experienced in sales conversations (or eager to develop this skill), and motivated by performance. The role involves running sales calls with business owners, understanding their needs, presenting the product, handling objections, and closing deals.

We’re looking for people who are consistent, coachable, and enjoy working in a structured, fast-moving environment where performance and results are clearly measured.

*Key Responsibilities*

* Run sales calls with qualified leads (booked meetings provided or self-generated depending on structure)
* Speak with business owners and decision-makers
* Understand customer needs and present Amaya AI effectively
* Handle objections and guide prospects through the decision-making process
* Close deals and convert opportunities into customers
* Maintain accurate notes and update CRM / tracking systems

*Requirements*

* Strong communication and persuasion skills
* Confidence running sales conversations with decision-makers
* Ability to handle objections professionally
* Self-motivated and able to work independently
* Consistent work ethic and willingness to learn
* Previous sales experience (B2B, closing, SDR, or call-based roles) is beneficial but not required

*Role Expectations*

* Attend and run sales calls consistently
* Follow up on leads when required
* Maintain organised pipeline and notes
* Focus on closing qualified opportunities
* Maintain high standards of communication and professionalism

*Compensation*

* Commission-based role
* Earn per deal closed (approx. *25% commission*)
* Uncapped earning potential based on performance

*This Role May Not Be Suitable If You:*

* Prefer a fixed salary or hourly structure
* Are not comfortable running sales calls
* Struggle with rejection or objection handling
* Require constant supervision or structure

*What We Offer*

* Fully remote work environment
* Flexible schedule
* Opportunity to work in a fast-growing SaaS environment
* High earning potential based on performance
* Clear progression opportunities within the sales team

*Application Process*

Shortlisted candidates will be contacted and asked to complete a short screening task, including a brief voice note to assess communication and sales ability.

Only candidates who complete this step will be considered.

*Additional Information*

Start Date: Immediate

Job Type: Contract
Contract length: 800 months

Pay: $110,000.00 – $200,000.00 per year

Work Location: Remote

description:
Talent Acquisition & HR Specialist — Remote

Full-time | 100% Remote | Play a key role for a $3.3B Global AutoTech Unicorn | 3-4 days per week (up to 30 hours)

About Cars24 Australia

Cars24 is one of the world's largest AutoTech companies — and in Australia, we've built the country's largest online pre-loved car dealership. We're changing how Australians buy cars: fully online, transparent, and backed by a world-first 30-day money-back guarantee.

Behind that experience is a fast-scaling team across sales, operations, customer experience and refurbishment — and we're growing fast.

This role sits inside that growth story. You'll work remotely, directly under the Cars24 Australia People & Culture Head, supporting hiring and HR admin across the entire Australia business.

Why This Role

This is not a typical recruiter role. You won't be one of dozens working in a large agency or shared services team. You'll be working directly with the Australia HR lead, senior hiring managers, and executives across both Australia and India — getting real visibility into how a high-growth global business builds its teams.

If you're sharp, fast, and genuinely enjoy speaking to people, this role offers something most remote recruitment roles don't: direct exposure to a global business, a clear pathway into broader HR and People functions, and the kind of ownership that accelerates a career 100x.

What You'll Do

Talent Acquisition & Candidate Experience

Manage end-to-end recruitment across multiple role types — sales, operations, customer experience, refurbishment, tech and corporate
Conduct structured phone screenings with Australian candidates — speed and quality both matter
Represent Cars24 Australia as a compelling, high-performance place to work
Coordinate interview scheduling with hiring managers and ensure a smooth, professional candidate journey throughout

Stakeholder Management

Liaise with hiring managers across Australia and India to understand role requirements and gather timely feedback
Drive next steps confidently — progression, rejection, offer coordination
Maintain clear and proactive communication with all parties at every stage

Recruitment Operations

Post roles across SEEK, LinkedIn and Employment Hero ATS
Keep candidate pipelines organised and data accurate at all times
Issue employment contracts, support onboarding coordination and assist with offboarding and employment changes
Maintain accurate records across HR systems

HR Exposure & Growth

Support broader People & Culture operations including onboarding documentation and HR administration
Learn directly from experienced HR leaders and gain exposure to Australian employment frameworks
Grow into broader HR, Talent Lead or People Operations responsibilities over time

✅ What You Bring

2-3+ years of experience in talent acquisition, recruitment coordination or HR
Exceptional spoken and written English — this is an Australian-facing role and non-negotiable
Experience working with international teams or in fully English-speaking professional environments
Hands-on experience with ATS platforms — Employment Hero or Workable strongly preferred
Confidence using AI tools including Claude, ChatGPT or Gemini as part of your daily workflow
Strong organisational skills and the ability to manage multiple open roles simultaneously
High ownership mindset — you follow through, close loops, and don't let candidates sit in limbo
Degree or qualification in Human Resources, Business Administration or a related field

What Makes This Different

Direct exposure to the Australia business of a $3.3B global AutoTech unicorn
Work closely with founders, executives and senior leaders across two countries
Real ownership — not just coordination, but genuine impact on candidate experience and hiring outcomes
Clear pathway into HR, Talent Lead or broader People Operations roles
Fully remote and flexible — built for someone who works well independently

The Kind of Person Who Will Thrive Here

You enjoy speaking to people and you're good at it. You move quickly and take pride in a strong candidate experience. You want more than task management — you want career exposure, global context, and the opportunity to grow fast inside a business that's scaling.

Apply Now

If you're looking for a remote role with real responsibility, global exposure and a genuine pathway forward — we'd like to hear from you.

Company Overview

At Cars24, our mission is simple: to make buying and selling cars easy, transparent and fully online. We exist to remove the friction, uncertainty and inefficiency that have traditionally defined the car industry. We value ownership, speed and customer obsession. We back data over opinion, action over perfection, and we hold ourselves accountable to high standards. Our culture is fast-paced and performance-driven, but grounded in trust and collaboration. We encourage people to think like builders, challenge assumptions and improve the system, not just operate within it. At our core, we are here to simplify one of life’s bigger purchases and give customers more control and confidence at every step of the journey.

  • Degree or qualification in Human Resources, Business Administration or a related field

What Makes This Different

  • Direct exposure to the Australia business of a $3.3B global AutoTech unicorn
  • Work closely with founders, executives and senior leaders across two countries
  • Real ownership — not just coordination, but genuine impact on candidate experience and hiring outcomes
  • Clear pathway into HR, Talent Lead or broader People Operations roles
  • Fully remote and flexible — built for someone who works well independently

The Kind of Person Who Will Thrive Here

You enjoy speaking to people and you're good at it. You move quickly and take pride in a strong candidate experience. You want more than task management — you want career exposure, global context, and the opportunity to grow fast inside a business that's scaling.

Apply Now

If you're looking for a remote role with real responsibility, global exposure and a genuine pathway forward — we'd like to hear from you.

Company Overview

At Cars24, our mission is simple: to make buying and selling cars easy, transparent and fully online. We exist to remove the friction, uncertainty and inefficiency that have traditionally defined the car industry. We value ownership, speed and customer obsession. We back data over opinion, action over perfection, and we hold ourselves accountable to high standards. Our culture is fast-paced and performance-driven, but grounded in trust and collaboration. We encourage people to think like builders, challenge assumptions and improve the system, not just operate within it. At our core, we are here to simplify one of life’s bigger purchases and give customers more control and confidence at every step of the journey.

description:
Graphic Designer

*Koala Ink Pty Ltd*
*Westfield Belconnen, Canberra ACT*
*Full-time | $60,000 package including super | Weekend roster required*

About Koala Ink

Koala Ink is a new retail print and personalisation shop opening at *Shop 161, Level 3, Westfield Belconnen*.

We combine *on-the-spot design*, *personalised products*, and *modern digital printing technology* to create customised items for customers in-store and online. Customers will be able to request artwork, upload files, and order personalised products through our website, as well as visit the shop for live design support.

We are looking for a reliable, creative and hands-on person to join us as a *Graphic Designer / Online Orders & UV Print Production team member*.

About the role

This is a *multi-role position* suited to someone who enjoys design, customer interaction, online order handling, and learning print production.

You will create print-ready artwork, assist customers in-store, respond to online artwork requests, and participate in UV print production using modern equipment, including a *Roland VersaOBJECT MO-240 UV printer*.

Training will be provided for UV print production, machine care, maintenance routines and shop workflows.

Key responsibilitiesDesign and customer artwork

You will:

* Create print-ready artwork quickly and accurately for personalised products
* Work directly with customers in-store, including live edits while customers are present
* Handle artwork requests received through website submissions, phone enquiries, chat/email and uploads
* Prepare files correctly for production, including sizing, templates, resolution, formats and naming conventions

Online orders and fulfilment

You will:

* Monitor and respond to online order and artwork requests
* Convert customer briefs into artwork drafts efficiently
* Request clarification from customers where required
* Support order tracking, packing, handover, shipping preparation and basic customer updates

UV print production

You will:

* Assist with UV print production using equipment such as the Roland MO-240
* Help with print setup, basic checks, safe handling and quality control
* Follow procedures carefully when working with high-value machinery
* Report any issues promptly

Equipment care and shop presentation

You will:

* Complete daily cleaning and basic maintenance tasks as required
* Keep the production area, workstation and shop tidy and organised
* Follow maintenance checklists and basic operating procedures
* Assist with general shop duties, file organisation, material handling and basic housekeeping

Marketing and social media

You should be comfortable occasionally appearing in simple company marketing content such as short clips, product showcases or behind-the-scenes videos. This is not an influencer role, but you should be comfortable being on camera from time to time.

What we are looking for

The ideal candidate will have:

* Strong skills in *Adobe Illustrator and Photoshop*
* Ability to create print-ready artwork quickly and accurately
* Confidence using AI creative tools to assist with concepts and artwork preparation
* Good communication skills with customers, both in person and online
* Ability to work under pressure when customers are waiting
* Basic computer/admin skills, including Word and Excel
* A reliable, punctual and careful approach to work
* A responsible attitude when working with expensive machinery

Highly regarded but not essential

Experience in any of the following would be an advantage:

* Digital printing or print production
* Roland, Mimaki or similar printing equipment
* E-commerce order handling
* Customer service or retail work
* Personalised products, signage, gifts or custom printing

What we offer

* Full-time role in a new retail print business
* Training on UV print production and Roland MO-240 workflows
* Exposure to both retail and online custom order systems
* A creative, fast-moving shop environment in a major shopping centre
* Opportunity to grow with the business as the product range expands

Important details

*Location:* Shop 161, Level 3, Westfield Belconnen, Canberra ACT
*Employment type:* Full-time
*Salary:* $60,000 package including super
*Roster:* Weekend work will be required as part of shopping centre trading hours

How to apply

Please apply with your resume and, where available, examples of your design work or portfolio.

We are looking for someone who is creative, reliable, hands-on, and willing to learn both the design and production side of a modern personalised printing business.

Pay: $55,000.00 – $60,000.00 per year

Work Location: In person

description:
*Donor Engagement & Marketing Strategist*

*Part-time, minimum 3 days per week, with flexibility to increaseMelbourne / hybridHeartburst Pty Ltd*

Heartburst helps nonprofits, faith-aligned organisations and purpose-driven teams build better digital systems for engagement, fundraising and growth.

We work across CRM, websites, donor journeys, automation, reporting, integrations and digital experience. Our clients are doing meaningful work, but their systems, communications and donor journeys are not always keeping up with the mission.

We are looking for a *Donor Engagement & Marketing Strategist* to help turn strategy into practical action for clients, while also supporting Heartburst’s own marketing.

This is not a traditional marketing manager role. It is a hands-on strategic role for someone who can think clearly, write well, use AI intelligently, engage with clients, and produce practical work.

*The role*

You will help clients improve how they engage supporters, donors, customers and communities.

This may include donor journey planning, campaign ideas, email content, marketing coordination, CRM-enabled engagement, workshops, case studies, newsletter content, campaign briefs and implementation planning.

You do not need to be a deep technical specialist in every tool. Google Ad Grants, advanced paid ads, deep CRM configuration and analytics setup can be supported by specialists. However, you need enough understanding to brief specialists well, work with technical teams and help clients make sensible decisions.

AI-assisted work is important to how we operate. We expect you to use AI tools confidently for research, ideation, drafting, repurposing, workshop preparation and campaign development — while still applying human judgement, accuracy, tone and commercial sense.

*What you will do*

You will support and sometimes lead work such as:

* Donor journey workshops, mapping and implementation planning
* Practical marketing and donor engagement strategy
* Campaign planning for nonprofits, ministries, schools and purpose-led organisations
* Email journey planning, copywriting and nurture content
* Persona, segmentation and messaging work
* CRM-enabled engagement planning, including tags, segments, automations, forms and reporting requirements
* Content briefs for landing pages, website updates and campaign assets
* Coordination with designers, developers, CRM specialists and external contractors
* Client meeting preparation, notes, follow-up actions and progress updates
* Campaign review and practical optimisation recommendations

You will also help keep Heartburst’s own marketing moving, including newsletters, case studies, award submissions, speaking applications, LinkedIn / website content, offer promotion and repurposing content from webinars, talks and proposals.

*What we are looking for*

You are likely to have *4–7+ years of experience* in marketing, digital, fundraising communications, donor engagement, content strategy or a related field.

You may have worked in an agency, nonprofit, ministry, school, consultancy, digital team or purpose-led organisation.

You should be able to:

* Think strategically, but still enjoy getting on the tools
* Write clearly and persuasively
* Turn messy client conversations into clear actions
* Prepare workshops, campaign plans and client-ready documents
* Use AI tools fluently without outsourcing your judgement
* Understand donor/supporter journeys and how communication builds trust over time
* Work with email marketing, CRM, website and campaign tools at a practical level
* Brief technical specialists clearly when deeper expertise is required
* Keep client work moving without needing constant supervision
* Communicate confidently with senior clients while staying practical and grounded

*What would make you stand out*

* Experience with nonprofits, charities, churches, schools, membership organisations or purpose-led organisations
* Experience with donor journeys, supporter engagement or fundraising communications
* Experience writing email journeys, appeals, newsletters or nurture campaigns
* Familiarity with platforms such as Mailchimp, Campaign Monitor, HubSpot, Virtuous, Salesforce, Blackbaud, Raisely, Brevo, WordPress or similar
* Experience coordinating Google Ad Grants or paid campaigns, even if not as a deep technical specialist
* Strong attention to detail without getting stuck in perfectionism
* Comfort working in a small team where priorities can shift quickly

*What this role is not*

This is not a pure social media role, a traditional fundraising role, a back-office administration role, a senior marketing manager role, or a specialist Google Ads / CRM technician role.

It is a role for someone who can help nonprofits clarify their message, engage supporters, improve donor journeys and turn strategy into practical marketing activity.

*Why Heartburst*

Heartburst is building a strong position in nonprofit CRM, donor engagement systems, digital experience and automation.

We are growing our work with organisations that need more than a website or a campaign. They need connected systems that help them engage supporters, improve retention, reduce friction and scale their impact.

You will join a small, capable team where your work will directly shape client outcomes and Heartburst’s own growth.

*Hours and remuneration*

This is a *part-time role of at least 3 days per week*, with some flexibility around exact days and the potential to increase as client demand grows.

Indicative full-time equivalent remuneration is *$100,000–$130,000 + super*, pro rata based on agreed days, depending on experience and capability.

*How to apply*

Please send us:

* Your CV or LinkedIn profile
* A short note outlining why this role interests you
* 2–3 examples of relevant work, such as campaign content, donor communications, strategy documents, email journeys, case studies, workshop outputs or marketing plans

We are less interested in polished corporate language and more interested in clear thinking, strong writing and practical evidence that you can help move meaningful work forward.

Job Types: Part-time, Permanent

Pay: $100,000.00 – $130,000.00 per year

Expected hours: 24 per week



Ability to commute/relocate:
* Blackburn North VIC 3130: Reliably commute or planning to relocate before starting work (Required)

Application Question(s):
* Which of the following best describes your experience level?

Mostly coordination / administration
Hands-on marketing execution
Hands-on strategy and execution
Marketing management / team leadership
Specialist technical marketing role
* Which of the following have you personally produced? Select all that apply.

Options:

Email campaigns or nurture sequences
Campaign plans
Marketing strategy documents
Donor/supporter communications
Case studies or website articles
Workshop materials
Client-ready reports or recommendations
Social media content only
None of the above
* How confident are you using AI tools such as ChatGPT, Claude or similar in your work?

Options:

I use AI regularly and confidently as part of my workflow
I use AI occasionally
I have experimented with AI but do not use it much
I do not currently use AI tools
* Are you comfortable working directly with clients or senior stakeholders?

Options:

Yes, I regularly lead or contribute to client/stakeholder meetings
Yes, I have some experience
I prefer mostly internal execution work
No
* What is your expected full-time equivalent salary, excluding super?

Experience:
* Digital marketing: 3 years (Required)

Work Authorisation:
* Australia (Required)

Work Location: Hybrid remote in Blackburn North VIC 3130

description:
We’re on a mission to build an ecosystem of products & education for Framer (no-code website design tool). We’re a fast-moving startup, with 10,000+ global customers, 35,000+ community of designers & epic clients such as Framer & Lovable (the world’s fastest growing startup).

*Learn more about us:* https://insertframe.io/about

Contractor, 2 days/wk to start. The hope is that we continue working together on a long term basis & this role becomes full-time.

*What you'll actually do*

You take direction and turn it into work that ships. That could look like:

* Designing social posts, thumbnails, and website graphics/updates
* Writing first drafts of emails, blogs, and captions
* Scheduling and managing our social content across channels
* Keeping our docs, showcase and content engine running
* Supporting our Head of Operations & Marketing on whatever's in front of us that week

*No two weeks look the same - Intentional.*

*Why work at Insert Frame?*

* Be at the forefront of the design industry
* A great team passionate about what we do
* Our trust. We want you to take ownership of your work!
* Learning & growth with Framer & no-code tools
* Work directly with the leadership team
* Work remotely or from our uber-cool Brunswick office

*What you need*

* Figma skills - not portfolio-perfect, but you know your way around
* A background in tech or startups
* Strong communication skills
* Human skills (we love & use AI, but people matter most in this role)
* You've run socials for yourself, a small business, or a side project
* You can take an early-stage idea and run with it until it's done
* You're comfortable with ambiguity and know how to find clarity fast

*Show us what you’ve got (30‑second video)*

Along with your application, we’d love a *30-second video*. It can be:

* A quick intro about who you are and what you’re into, *or*
* A short piece of content you’ve created that you’re proud of

This doesn’t need to be fancy or highly produced, your phone camera, Loom, whatever works. We just want to get a feel for your personality and how you think about content.

Send your video (or a link to it) to *georgia@insertframe.io* & *ryan@insertframe.io* with the subject line: `Insert Frame Application Video`

*Bonus points*

* You use Framer or are genuinely curious about it
* You have a side hustle (the weirder the better)
* You've worked in a fast-moving startup before
* You're Melbourne-based or want to be in the room
* Being super competitive at Mario Kart

*If this sounds like the role for you, apply now! *Got a question? Reach out to georgia@insertframe.io / ryan@insertframe.io

Job Types: Part-time, Contract

Pay: $25.00 – $34.00 per hour

Benefits:
* Work from home

Work Location: Hybrid remote in Brunswick VIC 3056

description:
Founded in 2017, Obsidian Security was created to close a critical gap: securing the SaaS applications where modern business happens—platforms like Microsoft 365, Salesforce, and hundreds more.

Backed by top investors including Greylock, Norwest Venture Partners, and IVP, we've built a complete SaaS security platform to reduce risk, detect and respond to threats, and prevent breaches at the source. Our team includes leaders who helped define the categories of endpoint and identity security at CrowdStrike, Okta, Cylance, and Carbon Black.

Now, we're transforming how SaaS is secured—in the era of agentic AI.

Today, Obsidian is trusted by global enterprises like Snowflake, T-Mobile, and Pure Storage. We protect more than 200 organizations across North America, Europe, the Middle East, Southeast Asia, Australia, and New Zealand—including many of the world's largest Fortune 1000 and Global 2000 companies.

With strong global momentum, a growing partner ecosystem including SentinelOne, Databricks, and Google Cloud, and a major fundraise on the horizon, we're scaling quickly toward long-term growth and IPO readiness. Join us as we define the future of SaaS security!

About the Team:

We're looking for a hardworking, focused and driven Enterprise Account Executive to channel energy, passion and initiative into new logo acquisition. You'll be responsible for developing and executing against a comprehensive account/territory plan whilst working collaboratively with our internal and external resources.

About the Role:

Proactively, identify, qualify and close sales pipeline across your territory and accounts
Close business to meet and exceed monthly, quarterly and annual business targets
Demonstrate an extensive understanding of the Obsidian Security offering and our value to our customers
Align with our partners and alliances to optimize opportunities
Partner with internal resources across Sales Engineering, Customer Success and Professional Services
Demonstrate accurate pipeline forecasting and management
Actively participate in our sales enablement training

About You:

10+ years of enterprise sales experience
Working knowledge of sales concepts, methods and techniques
Experience evangelizing new technology into F1000 accounts.
Able to maintain and manage existing client relationships and accounts.
Able to utilize existing client and/or C-Level relationships, as well as build new relationships across IT Security as well as other lines of business.
Self Starter that creates and maintains a sales pipeline by capturing accurate and complete information in selected CRM databases including activity, closing, project forecast, close ratios and market intelligence.
Strong ability to understand a customer's business issues and needs and be able to articulate and map back value to a solution.
Strong prospecting skills, deal qualification, and POV management skills, leading to acquisition of new business.
Team player with the ability to collaborate well with internal stakeholders or partners to drive opportunities to closure.
Need to maintain and keep up with market trends, competitor analysis, and market conditions which may impact customers.
Able to learn quickly and ramp to be able to effectively articulate and differentiate the value of our product to prospective clients.
descriptionHTML:
Founded in 2017, Obsidian Security was created to close a critical gap: securing the SaaS applications where modern business happens—platforms like Microsoft 365, Salesforce, and hundreds more.
Backed by top investors including Greylock, Norwest Venture Partners, and IVP, we've built a complete SaaS security platform to reduce risk, detect and respond to threats, and prevent breaches at the source. Our team includes leaders who helped define the categories of endpoint and identity security at CrowdStrike, Okta, Cylance, and Carbon Black.
Now, we're transforming how SaaS is secured—in the era of agentic AI.
Today, Obsidian is trusted by global enterprises like Snowflake, T-Mobile, and Pure Storage. We protect more than 200 organizations across North America, Europe, the Middle East, Southeast Asia, Australia, and New Zealand—including many of the world's largest Fortune 1000 and Global 2000 companies.
With strong global momentum, a growing partner ecosystem including SentinelOne, Databricks, and Google Cloud, and a major fundraise on the horizon, we're scaling quickly toward long-term growth and IPO readiness. Join us as we define the future of SaaS security!

About the Team:

We're looking for a hardworking, focused and driven Enterprise Account Executive to channel energy, passion and initiative into new logo acquisition. You'll be responsible for developing and executing against a comprehensive account/territory plan whilst working collaboratively with our internal and external resources.

About the Role:

  • Proactively, identify, qualify and close sales pipeline across your territory and accounts
  • Close business to meet and exceed monthly, quarterly and annual business targets
  • Demonstrate an extensive understanding of the Obsidian Security offering and our value to our customers
  • Align with our partners and alliances to optimize opportunities
  • Partner with internal resources across Sales Engineering, Customer Success and Professional Services
  • Demonstrate accurate pipeline forecasting and management
  • Actively participate in our sales enablement training

About You:

  • 10+ years of enterprise sales experience
  • Working knowledge of sales concepts, methods and techniques
  • Experience evangelizing new technology into F1000 accounts.
  • Able to maintain and manage existing client relationships and accounts.
  • Able to utilize existing client and/or C-Level relationships, as well as build new relationships across IT Security as well as other lines of business.
  • Self Starter that creates and maintains a sales pipeline by capturing accurate and complete information in selected CRM databases including activity, closing, project forecast, close ratios and market intelligence.
  • Strong ability to understand a customer's business issues and needs and be able to articulate and map back value to a solution.
  • Strong prospecting skills, deal qualification, and POV management skills, leading to acquisition of new business.
  • Team player with the ability to collaborate well with internal stakeholders or partners to drive opportunities to closure.
  • Need to maintain and keep up with market trends, competitor analysis, and market conditions which may impact customers.
  • Able to learn quickly and ramp to be able to effectively articulate and differentiate the value of our product to prospective clients.

description:
Work with cutting edge AI technology, making the world a safer and more secure place. DroneShield (ASX:DRO) offers an opportunity to solve some of world’s most challenging technical problems in the rapidly growing counter-drone sector.

Our customers operate in some of the most challenging and high-stakes environments in the world, including military organisations, government agencies, airports, critical infrastructure operators, and law enforcement. Protecting airspace in these settings requires technology that performs under pressure and teams that understand what’s at stake. At DroneShield, employees work at the leading edge of counter-drone innovation, helping to address real-world security challenges as drone threats continue to evolve globally.

With one of the largest listed defence company market capitalisations in Australia, now part of the ASX200 index, DroneShield is experiencing a period of hypergrowth. Revenue has surged from A$57 million in 2024 to over A$190 million in 2025, representing growth of more than 400% year-on-year, with record profitability and cashflow. The total addressable global market for counter-drone is assessed at approximately $100 billion, and is currently at the nascent stage with much of the growth still to come, with DroneShield well positioned as a global market leader, and the only public listed pure-play business in this sector.

The company has grown from 11 employees in 2017 to over 450 staff globally today, and is on track to reach around 550 by the end of 2026. This expansion includes investment of over A$50 million annually in R&D, a global pipeline exceeding A$2.5 billion, and continuous scaling of production capacity to meet accelerating demand.

The role is based at DroneShield’s central Sydney headquarters. Overseas on-the-ground presence includes Virginia (USA), Netherlands, Denmark, Mexico and Dubai, as well as distributors in over 70 countries worldwide.

About the role

The Trade Compliance Officer will be responsible for assisting a comprehensive global trade compliance program ensuring adherence to Australian and international export controls regulations. This role requires expertise in trade regulations and export controls related to goods for both munition and dual-use lists, with a particular focus on Australian regulations.

Responsibilities, Duties and Expectations

Support the global trade compliance framework, ensuring alignment between Australian trade and export control requirements and the organisation’s global compliance standards
Assist the global trade compliance team with ensuring all import and export activities comply with Australian regulations, while supporting compliance with international export control regimes, including U.S. (ITAR/EAR) and European regulations
Act as a regional point of contact for Australia, providing guidance and support to global stakeholders on Australian export controls and regulatory requirements
Monitor, interpret and communicate changes in Australian export control and customs regulations to the global trade compliance team, supporting export assessments and implementation activities
Assist with export control classifications for goods, technology and software, ensuring classifications are communicated and consistent across global systems and jurisdictions
Support the preparation, drafting and submission of export/import permits under the Australian regulatory framework, in coordination with global licensing requirements
Maintain accurate record‑keeping and export registers, ensuring documentation meets both Australian and International regulatory requirements
Contribute to global trade compliance initiatives, including process improvement, internal controls, training, and audit readiness activities

Qualifications, Experience and Skills

Education: Bachelor’s or Master degree in Law, International Business, Supply Chain Management, Logistics Management or proven equivalent field experience
Minimum 1–2 years’ experience in trade compliance, export controls, customs brokerage or a related regulatory role
Experience supporting or working within a multi-jurisdictional or global trade compliance environment;
Defence or aerospace industry experience strongly preferred
Demonstrable knowledge of Australian export control and customs regulations, with awareness of major international export control regimes

Note for recruitment agencies: We do not accept unsolicited candidates from external recruiters unless specifically instructed.

description:
About Us:

Innovation is and will always be the core of SAP Fioneer, and it is the promise of why we were spun out of SAP: agility, innovation, and delivery.

SAP Fioneer builds on a heritage of outstanding technology and a deep understanding of corporate and consumer demands. At the heart of it all it is simple: We bring financial services to the next level with innovative software solutions and platforms.

We are helping companies in the financial services industry to achieve speed, scalability, and cost-efficiency through digital business innovation, cloud technology, and solutions that cover banking and insurance processes end-to-end.

A global company, with rapid growth, innovative people, and a lean organization makes SAP Fioneer a place where you accelerate your future!

The SAP Fioneer Banking Technical Consultant will play a leading role in the SAP Technical team within the Banking Business Unit. Many of our customers are installing or upgrading to the latest version of our software platform. There is a large demand for people who can help us work with our clients in converting their software to the latest version of the platform and at the same time identify and realise value adding business opportunities together with our clients.

You will be responsible for both sales and delivery of SAP Fioneer banking related projects. This includes sales and pre-sales activities of opportunity identification, pre-sales presentation and proposal writing and submission. You will be responsible for the quality of client interaction including thought leadership and guidance on industry best practice, while also playing a key role in the delivery and implementation of the required technical solution. You will follow project standards within established timeframes and at defined quality levels, and ensure projects are completed according to schedule and client specifications. This position is client-facing and requires travel to the client site as the project warrants.

You will work as a trusted technical advisor to our banking clients on their transformation journeys. On this path, you get the chance to play high visibility roles with hands-on experience with Fioneer banking product portfolio.

You will provide the following technical advisory roles and services as part of SAP Fioneer Banking implementations:

Work closely with bank's Solution Architects, Product Owners, and Scrum Masters in defining the scope of work to be delivered
Providing SAP Fioneer Banking technical expertise to internal and external stakeholders
Providing thought leadership on platform conversion options and methods towards the latest releases of our software (TRBK on S/4 Hana)
Providing thought leadership on legacy system migration to SAP Banking platform
Participate in development and implementation of the SAP Fioneer team strategy.
Training and mentorship of junior resources.
Maintain, enhance, and broaden knowledge and skills of SAP Fioneer Banking applications, industry technology best practices and developments.
Interact with clients to provide them insights and possibilities within the Technology domain to help shape and craft their transformation journeys, with specific focus on the SAP Fioneer technical capabilities and integration patterns
Use subject matter knowledge to advise the client on the most suitable options, including when to make use of standard capabilities or when customisation is required
Functional knowledge in some of SAP Banking Modules like Deposit Management / Loans Management / Master Contract management is mandatory.
Consulting knowledge and experience in evaluating customer requirements, and propose Solution / Architecture approaches.
Proficiency in ABAP Technologies - ABAP Workbench, ABAP Data Dictionary, Report Programming, Data Migration, Screen Programming, SmartForms, Adobe forms, Authorizations, ALE/IDOC, Workflow, Web Dynpro ABAP, OO ABAP, Enhancements & Modifications.
Recording of requirements from the specialist department, e.g. via workshops or coordination meetings, and creation of solution concepts as a basis for the implementation of software
Create the necessary design and build artefacts
Provide SAP Fioneer Design Authority for the approved scope of work
Produce SAP Fioneer Technical architecture and design documents
Produce SAP Fioneer Software configuration design documents
Produce SAP Fioneer Software integration and interface design documentation
Collaborate in integrating SAP Fioneer solutions into channels and downstream systems

Requirements

Bachelor or master’s degree in finance, computer science, technology, or engineering
8+ years of consulting experience in at least 3 of the SAP Core banking domains like Deposits, Loans, Master Contracts, Collateral Management & Payments
Experience with modern SAP technologies such as API management, REST/OData, and event‑driven architecture.
Solid technology architecture knowledge and understanding, ideally in software or cloud solutions
Proven industry relevant track record of in-depth knowledge of SAP Fioneer technical platform capabilities and functional capabilities, implementations, associated standards, practices and regulations
Client facing service experience required. Engagement across stakeholders and ability to run information gathering and sharing sessions
Maintain a strong understanding of emerging AI trends and apply AI‑driven techniques to enhance analysis, automation, and delivery efficiency within SAP Fioneer Banking implementations.

Technical Skills:

Solid SAP Fioneer Banking Technical project experience, including implementation, platform upgrade, legacy migrations and data migration
A working knowledge of domestic and international banking domains
Solid Business Writing Skills (business cases, proposals)
Solid Presentation and Facilitation Skills
Eagerness to learn and to take responsibility
You demonstrate ownership and accountability in everything you do

Soft Skills:

Strong analytical and problem-solving skills.
Good communication and teamwork abilities.
Willingness to learn and adapt to new technologies and processes.
Ability to work independently and handle multiple priorities.

Benefits

You will have the opportunity to collaborate with some of the brightest minds in the industry on an incredibly ambitious project to shape the ever-evolving financial sector. This is an environment where you can have it all - the agility, enthusiasm and dynamism of a start-up, combined with the established expertise, solid market presence and extensive customer network of a more established organisation.

At SAP Fioneer, you will benefit from a flexible work environment that encourages creativity and encourages you to think outside the box, bring new ideas to the table, and challenge the status quo. You will become part of a diverse and global team that we are proud of and are constantly growing and reinventing. As an employee, you will have the chance to chart your own career path and take advantage of competitive compensation packages and progression opportunities based on merit.

We offer pension plans and life insurance for financial security. Our health and wellbeing benefits include private medical insurance, wellness cover, and an employee assistance program.

SAP FIONEER DIVERSITY COMMITMENT

 SAP Fioneer believes in the power of innovation that each employee brings and would like to leverage the qualities and appreciate the unique competencies that each diverse person brings to the company.

description:
Amaya AI is a fast-growing SaaS startup that helps businesses better understand and optimise their sales and customer conversations using AI-driven insights.

We are currently expanding our remote sales team and are hiring *Appointment Setters* to support outbound outreach by booking qualified meetings with business owners and decision-makers.

This is a *fully remote, commission-based role* suited to individuals who are confident communicating with others, comfortable with outreach, and motivated by performance. The role involves starting conversations with business owners, identifying potential interest, and booking meetings for our sales team to follow up.

We’re looking for people who are consistent, coachable, and enjoy working in a structured, fast-moving environment where performance and results are clearly measured.

*Key Responsibilities*

* Conduct outbound outreach to businesses (primarily cold calling)
* Speak with decision-makers (e.g. owners, managers)
* Introduce the product and start conversations
* Identify potential interest and qualify leads
* Book meetings for the sales team
* Maintain accurate notes and update lead tracking systems

*Requirements*

* Strong verbal communication skills
* Comfortable speaking with new people daily
* Ability to handle rejection professionally
* Self-motivated and able to work independently
* Consistent work ethic and willingness to learn

_Previous sales, call centre, or outreach experience is beneficial but not required._

*Role Expectations*

* High daily outreach activity (calls and follow-ups)
* Consistent pipeline updates and organisation
* Focus on booking qualified meetings

*Compensation*

* Commission-based role
* Earn per deal closed from meetings you book (approx. *15% commission*)
* Uncapped earning potential based on performance

*This Role May Not Be Suitable If You:*

* Prefer a fixed salary or hourly structure
* Are not comfortable with outbound outreach
* Require constant supervision or direction

*What We Offer*

* Fully remote work environment
* Flexible schedule
* Opportunity to develop sales skills in a growing SaaS company
* Potential progression into a closing role

*Application Process*

Shortlisted candidates will be contacted and asked to complete a short screening task, including a brief voice note simulating a sales call.

Only candidates who complete this step will be considered.

*Additional Information*

* Start Date: Immediate

Job Type: Contract

Pay: $110,000.00 – $200,000.00 per year

Work Location: Remote

description:
Work with cutting edge AI technology, making the world a safer and more secure place. DroneShield (ASX:DRO) offers an opportunity to solve some of world’s most challenging technical problems in the rapidly growing counter-drone sector.

Our customers operate in some of the most challenging and high-stakes environments in the world, including military organisations, government agencies, airports, critical infrastructure operators, and law enforcement. Protecting airspace in these settings requires technology that performs under pressure and teams that understand what’s at stake. At DroneShield, employees work at the leading edge of counter-drone innovation, helping to address real-world security challenges as drone threats continue to evolve globally.

With one of the largest listed defence company market capitalisations in Australia, now part of the ASX200 index, DroneShield is experiencing a period of hypergrowth. Revenue has surged from A$57 million in 2024 to over A$217 million in 2025, representing growth of more than 400% year-on-year, with record profitability and cashflow. The total addressable global market for counter-drone is assessed at approximately $100 billion, and is currently at the nascent stage with much of the growth still to come, with DroneShield well positioned as a global market leader, and the only public listed pure-play business in this sector.

The company has grown from 11 employees in 2017 to over 450 staff globally today, and is on track to reach around 550 by the end of 2026. This expansion includes investment of over A$50 million annually in R&D, a global pipeline exceeding A$2.5 billion, and continuous scaling of production capacity to meet accelerating demand.

The role is based at DroneShield’s central Sydney headquarters. Overseas on-the-ground presence includes Virginia (USA), Netherlands, Denmark, Mexico and Dubai, as well as distributors in over 70 countries worldwide.

About the role

In this role, you will be at the forefront of advancing our computer vision capabilities by integrating cameras and algorithms to build our next-generation AI-powered detection, identification, and tracking optical systems.

You’ll collaborate closely with system engineers, machine learning engineers, and product teams to ensure our product is performant, reliable, and optimised for real-world defence applications. This position offers the opportunity to work on unique, mission-critical challenges where your contributions directly impact global security outcomes.

Responsibilities, Duties and Expectations

Design, implement, benchmark, and optimise computer vision algorithms to enhance detection, classification, and tracking capabilities
Develop and adapt software interfaces to support the integration of new camera models and configurations
Collaborate with cross-functional teams to define technical requirements and ensure seamless integration of computer vision solutions

Qualifications, Experience and Skills

Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science, Engineering, or equivalent practical experience
More than 3 years of experience in software engineering
Proficiency in C++ development, including multi-threading, unit testing, benchmarking, and profiling
Experience programming in Python is required, and familiarity with Golang is preferred
Experience with computer vision libraries (e.g. OpenCV) or integrating AI models (e.g. ONNX, TensorRT)
Experience integrating cameras or similar sensors (e.g., LiDAR, thermal cameras)
Experience with edge computing or embedded systems for real-time applications (e.g. NVIDIA GPUs, CUDA, GStreamer)
Experience with AI/ML engineering is preferred
Experience with version control systems (e.g., Git) and workflow automation tools (e.g. GitLab CI, ClearML)
Previous ‘start-up’ or ‘scale-up’ experience is preferred
Strong problem-solving skills, attention to data quality, and effective cross-team communication
An abundance of ambition and motivation to grow the company and compete with the big players in the industry

Note for recruitment agencies: We do not accept unsolicited candidates from external recruiters unless specifically instructed.

Responsibilities, Duties and Expectations

  • Design, implement, benchmark, and optimise computer vision algorithms to enhance detection, classification, and tracking capabilities
  • Develop and adapt software interfaces to support the integration of new camera models and configurations
  • Collaborate with cross-functional teams to define technical requirements and ensure seamless integration of computer vision solutions

Qualifications, Experience and Skills

  • Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science, Engineering, or equivalent practical experience
  • More than 3 years of experience in software engineering
  • Proficiency in C++ development, including multi-threading, unit testing, benchmarking, and profiling
  • Experience programming in Python is required, and familiarity with Golang is preferred
  • Experience with computer vision libraries (e.g. OpenCV) or integrating AI models (e.g. ONNX, TensorRT)
  • Experience integrating cameras or similar sensors (e.g., LiDAR, thermal cameras)
  • Experience with edge computing or embedded systems for real-time applications (e.g. NVIDIA GPUs, CUDA, GStreamer)
  • Experience with AI/ML engineering is preferred
  • Experience with version control systems (e.g., Git) and workflow automation tools (e.g. GitLab CI, ClearML)
  • Previous ‘start-up’ or ‘scale-up’ experience is preferred
  • Strong problem-solving skills, attention to data quality, and effective cross-team communication
  • An abundance of ambition and motivation to grow the company and compete with the big players in the industry


Note for recruitment agencies: We do not accept unsolicited candidates from external recruiters unless specifically instructed.

positionName: Senior Python Software Engineer (Backend Workflows Team) - open to remote across ANZ
description:
Job Description

Join the team redefining how the world experiences design.

Hey, g'day, mabuhay, kia ora,你好, hallo, vítejte!

Thanks for stopping by. We know job hunting can be a little time consuming and you're probably keen to find out what's on offer, so we'll get straight to the point.

Where and how you can work

Our flagship campus is in Sydney. We also have a campus in Melbourne and co-working spaces in Brisbane, Perth and Adelaide. But you have choice in where and how you work. That means if you want to do your thing in the office (if you're near one), at home or a bit of both, it's up to you.

What you’d be doing in this role

As Canva scales change continues to be part of our DNA. But we like to think that's all part of the fun. So this will give you the flavour of the type of things you'll be working on when you start, but this will likely evolve.

At the moment, this role is focused on:

Shaping the Python language ecosystem with a strong product and platform mindset, defining clear development patterns and documentation in partnership with Infra and Product teams to support Canva’s broader AI strategy.
Architecting, building and delivering high-impact solutions that uplift the Python developer experience at Canva, ensuring high standards of engineering excellence.
Advocating for Python engineering best practices across the organization, influencing teams and aligning development patterns to Canva’s long-term strategy.
Developing internal observability tooling and metrics that give the team actionable insights, enabling informed, targeted decisions on where to invest in the Python ecosystem.
Driving org-wide ecosystem migrations and tooling transformations, owning design, rollout, telemetry, communication, and long-term maintenance strategies.
Shaping and executing the long-term vision for IDE developer tooling, ensuring our tools evolve to meet the needs of Python engineers and industry trends.

You're probably a match if

You have expert-level Python experience, with familiarity in modern development ecosystems and tools such as Bazel being a plus.
You have demonstrated leadership and ownership of complex, high-impact engineering initiatives, with a strong sense of accountability for outcomes.
You have a deep commitment to engineering excellence and systems architecture, with the ability to design for scale, reliability, and strong non-functional foundations.
You have extensive experience with containerization, Terraform, and GitOps, and a solid understanding of how to build and operate production-grade infrastructure.
You have a background in integrating ML applications into platform or backend systems, with an understanding of AI/ML toolchains and their operational requirements.
You can translate customer and developer needs into impactful technical solutions, applying a product mindset to improve developer experience at scale.

About The Group

We are part of Developer Workflows, shaping the end-to-end engineering experience to build, test, deploy, and operate software at Canva. We bring together developer advocacy, product thinking, and deep technical expertise. The Backend Workflows team focuses on empowering backend engineers by removing friction from the software development lifecycle and delivering intuitive, scalable workflows. We collaborate across platform and product teams to compose seamless backend experiences that support Canva’s rapid growth. Together, we create the foundations that enable every engineer to do the best work of their lives - and directly contribute to Canva’s long-term vision and success.

About the Role

We’re on a mission to elevate Python into a first-class experience across Canva, unlocking the full potential of our ML/AI and backend engineering teams. As a Senior Python Software Engineer in Backend Workflows, you will lead the design and delivery of workflows, tooling, and best practices that make Python development fast, reliable, and frictionless. Your work will shape how engineers write, test, build, and operate Python code everyday at scale - directly empowering teams who are building the AI-powered capabilities that define Canva’s future. We work closely with ML, backend, and platform engineers to deeply understand their challenges and craft solutions that accelerate their impact. This is a highly influential role where your contributions will have broad, long-lasting impact on Canva’s engineering velocity and AI strategy.

What's in it for you?

Achieving our crazy big goals motivates us to work hard - and we do - but you'll experience lots of moments of magic, connectivity and fun woven throughout life at Canva, too. We also offer a stack of benefits to set you up for every success in and outside of work.

Here's a taste of what's on offer:

Equity packages - we want our success to be yours too
Inclusive parental leave policy that supports all parents & carers
An annual Vibe & Thrive allowance to support your wellbeing, social connection, office setup & more
Flexible leave options that empower you to be a force for good, take time to recharge and supports you personally

Check out lifeatcanva.com for more info.

Other stuff to know

We see AI as a powerful amplifier of creativity and technology at Canva. We’re evolving how we assess AI skills in our Technology hiring experience - you’ll tackle interactive, real-time challenges that reflect the kind of work we do. In some interviews, you may also be asked to solve a problem using an AI tool to show how you approach challenges with tech by your side. Your recruitment partner will walk you through what to expect. We make hiring decisions based on your experience, skills and passion, as well as how you can enhance Canva and our culture.

When you apply, please tell us the pronouns you use and any reasonable adjustments you may need during the interview process. We celebrate all types of skills and backgrounds at Canva, so even if you don’t feel like your skills quite match what’s listed above - we still want to hear from you!

Please note that interviews are conducted virtually.

description:
Company Description

At NCS Australia, we believe in doing technology services better. Our commitment to quality, focus on people, and willingness to challenge traditional thinking set us apart. Our team brings this belief to life by partnering with our clients and communities to make tomorrow together.

We are committed to creating an environment that prioritises innovation, collaboration, and purposeful work. Our diverse team is empowered to make a meaningful impact with curiosity, creativity and resilience to shape better outcomes. Join us and accept the challenge of creating a better tomorrow.

Please note we would look at either a contract or permanent person for this position

Job Description

We are looking for a forward-thinking Product / Service Designer to help define the future of how customers interact with products in an AI-driven world. This role is focused on designing "future-state" experiences where AI agents—not just humans—act as the primary interface for discovery, comparison, and transactions.

You will bridge the gap between traditional user-centric design and the emerging field of machine-readable commerce, ensuring our product constructs are optimized for both human clarity and agentic efficiency.

The Role

As a Product / Service Designer, you will lead the research and design efforts to determine how AI agents discover and transact within our ecosystem. You will be responsible for translating complex technical possibilities into seamless service journeys and product information architectures.

Key Responsibilities:

Future-State Design: Design end-to-end product experiences specifically optimized for AI agent interactions and automated commerce.
Journey Mapping: Develop key user journeys that illustrate how agents assist in product discovery and facilitate secure transactions.
Agentic Research: Conduct deep-dive research into how AI agents (LLMs, personal assistants, etc.) search, compare, and execute purchases in the telco and technology sectors.
Product Architecture: Design agent-optimized product constructs, ensuring that product information is structured for maximum machine discoverability.
Strategic Balancing: Balance the needs of the human end-user with the technical requirements of the AI agents representing them.

Qualifications

Technical Requirements & Skills

We are looking for a designer who is comfortable working in "the grey" of emerging technology and can apply rigorous design thinking to unstructured problems.

Service Design & IA: Proven proficiency in service design methodology and complex product information architecture.
Advanced Research: Strong user research capabilities, with a track record of gathering insights in emerging tech domains.
AI Ecosystem Knowledge: Familiarity with Generative Engine Optimization (GEO) and the mechanics of how AI platforms consume and surface data.
Strategic Translation: The ability to turn abstract research findings into concrete design requirements for technical teams.

Additional Information

Why join us:

NCS Australia is where you can feel at home, nurturing your talents and skills as we make tomorrow together, one day at a time. Our benefits include paid parental leave, initiatives focused on your well-being and discounted health insurance. You will also enjoy discounts on various products and services and be regularly recognised and rewarded for high performance. We are committed to your career development through our Capability Fingerprint, industry and partner training programs, special interest groups, and an AI-driven learning platform. No matter where you are in your career, we offer meaningful work and opportunities for growth.

NCS Australia is an equal-opportunity employer, and we take pride in our commitment to valuing and supporting our people and the communities we serve.We are dedicated to attracting, retaining and developing our people regardless of gender identity, ethnicity, sexual orientation, disability and age. Applications are encouraged from all sectors of the community and we strongly encourage applications from the Veterans, Aboriginal and/or Torres Strait Islander community.

At NCS Australia, we are committed to supporting adjustments throughout the recruitment and selection process, as well as during employment. We actively support and encourage people with disability to apply.

Agencies:

We’ve got this. We request that you do not contact NCS employees outside of the Talent Acquisition team. NCS exclusively accepts resumes from agencies on our preferred supplier panel through the NCS Agency Portal. Agencies that submit resumes must have a valid fee agreement and be assigned to the particular requisition by the Talent Acquisition team. Any resumes that are submitted outside of this process will become the sole property of NCS. If a candidate is hired outside of this process, no fee or payment will be given.

Work rights and background checks:

To be eligible for a position with us, applicants will need to have valid work rights for Australia and be willing to undergo a comprehensive background checking process, including probity and police checks
