description:
Please note that we will never request payment or bank account information at any stage of the recruitment process. As we continue to grow our teams, we urge you to be cautious of fraudulent job postings or recruitment activities that misuse our company name and information. Please protect your personal information during any recruitment process. While Monks may contact potential candidates via LinkedIn, all applications must be submitted through our official website (monks.com/careers).

Role Summary

The Senior Digital Media Specialist is a strategic and leadership-focused professional responsible for managing key client accounts, leading media delivery projects, and driving business growth within the APAC region. They serve as a primary client contact for our key accounts, overseeing the delivery of integrated services, fostering strong relationships with budget holders and internal leads, and leading internal and external teams.The role involves strategic promotion of the media business, proactive leadership, a strong understanding of media investment strategies and continuous improvement initiatives.The Senior Digital Media Specialist acts as a bridge between clients and internal teams, ensuring high-quality execution, financial accountability, and alignment with overall business and media strategies.

Role Responsibilities
Client Management & Relationships: Develop and maintain strong external relationships with clients' operational and senior stakeholders. Lead client meetings, ensuring delivery of value, capturing feedback, and identifying new business opportunities.
Media Delivery & Oversight: Lead the planning, execution, and monitoring of media campaigns and services, ensuring alignment with client expectations and contractual SLAs. Oversee delivery teams, providing guidance and quality assurance.
Product & Service Knowledge: Maintain advanced knowledge of media products (from a technical and strategic lense), services, and platforms. Lead discussions internally and externally about media offerings, ensuring proper delivery and optimizations.
Business Development & Sales Support: Identify revenue opportunities, support pitching efforts, and assist in scoping and contracting processes with a thorough understanding of legal and commercial frameworks.
Strategy Promotion & Leadership: Promote APAC media and business strategies within the team and across client portfolios. Support the adoption of strategic initiatives to enhance client outcomes and operational efficiency.
Financial & Resource Management: Manage budgeting, billing, invoicing, and forecasts. Provide feedback on resource allocation, onboarding of new team members, and project profitability.
Partnerships & Industry Insights: Engage in vendor and technology partner feedback forums, keep certifications current, and surface new business opportunities. Share industry insights and case studies to foster continuous learning.
Team & People Development: Mentor and guide team members, participate in growth conversations, and foster a positive, collaborative team culture. Lead performance reviews and support team motivation.
Research & Industry Knowledge: Dedicate time to ongoing research, share learnings internally and with clients, and incorporate insights into strategic planning.
Experience:
Media Experience: 5+ years of experience in media account management, client servicing, or campaign delivery, with progressively increasing responsibility.
Strategic Thinking: Ability to promote and implement media strategies aligned with business goals
Client Management: Strong relationship-building skills with experience managing key accounts and engaging senior stakeholders.
Media Expertise: Deep understanding of media products, platforms, and services, including digital, managed media, advisory solutions, and R&A offerings.
Leadership Skills: Proven ability to collaborate as a leader within small teams or create initiatives and inspire teams.
Project & Delivery Management: Demonstrated ability to oversee complex projects, ensure timeliness, quality, and contractual adherence.
Educational Background: Bachelor's degree or higher in marketing, media, communications, business, or related fields.
Analytical & Market Insight Skills: Strong research skills, industry awareness, and an ability to leverage insights for strategic advantage.
Financial Acumen: Proficient in managing budgets, billing, invoicing, forecasts, and resource allocation.

#LI-CX1

About Monks

Monks is the global, digital-first, data-driven, unitary operating brand of S4 Capital plc. With a legacy of innovation and specialized expertise, Monks combines an extraordinary range of global Marketing and Technology Services to redefine how brands interact with the world. Through Monks.Flow, its flagship AI ecosystem for marketing orchestration, Monks transforms marketing into a growth engine, collapsing timelines and connecting brands to culture in real time. By deploying bespoke intelligent agents across disciplines and delivering culturally relevant, high-impact creative and digital solutions, Monks solves key critical business challenges across the entire brand enterprise to help brands sustain long-term impact.

Monks was named a Contender in The Forrester Wave™: Global Marketing Services, ranks among Cannes Lions' Top 10 Creative Companies (2022-25) and remains the only partner featured in AdExchanger's Programmatic Power Players list every year (2020-24). Named Adweek's first AI Agency of the Year (2023) and The One Show's inaugural AI Pioneer Organization, Monks was also awarded Business Intelligence Group's 2025 Excellence in Artificial Intelligence Award in both the Organizational and AI Product categories. As a trusted partner to cutting-edge innovators in tech, Monks earned titles such as Optimizely Experimentation Partner of the Year (2025), runner-up for the Adobe Firefly Partner Award (2024), and Workato's AI Visionary Customer Impact Award (2024). Additionally, Monks achieved a record-breaking number of FWAs and continues to hold the most of any partner.

We are an equal-opportunity employer committed to building a respectful and empowering work environment for all people to freely express themselves amongst colleagues who embrace diversity in all respects. Including fresh voices and unique points of view in all aspects of our business not only creates an environment where we can all grow and thrive but also increases our potential to produce work that better represents—and resonates with—the world around us.

description:
Company Description

Supporting millions of Aussies since 1988 with low fees and competitive long-term performance. Profits back to members, not shareholders

Closing date: Wednesday 13th May 2026.

Please note Rest does not accept speculative resumes from recruitment agencies

Rest will review applications prior to the closing date and may close the role earlier

Job Description

Established in 1988, Rest is one of Australia’s largest profit-to-member superannuation funds.

We support more than two million members, with around $100 billion of funds under management and are recognised as a responsible investment leader*. We believe when members understand and engage with their super, they’re more likely to get a better retirement outcome.

Everything we do at Rest is underpinned by our values and behaviours, we want to Be Daring, Keep it Simple, Take Action and Have Grit. To put it simply we want our people to thrive and love the work they do.

The Senior Manager, Member Strategy is responsible for shaping Rest’s member and employer acquisition and retention strategies in alignment with the group strategy. It is a key connecting role between the group strategy and Member teams, hence with a direct reporting line to the GM, Member Value and a dotted line to the GM Strategy. The role brings together external market insights, Voice of Customer (VOC) analysis and portfolio analytics to ensure the delivery of Rest’s strategy is meaningfully shaped by the member voice and focused on the strongest value creation for members. The role also provides strategic guidance to the Member division leadership team on specific focus areas and initiatives.

Key Accountabilities/Responsibilities

Develop the member and employer acquisition, growth and retention plans ensuring alignment with Rest’s purpose and group strategy, and Member divisional operational requirements.
Monitor and analyse external market trends, competitor activity, regulatory developments and demographic shifts to proactively shape member and employer strategy and planning.
Translate enterprise strategy and market insights into clear, actionable strategic plans that support long-term member value.
Design and refine strategies to drive member acquisition, employer channel growth and improved member engagement across Rest’s diverse membership segments.
Design and deliver customer segmentation strategy, driving alignment to segment priorities and opportunities across the enterprise.
Establish data‑led retention strategies, identifying key drivers of member attrition and developing interventions to improve loyalty and tenure.
Partner with Strategy, Finance and relevant Member teams to develop business cases to support strategic recommendations.
Partner with Experience Design and Journeys teams to ensure member strategies translate into coherent, high-impact experiences across all channels.
Provide strategic advice to the GM Member Value, CMO, GM Strategy and leadership teams on member and employer value, growth opportunities and risk considerations.
Support enterprise prioritisation by advocating for the member voice across forums, governance bodies, and planning processes.
Contribute to preparation of strategy papers, Board/Committee materials and executive updates to ensure consistency, quality, and alignment.
Contribute to an engaged, high-performing team environment that promotes collaboration, accountability, and continuous learning.

Qualifications

Relevant Business, Commerce or Finance degree.

Required experience, understanding or credentials including:

Financial Services Expertise: demonstrable experience within superannuation or financial services, with a deep understanding of member, product and regulatory environments.
Core Strategic Capability and Skills: Demonstrable consulting experience with a focus in problem solving, strategy development and ability to support strategic thinking, ideally with experience in Customer Strategy, Go to Market Strategy and Growth.
Strategic and Commercial Acumen: Proven ability to apply commercial judgment to shape strategic direction, make critical decisions and deliver outcomes to defined timeframes.
Financial Acumen: Proven ability to model and demonstrate financial outcomes of strategic recommendations.
Innovation and Problem Solving: With applied experience in HCD and customer-led strategy to bring creativity and complex problem-solving skills, and the ability to design and deliver innovative solutions that enhance member experience.
AI and Digital Mindset: Passion for leveraging data, analytics, and emerging technologies to uncover insights, measure performance, and inform continuous improvement.
Collaborative Leadership: Strong influencing skills with the ability to lead through others, fostering alignment and collaboration across diverse functions and teams. Demonstrated ability to manage senior stakeholders.
Attention to Detail and Perspective: Balances strategic vision with operational precision, ensuring initiatives are both high-impact and well-executed.
Effective Communication: Exceptional written and verbal communication skills, with the ability to translate complex insights and strategies into clear, compelling narratives.
Resilience and Prioritisation: Skilled in managing multiple priorities in complex, fast-paced environments while maintaining focus on strategic outcomes and member value.

Additional Information

Our benefits have been designed so you can tailor your experience with us and include:

Personal and professional development opportunities
Hybrid working
Purchase leave scheme and gender neutral 16 weeks paid parental leave
Super Contribution Continuation for 12 Months of parental leave
Linkedin Learning
Income Protection Insurance
Rest Excellence awards (peer recognition awards based on Rest’s values and behaviours)
Rest Stops - meeting free breaks

If you share our values, believe you can help make a difference for our members and want to be part of a leading superannuation fund with a Super culture, please click Apply Now.

Rest is committed to creating a flexible work environment and culture that embraces diversity, equity, and inclusion - where people feel welcome, safe to be themselves and inspired to do their best.

We value the different backgrounds, lived experiences and abilities our diverse team brings. We welcome and encourage applications from candidates of all ages, cultural backgrounds, faiths, gender identities, sexual orientations and thinking styles. This includes people with disability, neurodiverse individuals, Aboriginal & Torres Strait Islander peoples and those with disrupted work history due to career or other breaks.

Please note only people with the right to work in Australia will be considered.

*Funds under management as at 31 July 2025. Rest is recognised as a Responsible Investment Leader by the Responsible Investment Association Australia (RIAA) in its Responsible Investment Benchmark Report 2022.
descriptionHTML:
Company Description


Supporting millions of Aussies since 1988 with low fees and competitive long-term performance. Profits back to members, not shareholders

Closing date: Wednesday 13th May 2026.

Please note Rest does not accept speculative resumes from recruitment agencies

Rest will review applications prior to the closing date and may close the role earlier


Job Description


Established in 1988, Rest is one of Australia’s largest profit-to-member superannuation funds.

We support more than two million members, with around $100 billion of funds under management and are recognised as a responsible investment leader*. We believe when members understand and engage with their super, they’re more likely to get a better retirement outcome.

Everything we do at Rest is underpinned by our values and behaviours, we want to Be Daring, Keep it Simple, Take Action and Have Grit. To put it simply we want our people to thrive and love the work they do.

The Senior Manager, Member Strategy is responsible for shaping Rest’s member and employer acquisition and retention strategies in alignment with the group strategy. It is a key connecting role between the group strategy and Member teams, hence with a direct reporting line to the GM, Member Value and a dotted line to the GM Strategy. The role brings together external market insights, Voice of Customer (VOC) analysis and portfolio analytics to ensure the delivery of Rest’s strategy is meaningfully shaped by the member voice and focused on the strongest value creation for members. The role also provides strategic guidance to the Member division leadership team on specific focus areas and initiatives.

Key Accountabilities/Responsibilities

  • Develop the member and employer acquisition, growth and retention plans ensuring alignment with Rest’s purpose and group strategy, and Member divisional operational requirements.
  • Monitor and analyse external market trends, competitor activity, regulatory developments and demographic shifts to proactively shape member and employer strategy and planning.
  • Translate enterprise strategy and market insights into clear, actionable strategic plans that support long-term member value.
  • Design and refine strategies to drive member acquisition, employer channel growth and improved member engagement across Rest’s diverse membership segments.
  • Design and deliver customer segmentation strategy, driving alignment to segment priorities and opportunities across the enterprise.
  • Establish data‑led retention strategies, identifying key drivers of member attrition and developing interventions to improve loyalty and tenure.
  • Partner with Strategy, Finance and relevant Member teams to develop business cases to support strategic recommendations.
  • Partner with Experience Design and Journeys teams to ensure member strategies translate into coherent, high-impact experiences across all channels.
  • Provide strategic advice to the GM Member Value, CMO, GM Strategy and leadership teams on member and employer value, growth opportunities and risk considerations.
  • Support enterprise prioritisation by advocating for the member voice across forums, governance bodies, and planning processes.
  • Contribute to preparation of strategy papers, Board/Committee materials and executive updates to ensure consistency, quality, and alignment.
  • Contribute to an engaged, high-performing team environment that promotes collaboration, accountability, and continuous learning.

Qualifications

Required experience, understanding or credentials including:

  • Financial Services Expertise: demonstrable experience within superannuation or financial services, with a deep understanding of member, product and regulatory environments.
  • Core Strategic Capability and Skills: Demonstrable consulting experience with a focus in problem solving, strategy development and ability to support strategic thinking, ideally with experience in Customer Strategy, Go to Market Strategy and Growth.
  • Strategic and Commercial Acumen: Proven ability to apply commercial judgment to shape strategic direction, make critical decisions and deliver outcomes to defined timeframes.
  • Financial Acumen: Proven ability to model and demonstrate financial outcomes of strategic recommendations.
  • Innovation and Problem Solving: With applied experience in HCD and customer-led strategy to bring creativity and complex problem-solving skills, and the ability to design and deliver innovative solutions that enhance member experience.
  • AI and Digital Mindset: Passion for leveraging data, analytics, and emerging technologies to uncover insights, measure performance, and inform continuous improvement.
  • Collaborative Leadership: Strong influencing skills with the ability to lead through others, fostering alignment and collaboration across diverse functions and teams. Demonstrated ability to manage senior stakeholders.
  • Attention to Detail and Perspective: Balances strategic vision with operational precision, ensuring initiatives are both high-impact and well-executed.
  • Effective Communication: Exceptional written and verbal communication skills, with the ability to translate complex insights and strategies into clear, compelling narratives.
  • Resilience and Prioritisation: Skilled in managing multiple priorities in complex, fast-paced environments while maintaining focus on strategic outcomes and member value.

Additional Information


Our benefits have been designed so you can tailor your experience with us and include:

  • Personal and professional development opportunities
  • Hybrid working
  • Purchase leave scheme and gender neutral 16 weeks paid parental leave
  • Super Contribution Continuation for 12 Months of parental leave

positionName: Delivery Lead (Agentic Commerce Initiative) - Contract or Permanent
description:
Company Description

At NCS Australia, we believe in doing technology services better. Our commitment to quality, focus on people, and willingness to challenge traditional thinking set us apart. Our team brings this belief to life by partnering with our clients and communities to make tomorrow together.

We are committed to creating an environment that prioritises innovation, collaboration, and purposeful work. Our diverse team is empowered to make a meaningful impact with curiosity, creativity and resilience to shape better outcomes. Join us and accept the challenge of creating a better tomorrow.

Please note we would look at either a contract or permanent person for this position

Job Description

We are looking for a highly organized and strategic Delivery Lead to drive the execution of our Agentic Commerce initiative. This is a high-visibility role responsible for managing a complex, multi-workstream program that sits at the cutting edge of AI and digital commerce.

You will be the operational heartbeat of the program, ensuring that internal product teams, technical workstreams, and global partners are aligned and delivering against our strategic roadmap.

The Role

As the Delivery Lead, you will establish the governance and execution framework required to move the Agentic Commerce initiative from vision to reality. You will manage the intersection of product optimization, technical visibility enhancements, and a major strategic partnership.

Key Responsibilities:

Program Execution: Lead the end-to-end delivery of the Agentic Commerce initiative, ensuring all workstreams are tracking toward key milestones.
Governance Framework: Design and implement robust internal and external governance structures to manage decision-making and accountability.
Stakeholder & Partner Management: Act as the primary operational interface for our Google partnership and internal product leadership.
Dependency Management: Identify and resolve cross-functional dependencies across technical, product, and data workstreams.
Tracking & Reporting: Establish recurring governance cycles and program tracking to ensure transparent reporting on progress and risks.

Qualifications

Technical Requirements & Experience

We are seeking a delivery professional who thrives in high-scale, fast-paced environments where the technology is rapidly evolving.

Project/Program Leadership: Proven experience managing complex, multi-workstream initiatives with a track record of successful delivery.
Strategic Governance: Strong capability in designing governance frameworks and managing high-level stakeholder relationships.
Partnership Management: Experience managing external global partnerships and complex vendor relationships.
Agile & Tech Savvy: Deep familiarity with agile methodologies and a solid understanding of AI/product technology delivery.
Communication: Exceptional ability to translate technical milestones into executive-level progress reports.

Additional Information

Why join us:

NCS Australia is where you can feel at home, nurturing your talents and skills as we make tomorrow together, one day at a time. Our benefits include paid parental leave, initiatives focused on your well-being and discounted health insurance. You will also enjoy discounts on various products and services and be regularly recognised and rewarded for high performance. We are committed to your career development through our Capability Fingerprint, industry and partner training programs, special interest groups, and an AI-driven learning platform. No matter where you are in your career, we offer meaningful work and opportunities for growth.

NCS Australia is an equal-opportunity employer, and we take pride in our commitment to valuing and supporting our people and the communities we serve.We are dedicated to attracting, retaining and developing our people regardless of gender identity, ethnicity, sexual orientation, disability and age. Applications are encouraged from all sectors of the community and we strongly encourage applications from the Veterans, Aboriginal and/or Torres Strait Islander community.

At NCS Australia, we are committed to supporting adjustments throughout the recruitment and selection process, as well as during employment. We actively support and encourage people with disability to apply.

Agencies:

We’ve got this. We request that you do not contact NCS employees outside of the Talent Acquisition team. NCS exclusively accepts resumes from agencies on our preferred supplier panel through the NCS Agency Portal. Agencies that submit resumes must have a valid fee agreement and be assigned to the particular requisition by the Talent Acquisition team. Any resumes that are submitted outside of this process will become the sole property of NCS. If a candidate is hired outside of this process, no fee or payment will be given.

Work rights and background checks:

To be eligible for a position with us, applicants will need to have valid work rights for Australia and be willing to undergo a comprehensive background checking process, including probity and police checks

description:
Country/Region: AU
State: WA
City: Perth
Job ID: 16217
AI Program Lead
About Us

Fortescue is both a proud West Australian company and a global green solutions business. We are recognised for our culture, innovation and industry-leading development of infrastructure, mining assets and green energy initiatives.

Our Opportunity

Work Location: Perth Office - Fortescue's Perth office is located on the traditional lands of the Whadjuk people.

Roster: Monday to Friday

As the AI Program Lead, you will play a central role in coordinating and governing Fortescue’s AI program. This role is responsible for driving program-level discipline across a portfolio of AI domains and products, ensuring the pipeline is captured, prioritised, tracked and reported effectively for senior leaders and the Board. You will bring a strong PMO and program management capability, enabling quality execution, clear governance, and transparency of progress, risks and decisions across the program.

Key to the role is supporting the Product owners on valuation and value attribution. You will work closely with product teams, Finance Business Partners, FP&A and business stakeholders to ensure value cases are robust, assumptions are sound, Fortescue value standards are consistently applied, and commitments are tracked through to realised outcomes. This role will help embed a disciplined, credible approach to measuring and reporting value from AI investments.

The AI Program Lead will work across product teams, domain leaders, finance, strategy and technology stakeholders to support the successful delivery of AI-enabled products aligned to Fortescue’s business priorities.

Key Responsibilities

Manage program governance rhythms, including stage gate and investment review processes in our Steering Committee meetings, documenting actions and follow-up. Ensure program and governance standards are consistently applied across the AI portfolio
Maintain a high-quality view of the AI portfolio, including status, risks, dependencies, decisions, milestones and delivery confidence
Prepare high-quality reporting for directors, executive leaders and the Board, including updates on pipeline health, delivery progress, risks, value and key decisions required
Partner with product teams to support the development of robust business cases for AI initiatives using Fortescue value standards and frameworks, ensuring benefits are measured consistently and credibly
Work closely with Finance Business Partners, FP&A and relevant business stakeholders to validate assumptions, inputs, baselines and calculation methods
Track value commitments and delivery performance against approved expectations, highlighting variances and required interventions
Ensure value attribution is transparent, evidence-based and aligned with finance governance requirements
Identify cross-program risks, issues and interdependencies, and work with stakeholders to manage them proactively.

Qualifications and Experience

Bachelor’s degree in Business, Finance, Engineering, Technology or a related field
Significant experience in program management, PMO, strategy, finance business partnering, or a similar role in a complex environment
Proven experience coordinating portfolios or large cross-functional programs with strong governance and executive reporting requirements
Strong capability in business case development, valuation, benefits management or value tracking
Excellent written and verbal communication skills, including experience preparing materials for senior executives and boards
Demonstrated ability to manage multiple stakeholders, drive follow-through, and improve quality and discipline across a program
Familiarity with AI, digital, technology or software delivery programs will be highly regarded
Highly organised and detail-oriented, with strong ownership and accountability for outcomes
Strong commercial and financial acumen, able to navigate ambiguity and bring clarity to complex problems
Collaborative and influential, with strong stakeholder management skills and the confidence to challenge assumptions constructively.

Our Commitment

Fortescue is deeply committed to providing a safe culture that builds respect, fosters inclusiveness, and values diversity. We celebrate individual strengths and team members from all backgrounds are encouraged to bring their whole selves to work. Our global workforce drives and promotes an inclusive culture, both within our organisation and throughout the communities we interact with across the world. Diverse backgrounds include First Nations Peoples, people with differing abilities, LGBTIQ+ community, gender, neurodiverse, cultural diversity, all age groups, and those with an intersectional or multiple diverse characteristics. We encourage candidates from all backgrounds to apply.

https://fortescue.com/careers

Internal Candidates / Current Contractors please apply via Success Factors Careers Portal. For further information on how to apply please visit the Fortescue Hub.

Fortescue reserves the right to close applications early should a suitable pool of candidates be identified. Fortescue will never contact you to ask for payment of any kind, whether directly or through a third party.

positionName: Data Scientist (AI Visibility & Commerce Analytics) - Contract
description:
Company Description

At NCS Australia, we believe in doing technology services better. Our commitment to quality, focus on people, and willingness to challenge traditional thinking set us apart. Our team brings this belief to life by partnering with our clients and communities to make tomorrow together.

We are committed to creating an environment that prioritises innovation, collaboration, and purposeful work. Our diverse team is empowered to make a meaningful impact with curiosity, creativity and resilience to shape better outcomes. Join us and accept the challenge of creating a better tomorrow.

Please note we would look at either a contract or permanent person for this position

Job Description

We are seeking a Data Scientist to join our team in Sydney. This is a pioneering role focused on defining how we measure success in the "Agentic Web." You will be responsible for building the measurement framework that tracks how AI agents discover our products, how they rank them, and how effectively they convert those discoveries into commerce.

As the bridge between raw data and AI strategy, you will provide the insights necessary to optimize our presence across the global AI ecosystem.

The Role

Your mission is to move beyond traditional web analytics into the world of Generative Engine Optimization (GEO). You will establish the baselines, metrics, and tools required to understand how LLMs and AI agents perceive our product suite.

Key Responsibilities:

Framework Design: Establish a comprehensive measurement framework for agent visibility and commerce performance.
Metric Definition: Define and track core metrics for "agent discoverability"—understanding how and why an AI agent selects a specific product.
Tooling & Integration: Integrate advanced measurement tools and data platforms to capture non-traditional traffic and agent interactions.
Performance Baselining: Establish current performance baselines and build the infrastructure for ongoing monitoring of AI platform rankings.
Impact Analysis: Conduct deep-dive analyses on how technical optimizations (such as LLMs.txt, Schema updates, and crawler configurations) impact product discovery.

Qualifications

Technical Requirements

We are looking for a data scientist who is comfortable with high-ambiguity environments and possesses a deep curiosity about AI behavior.

Analytics Expertise: Strong experience in designing analytics and metrics frameworks from the ground up.
Visibility Tracking: Proven proficiency with SEO/GEO analytics, web visibility tracking, and data integration platforms.
AI Knowledge: A solid understanding of LLM behavior, AI platform mechanics, and how product ranking algorithms function in a generative context.
Data Engineering: Experience setting up data collection pipelines for emerging technology domains.

Additional Information

Why join us:

NCS Australia is where you can feel at home, nurturing your talents and skills as we make tomorrow together, one day at a time. Our benefits include paid parental leave, initiatives focused on your well-being and discounted health insurance. You will also enjoy discounts on various products and services and be regularly recognised and rewarded for high performance. We are committed to your career development through our Capability Fingerprint, industry and partner training programs, special interest groups, and an AI-driven learning platform. No matter where you are in your career, we offer meaningful work and opportunities for growth.

NCS Australia is an equal-opportunity employer, and we take pride in our commitment to valuing and supporting our people and the communities we serve.We are dedicated to attracting, retaining and developing our people regardless of gender identity, ethnicity, sexual orientation, disability and age. Applications are encouraged from all sectors of the community and we strongly encourage applications from the Veterans, Aboriginal and/or Torres Strait Islander community.

At NCS Australia, we are committed to supporting adjustments throughout the recruitment and selection process, as well as during employment. We actively support and encourage people with disability to apply.

Agencies:

We’ve got this. We request that you do not contact NCS employees outside of the Talent Acquisition team. NCS exclusively accepts resumes from agencies on our preferred supplier panel through the NCS Agency Portal. Agencies that submit resumes must have a valid fee agreement and be assigned to the particular requisition by the Talent Acquisition team. Any resumes that are submitted outside of this process will become the sole property of NCS. If a candidate is hired outside of this process, no fee or payment will be given.

No matter where you are in your career, we offer meaningful work and opportunities for growth.

positionName: Senior Software Engineer - Full Stack, with a FE focus
description:
Title: Senior Software Engineering Consultant

Position: Permanent Full Time

Location: This is a hybrid role based in Sydney with a blend of working from home & on-site with clients. You must have a current visa and full working rights in Australia.

About the role:

As a DiUS consultant, you will be partnering with clients to develop and deliver custom software products and digital solutions; getting the most out of their technology landscape, and leaving them in a better place. This hands-on role is more than just systems and coding - it’s also about asking the right questions, understanding the needs, thinking outside the box to creatively develop solutions, driving best practice, developing strong client relationships and collaborating on the bigger picture.

Throughout your time with DiUS, you will work on different business projects in a range of digital web, data and mobile capabilities, while increasingly factoring in AI powered engineering practices.

This will accelerate your growth as each client will have different technologies, tools & frameworks, as well as different business challenges, solutions, and stakeholders. We work on problems our clients aren’t able to solve themselves.

About DiUS:

DiUS is an Australian B2B technology services company with an excellent reputation as a trusted consulting partner. We’re a go-to company for helping organisations of all sizes tackle hard engineering problems, their delivery practices, modernise applications, and design, build and deliver a new product to market.

We have a powerful ability to figure out the best path forward for each of our clients by drawing upon our broad range of deep and specialist capabilities: software engineering, experience design, data and analytics, machine learning and AI.

About You:

You will have a dynamic background as a full stack software engineer, proficient in both back end and front end development, with lots of breadth across each. You enjoy variety and have spent time learning different tech stacks in your previous roles, adapting to different environments.

Experienced across the entire SDLC, you can effectively design customised solutions, deploy to cloud platforms and work with Infrastructure as Code, with a focus on building secure, scalable, and high-performing systems.

Whether your experience is from consulting, or working across multiple projects, you have a natural leadership style that builds trust with the people you work with. You have experience leading projects and small teams end to end, and you are flexible and adaptable, navigating the grey areas comfortably as you step into, and sometimes lead, different projects - you may be designing a new system and platform features this month, and then building agents next month.

Main Tech Skills Needed:

Full-stack software engineer with 10+ years of experience in various back end and front end tech languages such as: Java, Node, NestJS, C#/ .net, Python, JavaScript, Typescript, React, Next.js, Angular, Vue

Strong experience in React & Next.js is required

Purposeful design and implementation of software solutions (yes, from scratch)

Commercial experience deploying code into the cloud (AWS, Azure, GCP) & IaC tools

Solid level of proficiency in modern architectural practices

Experience in AI assisted engineering across the software development lifecycle

Experience in using LLMs, RAG and agentic frameworks to deliver AI solutions

Main Consulting Skills Needed:

Able to openly collaborate and listen; understand and refine requirements, & iterate on delivery outcomes

Able to problem solve; identify purposeful solutions, factoring in how to integrate into a wider solution/system

Able to navigate ambiguity; land on your feet and find the way forward

Adaptable to different paces and environments; from the complexity of a large enterprise or influencing in a fast-paced start-up

Excellent communication skills

Able to efficiently self manage and coordinate / prioritise tasks and workload

Curious with a growth mindset, always seeking to learn and investing in own development

Passionate about spreading change through thought leadership, mentoring, meetups, writing blogs or staying active on GitHub.

A change champion who can influence and drive transformation

Want to know more about this role, or think you've got what it takes? Apply now!

Note to Recruitment Agencies: we got this - no need to reach out please!

description:
Company Description

At NCS Australia, we believe in doing technology services better. Our commitment to quality, focus on people, and willingness to challenge traditional thinking set us apart. Our team brings this belief to life by partnering with our clients and communities to make tomorrow together.

We are committed to creating an environment that prioritises innovation, collaboration, and purposeful work. Our diverse team is empowered to make a meaningful impact with curiosity, creativity and resilience to shape better outcomes. Join us and accept the challenge of creating a better tomorrow.

Please note we would look at either a contract or permanent person for this position

Job Description

We are looking for a Senior API Engineer to join our team in Sydney. This is a high-impact role focused on building the bridge between our core products and the next generation of AI agents. You will be responsible for developing the API infrastructure that enables AI agents to discover, interact with, and execute transactions across our product suite.

This role sits at the intersection of product innovation and cutting-edge tech, working in close collaboration with our Google partnership and internal product teams to ensure our ecosystem is AI-ready.

The Role

As a Senior API Engineer, your primary mission is to ensure our API infrastructure is robust, scalable, and optimized for Unified Customer Platform (UCP) integration. You will play a lead role in assessing API readiness and building the feeds necessary for AI agent discoverability.

Key Responsibilities:

Infrastructure & Integration: Build and maintain high-performance API infrastructure and microservices to enable seamless AI agent access.
Strategic Assessment: Evaluate current API readiness for UCP integration and contribute to the technical roadmap for the remainder of the year.
AI Readiness: Implement product feeds and ensure APIs support complex transaction capabilities and agent discoverability.
Automation Innovation: Design and build automated solutions for AI discoverability, such as periodic scraping and publishing of LLMs.txt files to guide Large Language Models.
Stakeholder Collaboration: Work closely with the Tech Lead on UCP design and maintain a strong technical relationship with our Google partners.

Qualifications

Technical Requirements

We are looking for an engineer who thrives in complex, high-scale environments and has a deep understanding of how APIs power modern ecosystems.

API Expertise: Extensive experience in API builds, microservices architecture, and API gateway patterns.
Cloud Infrastructure: Strong hands-on experience with GCP (Google Cloud Platform) services.
Integration Experience: Proven track record of working with Google partnership integrations or similar large-scale third-party ecosystems.
AI Familiarity: A solid understanding of how AI agents and LLMs consume data (experience with LLMs.txt or similar machine-readable documentation is a plus).

Additional Information

Why join us:

NCS Australia is where you can feel at home, nurturing your talents and skills as we make tomorrow together, one day at a time. Our benefits include paid parental leave, initiatives focused on your well-being and discounted health insurance. You will also enjoy discounts on various products and services and be regularly recognised and rewarded for high performance. We are committed to your career development through our Capability Fingerprint, industry and partner training programs, special interest groups, and an AI-driven learning platform. No matter where you are in your career, we offer meaningful work and opportunities for growth.

NCS Australia is an equal-opportunity employer, and we take pride in our commitment to valuing and supporting our people and the communities we serve.We are dedicated to attracting, retaining and developing our people regardless of gender identity, ethnicity, sexual orientation, disability and age. Applications are encouraged from all sectors of the community and we strongly encourage applications from the Veterans, Aboriginal and/or Torres Strait Islander community.

At NCS Australia, we are committed to supporting adjustments throughout the recruitment and selection process, as well as during employment. We actively support and encourage people with disability to apply.

Agencies:

We’ve got this. We request that you do not contact NCS employees outside of the Talent Acquisition team. NCS exclusively accepts resumes from agencies on our preferred supplier panel through the NCS Agency Portal. Agencies that submit resumes must have a valid fee agreement and be assigned to the particular requisition by the Talent Acquisition team. Any resumes that are submitted outside of this process will become the sole property of NCS. If a candidate is hired outside of this process, no fee or payment will be given.

Work rights and background checks:

To be eligible for a position with us, applicants will need to have valid work rights for Australia and be willing to undergo a comprehensive background checking process, including probity and police checks

description:
Forward Networks is transforming how the world's most complex networks are managed and secured. Founded in 2013 by four Stanford Ph.D.s, we built the industry's first network digital twin, a mathematically precise model of the production network that gives IT teams unmatched visibility, verification, and agility across every major cloud and vendor environment.

Our customers include global leaders such as Goldman Sachs, PayPal, S&P Global, IBM, and Dell, as well as fast-growing enterprises and government agencies. According to IDC, Forward Networks customers realize an average of $14.2 million in annual benefits through improved efficiency and security.

Backed by world-class investors including Andreessen Horowitz, Goldman Sachs, MSD Partners, and Threshold Ventures, Forward Networks offers a people-centric, innovative culture where brilliant minds are shaping the future of network reliability, security, and AI-ready operations.

Forward Networks is looking for a Customer Care Engineer (Technical Support Engineer), Australia

Do you want to create a category and help build a special company?

Join a company that has been in the market 6+ years and has some of the top Federal agencies and F500/Global 2000 already buying and referenceable.

We are building a special team and hope you consider us if you want to have the experience of changing the networking world as we know it.

What you'll do:

As a Customer Care Engineer (Technical Support Engineer) for our Support team, you will be responsible for providing world class post-sales customer support and technical leadership to our client base.

Responsibilities:

Providing triage, prioritizing, and resolving technical issues for our top customers.

Identifying s/w bugs, recreate customer issues, and test/verify versions with s/w fix.

Working with Forward Engineering teams to resolve customer issues.

Escalating unresolved issues in a timely manner and maintaining direct communications with all stakeholders.

Requirements:

3+ years of technical customer support experience.

Experience working at a technical support center assisting a variety of customers.

Ability to cover NAM EST time zones

Participate in after hours on-call rotation

Experience:

Strong understanding of fundamentals in Networking, Cloud or Security

Strong written and interpersonal communications skills

Excellent analytical & problem-solving skills, combined with the ability to provide quick resolution to problems

Experience supporting a multi-vendor network (e.g. A10, Arista, Cisco, F5, Fortinet, Juniper, Palo Alto Networks, AWS, GCP etc.)

Proven ability to work cross-functionally within a team-oriented environment.

Highly Desired:

 B.S. Computer Science or equivalent educational experience.

Python scripting experience

Kubernetes experience

Linux fundamentals

API debugging

Cloud networking (AWS, Azure, GCP)

description:
Stake is looking for an AI Creative Strategist to join us at our brand new Sydney CBD location within a hybrid working model!

We're an Australian investing platform that helps ambitious people confidently grow their wealth. By offering seamless, immersive access to the share markets, we've empowered a new wave of investors. Stake has grown fast from a fintech startup into a robust financial services company. Today we have 750,000+ customers and more than A$5 billion under administration.

At Stake, we break barriers in everything we do. Just like our customers, we're driven by ambition. Our desire for continuous growth is what creates the collaborative and high-performance environment we work in. Though we're spread around the world, we're a unified team moving towards a shared vision: to unleash the investor in everyone.

Join us as we continue to reshape the financial industry, inspiring investor confidence and celebrating our wins along the way

About this role

As the AI Creative Strategist, you will be responsible for the end-to-end creative production and output that powers our ability to efficiently acquire users through paid social. Reporting to the Growth Lead, you will own a high-output creative production engine that drives strategy, execution, and optimisation across social-first acquisition channels, with a focus on scaling creative volume and performance. You will combine performance marketing, creative strategy, and production to build a system designed for speed, scale, and iteration. This role shifts acquisition from channel management to creative systems, where success is driven by the volume, quality, and velocity of ad creative. You will be a key driver in achieving growth targets, working cross-functionally to translate insights into assets, and assets into measurable acquisition outcomes.

This is a unique opportunity where you're able to utilise the most disruptive technology in performance marketing and will be given the reigns to create your vision for a world class production engine.

Key deliverables

Creative-Led Acquisition Strategy and Planning Develop and execute acquisition strategies that prioritise creative output as the primary growth lever. Define clear targets, KPIs, and budgets across channels, with a focus on scaling creative volume, speed, and efficiency.
Creative System Development Build and implement a modular creative framework. Structure ads into repeatable components (e.g. hooks, messaging, product, calls to action) and enable systematic production, testing, and iteration at scale.
Campaign Development and Execution Lead the development and execution of data-driven campaigns. Ensure effective targeting, messaging, and creative output across all channels, with a focus on continuous production and rapid iteration rather than one-off campaigns.
Testing and Experimentation Establish a rigorous testing framework for creative. Run high-frequency experiments, analyse performance, and iterate on promising concepts. Drive a culture of continuous experimentation and learning.
AI-Driven Creative Optimisation Leverage AI tools to enhance creative production, including generating visuals, voiceovers, and variations. Use AI to accelerate workflows, improve performance, and scale iteration.
Cross-Functional Collaboration Work closely with Product, Analytics, Design, and Marketing teams to align acquisition efforts with product roadmap, user experience, and broader marketing initiatives.

About you

Generally, Creative Strategists at Stake have a strong track record in acquisition or performance marketing, with 4+ years of relevant experience. They have a deep understanding of paid channels and a proven ability to drive measurable growth outcomes. They combine analytical thinking with creative judgment, and are comfortable working across strategy and execution. They are proactive, results-oriented, and thrive in fast-paced environments where testing and iteration are core to success. Experience working with creative production, UGC, or AI tools is highly regarded.

At Stake, we're more interested in your attitude than your resume. If you believe this is a role you want to jump into, please apply and let us know why in your cover letter!

Benefits of working at Stake

You'll have the opportunity to work with autonomy while learning from some of the best talent in the industry, playing a meaningful role in building a company that has loads of positive momentum and excitement around it. Having said that, we also have perks.

Please see on our Careers website (https://hellostake.com/au/careers) the list of benefits under our Grow with Stake program. They include:

Career relevant development and training with professional development allowance
Up to 16 weeks of paid parental leave
Childcare allowance
Sonder - healthcare, safety support and EAP for you and your immediate family
Additional leave types (Swap days, Bear Days, TOIL, Compassionate, Sorry Business)
Discretionary performance bonus and talent referral bonus
Employee Share Options scheme for senior leaders
Stake products: SMSF 30% discount on yearly membership fee, $295 AUD annual trading credit and Stake Black account
Recognition awards and team celebrations

Stake is an equal opportunity employer that values diversity. We're proactive about providing an inclusive work environment and do not discriminate based on race, religion, nationality, gender, sexual orientation, age or disability status. We're also endorsed by WORK180 as a great employer for women.

It all comes together to explain why Stake placed 1st in the 2024 AFR BOSS Best Places to Work list for the Banking, Superannuation & Financial Services category, across all of Australia and New Zealand.

So what happens next?

Once we've received your application, we'll give it the attention it deserves and you'll hear from us either way. We're committed to responding to every applicant. If we think you'd be a good addition to our team, we'll give you a call and from there, you'll cruise through our recruitment process.

To all recruitment agencies: Stake does not accept agency resumes. Please do not forward resumes to our jobs alias or directly to Stake employees. Stake is not responsible for any fees related to unsolicited resumes.

Once we've received your application, we'll give it the attention it deserves and you'll hear from us either way. We're committed to responding to every applicant. If we think you'd be a good addition to our team, we'll give you a call and from there, you'll cruise through our recruitment process.

To all recruitment agencies: Stake does not accept agency resumes. Please do not forward resumes to our jobs alias or directly to Stake employees. Stake is not responsible for any fees related to unsolicited resumes.

description:
You will primarily support the business with incident and issue management, test and monitor the effectiveness of controls, compliance with obligations and risk appetite, as well as provide risk insights by identifying new / emerging risks and regulatory change, to support the consistent delivery of meaningful value to our customers. Where risk events or compliance breaches occur, you will drive, track and monitor remediation and treatment of risks outside of appetite to fulfill our commitment to our customers and communities.

You will support the consistent implementation of change by ensuring that Great Southern Bank’s frameworks, policies, supporting standards, processes and procedures are applied, with a focus on embedding divisional senior leader accountability for risk management.

You’ll make an impact by:

Act as a trust business partner to help manage incidents and issues
Assisting the team in coordinating risk and control identification
Supporting risk assessments and managing risk acceptances
Working with the Senior Advisors and stakeholders to set key risk indicators (KRIs), thresholds and triggers
Perform and Review control design and operating effectiveness
Documenting and providing assurance that reasonable steps are being taken to meet accountabilities
Leveraging AI tools to improve processes and efficiency

Do things differently with us

To succeed in this role, you will have:

Minimum 1-3 years in financial services with a sound understanding of operational risk and compliance in the financial services industry
Demonstrated Experience in Financial Services Operations, Risk or Business Controls function
Experience in data analysis using SQL and Data Bricks is highly desirable
Experience in AI Risk and usage of AI tools is desirable
Controls testing and assurance activities highly desirable
Degree in management, business, finance or related discipline (desirable)
Knowledge of relevant Finance industry standards, acts, codes and legislation (e.g., APRA, Consumer Credit Code, Credit Union Code of Practice, Privacy Act, Trade Practices Act, FSRA) (essential)

Why Great Southern Bank?

Whatever your role with us, you’ll be part of a force for good. We’re owned by our customers right here in Australia, so our success benefits everyday people and families. Working at Great Southern Bank, you’ll also enjoy a range of fantastic benefits, some examples include:

Helping you unlock a brighter future by investing in your development and wellbeing, offering you study leave, a day for you and professional wellbeing coaching.
Benefits that support you and your wellbeing so that you can thrive at work and at home, today and tomorrow. Enjoy access to our wellbeing centre and the flexibility to work from home two days a week.
Helping you to achieve your financial goals and positively impact your family and community. We offer discounts on a wide range of Great Southern Bank products and services such as savings and transaction accounts, insurance, home loans and more!

Imagine working for a bank that truly helps people. You can.

Questions? Please contact our Talent Acquisition team at careers@gsb.com.au

At Great Southern Bank, we’re building a workplace where everyone belongs. We value diverse backgrounds, cultures, abilities and experiences - including Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander peoples, veterans, people with disability, and the LGBTQ+ community - because it makes us stronger for our people, customers and communities. Through flexible working, collaboration and fair, inclusive decision-making, we empower our people to do their best every day.

We move with purpose, are curious about what’s next, remain focused on what matters most, and are always ready to find smarter ways to help our customers and communities thrive. Our people bring energy and initiative to a fast-changing environment, making thoughtful decisions that balance doing good for our customers with driving sustainable success for our business. If you enjoy working in a place where priorities evolve, ideas are valued, and every action contributes to something bigger, you’ll feel right at home here at Great Southern Bank.

description:
AI Job Summary
Have Vocational Registration or Fellowship of the Royal Australian College of General Practitioners.
Relevant experience delivering health care to Aboriginal communities as a senior team member, with ability to lead GPs.
Knowledge of the Medicare billing framework and willingness to appropriately maximize Medicare billing.
Sign up or log in to analyse my fit
Role Type
On-site • Permanent • Full-time • Executive
Pay Rate
310000 AUD – 355000 AUD (Annum)
Description

About the Opportunity

Are you passionate about making an impact while working in a dynamic and supportive team?

Join Puntukurnu Aboriginal Medical Services (PAMS) as a Senior Medical Officer

As a member of a multidisciplinary team, the Senior Medical Officer provides high quality, client focused and culturally appropriate primary health care services to the Aboriginal people of Newman and remote communities of Jigalong, Punmu, Parnngurr and Kunawarritji.

Key Responsibilities:

·Lead clinical governance, quality improvement, and accreditation (AGPAL) activities
·Deliver high-quality, culturally appropriate GP services in a multidisciplinary team
·Drive improved health outcomes through prevention, chronic disease management, and health promotion
·Provide strategic clinical advice and support organisational planning and service delivery
·Supervise and mentor clinical staff, supporting workforce development and capability
·Ensure best practice in documentation, Medicare billing, reporting, and KPI performance

About You

To be considered for this position, you must have:

·Uphold and contribute to the organisation’s mission, values, and objectives
·Comply with all policies, procedures, and relevant legislation
·Foster teamwork, open communication, and a positive workplace culture
·Participate in professional development, training, and Employee Development Days
·Support continuous quality improvement and quality management systems
·Adhere to Work Health & Safety requirements, including identifying and managing risks.

The position involves working with a multicultural organisation where the majority of staff, clients and stakeholders identify as Aboriginal or Torres Strait Islander. Candidates must be able to demonstrate an understanding of the issues affecting Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander people and their ability to communicate sensitively and effectively with Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander people.

As per section 51 of the Equal Opportunity Act 1984 (WA) PAMS actively seeks to increase the diversity of our workforce to better meet the differing needs of our clients and stakeholders and to improve equal opportunity outcomes for our employees. Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander people are strongly encouraged to apply.

Please note: In addition to the above skills and experience, relevant licences and eligibility criteria apply (as per the Job Description).

Applicants for this position must be legally entitled to work in Australia and will be required to provide evidence of this entitlement. Acceptable evidence includes an Australian or New Zealand passport, birth certificate, or foreign passport with relevant visa.

Remuneration & Benefits

Attractive base salary of $310,000 – $355,000 per annum (excluding superannuation)
5 weeks’ annual leave plus 17.5% leave loading
11% superannuation
Salary packaging up to $15,900 plus entertainment benefits
Power subsidy allowance and PAMS-provided accommodation
Two return flights per year
Employee Assistance Program (EAP)
Retention incentives in line with PAMS Employee Remuneration Policy

About the Organisation

With an administrative hub based in Newman, in the Pilbara region of Western Australia, The Puntukurnu Aboriginal Medical Service (PAMS) provides holistic primary health care to the individuals and families within the remote communities of Jigalong, Parnngurr, Punmu and Kunawarritji.

PAMS aims to improve the standard of health of the Martu people and all Aboriginal people living in the East Pilbara region of Western Australia through the provision of community based, comprehensive primary health care services. We exist to promote the health and well-being of individual community members and to relieve sickness and the suffering of the indigenous community as a whole. We are passionate about providing the highest standard of patient care whilst incorporating a holistic approach toward diagnosis and management of illness.

Application Process

We require you to submit:

A current comprehensive CV including the contact details of two current referees and;
A cover letter no more than 2 pages that clearly outlines the skills and experience you have gained to make you suitable for this position.

Screening and assessment will commence immediately.

For further information including, please contact the People and Culture Team on 08 9177 8307 or pamshr@puntukurnu.com.

description:
Work options: Hybrid

We are passionate about people making their own decisions about where and when they work. Our aim is to facilitate hybrid working (mix of in the office and from home) where possible to support our people to be effective, empowered, and productive to achieve both their career and personal goals. Because we recognise that working flexibly means different things to different people, flexible working exists in many forms.

Who are we?

Humanforceʼs vision is to make work easier and life better for frontline and flexible workforces.

Humanforce provides the market leading, best-in-one human capital management (HCM) suite for frontline and flexible workforces - without compromise. Our employee centered, intelligent and compliant HCM suite is highly integrated and composable, and consists of Workforce Management (WFM), HR, Talent, Payroll, and Wellbeing.

Humanforce has built strong foundations since its founding in 2002. We help over 2300 customers and almost 1 million employees under management in 30+ countries, across a wide range of industries, including aged, child and health care; education; hospitality; retail; local government and more. Today, we have offices across Australia, New Zealand, United Kingdom, North America and the Philippines.

Customers choose Humanforce because we enable them to deliver an exceptional employee experience, build a compelling employee value proposition, and connect the flow of the worlds talent with the growth, productivity and efficiency objectives of frontline and flexible workforces.

AI at Humanforce

At Humanforce, we're not experimenting at the edges of AI — we're redesigning the core of how we operate. We see AI as a fundamental shift, not an incremental improvement, and we're deliberately moving toward becoming an AI-native organisation.

That means rethinking how work gets done end-to-end: how we build our products, make decisions, serve customers, and scale impact. We're not layering AI onto old ways of working — we're redefining and reimagining our processes, tools, and team structures to take full advantage of what AI makes possible.

Who you are

You are a commercially and customer-minded product manager with domain expertise in HRIS or HR technology. You understand how HR practitioners and people teams actually work - not just in theory, but in practice and you bring that insight directly into how you define, prioritise, and ship product.

You thrive in a delivery-focused environment where you own your roadmap, make decisions with incomplete information, and move quickly. You're comfortable working deeply embedded within an engineering team, know how to build trust fast, and communicate clearly with both technical and non-technical stakeholders.

You'll drive product thinking, own near-term priorities including a critical position management capability, and contribute to the development of AI-powered HRIS features that are shaping the future of how HR teams operate.

What you will do
Own the product roadmap for your area of the HRM Tribe, driving discovery, prioritisation, and delivery of near-term capabilities — including key upcoming strategic projects; Humanforce's position management feature and the LMS product
Lead the development of HR agentic workflows: work with engineering to design, define, and ship AI-powered automation within the HRIS product that meaningfully improves how HR practitioners operate
Translate customer pain points into well-defined product requirements — writing clear PRDs, acceptance criteria, and specifications that engineering can act on without ambiguity
Be embedded in delivery: run sprint planning, backlog refinement, sprint reviews, and retrospectives as an active and accountable member of the engineering team
Build and maintain strong customer feedback loops — conducting discovery interviews, synthesising insights, and ensuring real customer needs shape every product decision
Partner closely with cross-functional stakeholders including engineering leads, design, sales, implementation, and customer success to align on priorities and communicate trade-offs
Represent your product area to internal leadership: provide clear visibility on roadmap progress, risks, and outcomes on a regular cadence
 What you’ll need
2 - 4 years of product management experience in a B2B SaaS environment, with a track record of owning features end-to-end from discovery through to launch
Strong HRIS or HR system experience, either as a PM who has built HR products, or as a practitioner or implementer with deep knowledge of how these systems work (position management, org hierarchies, leave accruals, and similar concepts)
Genuine customer acumen; you regularly talk to customers yourself, can distinguish between what people say they want and what they actually need, and let that insight drive product direction
Collaborative working style with the ability to operate effectively across multi-site engineering teams in Brisbane, Melbourne, and Sydney.
Clear, direct communicator who can make confident decisions, explain trade-offs to both technical and non-technical audiences, and deliver difficult messages constructively.
Comfort with ambiguity and pace, you can operate with a high degree of autonomy in a fast-moving, high-growth environment without needing a perfectly defined brief.
Demonstrated use of AI tools in your day-to-day work, or strong and genuine eagerness to adopt and apply them, including in the context of building AI-enabled products.
Some ‘nice to haves’
Experience with payroll systems or workforce management products
Hands-on familiarity with AI tools such as Claude, Cursor, or similar, and experience incorporating them into product workflows or building AI-adjacent features
Background in a PE-backed or high-growth technology business where pace, autonomy, and adaptability are table stakes
Our values
We are bold
We are all in
We are customer obsessed
We do what we say
We are good humans
Our approach to flexibility

We are passionate about people making their own decisions about where and when they work. Our hybrid model of minimum two days a week of in office moments supports flexibility tailored to individual and team needs, empowering people to achieve their career and personal goals.

Benefits
A truly flexible workplace through our Flex@HF approach
The opportunity to be part of a fast-growing global tech company
A focus on learning and development through Humanforce HR
A generous talent referral program – know great people, be rewarded
12 weeks paid parental leave for primary careers, 4 weeks for secondary
4 extra days leave to focus on your wellbeing
Contemporary and practical Employee Assistance Program
A cool reward and recognition program – shout to your colleagues and earn points to spend
Access to our own financial wellbeing platform Thrive – including earned wage access, tools to budget and save, perks and cashback across 100s of Australian retailers
Fun, collaborative culture with passionate people
A workplace where you can genuinely improve the world of work!

We are a diverse and dispersed organisation and are actively looking to grow our team with individuals from all diverse backgrounds. We encourage applicants from all backgrounds, cultures, ages, genders, neurodiversity, religions, sexual orientations, and experiences to apply.

  • Genuine customer acumen; you regularly talk to customers yourself, can distinguish between what people say they want and what they actually need, and let that insight drive product direction

  • Experience with payroll systems or workforce management products

Our values

  • We are bold

Our approach to flexibility

  • A truly flexible workplace through our Flex@HF approach
  • The opportunity to be part of a fast-growing global tech company

  • 4 extra days leave to focus on your wellbeing

  • Access to our own financial wellbeing platform Thrive – including earned wage access, tools to budget and save, perks and cashback across 100s of Australian retailers
  • Fun, collaborative culture with passionate people
  • A workplace where you can genuinely improve the world of work!

We are a diverse and dispersed organisation and are actively looking to grow our team with individuals from all diverse backgrounds. We encourage applicants from all backgrounds, cultures, ages, genders, neurodiversity, religions, sexual orientations, and experiences to apply.

description:
Create your best future and join Westpac as Head of Data & Intelligence

What’s the Role:

As Head of Data & Intelligence, this role is accountable for lifting the division’s data and analytics maturity through modern data foundations, reusable data products, and embedded decision intelligence. It leads the use of advanced analytics, AI and the Westpac Intelligence Layer to deliver trusted, scalable insight, improve decision‑making, and drive productivity across the business. The role owns divisional data and analytics prioritisation, value realisation and governance, while building sustainable capability and ensuring AI and analytics are delivered safely, responsibly and in line with enterprise standards

Key Responsibilities:

Design and deliver a modern, scalable data ecosystem that embeds analytics, decision intelligence and AI into customer and business journeys.
Accelerate insight‑to‑action by integrating data‑driven decisioning into day‑to‑day business and operational processes.
Drive measurable improvements in customer outcomes, including engagement, personalisation and NPS, while supporting sustainable revenue growth through data‑led decisions.
Ensure all data, analytics and AI initiatives deliver quantifiable value and clearly defined ROI.
Establish and maintain trusted, explainable and compliant data, analytics and automation solutions that operate within risk appetite.
Enable the safe and confident adoption of advanced analytics and AI at scale through strong governance and Responsible AI practices.
Build an AI‑enabled, data‑led division with decision‑making embedded into operating rhythms, supported by the Westpac Intelligence Layer and agentic capabilities.
Align workforce capability and operating model with the Strategic Workforce Plan to ensure long‑term sustainability.

What do I need?

Senior leadership experience delivering large‑scale data, analytics, AI or technology‑enabled transformation with measurable business outcomes.
Experience across Enterprise functions including (Finance, Risk, etc) to drive reuseability and work with the heads across each vertical domains under Enterprise Functions.
Proven end‑to‑end delivery of data, analytics and AI initiatives driving revenue growth, productivity uplift, cost reduction and improved customer outcomes.
Strong commercial acumen, with the ability to prioritise data and AI investments based on ROI and business impact.
Executive‑level influence, shaping strategic decisions, funding priorities and enterprise direction.
Demonstrated operating‑model and change leadership, embedding analytics and decision intelligence into business decision‑making and operating rhythms.
Strong technical judgement across modern data ecosystems, data products and reusable platform foundations.
Practical experience scaling AI (ML and GenAI) and advanced analytics into production with governance, monitoring and value tracking.
Proven capability to build sustainable data and AI capability, including workforce strategy, talent pipelines, succession planning and operating effectively in regulated environments.

Why join us?

We’re obsessed with becoming our customers' #1 banking partner for life and we’re looking for people who are passionate about helping us achieve that goal. In return, we’re committed to making Westpac the best place to work in the country. Here are just a few of the ways we’re already doing that:

Special offers on banking products and discounts from top brands, including generous employee only mortgage rates!
Flexible work arrangements to help you achieve a greater work/life balance, and a variety of leave options including Culture, Lifestyle and Wellbeing leave.
Tailored learning and development opportunities to help your grow your career within the bank.
Lots of opportunities to ‘give back’ to the Community by getting involved in our many volunteering initiatives.

Create your future today

To get started, simply click on the APPLY or APPLY NOW button

We’re all about creating a supportive and inclusive community. We welcome everyone no matter your age, gender, background, or abilities. We also provide additional support to welcome our veterans, Indigenous Australians and neurodiverse community.

If you need any adjustments during the recruitment process, you can find out more information and additional contact details by visiting the "People with Disability and/or needing Accessibility Requirements" page on our website .

We’re all about helping our people grow and succeed. Whether you’re a current employee, trainee, or intern, we’re committed to your career development Check out our FAQs and internal support resources here.
